# Adaptive Strategies - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

[unclecode/crawl4ai 61.8k 6.3k](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai)

Advanced Adaptive Strategies
============================

Overview
--------

While the default adaptive crawling configuration works well for most use cases, understanding the underlying strategies and scoring mechanisms allows you to fine-tune the crawler for specific domains and requirements.

The Three-Layer Scoring System
------------------------------

### 1\. Coverage Score

Coverage measures how comprehensively your knowledge base covers the query terms and related concepts.

#### Mathematical Foundation

`Coverage(K, Q) = Σ(t ∈ Q) score(t, K) / |Q|  where score(t, K) = doc_coverage(t) × (1 + freq_boost(t))`
Copy

#### Components

*   **Document Coverage**: Percentage of documents containing the term
*   **Frequency Boost**: Logarithmic bonus for term frequency
*   **Query Decomposition**: Handles multi-word queries intelligently

#### Tuning Coverage

`# For technical documentation with specific terminology config = AdaptiveConfig(     confidence_threshold=0.85,  # Require high coverage     top_k_links=5              # Cast wider net )  # For general topics with synonyms config = AdaptiveConfig(     confidence_threshold=0.6,   # Lower threshold     top_k_links=2              # More focused )`
Copy

### 2\. Consistency Score

Consistency evaluates whether the information across pages is coherent and non-contradictory.

#### How It Works

1.  Extracts key statements from each document
2.  Compares statements across documents
3.  Measures agreement vs. contradiction
4.  Returns normalized score (0-1)

#### Practical Impact

*   **High consistency (>0.8)**: Information is reliable and coherent
*   **Medium consistency (0.5-0.8)**: Some variation, but generally aligned
*   **Low consistency (<0.5)**: Conflicting information, need more sources

### 3\. Saturation Score

Saturation detects when new pages stop providing novel information.

#### Detection Algorithm

`# Tracks new unique terms per page new_terms_page_1 = 50 new_terms_page_2 = 30  # 60% of first new_terms_page_3 = 15  # 50% of second new_terms_page_4 = 5   # 33% of third # Saturation detected: rapidly diminishing returns`
Copy

#### Configuration

`config = AdaptiveConfig(     min_gain_threshold=0.1  # Stop if <10% new information )`
Copy

Link Ranking Algorithm
----------------------

### Expected Information Gain

Each uncrawled link is scored based on:

`ExpectedGain(link) = Relevance × Novelty × Authority`
Copy

#### 1\. Relevance Scoring

Uses BM25 algorithm on link preview text:

`relevance = BM25(link.preview_text, query)`
Copy

Factors: - Term frequency in preview - Inverse document frequency - Preview length normalization

#### 2\. Novelty Estimation

Measures how different the link appears from already-crawled content:

`novelty = 1 - max_similarity(preview, knowledge_base)`
Copy

Prevents crawling duplicate or highly similar pages.

#### 3\. Authority Calculation

URL structure and domain analysis:

`authority = f(domain_rank, url_depth, url_structure)`
Copy

Factors: - Domain reputation - URL depth (fewer slashes = higher authority) - Clean URL structure

Domain-Specific Configurations
------------------------------

### Technical Documentation

`tech_doc_config = AdaptiveConfig(     confidence_threshold=0.85,     max_pages=30,     top_k_links=3,     min_gain_threshold=0.05  # Keep crawling for small gains )`
Copy

Rationale: - High threshold ensures comprehensive coverage - Lower gain threshold captures edge cases - Moderate link following for depth

### News & Articles

`news_config = AdaptiveConfig(     confidence_threshold=0.6,     max_pages=10,     top_k_links=5,     min_gain_threshold=0.15  # Stop quickly on repetition )`
Copy

Rationale: - Lower threshold (articles often repeat information) - Higher gain threshold (avoid duplicate stories) - More links per page (explore different perspectives)

### E-commerce

`ecommerce_config = AdaptiveConfig(     confidence_threshold=0.7,     max_pages=20,     top_k_links=2,     min_gain_threshold=0.1 )`
Copy

Rationale: - Balanced threshold for product variations - Focused link following (avoid infinite products) - Standard gain threshold

### Research & Academic

`research_config = AdaptiveConfig(     confidence_threshold=0.9,     max_pages=50,     top_k_links=4,     min_gain_threshold=0.02  # Very low - capture citations )`
Copy

Rationale: - Very high threshold for completeness - Many pages allowed for thorough research - Very low gain threshold to capture references

Performance Optimization
------------------------

### Memory Management

`# For large crawls, use streaming config = AdaptiveConfig(     max_pages=100,     save_state=True,     state_path="large_crawl.json" )  # Periodically clean state if len(state.knowledge_base) > 1000:     # Keep only the top 500 most relevant docs     top_content = adaptive.get_relevant_content(top_k=500)     keep_indices = {d["index"] for d in top_content}     state.knowledge_base = [         doc for i, doc in enumerate(state.knowledge_base) if i in keep_indices     ]`
Copy

### Parallel Processing

`# Use multiple start points start_urls = [     "https://docs.example.com/intro",     "https://docs.example.com/api",     "https://docs.example.com/guides" ]  # Crawl in parallel tasks = [     adaptive.digest(url, query)     for url in start_urls ] results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)`
Copy

Debugging & Analysis
--------------------

### Enable Verbose Logging

`import logging  logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG) adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler, config, verbose=True)`
Copy

### Analyze Crawl Patterns

`# After crawling state = await adaptive.digest(start_url, query)  # Analyze link selection print("Link selection order:") for i, url in enumerate(state.crawl_order):     print(f"{i+1}. {url}")  # Analyze term discovery print("\nTerm discovery rate:") for i, new_terms in enumerate(state.new_terms_history):     print(f"Page {i+1}: {new_terms} new terms")  # Analyze score progression print("\nScore progression:") print(f"Coverage: {state.metrics['coverage_history']}") print(f"Saturation: {state.metrics['saturation_history']}")`
Copy

### Export for Analysis

`# Export detailed metrics import json  metrics = {     "query": query,     "total_pages": len(state.crawled_urls),     "confidence": adaptive.confidence,     "coverage_stats": adaptive.coverage_stats,     "crawl_order": state.crawl_order,     "term_frequencies": dict(state.term_frequencies),     "new_terms_history": state.new_terms_history }  with open("crawl_analysis.json", "w") as f:     json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)`
Copy

Custom Strategies
-----------------

### Implementing a Custom Strategy

`from crawl4ai.adaptive_crawler import CrawlStrategy  class DomainSpecificStrategy(CrawlStrategy):     def calculate_coverage(self, state: CrawlState) -> float:         # Custom coverage calculation         # e.g., weight certain terms more heavily         pass      def calculate_consistency(self, state: CrawlState) -> float:         # Custom consistency logic         # e.g., domain-specific validation         pass      def rank_links(self, links: List[Link], state: CrawlState) -> List[Link]:         # Custom link ranking         # e.g., prioritize specific URL patterns         pass  # Use custom strategy adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(     crawler,     config=config,     strategy=DomainSpecificStrategy() )`
Copy

### Combining Strategies

`class HybridStrategy(CrawlStrategy):     def __init__(self):         self.strategies = [             TechnicalDocStrategy(),             SemanticSimilarityStrategy(),             URLPatternStrategy()         ]      def calculate_confidence(self, state: CrawlState) -> float:         # Weighted combination of strategies         scores = [s.calculate_confidence(state) for s in self.strategies]         weights = [0.5, 0.3, 0.2]         return sum(s * w for s, w in zip(scores, weights))`
Copy

Best Practices
--------------

### 1\. Start Conservative

Begin with default settings and adjust based on results:

`# Start with defaults result = await adaptive.digest(url, query)  # Analyze and adjust if adaptive.confidence < 0.7:     config.max_pages += 10     config.confidence_threshold -= 0.1`
Copy

### 2\. Monitor Resource Usage

`import psutil  # Check memory before large crawls memory_percent = psutil.virtual_memory().percent if memory_percent > 80:     config.max_pages = min(config.max_pages, 20)`
Copy

### 3\. Use Domain Knowledge

`# For API documentation if "api" in start_url:     config.top_k_links = 2  # APIs have clear structure  # For blogs if "blog" in start_url:     config.min_gain_threshold = 0.2  # Avoid similar posts`
Copy

### 4\. Validate Results

`# Always validate the knowledge base relevant_content = adaptive.get_relevant_content(top_k=10)  # Check coverage query_terms = set(query.lower().split()) covered_terms = set()  for doc in relevant_content:     content_lower = doc['content'].lower()     for term in query_terms:         if term in content_lower:             covered_terms.add(term)  coverage_ratio = len(covered_terms) / len(query_terms) print(f"Query term coverage: {coverage_ratio:.0%}")`
Copy

Next Steps
----------

*   Explore [Custom Strategy Implementation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/tutorials/custom-adaptive-strategies.md)
    
*   Learn about [Knowledge Base Management](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/tutorials/knowledge-base-management.md)
    
*   See [Performance Benchmarks](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/benchmarks/adaptive-performance.md)

Crawl4AI is the #1 trending GitHub repository, actively maintained by a vibrant community. It delivers blazing-fast, AI-ready web crawling tailored for large language models, AI agents, and data pipelines. Fully open source, flexible, and built for real-time performance, **Crawl4AI** empowers developers with unmatched speed, precision, and deployment ease.

🆕 AI Assistant Skill Now Available!
------------------------------------

### 🤖 Crawl4AI Skill for Claude & AI Assistants

Supercharge your AI coding assistant with complete Crawl4AI knowledge! Download our comprehensive skill package that includes:

*   📚 Complete SDK reference (23K+ words)
*   🚀 Ready-to-use extraction scripts
*   ⚡ Schema generation for efficient scraping
*   🔧 Version 0.7.4 compatible

[📦 Download Skill Package](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/assets/crawl4ai-skill.zip)

Works with Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, and other AI coding assistants. Import the .zip file into your AI assistant's skill/knowledge system.

🎯 New: Adaptive Web Crawling
-----------------------------

Crawl4AI now features intelligent adaptive crawling that knows when to stop! Using advanced information foraging algorithms, it determines when sufficient information has been gathered to answer your query.

[Learn more about Adaptive Crawling →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/)

Quick Start
-----------

Here's a quick example to show you how easy it is to use Crawl4AI with its asynchronous capabilities:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-5)     # Create an instance of AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-6)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-7)         # Run the crawler on a URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-8)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://crawl4ai.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-10)         # Print the extracted content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-11)         print(result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-13) # Run the async main function [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/#__codelineno-0-14) asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

Video Tutorial
--------------

* * *

What Does Crawl4AI Do?
----------------------

Crawl4AI is a feature-rich crawler and scraper that aims to:

1. **Generate Clean Markdown**: Perfect for RAG pipelines or direct ingestion into LLMs.  
2. **Structured Extraction**: Parse repeated patterns with CSS, XPath, or LLM-based extraction.  
3. **Advanced Browser Control**: Hooks, proxies, stealth modes, session re-use—fine-grained control.  
4. **High Performance**: Parallel crawling, chunk-based extraction, real-time use cases.  
5. **Open Source**: No forced API keys, no paywalls—everyone can access their data.

**Core Philosophies**: - **Democratize Data**: Free to use, transparent, and highly configurable.  
\- **LLM Friendly**: Minimally processed, well-structured text, images, and metadata, so AI models can easily consume it.

* * *

Documentation Structure
-----------------------

To help you get started, we’ve organized our docs into clear sections:

*   **Setup & Installation**  
    Basic instructions to install Crawl4AI via pip or Docker.
*   **Quick Start**  
    A hands-on introduction showing how to do your first crawl, generate Markdown, and do a simple extraction.
*   **Core**  
    Deeper guides on single-page crawling, advanced browser/crawler parameters, content filtering, and caching.
*   **Advanced**  
    Explore link & media handling, lazy loading, hooking & authentication, proxies, session management, and more.
*   **Extraction**  
    Detailed references for no-LLM (CSS, XPath) vs. LLM-based strategies, chunking, and clustering approaches.
*   **API Reference**  
    Find the technical specifics of each class and method, including `AsyncWebCrawler`, `arun()`, and `CrawlResult`.

Throughout these sections, you’ll find code samples you can **copy-paste** into your environment. If something is missing or unclear, raise an issue or PR.

* * *

How You Can Support
-------------------

*   **Star & Fork**: If you find Crawl4AI helpful, star the repo on GitHub or fork it to add your own features.
*   **File Issues**: Encounter a bug or missing feature? Let us know by filing an issue, so we can improve.
*   **Pull Requests**: Whether it’s a small fix, a big feature, or better docs—contributions are always welcome.
*   **Join Discord**: Come chat about web scraping, crawling tips, or AI workflows with the community.
*   **Spread the Word**: Mention Crawl4AI in your blog posts, talks, or on social media.

**Our mission**: to empower everyone—students, researchers, entrepreneurs, data scientists—to access, parse, and shape the world’s data with speed, cost-efficiency, and creative freedom.

* * *

Quick Links
-----------

*   **[GitHub Repo](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai)
    **
*   **[Installation Guide](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/)
    **
*   **[Quick Start](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/)

*   **[API Reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/)
    **
*   **[Changelog](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
    **

Thank you for joining me on this journey. Let’s keep building an **open, democratic** approach to data extraction and AI together.

Happy Crawling!  
— _Unclecode, Founder & Maintainer of Crawl4AI_

# Contributing Guide - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Contributing to Crawl4AI
========================

Welcome to the Crawl4AI project! As an open-source library for web crawling and AI integration, we value contributions from the community. This guide explains our branching strategy, how to contribute effectively, and the overall release process. Our goal is to maintain a stable, collaborative environment where bug fixes, features, and improvements can be integrated smoothly while allowing for experimental development.

We follow a GitFlow-inspired workflow to ensure predictability and quality. Releases occur approximately every two weeks, with a focus on semantic versioning, comprehensive documentation, and user-friendly updates.

Core Branches
-------------

*   **main**: The stable branch containing production-ready code. It's always identical to the latest released version and is tagged for releases. Do not submit PRs directly here.
*   **develop**: The primary integration branch for ongoing development. This is where all contributions (bug fixes, minor features, documentation updates) are merged. Submit your pull requests targeting this branch.
*   **next**: Reserved for the lead maintainer (Unclecode) to experiment with major features, refactors, or cutting-edge changes. These are merged into `develop` when ready.
*   **release/vX.Y.Z**: Temporary branches created from `develop` for final release preparations (e.g., version bumps, demos, release notes). These are short-lived and deleted after the release.

Contributor Workflow
--------------------

We encourage contributions of all kinds: bug fixes, new features, documentation improvements, tests, or even Docker enhancements. Follow these steps to contribute:

1.  **Fork the Repository**: Create your own fork on GitHub.
2.  **Create a Branch**: Base your work on the `develop` branch.
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/CONTRIBUTING/#__codelineno-0-1) git checkout develop [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/CONTRIBUTING/#__codelineno-0-2) git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name  # Or bugfix/your-bugfix-name`
    
3.  **Make Changes**:
    
    *   Implement your feature or fix.
    *   If updating documentation (e.g., README.md, mkdocs.yml, or docs/blog/), ensure version references are consistent (e.g., update site\_name in mkdocs.yml to reflect the upcoming version if relevant).
    *   For Docker-related changes (e.g., Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, or docs/md\_v2/core/docker-deployment.md), test locally and include build instructions in your PR description.
    *   Add tests if applicable (run `pytest` to verify).
    *   Follow code style guidelines (use black for formatting).
4.  **Commit and Push**:
    *   Use descriptive commit messages (e.g., "Fix: Resolve issue with async crawling").
    *   Push to your fork: `git push origin feature/your-feature-name`.
5.  **Submit a Pull Request**:
    *   Target the `develop` branch.
    *   Provide a clear description: What does it do? Link to any issues. Include screenshots or code examples if helpful.
    *   If your change affects documentation or Docker, mention how it aligns with the version (e.g., "Updates Docker docs for v0.7.0 compatibility").
    *   We'll review and merge approved PRs into `develop`.
6.  **Discuss Large Changes**: For major features or experimental ideas, open an issue first to align with the project's direction.

If your PR involves breaking changes, include a migration guide in the description.

Lead Maintainer's Workflow (For Reference)
------------------------------------------

*   The lead maintainer (Unclecode) uses the `next` branch for isolated experimental work.
*   Features from `next` are periodically merged into `develop` (via rebase and merge) to keep everything in sync.
*   This isolation ensures your contributions aren't disrupted by ongoing major changes.

Release Process (High-Level Overview)
-------------------------------------

Releases happen bi-weekly to ship improvements regularly. As a contributor, your merged changes in `develop` will be included in the next release unless specified otherwise. Here's a summary of what happens:

*   **Preparation**: A temporary `release/vX.Y.Z` branch is created from `develop`. Any ready features from `next` are merged here.
*   **Final Updates**:
    *   Version bump in code (e.g., `__version__.py`).
    *   Creation of a demo script in `examples/` to showcase new features.
    *   Writing release notes in `docs/blog/` (personal "I" voice from Unclecode, with code examples, impacts, and migration guides if needed).
    *   Documentation updates: README.md (highlights, version refs), mkdocs.yml (site\_name with version), docs/blog/index.md (add new release), and copying notes to `docs/md_v2/blog/releases/`.
    *   Docker updates: Dockerfile (version arg), docker-compose.yml, deploy/docker/README.md, and docs/md\_v2/core/docker-deployment.md. A release candidate image (e.g., `X.Y.Z-r1`) is built and tested.
*   **Testing and Merge**: Full tests run; changes committed and merged to `main` with a tag.
*   **Publication**: Tagged release on GitHub (with notes), publish to PyPI, and push Docker images (stable and `latest` after testing).
*   **Sync**: Back-merge to `develop` and reset `next` for the next cycle.

Semantic versioning is used: MAJOR for breaking changes, MINOR for features, PATCH for fixes. Pre-releases (e.g., `-rc1`) may be used for testing.

If your contribution requires Docker testing or affects docs, it may be part of this step—feel free to suggest updates in your PR.

Benefits of This Approach
-------------------------

*   **Stability**: `main` is always reliable for users.
*   **Collaboration**: Fixed PR target (`develop`) makes contributing straightforward.
*   **Isolation**: Experimental work in `next` doesn't block team progress.
*   **User-Focused**: Releases include demos, detailed notes, and updated docs/Docker for easy adoption.
*   **Predictability**: Bi-weekly cadence keeps the project active.

Checklist for Contributors
--------------------------

Before submitting a PR:

*   \[ \] Based on and targeting `develop`.
*   \[ \] Tests pass (`pytest`).
*   \[ \] Docs updated if needed (e.g., version refs in mkdocs.yml, Docker files).
*   \[ \] No breaking changes without a migration guide.
*   \[ \] Descriptive title and description.

Common Issues
-------------

*   **Merge Conflicts**: Rebase your branch on latest `develop` before PR.
*   **Docker Builds**: Test multi-arch (amd64/arm64) locally if changing Dockerfile.
*   **Version Consistency**: Ensure any version mentions match semantic rules.

Communication
-------------

*   Open issues for discussions or bugs.
*   Join our Discord (link in README) for real-time help.
*   After releases, announcements go to GitHub, Discord, and social media.

Thanks for contributing to Crawl4AI — we appreciate your help in making it better!

_Last Updated: Feb 3, 2026_

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Crawl Dispatcher - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl Dispatcher
================

We’re excited to announce a **Crawl Dispatcher** module that can handle **thousands** of crawling tasks simultaneously. By efficiently managing system resources (memory, CPU, network), this dispatcher ensures high-performance data extraction at scale. It also provides **real-time monitoring** of each crawler’s status, memory usage, and overall progress.

Stay tuned—this feature is **coming soon** in an upcoming release of Crawl4AI! For the latest news, keep an eye on our changelogs and follow [@unclecode](https://twitter.com/unclecode)
 on X.

Below is a **sample** of how the dispatcher’s performance monitor might look in action:

![Crawl Dispatcher Performance Monitor](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/assets/images/dispatcher.png)

We can’t wait to bring you this streamlined, **scalable** approach to multi-URL crawling—**watch this space** for updates!

# File Downloading - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Download Handling in Crawl4AI
=============================

This guide explains how to use Crawl4AI to handle file downloads during crawling. You'll learn how to trigger downloads, specify download locations, and access downloaded files.

Enabling Downloads
------------------

To enable downloads, set the `accept_downloads` parameter in the `BrowserConfig` object and pass it to the crawler.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-1) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-3) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-4)     config = BrowserConfig(accept_downloads=True)  # Enable downloads globally [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-6)         # ... your crawling logic ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-0-8) asyncio.run(main())`

Specifying Download Location
----------------------------

Specify the download directory using the `downloads_path` attribute in the `BrowserConfig` object. If not provided, Crawl4AI defaults to creating a "downloads" directory inside the `.crawl4ai` folder in your home directory.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-2) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-4) downloads_path = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "my_downloads")  # Custom download path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-5) os.makedirs(downloads_path, exist_ok=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-7) config = BrowserConfig(accept_downloads=True, downloads_path=downloads_path) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-9) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-11)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-1-12)         # ...`

Triggering Downloads
--------------------

Downloads are typically triggered by user interactions on a web page, such as clicking a download button. Use `js_code` in `CrawlerRunConfig` to simulate these actions and `wait_for` to allow sufficient time for downloads to start.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai.async_configs import CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-3) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-4)     js_code=""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-5)         const downloadLink = document.querySelector('a[href$=".exe"]'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-6)         if (downloadLink) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-7)             downloadLink.click(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-8)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-9)     """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-10)     wait_for=5  # Wait 5 seconds for the download to start [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-2-13) result = await crawler.arun(url="https://www.python.org/downloads/", config=config)`

Accessing Downloaded Files
--------------------------

The `downloaded_files` attribute of the `CrawlResult` object contains paths to downloaded files.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-1) if result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-2)     print("Downloaded files:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-3)     for file_path in result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-4)         print(f"- {file_path}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-5)         file_size = os.path.getsize(file_path) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-6)         print(f"- File size: {file_size} bytes") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-7) else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-3-8)     print("No files downloaded.")`

Example: Downloading Multiple Files
-----------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-1) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-2) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-3) from pathlib import Path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-5) async def download_multiple_files(url: str, download_path: str): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-6)     config = BrowserConfig(accept_downloads=True, downloads_path=download_path) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-7)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-8)         run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-9)             js_code=""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-10)                 const downloadLinks = document.querySelectorAll('a[download]'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-11)                 for (const link of downloadLinks) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-12)                     link.click(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-13)                     // Delay between clicks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-14)                     await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000));   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-15)                 } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-16)             """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-17)             wait_for=10  # Wait for all downloads to start [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-18)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-19)         result = await crawler.arun(url=url, config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-21)         if result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-22)             print("Downloaded files:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-23)             for file in result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-24)                 print(f"- {file}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-25)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-26)             print("No files downloaded.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-28) # Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-29) download_path = os.path.join(Path.home(), ".crawl4ai", "downloads") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-30) os.makedirs(download_path, exist_ok=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/file-downloading/#__codelineno-4-32) asyncio.run(download_multiple_files("https://www.python.org/downloads/windows/", download_path))`

Important Considerations
------------------------

*   **Browser Context:** Downloads are managed within the browser context. Ensure `js_code` correctly targets the download triggers on the webpage.
*   **Timing:** Use `wait_for` in `CrawlerRunConfig` to manage download timing.
*   **Error Handling:** Handle errors to manage failed downloads or incorrect paths gracefully.
*   **Security:** Scan downloaded files for potential security threats before use.

This revised guide ensures consistency with the `Crawl4AI` codebase by using `BrowserConfig` and `CrawlerRunConfig` for all download-related configurations. Let me know if further adjustments are needed!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Anti-Bot & Fallback - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Anti-Bot Detection & Fallback
=============================

When crawling sites protected by anti-bot systems (Akamai, Cloudflare, PerimeterX, DataDome, Imperva, etc.), requests often get blocked with CAPTCHAs, 403 responses, or empty pages. Crawl4AI provides a layered retry and fallback system that automatically detects blocking and escalates through multiple strategies until content is retrieved.

How Detection Works
-------------------

After each crawl attempt, Crawl4AI inspects the HTTP status code and HTML content for known anti-bot signals:

*   **HTTP 403/429** with short or empty response bodies
*   **Challenge pages** — Cloudflare "Just a moment", Akamai "Access Denied", PerimeterX block pages
*   **CAPTCHA injection** — reCAPTCHA, hCaptcha, or vendor-specific challenges on otherwise empty pages
*   **Firewall blocks** — Imperva/Incapsula resource iframes, Sucuri firewall pages, Cloudflare error codes

Detection uses structural HTML markers (specific element IDs, script sources, form actions) rather than generic keywords to minimize false positives. A normal page that happens to mention "CAPTCHA" or "Cloudflare" in its content will not be flagged.

When all attempts fail and blocking is still detected, the result is returned with `success=False` and `error_message` describing the block reason.

Configuration Options
---------------------

All anti-bot retry options live on `CrawlerRunConfig`:

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `proxy_config` | `ProxyConfig`, `list[ProxyConfig]`, or `None` | `None` | Single proxy or ordered list of proxies to try. Each retry round iterates through the full list. Use `"direct"` or `ProxyConfig.DIRECT` in a list to explicitly try without a proxy. |
| `max_retries` | `int` | `0` | Number of retry rounds when blocking is detected. `0` = no retries. |
| `fallback_fetch_function` | `async (str) -> str` | `None` | Async function called as last resort. Takes URL, returns raw HTML. |

Escalation Chain
----------------

Each retry round tries every proxy in `proxy_config` in order. If all rounds are exhausted and the page is still blocked, the fallback fetch function is called as a last resort.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-1) For each round (1 + max_retries rounds): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-2)     1. Try proxy_config[0] (or direct if proxy_config is None) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-3)     2. If blocked → try proxy_config[1] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-4)     3. If blocked → try proxy_config[2] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-5)     4. ... continue through all proxies [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-6)     5. If any attempt succeeds → done [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-8) If all rounds exhausted and still blocked: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-0-9)     6. Call fallback_fetch_function(url) → process returned HTML`

Worst-case attempts before the fetch function: `(1 + max_retries) x len(proxy_config)`

Crawl Stats
-----------

Every crawl result includes a `crawl_stats` dict with detailed attempt tracking:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-1) result.crawl_stats = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-2)     "attempts": 3,                    # total browser attempts made [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-3)     "retries": 1,                     # retry rounds used (0 = succeeded first round) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-4)     "proxies_used": [                 # ordered list of every attempt [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-5)         {"proxy": None,               "status_code": 403, "blocked": True,  "reason": "Akamai block (Reference #)"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-6)         {"proxy": "proxy.io:8080",    "status_code": 403, "blocked": True,  "reason": "Akamai block (Reference #)"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-7)         {"proxy": "premium.io:9090",  "status_code": 200, "blocked": False, "reason": ""}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-8)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-9)     "fallback_fetch_used": False,     # whether fallback_fetch_function was called [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-10)     "resolved_by": "proxy",           # "direct" | "proxy" | "fallback_fetch" | null (all failed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-1-11) }`

Usage Examples
--------------

### Simple Retry (No Proxy)

Retry the crawl up to 3 times when blocking is detected. Useful when blocks are intermittent or IP-based.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-4) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=BrowserConfig(headless=True)) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-5)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-6)         url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-7)         config=CrawlerRunConfig(max_retries=3), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-2-8)     )`

### Single Proxy

Pass a single `ProxyConfig` — it's used on every attempt. Same behavior as always.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai.async_configs import ProxyConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-3) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-4)     max_retries=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-5)     proxy_config=ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-6)         server="http://proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-7)         username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-8)         password="pass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-9)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-3-10) )`

### Direct-First, Then Proxies

Try without a proxy first, then escalate to proxies if blocked. Use `ProxyConfig.DIRECT` (or the string `"direct"`) in the list to represent a no-proxy attempt.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-2)     max_retries=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-3)     proxy_config=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-4)         ProxyConfig.DIRECT,  # Try without proxy first [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-5)         ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-6)             server="http://datacenter-proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-7)             username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-8)             password="pass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-9)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-10)         ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-11)             server="http://residential-proxy.example.com:9090", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-12)             username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-13)             password="pass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-14)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-15)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-4-16) )`

With this setup, each round tries direct first, then datacenter, then residential. With `max_retries=1`, worst case is 2 rounds x 3 steps = 6 attempts.

### Proxy List (Escalation)

Pass a list of proxies. They're tried in order — first one that works wins. Within each retry round, the entire list is tried again.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-2)     max_retries=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-3)     proxy_config=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-4)         ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-5)             server="http://datacenter-proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-6)             username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-7)             password="pass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-8)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-9)         ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-10)             server="http://residential-proxy.example.com:9090", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-11)             username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-12)             password="pass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-13)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-14)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-5-15) )`

With this setup, each round tries the datacenter proxy first, then the residential proxy. With `max_retries=1`, worst case is 2 rounds x 2 proxies = 4 attempts.

### Fallback Fetch Function

When all browser-based attempts fail, call a custom async function as a last resort. This function receives the URL and must return raw HTML as a string. The returned HTML is processed through the normal pipeline (markdown generation, extraction, etc.).

This is useful when you have access to a scraping API, a pre-fetched cache, or any other source of HTML.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-1) import aiohttp [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-3) async def my_scraping_api(url: str) -> str: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-4)     """Fetch HTML via an external scraping API.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-5)     async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-6)         async with session.get( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-7)             "https://api.my-scraping-service.com/fetch", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-8)             params={"url": url, "format": "html"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-9)             headers={"Authorization": "Bearer MY_TOKEN"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-10)         ) as resp: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-11)             if resp.status == 200: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-12)                 return await resp.text() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-13)             raise RuntimeError(f"API error: {resp.status}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-15) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-16)     max_retries=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-17)     fallback_fetch_function=my_scraping_api, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-6-18) )`

The function can do anything — call an API, read from a database, return cached HTML, or make a simple HTTP request with a different library. Crawl4AI does not care how the HTML is obtained.

### Full Escalation (All Features Combined)

This example combines every layer: stealth mode, a list of proxies tried in order, retries, and a final fetch function.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-1) import aiohttp [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-3) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, ProxyConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-5) # Last-resort: fetch HTML via an external service [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-6) async def external_fetch(url: str) -> str: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-7)     async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-8)         async with session.post( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-9)             "https://api.my-service.com/scrape", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-10)             json={"url": url, "render_js": True}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-11)             headers={"Authorization": "Bearer MY_TOKEN"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-12)         ) as resp: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-13)             return await resp.text() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-15) browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-16)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-17)     enable_stealth=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-18) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-20) crawl_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-21)     magic=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-22)     wait_until="load", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-23)     max_retries=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-25)     # Proxies tried in order — cheapest first [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-26)     proxy_config=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-27)         ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-28)             server="http://datacenter-proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-29)             username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-30)             password="pass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-31)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-32)         ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-33)             server="http://residential-proxy.example.com:9090", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-34)             username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-35)             password="pass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-36)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-37)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-39)     # Last resort — called after all retries and proxies are exhausted [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-40)     fallback_fetch_function=external_fetch, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-41) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-43) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-44)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-45)         url="https://protected-site.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-46)         config=crawl_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-47)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-49)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-50)         print(f"Got {len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)} chars of markdown") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-51)         print(f"Resolved by: {result.crawl_stats['resolved_by']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-52)         print(f"Attempts: {result.crawl_stats['attempts']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-53)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/#__codelineno-7-54)         print(f"All attempts failed: {result.error_message}")`

**What happens step by step:**

| Round | Attempt | What runs |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1   | 1   | Datacenter proxy — blocked |
| 1   | 2   | Residential proxy — blocked |
| 2   | 1   | Datacenter proxy — blocked |
| 2   | 2   | Residential proxy — blocked |
| 3   | 1   | Datacenter proxy — blocked |
| 3   | 2   | Residential proxy — blocked |
| \-  | \-  | `external_fetch(url)` called — returns HTML |

That's up to 6 browser attempts + 1 function call before giving up.

Tips
----

*   **Start with `max_retries=0`** and a `fallback_fetch_function` if you just want a safety net without burning time on retries.
*   **Order proxies cheapest-first** — datacenter proxies before residential, residential before premium.
*   **Combine with stealth mode** — `BrowserConfig(enable_stealth=True)` and `CrawlerRunConfig(magic=True)` reduce the chance of being blocked in the first place.
*   **`wait_until="load"`** is important for anti-bot sites — the default `domcontentloaded` can return before the anti-bot sensor finishes.
*   **Check `crawl_stats`** to understand what happened — how many attempts, which proxy worked, whether the fallback function was needed.

See Also
--------

*   [Proxy & Security](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/)
     — Proxy setup, authentication, and rotation
*   [Undetected Browser](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/undetected-browser/)
     — Stealth mode and browser fingerprint evasion
*   [Session Management](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/)
     — Maintaining sessions across requests

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Overview - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Overview of Some Important Advanced Features
============================================

(Proxy, PDF, Screenshot, SSL, Headers, & Storage State)

Crawl4AI offers multiple power-user features that go beyond simple crawling. This tutorial covers:

1. **Proxy Usage**  
2. **Capturing PDFs & Screenshots**  
3. **Handling SSL Certificates**  
4. **Custom Headers**  
5. **Session Persistence & Local Storage**  
6. **Robots.txt Compliance**

> **Prerequisites**  
> \- You have a basic grasp of [AsyncWebCrawler Basics](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/)
>   
> \- You know how to run or configure your Python environment with Playwright installed

* * *

1\. Proxy Usage
---------------

If you need to route your crawl traffic through a proxy—whether for IP rotation, geo-testing, or privacy—Crawl4AI supports it via `BrowserConfig.proxy_config`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-5)     browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-6)         proxy_config={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-7)             "server": "http://proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-8)             "username": "myuser", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-9)             "password": "mypass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-10)         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-11)         headless=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-13)     crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-14)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-18)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-19)             url="https://www.whatismyip.com/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-20)             config=crawler_cfg [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-21)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-22)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-23)             print("[OK] Page fetched via proxy.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-24)             print("Page HTML snippet:", result.html[:200]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-25)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-26)             print("[ERROR]", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-28) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-0-29)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key Points**  
\- **`proxy_config`** expects a dict with `server` and optional auth credentials.  
\- Many commercial proxies provide an HTTP/HTTPS “gateway” server that you specify in `server`.  
\- If your proxy doesn’t need auth, omit `username`/`password`.

* * *

2\. Capturing PDFs & Screenshots
--------------------------------

Sometimes you need a visual record of a page or a PDF “printout.” Crawl4AI can do both in one pass:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-1) import os, asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-2) from base64 import b64decode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CacheMode, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-6)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-7)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-8)         screenshot=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-9)         pdf=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-12)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-13)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-14)             url="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_common_misconceptions", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-15)             config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-16)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-17)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-18)             print(f"Screenshot data present: {result.screenshot is not None}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-19)             print(f"PDF data present: {result.pdf is not None}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-21)             if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-22)                 print(f"[OK] Screenshot captured, size: {len(result.screenshot)} bytes") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-23)                 with open("wikipedia_screenshot.png", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-24)                     f.write(b64decode(result.screenshot)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-25)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-26)                 print("[WARN] Screenshot data is None.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-28)             if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-29)                 print(f"[OK] PDF captured, size: {len(result.pdf)} bytes") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-30)                 with open("wikipedia_page.pdf", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-31)                     f.write(result.pdf) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-32)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-33)                 print("[WARN] PDF data is None.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-35)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-36)             print("[ERROR]", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-38) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-1-39)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Why PDF + Screenshot?**  
\- Large or complex pages can be slow or error-prone with “traditional” full-page screenshots.  
\- Exporting a PDF is more reliable for very long pages. Crawl4AI automatically converts the first PDF page into an image if you request both.

**Relevant Parameters**  
\- **`pdf=True`**: Exports the current page as a PDF (base64-encoded in `result.pdf`).  
\- **`screenshot=True`**: Creates a screenshot (base64-encoded in `result.screenshot`).  
\- **`scan_full_page`** or advanced hooking can further refine how the crawler captures content.

* * *

3\. Handling SSL Certificates
-----------------------------

If you need to verify or export a site’s SSL certificate—for compliance, debugging, or data analysis—Crawl4AI can fetch it during the crawl:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-1) import asyncio, os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-5)     tmp_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "tmp") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-6)     os.makedirs(tmp_dir, exist_ok=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-8)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-9)         fetch_ssl_certificate=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-10)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-11)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-13)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-14)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-16)         if result.success and result.ssl_certificate: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-17)             cert = result.ssl_certificate [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-18)             print("\nCertificate Information:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-19)             print(f"Issuer (CN): {cert.issuer.get('CN', '')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-20)             print(f"Valid until: {cert.valid_until}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-21)             print(f"Fingerprint: {cert.fingerprint}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-23)             # Export in multiple formats: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-24)             cert.to_json(os.path.join(tmp_dir, "certificate.json")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-25)             cert.to_pem(os.path.join(tmp_dir, "certificate.pem")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-26)             cert.to_der(os.path.join(tmp_dir, "certificate.der")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-28)             print("\nCertificate exported to JSON/PEM/DER in 'tmp' folder.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-29)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-30)             print("[ERROR] No certificate or crawl failed.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-32) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-2-33)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key Points**  
\- **`fetch_ssl_certificate=True`** triggers certificate retrieval.  
\- `result.ssl_certificate` includes methods (`to_json`, `to_pem`, `to_der`) for saving in various formats (handy for server config, Java keystores, etc.).

* * *

4\. Custom Headers
------------------

Sometimes you need to set custom headers (e.g., language preferences, authentication tokens, or specialized user-agent strings). You can do this in multiple ways:

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-5)     # Option 1: Set headers at the crawler strategy level [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-6)     crawler1 = AsyncWebCrawler( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-7)         # The underlying strategy can accept headers in its constructor [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-8)         crawler_strategy=None  # We'll override below for clarity [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-10)     crawler1.crawler_strategy.update_user_agent("MyCustomUA/1.0") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-11)     crawler1.crawler_strategy.set_custom_headers({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-12)         "Accept-Language": "fr-FR,fr;q=0.9" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-13)     }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-14)     result1 = await crawler1.arun("https://www.example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-15)     print("Example 1 result success:", result1.success) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-17)     # Option 2: Pass headers directly to `arun()` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-18)     crawler2 = AsyncWebCrawler() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-19)     result2 = await crawler2.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-20)         url="https://www.example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-21)         headers={"Accept-Language": "es-ES,es;q=0.9"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-22)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-23)     print("Example 2 result success:", result2.success) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-25) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-3-26)     asyncio.run(main())``

**Notes**  
\- Some sites may react differently to certain headers (e.g., `Accept-Language`).  
\- If you need advanced user-agent randomization or client hints, see [Identity-Based Crawling (Anti-Bot)](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/)
 or use `UserAgentGenerator`.

* * *

5\. Session Persistence & Local Storage
---------------------------------------

Crawl4AI can preserve cookies and localStorage so you can continue where you left off—ideal for logging into sites or skipping repeated auth flows.

### 5.1 `storage_state`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-5)     storage_dict = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-6)         "cookies": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-7)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-8)                 "name": "session", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-9)                 "value": "abcd1234", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-10)                 "domain": "example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-11)                 "path": "/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-12)                 "expires": 1699999999.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-13)                 "httpOnly": False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-14)                 "secure": False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-15)                 "sameSite": "None" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-16)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-17)         ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-18)         "origins": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-19)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-20)                 "origin": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-21)                 "localStorage": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-22)                     {"name": "token", "value": "my_auth_token"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-23)                 ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-24)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-25)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-26)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-28)     # Provide the storage state as a dictionary to start "already logged in" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-29)     async with AsyncWebCrawler( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-30)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-31)         storage_state=storage_dict [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-32)     ) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-33)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/protected") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-34)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-35)             print("Protected page content length:", len(result.html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-36)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-37)             print("Failed to crawl protected page") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-39) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-4-40)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 5.2 Exporting & Reusing State

You can sign in once, export the browser context, and reuse it later—without re-entering credentials.

*   **`await context.storage_state(path="my_storage.json")`**: Exports cookies, localStorage, etc. to a file.
*   Provide `storage_state="my_storage.json"` on subsequent runs to skip the login step.

**See**: [Detailed session management tutorial](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/)
 or [Explanations → Browser Context & Managed Browser](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/)
 for more advanced scenarios (like multi-step logins, or capturing after interactive pages).

* * *

6\. Robots.txt Compliance
-------------------------

Crawl4AI supports respecting robots.txt rules with efficient caching:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-5)     # Enable robots.txt checking in config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-6)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-7)         check_robots_txt=True  # Will check and respect robots.txt rules [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-11)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-12)             "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-13)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-16)         if not result.success and result.status_code == 403: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-17)             print("Access denied by robots.txt") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-19) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-5-20)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key Points** - Robots.txt files are cached locally for efficiency - Cache is stored in `~/.crawl4ai/robots/robots_cache.db` - Cache has a default TTL of 7 days - If robots.txt can't be fetched, crawling is allowed - Returns 403 status code if URL is disallowed

* * *

Putting It All Together
-----------------------

Here’s a snippet that combines multiple “advanced” features (proxy, PDF, screenshot, SSL, custom headers, and session reuse) into one run. Normally, you’d tailor each setting to your project’s needs.
