`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-1) import os, asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-2) from base64 import b64decode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-6)     # 1. Browser config with proxy + headless [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-7)     browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-8)         proxy_config={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-9)             "server": "http://proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-10)             "username": "myuser", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-11)             "password": "mypass", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-12)         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-13)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-14)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-16)     # 2. Crawler config with PDF, screenshot, SSL, custom headers, and ignoring caches [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-17)     crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-18)         pdf=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-19)         screenshot=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-20)         fetch_ssl_certificate=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-21)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-22)         headers={"Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.8"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-23)         storage_state="my_storage.json",  # Reuse session from a previous sign-in [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-24)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-25)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-27)     # 3. Crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-28)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-29)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-30)             url = "https://secure.example.com/protected",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-31)             config=crawler_cfg [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-32)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-34)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-35)             print("[OK] Crawled the secure page. Links found:", len(result.links.get("internal", []))) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-36) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-37)             # Save PDF & screenshot [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-38)             if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-39)                 with open("result.pdf", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-40)                     f.write(b64decode(result.pdf)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-41)             if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-42)                 with open("result.png", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-43)                     f.write(b64decode(result.screenshot)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-44) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-45)             # Check SSL cert [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-46)             if result.ssl_certificate: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-47)                 print("SSL Issuer CN:", result.ssl_certificate.issuer.get("CN", "")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-48)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-49)             print("[ERROR]", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-50) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-51) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-6-52)     asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

* * *

7\. Anti-Bot Features (Stealth Mode & Undetected Browser)
---------------------------------------------------------

Crawl4AI provides two powerful features to bypass bot detection:

### 7.1 Stealth Mode

Stealth mode uses playwright-stealth to modify browser fingerprints and behaviors. Enable it with a simple flag:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-7-1) browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-7-2)     enable_stealth=True,  # Activates stealth mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-7-3)     headless=False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-7-4) )`

**When to use**: Sites with basic bot detection (checking navigator.webdriver, plugins, etc.)

### 7.2 Undetected Browser

For advanced bot detection, use the undetected browser adapter:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-1) from crawl4ai import UndetectedAdapter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai.async_crawler_strategy import AsyncPlaywrightCrawlerStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-4) # Create undetected adapter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-5) adapter = UndetectedAdapter() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-6) strategy = AsyncPlaywrightCrawlerStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-7)     browser_config=browser_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-8)     browser_adapter=adapter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-11) async with AsyncWebCrawler(crawler_strategy=strategy, config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-8-12)     # Your crawling code`

**When to use**: Sites with sophisticated bot detection (Cloudflare, DataDome, etc.)

### 7.3 Combining Both

For maximum evasion, combine stealth mode with undetected browser:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-9-1) browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-9-2)     enable_stealth=True,  # Enable stealth [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-9-3)     headless=False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-9-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-9-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/advanced-features/#__codelineno-9-6) adapter = UndetectedAdapter()  # Use undetected browser`

### Choosing the Right Approach

| Detection Level | Recommended Approach |
| --- | --- |
| No protection | Regular browser |
| Basic checks | Regular + Stealth mode |
| Advanced protection | Undetected browser |
| Maximum evasion | Undetected + Stealth mode |

**Best Practice**: Start with regular browser + stealth mode. Only use undetected browser if needed, as it may be slightly slower.

See [Undetected Browser Mode](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/undetected-browser/)
 for detailed examples.

* * *

Conclusion & Next Steps
-----------------------

You've now explored several **advanced** features:

*   **Proxy Usage**
*   **PDF & Screenshot** capturing for large or critical pages
*   **SSL Certificate** retrieval & exporting
*   **Custom Headers** for language or specialized requests
*   **Session Persistence** via storage state
*   **Robots.txt Compliance**
*   **Anti-Bot Features** (Stealth Mode & Undetected Browser)

With these power tools, you can build robust scraping workflows that mimic real user behavior, handle secure sites, capture detailed snapshots, manage sessions across multiple runs, and bypass bot detection—streamlining your entire data collection pipeline.

**Note**: In future versions, we may enable stealth mode and undetected browser by default. For now, users should explicitly enable these features when needed.

**Last Updated**: 2025-01-17

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Lazy Loading - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Handling Lazy-Loaded Images
---------------------------

Many websites now load images **lazily** as you scroll. If you need to ensure they appear in your final crawl (and in `result.media`), consider:

1. **`wait_for_images=True`** – Wait for images to fully load.  
2. **`scan_full_page`** – Force the crawler to scroll the entire page, triggering lazy loads.  
3. **`scroll_delay`** – Add small delays between scroll steps.

**Note**: If the site requires multiple “Load More” triggers or complex interactions, see the [Page Interaction docs](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/)
. For sites with virtual scrolling (Twitter/Instagram style), see the [Virtual Scroll docs](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/virtual-scroll/)
.

### Example: Ensuring Lazy Images Appear

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai.async_configs import CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-6)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-7)         # Force the crawler to wait until images are fully loaded [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-8)         wait_for_images=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-10)         # Option 1: If you want to automatically scroll the page to load images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-11)         scan_full_page=True,  # Tells the crawler to try scrolling the entire page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-12)         scroll_delay=0.5,     # Delay (seconds) between scroll steps [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-14)         # Option 2: If the site uses a 'Load More' or JS triggers for images, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-15)         # you can also specify js_code or wait_for logic here. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-17)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-18)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-19)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-21)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=BrowserConfig(headless=True)) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-22)         result = await crawler.arun("https://www.example.com/gallery", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-24)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-25)             images = result.media.get("images", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-26)             print("Images found:", len(images)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-27)             for i, img in enumerate(images[:5]): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-28)                 print(f"[Image {i}] URL: {img['src']}, Score: {img.get('score','N/A')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-29)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-30)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-32) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-0-33)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Explanation**:

*   **`wait_for_images=True`**  
    The crawler tries to ensure images have finished loading before finalizing the HTML.
*   **`scan_full_page=True`**  
    Tells the crawler to attempt scrolling from top to bottom. Each scroll step helps trigger lazy loading.
*   **`scroll_delay=0.5`**  
    Pause half a second between each scroll step. Helps the site load images before continuing.

**When to Use**:

*   **Lazy-Loading**: If images appear only when the user scrolls into view, `scan_full_page` + `scroll_delay` helps the crawler see them.
*   **Heavier Pages**: If a page is extremely long, be mindful that scanning the entire page can be slow. Adjust `scroll_delay` or the max scroll steps as needed.

* * *

Combining with Other Link & Media Filters
-----------------------------------------

You can still combine **lazy-load** logic with the usual **exclude\_external\_images**, **exclude\_domains**, or link filtration:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-2)     wait_for_images=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-3)     scan_full_page=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-4)     scroll_delay=0.5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-6)     # Filter out external images if you only want local ones [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-7)     exclude_external_images=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-9)     # Exclude certain domains for links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-10)     exclude_domains=["spammycdn.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/lazy-loading/#__codelineno-1-11) )`

This approach ensures you see **all** images from the main domain while ignoring external ones, and the crawler physically scrolls the entire page so that lazy-loading triggers.

* * *

Tips & Troubleshooting
----------------------

1. **Long Pages**  
\- Setting `scan_full_page=True` on extremely long or infinite-scroll pages can be resource-intensive.  
\- Consider using [hooks](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/)
 or specialized logic to load specific sections or “Load More” triggers repeatedly.

2. **Mixed Image Behavior**  
\- Some sites load images in batches as you scroll. If you’re missing images, increase your `scroll_delay` or call multiple partial scrolls in a loop with JS code or hooks.

3. **Combining with Dynamic Wait**  
\- If the site has a placeholder that only changes to a real image after a certain event, you might do `wait_for="css:img.loaded"` or a custom JS `wait_for`.

4. **Caching**  
\- If `cache_mode` is enabled, repeated crawls might skip some network fetches. If you suspect caching is missing new images, set `cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS` for fresh fetches.

* * *

With **lazy-loading** support, **wait\_for\_images**, and **scan\_full\_page** settings, you can capture the entire gallery or feed of images you expect—even if the site only loads them as the user scrolls. Combine these with the standard media filtering and domain exclusion for a complete link & media handling strategy.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Hooks & Auth - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Hooks & Auth in AsyncWebCrawler
===============================

Crawl4AI’s **hooks** let you customize the crawler at specific points in the pipeline:

1. **`on_browser_created`** – After browser creation.  
2. **`on_page_context_created`** – After a new context & page are created.  
3. **`before_goto`** – Just before navigating to a page.  
4. **`after_goto`** – Right after navigation completes.  
5. **`on_user_agent_updated`** – Whenever the user agent changes.  
6. **`on_execution_started`** – Once custom JavaScript execution begins.  
7. **`before_retrieve_html`** – Just before the crawler retrieves final HTML.  
8. **`before_return_html`** – Right before returning the HTML content.

**Important**: Avoid heavy tasks in `on_browser_created` since you don’t yet have a page context. If you need to _log in_, do so in **`on_page_context_created`**.

> note "Important Hook Usage Warning" **Avoid Misusing Hooks**: Do not manipulate page objects in the wrong hook or at the wrong time, as it can crash the pipeline or produce incorrect results. A common mistake is attempting to handle authentication prematurely—such as creating or closing pages in `on_browser_created`.
> 
> **Use the Right Hook for Auth**: If you need to log in or set tokens, use `on_page_context_created`. This ensures you have a valid page/context to work with, without disrupting the main crawling flow.
> 
> **Identity-Based Crawling**: For robust auth, consider identity-based crawling (or passing a session ID) to preserve state. Run your initial login steps in a separate, well-defined process, then feed that session to your main crawl—rather than shoehorning complex authentication into early hooks. Check out [Identity-Based Crawling](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/)
>  for more details.
> 
> **Be Cautious**: Overwriting or removing elements in the wrong hook can compromise the final crawl. Keep hooks focused on smaller tasks (like route filters, custom headers), and let your main logic (crawling, data extraction) proceed normally.

Below is an example demonstration.

* * *

Example: Using Hooks in AsyncWebCrawler
---------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-4) from playwright.async_api import Page, BrowserContext [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-7)     print("🔗 Hooks Example: Demonstrating recommended usage") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-9)     # 1) Configure the browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-10)     browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-11)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-12)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-15)     # 2) Configure the crawler run [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-16)     crawler_run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-17)         js_code="window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-18)         wait_for="body", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-19)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-20)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-22)     # 3) Create the crawler instance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-23)     crawler = AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-25)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-26)     # Define Hook Functions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-27)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-29)     async def on_browser_created(browser, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-30)         # Called once the browser instance is created (but no pages or contexts yet) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-31)         print("[HOOK] on_browser_created - Browser created successfully!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-32)         # Typically, do minimal setup here if needed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-33)         return browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-35)     async def on_page_context_created(page: Page, context: BrowserContext, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-36)         # Called right after a new page + context are created (ideal for auth or route config). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-37)         print("[HOOK] on_page_context_created - Setting up page & context.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-39)         # Example 1: Route filtering (e.g., block images) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-40)         async def route_filter(route): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-41)             if route.request.resource_type == "image": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-42)                 print(f"[HOOK] Blocking image request: {route.request.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-43)                 await route.abort() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-44)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-45)                 await route.continue_() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-46) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-47)         await context.route("**", route_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-49)         # Example 2: (Optional) Simulate a login scenario [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-50)         # (We do NOT create or close pages here, just do quick steps if needed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-51)         # e.g., await page.goto("https://example.com/login") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-52)         # e.g., await page.fill("input[name='username']", "testuser") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-53)         # e.g., await page.fill("input[name='password']", "password123") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-54)         # e.g., await page.click("button[type='submit']") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-55)         # e.g., await page.wait_for_selector("#welcome") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-56)         # e.g., await context.add_cookies([...]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-57)         # Then continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-58) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-59)         # Example 3: Adjust the viewport [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-60)         await page.set_viewport_size({"width": 1080, "height": 600}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-61)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-62) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-63)     async def before_goto( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-64)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext, url: str, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-65)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-66)         # Called before navigating to each URL. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-67)         print(f"[HOOK] before_goto - About to navigate: {url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-68)         # e.g., inject custom headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-69)         await page.set_extra_http_headers({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-70)             "Custom-Header": "my-value" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-71)         }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-72)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-73) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-74)     async def after_goto( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-75)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-76)         url: str, response, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-77)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-78)         # Called after navigation completes. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-79)         print(f"[HOOK] after_goto - Successfully loaded: {url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-80)         # e.g., wait for a certain element if we want to verify [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-81)         try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-82)             await page.wait_for_selector('.content', timeout=1000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-83)             print("[HOOK] Found .content element!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-84)         except: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-85)             print("[HOOK] .content not found, continuing anyway.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-86)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-87) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-88)     async def on_user_agent_updated( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-89)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-90)         user_agent: str, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-91)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-92)         # Called whenever the user agent updates. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-93)         print(f"[HOOK] on_user_agent_updated - New user agent: {user_agent}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-94)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-95) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-96)     async def on_execution_started(page: Page, context: BrowserContext, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-97)         # Called after custom JavaScript execution begins. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-98)         print("[HOOK] on_execution_started - JS code is running!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-99)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-100) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-101)     async def before_retrieve_html(page: Page, context: BrowserContext, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-102)         # Called before final HTML retrieval. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-103)         print("[HOOK] before_retrieve_html - We can do final actions") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-104)         # Example: Scroll again [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-105)         await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-106)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-107) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-108)     async def before_return_html( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-109)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext, html: str, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-110)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-111)         # Called just before returning the HTML in the result. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-112)         print(f"[HOOK] before_return_html - HTML length: {len(html)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-113)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-114) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-115)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-116)     # Attach Hooks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-117)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-118) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-119)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook("on_browser_created", on_browser_created) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-120)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-121)         "on_page_context_created", on_page_context_created [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-122)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-123)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook("before_goto", before_goto) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-124)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook("after_goto", after_goto) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-125)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-126)         "on_user_agent_updated", on_user_agent_updated [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-127)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-128)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-129)         "on_execution_started", on_execution_started [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-130)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-131)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-132)         "before_retrieve_html", before_retrieve_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-133)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-134)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-135)         "before_return_html", before_return_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-136)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-137) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-138)     await crawler.start() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-139) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-140)     # 4) Run the crawler on an example page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-141)     url = "https://example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-142)     result = await crawler.arun(url, config=crawler_run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-143) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-144)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-145)         print("\nCrawled URL:", result.url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-146)         print("HTML length:", len(result.html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-147)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-148)         print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-149) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-150)     await crawler.close() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-151) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-152) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/#__codelineno-0-153)     asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

Hook Lifecycle Summary
----------------------

1. **`on_browser_created`**:  
\- Browser is up, but **no** pages or contexts yet.  
\- Light setup only—don’t try to open or close pages here (that belongs in `on_page_context_created`).

2. **`on_page_context_created`**:  
\- Perfect for advanced **auth** or route blocking.  
\- You have a **page** + **context** ready but haven’t navigated to the target URL yet.

3. **`before_goto`**:  
\- Right before navigation. Typically used for setting **custom headers** or logging the target URL.

4. **`after_goto`**:  
\- After page navigation is done. Good place for verifying content or waiting on essential elements.

5. **`on_user_agent_updated`**:  
\- Whenever the user agent changes (for stealth or different UA modes).

6. **`on_execution_started`**:  
\- If you set `js_code` or run custom scripts, this runs once your JS is about to start.

7. **`before_retrieve_html`**:  
\- Just before the final HTML snapshot is taken. Often you do a final scroll or lazy-load triggers here.

8. **`before_return_html`**:  
\- The last hook before returning HTML to the `CrawlResult`. Good for logging HTML length or minor modifications.

* * *

When to Handle Authentication
-----------------------------

**Recommended**: Use **`on_page_context_created`** if you need to:

*   Navigate to a login page or fill forms
*   Set cookies or localStorage tokens
*   Block resource routes to avoid ads

This ensures the newly created context is under your control **before** `arun()` navigates to the main URL.

* * *

Additional Considerations
-------------------------

*   **Session Management**: If you want multiple `arun()` calls to reuse a single session, pass `session_id=` in your `CrawlerRunConfig`. Hooks remain the same.
*   **Performance**: Hooks can slow down crawling if they do heavy tasks. Keep them concise.
*   **Error Handling**: If a hook fails, the overall crawl might fail. Catch exceptions or handle them gracefully.
*   **Concurrency**: If you run `arun_many()`, each URL triggers these hooks in parallel. Ensure your hooks are thread/async-safe.

* * *

Conclusion
----------

Hooks provide **fine-grained** control over:

*   **Browser** creation (light tasks only)
*   **Page** and **context** creation (auth, route blocking)
*   **Navigation** phases
*   **Final HTML** retrieval

Follow the recommended usage: - **Login** or advanced tasks in `on_page_context_created`  
\- **Custom headers** or logs in `before_goto` / `after_goto`  
\- **Scrolling** or final checks in `before_retrieve_html` / `before_return_html`

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Identity Based Crawling - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Preserve Your Identity with Crawl4AI
====================================

Crawl4AI empowers you to navigate and interact with the web using your **authentic digital identity**, ensuring you’re recognized as a human and not mistaken for a bot. This tutorial covers:

1. **Managed Browsers** – The recommended approach for persistent profiles and identity-based crawling.  
2. **Magic Mode** – A simplified fallback solution for quick automation without persistent identity.

* * *

1\. Managed Browsers: Your Digital Identity Solution
----------------------------------------------------

**Managed Browsers** let developers create and use **persistent browser profiles**. These profiles store local storage, cookies, and other session data, letting you browse as your **real self**—complete with logins, preferences, and cookies.

### Key Benefits

*   **Authentic Browsing Experience**: Retain session data and browser fingerprints as though you’re a normal user.
*   **Effortless Configuration**: Once you log in or solve CAPTCHAs in your chosen data directory, you can re-run crawls without repeating those steps.
*   **Empowered Data Access**: If you can see the data in your own browser, you can automate its retrieval with your genuine identity.

* * *

Below is a **partial update** to your **Managed Browsers** tutorial, specifically the section about **creating a user-data directory** using **Playwright’s Chromium** binary rather than a system-wide Chrome/Edge. We’ll show how to **locate** that binary and launch it with a `--user-data-dir` argument to set up your profile. You can then point `BrowserConfig.user_data_dir` to that folder for subsequent crawls.

* * *

### Creating a User Data Directory (Command-Line Approach via Playwright)

If you installed Crawl4AI (which installs Playwright under the hood), you already have a Playwright-managed Chromium on your system. Follow these steps to launch that **Chromium** from your command line, specifying a **custom** data directory:

1. **Find** the Playwright Chromium binary: - On most systems, installed browsers go under a `~/.cache/ms-playwright/` folder or similar path.  
\- To see an overview of installed browsers, run:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-0-1) python -m playwright install --dry-run`

or

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-1-1) playwright install --dry-run`

(depending on your environment). This shows where Playwright keeps Chromium.

*   For instance, you might see a path like:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-2-1) ~/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1234/chrome-linux/chrome`
    
    on Linux, or a corresponding folder on macOS/Windows.

2. **Launch** the Playwright Chromium binary with a **custom** user-data directory:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-3-1) # Linux example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-3-2) ~/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1234/chrome-linux/chrome \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-3-3)     --user-data-dir=/home/<you>/my_chrome_profile`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-4-1) # macOS example (Playwright’s internal binary) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-4-2) ~/Library/Caches/ms-playwright/chromium-1234/chrome-mac/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-4-3)     --user-data-dir=/Users/<you>/my_chrome_profile`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-5-1) # Windows example (PowerShell/cmd) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-5-2) "C:\Users\<you>\AppData\Local\ms-playwright\chromium-1234\chrome-win\chrome.exe" ^ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-5-3)     --user-data-dir="C:\Users\<you>\my_chrome_profile"`

**Replace** the path with the actual subfolder indicated in your `ms-playwright` cache structure.  
\- This **opens** a fresh Chromium with your new or existing data folder.  
\- **Log into** any sites or configure your browser the way you want.  
\- **Close** when done—your profile data is saved in that folder.

3. **Use** that folder in **`BrowserConfig.user_data_dir`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-3) browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-4)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-5)     use_managed_browser=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-6)     user_data_dir="/home/<you>/my_chrome_profile", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-7)     browser_type="chromium" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-6-8) )`

\- Next time you run your code, it reuses that folder—**preserving** your session data, cookies, local storage, etc.

* * *

### Creating a Profile Using the Crawl4AI CLI (Easiest)

If you prefer a guided, interactive setup, use the built-in CLI to create and manage persistent browser profiles.

1.⠀Launch the profile manager:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-7-1) crwl profiles`

2.⠀Choose "Create new profile" and enter a profile name. A Chromium window opens so you can log in to sites and configure settings. When finished, return to the terminal and press `q` to save the profile.

3.⠀Profiles are saved under `~/.crawl4ai/profiles/<profile_name>` (for example: `/home/<you>/.crawl4ai/profiles/test_profile_1`) along with a `storage_state.json` for cookies and session data.

4.⠀Optionally, choose "List profiles" in the CLI to view available profiles and their paths.

5.⠀Use the saved path with `BrowserConfig.user_data_dir`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-3) profile_path = "/home/<you>/.crawl4ai/profiles/test_profile_1" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-5) browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-6)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-7)     use_managed_browser=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-8)     user_data_dir=profile_path, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-9)     browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-12) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-8-13)     result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com/private")`

The CLI also supports listing and deleting profiles, and even testing a crawl directly from the menu.

* * *

3\. Using Managed Browsers in Crawl4AI
--------------------------------------

Once you have a data directory with your session data, pass it to **`BrowserConfig`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-5)     # 1) Reference your persistent data directory [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-6)     browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-7)         headless=True,             # 'True' for automated runs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-8)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-9)         use_managed_browser=True,  # Enables persistent browser strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-10)         browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-11)         user_data_dir="/path/to/my-chrome-profile" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-14)     # 2) Standard crawl config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-15)     crawl_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-16)         wait_for="css:.logged-in-content" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-17)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-19)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-20)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com/private", config=crawl_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-21)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-22)             print("Successfully accessed private data with your identity!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-23)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-24)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-26) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-9-27)     asyncio.run(main())`

### Workflow

1. **Login** externally (via CLI or your normal Chrome with `--user-data-dir=...`).  
2. **Close** that browser.  
3. **Use** the same folder in `user_data_dir=` in Crawl4AI.  
4. **Crawl** – The site sees your identity as if you’re the same user who just logged in.

* * *

4\. Magic Mode: Simplified Automation
-------------------------------------

If you **don’t** need a persistent profile or identity-based approach, **Magic Mode** offers a quick way to simulate human-like browsing without storing long-term data.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-4)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-5)         url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-6)         config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-7)             magic=True,  # Simplifies a lot of interaction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-8)             remove_overlay_elements=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-9)             page_timeout=60000 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-10)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-10-11)     )`

**Magic Mode**:

*   Simulates a user-like experience
*   Randomizes user agent & navigator
*   Randomizes interactions & timings
*   Masks automation signals
*   Attempts pop-up handling

**But** it’s no substitute for **true** user-based sessions if you want a fully legitimate identity-based solution.

* * *

5\. Comparing Managed Browsers vs. Magic Mode
---------------------------------------------

| Feature | **Managed Browsers** | **Magic Mode** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Session Persistence** | Full localStorage/cookies retained in user\_data\_dir | No persistent data (fresh each run) |
| **Genuine Identity** | Real user profile with full rights & preferences | Emulated user-like patterns, but no actual identity |
| **Complex Sites** | Best for login-gated sites or heavy config | Simple tasks, minimal login or config needed |
| **Setup** | External creation of user\_data\_dir, then use in Crawl4AI | Single-line approach (`magic=True`) |
| **Reliability** | Extremely consistent (same data across runs) | Good for smaller tasks, can be less stable |

* * *

6\. Using the BrowserProfiler Class
-----------------------------------

Crawl4AI provides a dedicated `BrowserProfiler` class for managing browser profiles, making it easy to create, list, and delete profiles for identity-based browsing.

### Creating and Managing Profiles with BrowserProfiler

The `BrowserProfiler` class offers a comprehensive API for browser profile management:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-2) from crawl4ai import BrowserProfiler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-4) async def manage_profiles(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-5)     # Create a profiler instance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-6)     profiler = BrowserProfiler() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-8)     # Create a profile interactively - opens a browser window [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-9)     profile_path = await profiler.create_profile( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-10)         profile_name="my-login-profile"  # Optional: name your profile [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-11)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-13)     print(f"Profile saved at: {profile_path}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-15)     # List all available profiles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-16)     profiles = profiler.list_profiles() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-18)     for profile in profiles: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-19)         print(f"Profile: {profile['name']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-20)         print(f"  Path: {profile['path']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-21)         print(f"  Created: {profile['created']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-22)         print(f"  Browser type: {profile['type']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-24)     # Get a specific profile path by name [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-25)     specific_profile = profiler.get_profile_path("my-login-profile") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-27)     # Delete a profile when no longer needed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-28)     success = profiler.delete_profile("old-profile-name") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-11-30) asyncio.run(manage_profiles())`

**How profile creation works:** 1. A browser window opens for you to interact with 2. You log in to websites, set preferences, etc. 3. When you're done, press 'q' in the terminal to close the browser 4. The profile is saved in the Crawl4AI profiles directory 5. You can use the returned path with `BrowserConfig.user_data_dir`

### Interactive Profile Management

The `BrowserProfiler` also offers an interactive management console that guides you through profile creation, listing, and deletion:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-2) from crawl4ai import BrowserProfiler, AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-4) # Define a function to use a profile for crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-5) async def crawl_with_profile(profile_path, url): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-6)     browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-7)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-8)         use_managed_browser=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-9)         user_data_dir=profile_path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-12)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-13)         result = await crawler.arun(url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-14)         return result [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-16) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-17)     # Create a profiler instance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-18)     profiler = BrowserProfiler() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-20)     # Launch the interactive profile manager [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-21)     # Passing the crawl function as a callback adds a "crawl with profile" option [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-22)     await profiler.interactive_manager(crawl_callback=crawl_with_profile) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-12-24) asyncio.run(main())`

### Legacy Methods

For backward compatibility, the previous methods on `ManagedBrowser` are still available, but they delegate to the new `BrowserProfiler` class:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-13-1) from crawl4ai.browser_manager import ManagedBrowser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-13-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-13-3) # These methods still work but use BrowserProfiler internally [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-13-4) profiles = ManagedBrowser.list_profiles()`

### Complete Example

See the full example in `docs/examples/identity_based_browsing.py` for a complete demonstration of creating and using profiles for authenticated browsing using the new `BrowserProfiler` class.

* * *

7\. Locale, Timezone, and Geolocation Control
---------------------------------------------

In addition to using persistent profiles, Crawl4AI supports customizing your browser's locale, timezone, and geolocation settings. These features enhance your identity-based browsing experience by allowing you to control how websites perceive your location and regional settings.

### Setting Locale and Timezone

You can set the browser's locale and timezone through `CrawlerRunConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-4)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-5)         url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-6)         config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-7)             # Set browser locale (language and region formatting) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-8)             locale="fr-FR",  # French (France) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-10)             # Set browser timezone [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-11)             timezone_id="Europe/Paris", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-13)             # Other normal options... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-14)             magic=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-15)             page_timeout=60000 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-16)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-14-17)     )`

**How it works:** - `locale` affects language preferences, date formats, number formats, etc. - `timezone_id` affects JavaScript's Date object and time-related functionality - These settings are applied when creating the browser context and maintained throughout the session

### Configuring Geolocation

Control the GPS coordinates reported by the browser's geolocation API:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, GeolocationConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-4)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-5)         url="https://maps.google.com",  # Or any location-aware site [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-6)         config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-7)             # Configure precise GPS coordinates [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-8)             geolocation=GeolocationConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-9)                 latitude=48.8566,   # Paris coordinates [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-10)                 longitude=2.3522, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-11)                 accuracy=100        # Accuracy in meters (optional) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-12)             ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-14)             # This site will see you as being in Paris [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-15)             page_timeout=60000 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-16)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-15-17)     )`

**Important notes:** - When `geolocation` is specified, the browser is automatically granted permission to access location - Websites using the Geolocation API will receive the exact coordinates you specify - This affects map services, store locators, delivery services, etc. - Combined with the appropriate `locale` and `timezone_id`, you can create a fully consistent location profile

### Combining with Managed Browsers

These settings work perfectly with managed browsers for a complete identity solution:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-1) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-2)     AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-3)     GeolocationConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-6) browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-7)     use_managed_browser=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-8)     user_data_dir="/path/to/my-profile", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-9)     browser_type="chromium" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-12) crawl_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-13)     # Location settings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-14)     locale="es-MX",                  # Spanish (Mexico) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-15)     timezone_id="America/Mexico_City", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-16)     geolocation=GeolocationConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-17)         latitude=19.4326,            # Mexico City [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-18)         longitude=-99.1332 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-19)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-20) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-22) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#__codelineno-16-23)     result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=crawl_config)`

Combining persistent profiles with precise geolocation and region settings gives you complete control over your digital identity.

8\. Summary
-----------

*   **Create** your user-data directory either:
*   By launching Chrome/Chromium externally with `--user-data-dir=/some/path`
*   Or by using the built-in `BrowserProfiler.create_profile()` method
*   Or through the interactive interface with `profiler.interactive_manager()`
*   **Log in** or configure sites as needed, then close the browser
*   **Reference** that folder in `BrowserConfig(user_data_dir="...")` + `use_managed_browser=True`
*   **Customize** identity aspects with `locale`, `timezone_id`, and `geolocation`
*   **List and reuse** profiles with `BrowserProfiler.list_profiles()`
*   **Manage** your profiles with the dedicated `BrowserProfiler` class
*   Enjoy **persistent** sessions that reflect your real identity
*   If you only need quick, ephemeral automation, **Magic Mode** might suffice

**Recommended**: Always prefer a **Managed Browser** for robust, identity-based crawling and simpler interactions with complex sites. Use **Magic Mode** for quick tasks or prototypes where persistent data is unnecessary.

With these approaches, you preserve your **authentic** browsing environment, ensuring the site sees you exactly as a normal user—no repeated logins or wasted time.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Network & Console Capture - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Network Requests & Console Message Capturing
============================================

Crawl4AI can capture all network requests and browser console messages during a crawl, which is invaluable for debugging, security analysis, or understanding page behavior.

Configuration
-------------

To enable network and console capturing, use these configuration options:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-0-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-0-3) # Enable both network request capture and console message capture [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-0-4) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-0-5)     capture_network_requests=True,  # Capture all network requests and responses [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-0-6)     capture_console_messages=True   # Capture all browser console output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-0-7) )`

Example Usage
-------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-6)     # Enable both network request capture and console message capture [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-7)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-8)         capture_network_requests=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-9)         capture_console_messages=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-12)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-13)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-14)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-15)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-16)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-18)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-19)             # Analyze network requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-20)             if result.network_requests: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-21)                 print(f"Captured {len(result.network_requests)} network events") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-23)                 # Count request types [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-24)                 request_count = len([r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "request"]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-25)                 response_count = len([r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "response"]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-26)                 failed_count = len([r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "request_failed"]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-28)                 print(f"Requests: {request_count}, Responses: {response_count}, Failed: {failed_count}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-30)                 # Find API calls [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-31)                 api_calls = [r for r in result.network_requests  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-32)                             if r.get("event_type") == "request" and "api" in r.get("url", "")] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-33)                 if api_calls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-34)                     print(f"Detected {len(api_calls)} API calls:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-35)                     for call in api_calls[:3]:  # Show first 3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-36)                         print(f"  - {call.get('method')} {call.get('url')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-38)             # Analyze console messages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-39)             if result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-40)                 print(f"Captured {len(result.console_messages)} console messages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-42)                 # Group by type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-43)                 message_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-44)                 for msg in result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-45)                     msg_type = msg.get("type", "unknown") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-46)                     message_types[msg_type] = message_types.get(msg_type, 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-48)                 print("Message types:", message_types) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-50)                 # Show errors (often the most important) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-51)                 errors = [msg for msg in result.console_messages if msg.get("type") == "error"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-52)                 if errors: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-53)                     print(f"Found {len(errors)} console errors:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-54)                     for err in errors[:2]:  # Show first 2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-55)                         print(f"  - {err.get('text', '')[:100]}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-56) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-57)             # Export all captured data to a file for detailed analysis [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-58)             with open("network_capture.json", "w") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-59)                 json.dump({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-60)                     "url": result.url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-61)                     "network_requests": result.network_requests or [], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-62)                     "console_messages": result.console_messages or [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-63)                 }, f, indent=2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-64) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-65)             print("Exported detailed capture data to network_capture.json") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-66) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-67) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-1-68)     asyncio.run(main())`

Captured Data Structure
-----------------------

### Network Requests
