The `result.network_requests` contains a list of dictionaries, each representing a network event with these common fields:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `event_type` | Type of event: `"request"`, `"response"`, or `"request_failed"` |
| `url` | The URL of the request |
| `timestamp` | Unix timestamp when the event was captured |

#### Request Event Fields

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-2)   "event_type": "request", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-3)   "url": "https://example.com/api/data.json", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-4)   "method": "GET", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-5)   "headers": {"User-Agent": "...", "Accept": "..."}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-6)   "post_data": "key=value&otherkey=value", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-7)   "resource_type": "fetch", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-8)   "is_navigation_request": false, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-9)   "timestamp": 1633456789.123 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-2-10) }`

#### Response Event Fields

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-2)   "event_type": "response", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-3)   "url": "https://example.com/api/data.json", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-4)   "status": 200, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-5)   "status_text": "OK", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-6)   "headers": {"Content-Type": "application/json", "Cache-Control": "..."}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-7)   "from_service_worker": false, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-8)   "request_timing": {"requestTime": 1234.56, "receiveHeadersEnd": 1234.78}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-9)   "timestamp": 1633456789.456 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-3-10) }`

#### Failed Request Event Fields

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-2)   "event_type": "request_failed", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-3)   "url": "https://example.com/missing.png", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-4)   "method": "GET", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-5)   "resource_type": "image", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-6)   "failure_text": "net::ERR_ABORTED 404", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-7)   "timestamp": 1633456789.789 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-4-8) }`

### Console Messages

The `result.console_messages` contains a list of dictionaries, each representing a console message with these common fields:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `type` | Message type: `"log"`, `"error"`, `"warning"`, `"info"`, etc. |
| `text` | The message text |
| `timestamp` | Unix timestamp when the message was captured |

#### Console Message Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-5-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-5-2)   "type": "error", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-5-3)   "text": "Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'length' of undefined", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-5-4)   "location": "https://example.com/script.js:123:45", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-5-5)   "timestamp": 1633456790.123 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/#__codelineno-5-6) }`

Key Benefits
------------

*   **Full Request Visibility**: Capture all network activity including:
*   Requests (URLs, methods, headers, post data)
*   Responses (status codes, headers, timing)
*   Failed requests (with error messages)
    
*   **Console Message Access**: View all JavaScript console output:
    
*   Log messages
*   Warnings
*   Errors with stack traces
*   Developer debugging information
    
*   **Debugging Power**: Identify issues such as:
    
*   Failed API calls or resource loading
*   JavaScript errors affecting page functionality
*   CORS or other security issues
*   Hidden API endpoints and data flows
    
*   **Security Analysis**: Detect:
    
*   Unexpected third-party requests
*   Data leakage in request payloads
*   Suspicious script behavior
    
*   **Performance Insights**: Analyze:
    
*   Request timing data
*   Resource loading patterns
*   Potential bottlenecks

Use Cases
---------

1.  **API Discovery**: Identify hidden endpoints and data flows in single-page applications
2.  **Debugging**: Track down JavaScript errors affecting page functionality
3.  **Security Auditing**: Detect unwanted third-party requests or data leakage
4.  **Performance Analysis**: Identify slow-loading resources
5.  **Ad/Tracker Analysis**: Detect and catalog advertising or tracking calls

This capability is especially valuable for complex sites with heavy JavaScript, single-page applications, or when you need to understand the exact communication happening between a browser and servers.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# PDF Parsing - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

PDF Processing Strategies
=========================

Crawl4AI provides specialized strategies for handling and extracting content from PDF files. These strategies allow you to seamlessly integrate PDF processing into your crawling workflows, whether the PDFs are hosted online or stored locally.

`PDFCrawlerStrategy`
--------------------

### Overview

`PDFCrawlerStrategy` is an implementation of `AsyncCrawlerStrategy` designed specifically for PDF documents. Instead of interpreting the input URL as an HTML webpage, this strategy treats it as a pointer to a PDF file. It doesn't perform deep crawling or HTML parsing itself but rather prepares the PDF source for a dedicated PDF scraping strategy. Its primary role is to identify the PDF source (web URL or local file) and pass it along the processing pipeline in a way that `AsyncWebCrawler` can handle.

### When to Use

Use `PDFCrawlerStrategy` when you need to: - Process PDF files using the `AsyncWebCrawler`. - Handle PDFs from both web URLs (e.g., `https://example.com/document.pdf`) and local file paths (e.g., `file:///path/to/your/document.pdf`). - Integrate PDF content extraction into a unified `CrawlResult` object, allowing consistent handling of PDF data alongside web page data.

### Key Methods and Their Behavior

*   **`__init__(self, logger: AsyncLogger = None)`**:
    *   Initializes the strategy.
    *   `logger`: An optional `AsyncLogger` instance (from `crawl4ai.async_logger`) for logging purposes.
*   **`async crawl(self, url: str, **kwargs) -> AsyncCrawlResponse`**:
    *   This method is called by the `AsyncWebCrawler` during the `arun` process.
    *   It takes the `url` (which should point to a PDF) and creates a minimal `AsyncCrawlResponse`.
    *   The `html` attribute of this response is typically empty or a placeholder, as the actual PDF content processing is deferred to the `PDFContentScrapingStrategy` (or a similar PDF-aware scraping strategy).
    *   It sets `response_headers` to indicate "application/pdf" and `status_code` to 200.
*   **`async close(self)`**:
    *   A method for cleaning up any resources used by the strategy. For `PDFCrawlerStrategy`, this is usually minimal.
*   **`async __aenter__(self)` / `async __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb)`**:
    *   Enables asynchronous context management for the strategy, allowing it to be used with `async with`.

### Example Usage

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai.processors.pdf import PDFCrawlerStrategy, PDFContentScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-6)     # Initialize the PDF crawler strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-7)     pdf_crawler_strategy = PDFCrawlerStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-9)     # PDFCrawlerStrategy is typically used in conjunction with PDFContentScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-10)     # The scraping strategy handles the actual PDF content extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-11)     pdf_scraping_strategy = PDFContentScrapingStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-12)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(scraping_strategy=pdf_scraping_strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-14)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(crawler_strategy=pdf_crawler_strategy) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-15)         # Example with a remote PDF URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-16)         pdf_url = "https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.06825.pdf" # A public PDF from arXiv [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-18)         print(f"Attempting to process PDF: {pdf_url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-19)         result = await crawler.arun(url=pdf_url, config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-21)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-22)             print(f"Successfully processed PDF: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-23)             print(f"Metadata Title: {result.metadata.get('title', 'N/A')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-24)             # Further processing of result.markdown, result.media, etc. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-25)             # would be done here, based on what PDFContentScrapingStrategy extracts. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-26)             if result.markdown and hasattr(result.markdown, 'raw_markdown'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-27)                 print(f"Extracted text (first 200 chars): {result.markdown.raw_markdown[:200]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-28)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-29)                 print("No markdown (text) content extracted.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-30)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-31)             print(f"Failed to process PDF: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-33) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-0-34)     asyncio.run(main())`

### Pros and Cons

**Pros:** - Enables `AsyncWebCrawler` to handle PDF sources directly using familiar `arun` calls. - Provides a consistent interface for specifying PDF sources (URLs or local paths). - Abstracts the source handling, allowing a separate scraping strategy to focus on PDF content parsing.

**Cons:** - Does not perform any PDF data extraction itself; it strictly relies on a compatible scraping strategy (like `PDFContentScrapingStrategy`) to process the PDF. - Has limited utility on its own; most of its value comes from being paired with a PDF-specific content scraping strategy.

* * *

`PDFContentScrapingStrategy`
----------------------------

### Overview

`PDFContentScrapingStrategy` is an implementation of `ContentScrapingStrategy` designed to extract text, metadata, and optionally images from PDF documents. It is intended to be used in conjunction with a crawler strategy that can provide it with a PDF source, such as `PDFCrawlerStrategy`. This strategy uses the `NaivePDFProcessorStrategy` internally to perform the low-level PDF parsing.

### When to Use

Use `PDFContentScrapingStrategy` when your `AsyncWebCrawler` (often configured with `PDFCrawlerStrategy`) needs to: - Extract textual content page by page from a PDF document. - Retrieve standard metadata embedded within the PDF (e.g., title, author, subject, creation date, page count). - Optionally, extract images contained within the PDF pages. These images can be saved to a local directory or made available for further processing. - Produce a `ScrapingResult` that can be converted into a `CrawlResult`, making PDF content accessible in a manner similar to HTML web content (e.g., text in `result.markdown`, metadata in `result.metadata`).

### Key Configuration Attributes

When initializing `PDFContentScrapingStrategy`, you can configure its behavior using the following attributes: - **`extract_images: bool = False`**: If `True`, the strategy will attempt to extract images from the PDF. - **`save_images_locally: bool = False`**: If `True` (and `extract_images` is also `True`), extracted images will be saved to disk in the `image_save_dir`. If `False`, image data might be available in another form (e.g., base64, depending on the underlying processor) but not saved as separate files by this strategy. - **`image_save_dir: str = None`**: Specifies the directory where extracted images should be saved if `save_images_locally` is `True`. If `None`, a default or temporary directory might be used. - **`batch_size: int = 4`**: Defines how many PDF pages are processed in a single batch. This can be useful for managing memory when dealing with very large PDF documents. - **`logger: AsyncLogger = None`**: An optional `AsyncLogger` instance for logging.

### Key Methods and Their Behavior

*   **`__init__(self, save_images_locally: bool = False, extract_images: bool = False, image_save_dir: str = None, batch_size: int = 4, logger: AsyncLogger = None)`**:
    *   Initializes the strategy with configurations for image handling, batch processing, and logging. It sets up an internal `NaivePDFProcessorStrategy` instance which performs the actual PDF parsing.
*   **`scrap(self, url: str, html: str, **params) -> ScrapingResult`**:
    *   This is the primary synchronous method called by the crawler (via `ascrap`) to process the PDF.
    *   `url`: The path or URL to the PDF file (provided by `PDFCrawlerStrategy` or similar).
    *   `html`: Typically an empty string when used with `PDFCrawlerStrategy`, as the content is a PDF, not HTML.
    *   It first ensures the PDF is accessible locally (downloads it to a temporary file if `url` is remote).
    *   It then uses its internal PDF processor to extract text, metadata, and images (if configured).
    *   The extracted information is compiled into a `ScrapingResult` object:
        *   `cleaned_html`: Contains an HTML-like representation of the PDF, where each page's content is often wrapped in a `<div>` with page number information.
        *   `media`: A dictionary where `media["images"]` will contain information about extracted images if `extract_images` was `True`.
        *   `links`: A dictionary where `links["urls"]` can contain URLs found within the PDF content.
        *   `metadata`: A dictionary holding PDF metadata (e.g., title, author, num\_pages).
*   **`async ascrap(self, url: str, html: str, **kwargs) -> ScrapingResult`**:
    *   The asynchronous version of `scrap`. Under the hood, it typically runs the synchronous `scrap` method in a separate thread using `asyncio.to_thread` to avoid blocking the event loop.
*   **`_get_pdf_path(self, url: str) -> str`**:
    *   A private helper method to manage PDF file access. If the `url` is remote (http/https), it downloads the PDF to a temporary local file and returns its path. If `url` indicates a local file (`file://` or a direct path), it resolves and returns the local path.

### Example Usage

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-3) from crawl4ai.processors.pdf import PDFCrawlerStrategy, PDFContentScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-4) import os # For creating image directory [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-7)     # Define the directory for saving extracted images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-8)     image_output_dir = "./my_pdf_images" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-9)     os.makedirs(image_output_dir, exist_ok=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-11)     # Configure the PDF content scraping strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-12)     # Enable image extraction and specify where to save them [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-13)     pdf_scraping_cfg = PDFContentScrapingStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-14)         extract_images=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-15)         save_images_locally=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-16)         image_save_dir=image_output_dir, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-17)         batch_size=2 # Process 2 pages at a time for demonstration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-18)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-20)     # The PDFCrawlerStrategy is needed to tell AsyncWebCrawler how to "crawl" a PDF [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-21)     pdf_crawler_cfg = PDFCrawlerStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-23)     # Configure the overall crawl run [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-24)     run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-25)         scraping_strategy=pdf_scraping_cfg # Use our PDF scraping strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-26)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-28)     # Initialize the crawler with the PDF-specific crawler strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-29)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(crawler_strategy=pdf_crawler_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-30)         pdf_url = "https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.06825.pdf" # Example PDF [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-32)         print(f"Starting PDF processing for: {pdf_url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-33)         result = await crawler.arun(url=pdf_url, config=run_cfg) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-35)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-36)             print("\n--- PDF Processing Successful ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-37)             print(f"Processed URL: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-39)             print("\n--- Metadata ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-40)             for key, value in result.metadata.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-41)                 print(f"  {key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-43)             if result.markdown and hasattr(result.markdown, 'raw_markdown'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-44)                 print(f"\n--- Extracted Text (Markdown Snippet) ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-45)                 print(result.markdown.raw_markdown[:500].strip() + "...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-46)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-47)                 print("\nNo text (markdown) content extracted.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-49)             if result.media and result.media.get("images"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-50)                 print(f"\n--- Image Extraction ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-51)                 print(f"Extracted {len(result.media['images'])} image(s).") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-52)                 for i, img_info in enumerate(result.media["images"][:2]): # Show info for first 2 images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-53)                     print(f"  Image {i+1}:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-54)                     print(f"    Page: {img_info.get('page')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-55)                     print(f"    Format: {img_info.get('format', 'N/A')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-56)                     if img_info.get('path'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-57)                         print(f"    Saved at: {img_info.get('path')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-58)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-59)                 print("\nNo images were extracted (or extract_images was False).") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-60)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-61)             print(f"\n--- PDF Processing Failed ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-62)             print(f"Error: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-63) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-64) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/pdf-parsing/#__codelineno-1-65)     asyncio.run(main())`

### Pros and Cons

**Pros:** - Provides a comprehensive way to extract text, metadata, and (optionally) images from PDF documents. - Handles both remote PDFs (via URL) and local PDF files. - Configurable image extraction allows saving images to disk or accessing their data. - Integrates smoothly with the `CrawlResult` object structure, making PDF-derived data accessible in a way consistent with web-scraped data. - The `batch_size` parameter can help in managing memory consumption when processing large or numerous PDF pages.

**Cons:** - Extraction quality and performance can vary significantly depending on the PDF's complexity, encoding, and whether it's image-based (scanned) or text-based. - Image extraction can be resource-intensive (both CPU and disk space if `save_images_locally` is true). - Relies on `NaivePDFProcessorStrategy` internally, which might have limitations with very complex layouts, encrypted PDFs, or forms compared to more sophisticated PDF parsing libraries. Scanned PDFs will not yield text unless an OCR step is performed (which is not part of this strategy by default). - Link extraction from PDFs can be basic and depends on how hyperlinks are embedded in the document.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Multi-URL Crawling - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Advanced Multi-URL Crawling with Dispatchers
============================================

> **Heads Up**: Crawl4AI supports advanced dispatchers for **parallel** or **throttled** crawling, providing dynamic rate limiting and memory usage checks. The built-in `arun_many()` function uses these dispatchers to handle concurrency efficiently.

1\. Introduction
----------------

When crawling many URLs:

*   **Basic**: Use `arun()` in a loop (simple but less efficient)
*   **Better**: Use `arun_many()`, which efficiently handles multiple URLs with proper concurrency control
*   **Best**: Customize dispatcher behavior for your specific needs (memory management, rate limits, etc.)

**Why Dispatchers?**

*   **Adaptive**: Memory-based dispatchers can pause or slow down based on system resources
*   **Rate-limiting**: Built-in rate limiting with exponential backoff for 429/503 responses
*   **Real-time Monitoring**: Live dashboard of ongoing tasks, memory usage, and performance
*   **Flexibility**: Choose between memory-adaptive or semaphore-based concurrency

* * *

2\. Core Components
-------------------

### 2.1 Rate Limiter

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-1) class RateLimiter: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-2)     def __init__( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-3)         # Random delay range between requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-4)         base_delay: Tuple[float, float] = (1.0, 3.0),   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-6)         # Maximum backoff delay [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-7)         max_delay: float = 60.0,                         [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-9)         # Retries before giving up [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-10)         max_retries: int = 3,                           [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-12)         # Status codes triggering backoff [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-13)         rate_limit_codes: List[int] = [429, 503]         [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-0-14)     )`

Here’s the revised and simplified explanation of the **RateLimiter**, focusing on constructor parameters and adhering to your markdown style and mkDocs guidelines.

#### RateLimiter Constructor Parameters

The **RateLimiter** is a utility that helps manage the pace of requests to avoid overloading servers or getting blocked due to rate limits. It operates internally to delay requests and handle retries but can be configured using its constructor parameters.

**Parameters of the `RateLimiter` constructor:**

1. **`base_delay`** (`Tuple[float, float]`, default: `(1.0, 3.0)`)  
  The range for a random delay (in seconds) between consecutive requests to the same domain.

*   A random delay is chosen between `base_delay[0]` and `base_delay[1]` for each request.
*   This prevents sending requests at a predictable frequency, reducing the chances of triggering rate limits.

**Example:**  
If `base_delay = (2.0, 5.0)`, delays could be randomly chosen as `2.3s`, `4.1s`, etc.

* * *

2. **`max_delay`** (`float`, default: `60.0`)  
  The maximum allowable delay when rate-limiting errors occur.

*   When servers return rate-limit responses (e.g., 429 or 503), the delay increases exponentially with jitter.
*   The `max_delay` ensures the delay doesn’t grow unreasonably high, capping it at this value.

**Example:**  
For a `max_delay = 30.0`, even if backoff calculations suggest a delay of `45s`, it will cap at `30s`.

* * *

3. **`max_retries`** (`int`, default: `3`)  
  The maximum number of retries for a request if rate-limiting errors occur.

*   After encountering a rate-limit response, the `RateLimiter` retries the request up to this number of times.
*   If all retries fail, the request is marked as failed, and the process continues.

**Example:**  
If `max_retries = 3`, the system retries a failed request three times before giving up.

* * *

4. **`rate_limit_codes`** (`List[int]`, default: `[429, 503]`)  
  A list of HTTP status codes that trigger the rate-limiting logic.

*   These status codes indicate the server is overwhelmed or actively limiting requests.
*   You can customize this list to include other codes based on specific server behavior.

**Example:**  
If `rate_limit_codes = [429, 503, 504]`, the crawler will back off on these three error codes.

* * *

**How to Use the `RateLimiter`:**

Here’s an example of initializing and using a `RateLimiter` in your project:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import RateLimiter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-3) # Create a RateLimiter with custom settings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-4) rate_limiter = RateLimiter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-5)     base_delay=(2.0, 4.0),  # Random delay between 2-4 seconds [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-6)     max_delay=30.0,         # Cap delay at 30 seconds [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-7)     max_retries=5,          # Retry up to 5 times on rate-limiting errors [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-8)     rate_limit_codes=[429, 503]  # Handle these HTTP status codes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-11) # RateLimiter will handle delays and retries internally [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-1-12) # No additional setup is required for its operation`

The `RateLimiter` integrates seamlessly with dispatchers like `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` and `SemaphoreDispatcher`, ensuring requests are paced correctly without user intervention. Its internal mechanisms manage delays and retries to avoid overwhelming servers while maximizing efficiency.

### 2.2 Crawler Monitor

The CrawlerMonitor provides real-time visibility into crawling operations:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai import CrawlerMonitor, DisplayMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-2) monitor = CrawlerMonitor( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-3)     # Maximum rows in live display [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-4)     max_visible_rows=15,           [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-6)     # DETAILED or AGGREGATED view [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-7)     display_mode=DisplayMode.DETAILED   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-2-8) )`

**Display Modes**:

1.  **DETAILED**: Shows individual task status, memory usage, and timing
2.  **AGGREGATED**: Displays summary statistics and overall progress

* * *

3\. Available Dispatchers
-------------------------

### 3.1 MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher (Default)

Automatically manages concurrency based on system memory usage:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai.async_dispatcher import MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-3) dispatcher = MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-4)     memory_threshold_percent=90.0,  # Pause if memory exceeds this [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-5)     check_interval=1.0,             # How often to check memory [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-6)     max_session_permit=10,          # Maximum concurrent tasks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-7)     rate_limiter=RateLimiter(       # Optional rate limiting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-8)         base_delay=(1.0, 2.0), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-9)         max_delay=30.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-10)         max_retries=2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-11)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-12)     monitor=CrawlerMonitor(         # Optional monitoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-13)         max_visible_rows=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-14)         display_mode=DisplayMode.DETAILED [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-3-16) )`

**Constructor Parameters:**

1. **`memory_threshold_percent`** (`float`, default: `90.0`)  
  Specifies the memory usage threshold (as a percentage). If system memory usage exceeds this value, the dispatcher pauses crawling to prevent system overload.

2. **`check_interval`** (`float`, default: `1.0`)  
  The interval (in seconds) at which the dispatcher checks system memory usage.

3. **`max_session_permit`** (`int`, default: `10`)  
  The maximum number of concurrent crawling tasks allowed. This ensures resource limits are respected while maintaining concurrency.

4. **`memory_wait_timeout`** (`float`, default: `600.0`)   Optional timeout (in seconds). If memory usage exceeds `memory_threshold_percent` for longer than this duration, a `MemoryError` is raised.

5. **`rate_limiter`** (`RateLimiter`, default: `None`)  
  Optional rate-limiting logic to avoid server-side blocking (e.g., for handling 429 or 503 errors). See **RateLimiter** for details.

6. **`monitor`** (`CrawlerMonitor`, default: `None`)  
  Optional monitoring for real-time task tracking and performance insights. See **CrawlerMonitor** for details.

* * *

### 3.2 SemaphoreDispatcher

Provides simple concurrency control with a fixed limit:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-1) from crawl4ai.async_dispatcher import SemaphoreDispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-3) dispatcher = SemaphoreDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-4)     max_session_permit=20,         # Maximum concurrent tasks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-5)     rate_limiter=RateLimiter(      # Optional rate limiting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-6)         base_delay=(0.5, 1.0), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-7)         max_delay=10.0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-8)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-9)     monitor=CrawlerMonitor(        # Optional monitoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-10)         max_visible_rows=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-11)         display_mode=DisplayMode.DETAILED [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-4-13) )`

**Constructor Parameters:**

1. **`max_session_permit`** (`int`, default: `20`)  
  The maximum number of concurrent crawling tasks allowed, irrespective of semaphore slots.

2. **`rate_limiter`** (`RateLimiter`, default: `None`)  
  Optional rate-limiting logic to avoid overwhelming servers. See **RateLimiter** for details.

3. **`monitor`** (`CrawlerMonitor`, default: `None`)  
  Optional monitoring for tracking task progress and resource usage. See **CrawlerMonitor** for details.

* * *

4\. Usage Examples
------------------

### 4.1 Batch Processing (Default)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-1) async def crawl_batch(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-2)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=True, verbose=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-3)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-4)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-5)         stream=False  # Default: get all results at once [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-6)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-8)     dispatcher = MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-9)         memory_threshold_percent=70.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-10)         check_interval=1.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-11)         max_session_permit=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-12)         monitor=CrawlerMonitor( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-13)             display_mode=DisplayMode.DETAILED [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-18)         # Get all results at once [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-19)         results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-20)             urls=urls, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-21)             config=run_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-22)             dispatcher=dispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-23)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-25)         # Process all results after completion [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-26)         for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-27)             if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-28)                 await process_result(result) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-29)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-5-30)                 print(f"Failed to crawl {result.url}: {result.error_message}")`

**Review:**  
\- **Purpose:** Executes a batch crawl with all URLs processed together after crawling is complete.  
\- **Dispatcher:** Uses `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` to manage concurrency and system memory.  
\- **Stream:** Disabled (`stream=False`), so all results are collected at once for post-processing.  
\- **Best Use Case:** When you need to analyze results in bulk rather than individually during the crawl.

* * *

### 4.2 Streaming Mode

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-1) async def crawl_streaming(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-2)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=True, verbose=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-3)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-4)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-5)         stream=True  # Enable streaming mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-6)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-8)     dispatcher = MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-9)         memory_threshold_percent=70.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-10)         check_interval=1.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-11)         max_session_permit=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-12)         monitor=CrawlerMonitor( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-13)             display_mode=DisplayMode.DETAILED [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-18)         # Process results as they become available [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-19)         async for result in await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-20)             urls=urls, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-21)             config=run_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-22)             dispatcher=dispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-23)         ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-24)             if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-25)                 # Process each result immediately [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-26)                 await process_result(result) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-27)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-6-28)                 print(f"Failed to crawl {result.url}: {result.error_message}")`

**Review:**  
\- **Purpose:** Enables streaming to process results as soon as they’re available.  
\- **Dispatcher:** Uses `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` for concurrency and memory management.  
\- **Stream:** Enabled (`stream=True`), allowing real-time processing during crawling.  
\- **Best Use Case:** When you need to act on results immediately, such as for real-time analytics or progressive data storage.

* * *

### 4.3 Semaphore-based Crawling

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-1) async def crawl_with_semaphore(urls): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-2)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=True, verbose=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-3)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-5)     dispatcher = SemaphoreDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-6)         semaphore_count=5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-7)         rate_limiter=RateLimiter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-8)             base_delay=(0.5, 1.0), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-9)             max_delay=10.0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-10)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-11)         monitor=CrawlerMonitor( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-12)             max_visible_rows=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-13)             display_mode=DisplayMode.DETAILED [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-18)         results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-19)             urls,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-20)             config=run_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-21)             dispatcher=dispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-22)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-7-23)         return results`

**Review:**  
\- **Purpose:** Uses `SemaphoreDispatcher` to limit concurrency with a fixed number of slots.  
\- **Dispatcher:** Configured with a semaphore to control parallel crawling tasks.  
\- **Rate Limiter:** Prevents servers from being overwhelmed by pacing requests.  
\- **Best Use Case:** When you want precise control over the number of concurrent requests, independent of system memory.

* * *

### 4.4 Robots.txt Consideration

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-5)     urls = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-6)         "https://example1.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-7)         "https://example2.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-8)         "https://example3.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-9)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-11)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-12)         cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-13)         check_robots_txt=True,  # Will respect robots.txt for each URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-14)         semaphore_count=3      # Max concurrent requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-18)         async for result in crawler.arun_many(urls, config=config): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-19)             if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-20)                 print(f"Successfully crawled {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-21)             elif result.status_code == 403 and "robots.txt" in result.error_message: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-22)                 print(f"Skipped {result.url} - blocked by robots.txt") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-23)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-24)                 print(f"Failed to crawl {result.url}: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-26) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-8-27)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Review:**  
\- **Purpose:** Ensures compliance with `robots.txt` rules for ethical and legal web crawling.  
\- **Configuration:** Set `check_robots_txt=True` to validate each URL against `robots.txt` before crawling.  
\- **Dispatcher:** Handles requests with concurrency limits (`semaphore_count=3`).  
\- **Best Use Case:** When crawling websites that strictly enforce robots.txt policies or for responsible crawling practices.

* * *

5\. Dispatch Results
--------------------

Each crawl result includes dispatch information:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-1) @dataclass [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-2) class DispatchResult: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-3)     task_id: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-4)     memory_usage: float [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-5)     peak_memory: float [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-6)     start_time: datetime [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-7)     end_time: datetime [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-9-8)     error_message: str = ""`

Access via `result.dispatch_result`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-10-1) for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-10-2)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-10-3)         dr = result.dispatch_result [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-10-4)         print(f"URL: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-10-5)         print(f"Memory: {dr.memory_usage:.1f}MB") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-10-6)         print(f"Duration: {dr.end_time - dr.start_time}")`

6\. URL-Specific Configurations
-------------------------------

When crawling diverse content types, you often need different configurations for different URLs. For example: - PDFs need specialized extraction - Blog pages benefit from content filtering - Dynamic sites need JavaScript execution - API endpoints need JSON parsing

### 6.1 Basic URL Pattern Matching

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, MatchMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-2) from crawl4ai.processors.pdf import PDFContentScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-3) from crawl4ai.extraction_strategy import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-4) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-5) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-7) async def crawl_mixed_content(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-8)     # Configure different strategies for different content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-9)     configs = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-10)         # PDF files - specialized extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-11)         CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-12)             url_matcher="*.pdf", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-13)             scraping_strategy=PDFContentScrapingStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-14)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-16)         # Blog/article pages - content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-17)         CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-18)             url_matcher=["*/blog/*", "*/article/*"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-19)             markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-20)                 content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-21)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-22)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-24)         # Dynamic pages - JavaScript execution [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-25)         CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-26)             url_matcher=lambda url: 'github.com' in url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-27)             js_code="window.scrollTo(0, 500);" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-28)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-30)         # API endpoints - JSON extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-31)         CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-32)             url_matcher=lambda url: 'api' in url or url.endswith('.json'), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-33)             # Custome settings for JSON extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-34)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-36)         # Default config for everything else [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-37)         CrawlerRunConfig()  # No url_matcher means it matches ALL URLs (fallback) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-38)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-39) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-40)     # Mixed URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-41)     urls = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-42)         "https://www.w3.org/WAI/ER/tests/xhtml/testfiles/resources/pdf/dummy.pdf", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-43)         "https://blog.python.org/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-44)         "https://github.com/microsoft/playwright", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-45)         "https://httpbin.org/json", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-46)         "https://example.com/" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-47)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-49)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-50)         results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-51)             urls=urls, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-52)             config=configs  # Pass list of configs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-53)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-54) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-55)         for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-11-56)             print(f"{result.url}: {len(result.markdown)} chars")`

### 6.2 Advanced Pattern Matching

**Important**: A `CrawlerRunConfig` without `url_matcher` (or with `url_matcher=None`) matches ALL URLs. This makes it perfect as a default/fallback configuration.

The `url_matcher` parameter supports three types of patterns:

#### Glob Patterns (Strings)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-12-1) # Simple patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-12-2) "*.pdf"                    # Any PDF file [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-12-3) "*/api/*"                  # Any URL with /api/ in path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-12-4) "https://*.example.com/*"  # Subdomain matching [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-12-5) "*://example.com/blog/*"   # Any protocol`

#### Custom Functions

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-13-1) # Complex logic with lambdas [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-13-2) lambda url: url.startswith('https://') and 'secure' in url [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-13-3) lambda url: len(url) > 50 and url.count('/') > 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-13-4) lambda url: any(domain in url for domain in ['api.', 'data.', 'feed.'])`

#### Mixed Lists with AND/OR Logic

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-1) # Combine multiple conditions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-2) CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-3)     url_matcher=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-4)         "https://*",                        # Must be HTTPS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-5)         lambda url: 'internal' in url,      # Must contain 'internal' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-6)         lambda url: not url.endswith('.pdf') # Must not be PDF [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-7)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-8)     match_mode=MatchMode.AND  # ALL conditions must match [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-14-9) )`

### 6.3 Practical Example: News Site Crawler

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-1) async def crawl_news_site(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-2)     dispatcher = MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-3)         memory_threshold_percent=70.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-4)         rate_limiter=RateLimiter(base_delay=(1.0, 2.0)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-5)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-7)     configs = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-8)         # Homepage - light extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-9)         CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-10)             url_matcher=lambda url: url.rstrip('/') == 'https://news.ycombinator.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-11)             css_selector="nav, .headline", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-12)             extraction_strategy=None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-13)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-15)         # Article pages - full extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-16)         CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-17)             url_matcher="*/article/*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-18)             extraction_strategy=CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-19)                 semantic_filter="article content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-20)                 word_count_threshold=100 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-21)             ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-22)             screenshot=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-23)             excluded_tags=["nav", "aside", "footer"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-24)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-26)         # Author pages - metadata focus [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-27)         CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-28)             url_matcher="*/author/*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-29)             extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-30)                 "name": "h1.author-name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-31)                 "bio": ".author-bio", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-32)                 "articles": "article.post-card h2" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-33)             }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-34)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-36)         # Everything else [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-37)         CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-38)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-39) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-40)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-41)         results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-42)             urls=news_urls, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-43)             config=configs, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-44)             dispatcher=dispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-15-45)         )`

### 6.4 Best Practices

1.  **Order Matters**: Configs are evaluated in order - put specific patterns before general ones
2.  **Default Config Behavior**:
3.  A config without `url_matcher` matches ALL URLs
4.  Always include a default config as the last item if you want to handle all URLs
5.  Without a default config, unmatched URLs will fail with "No matching configuration found"
6.  **Test Your Patterns**: Use the config's `is_match()` method to test patterns:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-16-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig(url_matcher="*.pdf") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-16-2) print(config.is_match("https://example.com/doc.pdf"))  # True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-16-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-16-4) default_config = CrawlerRunConfig()  # No url_matcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/#__codelineno-16-5) print(default_config.is_match("https://any-url.com"))  # True - matches everything!`
    
7.  **Optimize for Performance**:
8.  Disable JS for static content
9.  Skip screenshots for data APIs
10.  Use appropriate extraction strategies

7\. Summary
-----------

1. **Two Dispatcher Types**:

*   MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher (default): Dynamic concurrency based on memory
*   SemaphoreDispatcher: Fixed concurrency limit

2. **Optional Components**:

*   RateLimiter: Smart request pacing and backoff
*   CrawlerMonitor: Real-time progress visualization

3. **Key Benefits**:

*   Automatic memory management
*   Built-in rate limiting
*   Live progress monitoring
*   Flexible concurrency control

Choose the dispatcher that best fits your needs:

*   **MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher**: For large crawls or limited resources
*   **SemaphoreDispatcher**: For simple, fixed-concurrency scenarios

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Proxy & Security - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Proxy & Security
================

This guide covers proxy configuration and security features in Crawl4AI, including SSL certificate analysis and proxy rotation strategies.

Understanding Proxy Configuration
---------------------------------

Crawl4AI recommends configuring proxies per request through `CrawlerRunConfig.proxy_config`. This gives you precise control, enables rotation strategies, and keeps examples simple enough to copy, paste, and run.

Basic Proxy Setup
-----------------

Configure proxies that apply to each crawl operation:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, ProxyConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-4) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(proxy_config=ProxyConfig(server="http://proxy.example.com:8080")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-5) # run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(proxy_config={"server": "http://proxy.example.com:8080"}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-6) # run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(proxy_config="http://proxy.example.com:8080") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-9) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-10)     browser_config = BrowserConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-11)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-12)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-13)         print(f"Success: {result.success} -> {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-16) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-0-17)     asyncio.run(main())`

Why request-level?

`CrawlerRunConfig.proxy_config` keeps each request self-contained, so swapping proxies or rotation strategies is just a matter of building a new run configuration.

Supported Proxy Formats
-----------------------

The `ProxyConfig.from_string()` method supports multiple formats:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import ProxyConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-3) # HTTP proxy with authentication [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-4) proxy1 = ProxyConfig.from_string("http://user:pass@192.168.1.1:8080") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-6) # HTTPS proxy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-7) proxy2 = ProxyConfig.from_string("https://proxy.example.com:8080") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-9) # SOCKS5 proxy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-10) proxy3 = ProxyConfig.from_string("socks5://proxy.example.com:1080") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-12) # Simple IP:port format [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-13) proxy4 = ProxyConfig.from_string("192.168.1.1:8080") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-15) # IP:port:user:pass format [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-1-16) proxy5 = ProxyConfig.from_string("192.168.1.1:8080:user:pass")`

Authenticated Proxies
---------------------

For proxies requiring authentication:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler,BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, ProxyConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-4) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-5)     proxy_config=ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-6)         server="http://proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-7)         username="your_username", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-8)         password="your_password", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-11) # Or dictionary style: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-12) # run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(proxy_config={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-13) #     "server": "http://proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-14) #     "username": "your_username", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-15) #     "password": "your_password", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-16) # }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-19) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-20)     browser_config = BrowserConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-21)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-22)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-23)         print(f"Success: {result.success} -> {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-26) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-2-27)     asyncio.run(main())`

Environment Variable Configuration
----------------------------------

Load proxies from environment variables for easy configuration:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-1) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai import ProxyConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-4) # Set environment variable [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-5) os.environ["PROXIES"] = "ip1:port1:user1:pass1,ip2:port2:user2:pass2,ip3:port3" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-7) # Load all proxies [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-8) proxies = ProxyConfig.from_env() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-9) print(f"Loaded {len(proxies)} proxies") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-11) # Use first proxy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-12) if proxies: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/proxy-security/#__codelineno-3-13)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(proxy_config=proxies[0])`

Rotating Proxies
----------------

Crawl4AI supports automatic proxy rotation to distribute requests across multiple proxy servers. Rotation is applied per request using a rotation strategy on `CrawlerRunConfig`.

### Proxy Rotation (recommended)
