[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-11)             config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-12)                 url=url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-13)                 session_id=session_id, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-14)                 js_code="document.querySelector('.load-more-button').click();" if page > 0 else None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-15)                 css_selector=".content-item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-16)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-17)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-19)             result = await crawler.arun(config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-20)             print(f"Page {page + 1}: Found {result.extracted_content.count('.content-item')} items") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-22)         await crawler.crawler_strategy.kill_session(session_id) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-2-24) asyncio.run(basic_session_crawl())`

This example shows: 1. Reusing the same `session_id` across multiple requests. 2. Executing JavaScript to load more content dynamically. 3. Properly closing the session to free resources.

* * *

Advanced Technique 1: Custom Execution Hooks
--------------------------------------------

> Warning: You might feel confused by the end of the next few examples 😅, so make sure you are comfortable with the order of the parts before you start this.

Use custom hooks to handle complex scenarios, such as waiting for content to load dynamically:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-1) async def advanced_session_crawl_with_hooks(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-2)     first_commit = "" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-4)     async def on_execution_started(page): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-5)         nonlocal first_commit [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-6)         try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-7)             while True: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-8)                 await page.wait_for_selector("li.commit-item h4") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-9)                 commit = await page.query_selector("li.commit-item h4") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-10)                 commit = await commit.evaluate("(element) => element.textContent").strip() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-11)                 if commit and commit != first_commit: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-12)                     first_commit = commit [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-13)                     break [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-14)                 await asyncio.sleep(0.5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-15)         except Exception as e: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-16)             print(f"Warning: New content didn't appear: {e}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-18)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-19)         session_id = "commit_session" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-20)         url = "https://github.com/example/repo/commits/main" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-21)         crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook("on_execution_started", on_execution_started) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-23)         js_next_page = """document.querySelector('a.pagination-next').click();""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-25)         for page in range(3): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-26)             config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-27)                 url=url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-28)                 session_id=session_id, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-29)                 js_code=js_next_page if page > 0 else None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-30)                 css_selector="li.commit-item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-31)                 js_only=page > 0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-32)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-33)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-35)             result = await crawler.arun(config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-36)             print(f"Page {page + 1}: Found {len(result.extracted_content)} commits") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-38)         await crawler.crawler_strategy.kill_session(session_id) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-39) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-3-40) asyncio.run(advanced_session_crawl_with_hooks())`

This technique ensures new content loads before the next action.

* * *

Advanced Technique 2: Integrated JavaScript Execution and Waiting
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Combine JavaScript execution and waiting logic for concise handling of dynamic content:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-1) async def integrated_js_and_wait_crawl(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-2)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-3)         session_id = "integrated_session" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-4)         url = "https://github.com/example/repo/commits/main" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-6)         js_next_page_and_wait = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-7)         (async () => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-8)             const getCurrentCommit = () => document.querySelector('li.commit-item h4').textContent.trim(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-9)             const initialCommit = getCurrentCommit(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-10)             document.querySelector('a.pagination-next').click(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-11)             while (getCurrentCommit() === initialCommit) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-12)                 await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100)); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-13)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-14)         })(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-15)         """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-17)         for page in range(3): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-18)             config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-19)                 url=url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-20)                 session_id=session_id, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-21)                 js_code=js_next_page_and_wait if page > 0 else None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-22)                 css_selector="li.commit-item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-23)                 js_only=page > 0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-24)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-25)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-27)             result = await crawler.arun(config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-28)             print(f"Page {page + 1}: Found {len(result.extracted_content)} commits") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-30)         await crawler.crawler_strategy.kill_session(session_id) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/session-management/#__codelineno-4-32) asyncio.run(integrated_js_and_wait_crawl())`

* * *

#### Common Use Cases for Sessions

1. **Authentication Flows**: Login and interact with secured pages.

2. **Pagination Handling**: Navigate through multiple pages.

3. **Form Submissions**: Fill forms, submit, and process results.

4. **Multi-step Processes**: Complete workflows that span multiple actions.

5. **Dynamic Content Navigation**: Handle JavaScript-rendered or event-triggered content.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# AsyncWebCrawler - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

AsyncWebCrawler
===============

The **`AsyncWebCrawler`** is the core class for asynchronous web crawling in Crawl4AI. You typically create it **once**, optionally customize it with a **`BrowserConfig`** (e.g., headless, user agent), then **run** multiple **`arun()`** calls with different **`CrawlerRunConfig`** objects.

**Recommended usage**:

1. **Create** a `BrowserConfig` for global browser settings. 

2. **Instantiate** `AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config)`. 

3. **Use** the crawler in an async context manager (`async with`) or manage start/close manually. 

4. **Call** `arun(url, config=crawler_run_config)` for each page you want.

* * *

1. Constructor Overview
-----------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-1) class AsyncWebCrawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-2)     def __init__( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-3)         self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-4)         crawler_strategy: Optional[AsyncCrawlerStrategy] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-5)         config: Optional[BrowserConfig] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-6)         always_bypass_cache: bool = False,           # deprecated [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-7)         always_by_pass_cache: Optional[bool] = None, # also deprecated [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-8)         base_directory: str = ..., [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-9)         thread_safe: bool = False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-10)         **kwargs, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-11)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-12)         """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-13)         Create an AsyncWebCrawler instance. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-15)         Args: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-16)             crawler_strategy:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-17)                 (Advanced) Provide a custom crawler strategy if needed. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-18)             config:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-19)                 A BrowserConfig object specifying how the browser is set up. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-20)             always_bypass_cache:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-21)                 (Deprecated) Use CrawlerRunConfig.cache_mode instead. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-22)             base_directory:      [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-23)                 Folder for storing caches/logs (if relevant). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-24)             thread_safe:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-25)                 If True, attempts some concurrency safeguards. Usually False. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-26)             **kwargs:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-27)                 Additional legacy or debugging parameters. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-28)         """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-29)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-31) ### Typical Initialization [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-33) ```python [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-34) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-36) browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-37)     browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-38)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-39)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-40) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-0-42) crawler = AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg)`

**Notes**:

*   **Legacy** parameters like `always_bypass_cache` remain for backward compatibility, but prefer to set **caching** in `CrawlerRunConfig`.

* * *

2. Lifecycle: Start/Close or Context Manager
--------------------------------------------

### 2.1 Context Manager (Recommended)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-1-1) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-1-2)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-1-3)     # The crawler automatically starts/closes resources`

When the `async with` block ends, the crawler cleans up (closes the browser, etc.).

### 2.2 Manual Start & Close

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-2-1) crawler = AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-2-2) await crawler.start() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-2-4) result1 = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-2-5) result2 = await crawler.arun("https://another.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-2-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-2-7) await crawler.close()`

Use this style if you have a **long-running** application or need full control of the crawler’s lifecycle.

* * *

3. Primary Method: `arun()`
---------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-3-1) async def arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-3-2)     self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-3-3)     url: str, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-3-4)     config: Optional[CrawlerRunConfig] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-3-5)     # Legacy parameters for backward compatibility... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-3-6) ) -> CrawlResult: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-3-7)     ...`

### 3.1 New Approach

You pass a `CrawlerRunConfig` object that sets up everything about a crawl—content filtering, caching, session reuse, JS code, screenshots, etc.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-2) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-4) run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-5)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-6)     css_selector="main.article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-7)     word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-8)     screenshot=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-11) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-12)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/news", config=run_cfg) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-13)     print("Crawled HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-14)     if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-4-15)         print("Screenshot base64 length:", len(result.screenshot))`

### 3.2 Legacy Parameters Still Accepted

For **backward** compatibility, `arun()` can still accept direct arguments like `css_selector=...`, `word_count_threshold=...`, etc., but we strongly advise migrating them into a **`CrawlerRunConfig`**.

* * *

4. Batch Processing: `arun_many()`
----------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-1) async def arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-2)     self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-3)     urls: List[str], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-4)     config: Optional[CrawlerRunConfig] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-5)     # Legacy parameters maintained for backwards compatibility... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-6) ) -> List[CrawlResult]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-7)     """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-8)     Process multiple URLs with intelligent rate limiting and resource monitoring. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-5-9)     """`

### 4.1 Resource-Aware Crawling

The `arun_many()` method now uses an intelligent dispatcher that:

*   Monitors system memory usage
*   Implements adaptive rate limiting
*   Provides detailed progress monitoring
*   Manages concurrent crawls efficiently

### 4.2 Example Usage

Check page [Multi-url Crawling](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/)
 for a detailed example of how to use `arun_many()`.

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-1) ### 4.3 Key Features [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-3) 1. **Rate Limiting** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-5)    - Automatic delay between requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-6)    - Exponential backoff on rate limit detection [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-7)    - Domain-specific rate limiting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-8)    - Configurable retry strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-10) 2. **Resource Monitoring** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-12)    - Memory usage tracking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-13)    - Adaptive concurrency based on system load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-14)    - Automatic pausing when resources are constrained [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-16) 3. **Progress Monitoring** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-18)    - Detailed or aggregated progress display [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-19)    - Real-time status updates [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-20)    - Memory usage statistics [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-22) 4. **Error Handling** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-24)    - Graceful handling of rate limits [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-25)    - Automatic retries with backoff [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-26)    - Detailed error reporting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-28) --- [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-30) ## 5. `CrawlResult` Output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-32) Each `arun()` returns a **`CrawlResult`** containing: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-34) - `url`: Final URL (if redirected). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-35) - `html`: Original HTML. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-36) - `cleaned_html`: Sanitized HTML. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-37) - `markdown_v2`: Deprecated. Instead just use regular `markdown` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-38) - `extracted_content`: If an extraction strategy was used (JSON for CSS/LLM strategies). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-39) - `screenshot`, `pdf`: If screenshots/PDF requested. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-40) - `media`, `links`: Information about discovered images/links. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-41) - `success`, `error_message`: Status info. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-43) For details, see [CrawlResult doc](./crawl-result.md). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-44) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-45) --- [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-46) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-47) ## 6. Quick Example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-49) Below is an example hooking it all together: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-50) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-51) ```python [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-52) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-53) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-54) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-55) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-56) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-57) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-58)     # 1. Browser config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-59)     browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-60)         browser_type="firefox", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-61)         headless=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-62)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-63)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-64) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-65)     # 2. Run config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-66)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-67)         "name": "Articles", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-68)         "baseSelector": "article.post", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-69)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-70)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-71)                 "name": "title",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-72)                 "selector": "h2",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-73)                 "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-74)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-75)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-76)                 "name": "url",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-77)                 "selector": "a",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-78)                 "type": "attribute",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-79)                 "attribute": "href" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-80)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-81)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-82)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-83) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-84)     run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-85)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-86)         extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-87)         word_count_threshold=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-88)         remove_overlay_elements=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-89)         wait_for="css:.post"  # Wait for posts to appear [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-90)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-91) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-92)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-93)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-94)             url="https://example.com/blog", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-95)             config=run_cfg [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-96)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-97) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-98)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-99)             print("Cleaned HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-100)             if result.extracted_content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-101)                 articles = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-102)                 print("Extracted articles:", articles[:2]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-103)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-104)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-105) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-6-106) asyncio.run(main())``

**Explanation**:

*   We define a **`BrowserConfig`** with Firefox, no headless, and `verbose=True`. 
*   We define a **`CrawlerRunConfig`** that **bypasses cache**, uses a **CSS** extraction schema, has a `word_count_threshold=15`, etc. 
*   We pass them to `AsyncWebCrawler(config=...)` and `arun(url=..., config=...)`.

* * *

7. Best Practices & Migration Notes
-----------------------------------

1. **Use** `BrowserConfig` for **global** settings about the browser’s environment.  2. **Use** `CrawlerRunConfig` for **per-crawl** logic (caching, content filtering, extraction strategies, wait conditions).  3. **Avoid** legacy parameters like `css_selector` or `word_count_threshold` directly in `arun()`. Instead:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-7-1) run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig(css_selector=".main-content", word_count_threshold=20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/async-webcrawler/#__codelineno-7-2) result = await crawler.arun(url="...", config=run_cfg)`

4. **Context Manager** usage is simplest unless you want a persistent crawler across many calls.

* * *

8. Summary
----------

**AsyncWebCrawler** is your entry point to asynchronous crawling:

*   **Constructor** accepts **`BrowserConfig`** (or defaults). 
*   **`arun(url, config=CrawlerRunConfig)`** is the main method for single-page crawls. 
*   **`arun_many(urls, config=CrawlerRunConfig)`** handles concurrency across multiple URLs. 
*   For advanced lifecycle control, use `start()` and `close()` explicitly. 

**Migration**:

*   If you used `AsyncWebCrawler(browser_type="chromium", css_selector="...")`, move browser settings to `BrowserConfig(...)` and content/crawl logic to `CrawlerRunConfig(...)`.

This modular approach ensures your code is **clean**, **scalable**, and **easy to maintain**. For any advanced or rarely used parameters, see the [BrowserConfig docs](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# arun() - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

`arun()` Parameter Guide (New Approach)
=======================================

In Crawl4AI’s **latest** configuration model, nearly all parameters that once went directly to `arun()` are now part of **`CrawlerRunConfig`**. When calling `arun()`, you provide:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-0-1) await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-0-2)     url="https://example.com",   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-0-3)     config=my_run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-0-4) )`

Below is an organized look at the parameters that can go inside `CrawlerRunConfig`, divided by their functional areas. For **Browser** settings (e.g., `headless`, `browser_type`), see [BrowserConfig](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
.

* * *

1. Core Usage
-------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-3) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-4)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-5)         verbose=True,            # Detailed logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-6)         cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED,  # Use normal read/write cache [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-7)         check_robots_txt=True,   # Respect robots.txt rules [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-8)         # ... other parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-11)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-12)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-13)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-14)             config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-15)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-17)         # Check if blocked by robots.txt [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-18)         if not result.success and result.status_code == 403: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-1-19)             print(f"Error: {result.error_message}")`

**Key Fields**: - `verbose=True` logs each crawl step.  - `cache_mode` decides how to read/write the local crawl cache.

* * *

2. Cache Control
----------------

**`cache_mode`** (default: `CacheMode.ENABLED`)  
Use a built-in enum from `CacheMode`:

*   `ENABLED`: Normal caching—reads if available, writes if missing.
*   `DISABLED`: No caching—always refetch pages.
*   `READ_ONLY`: Reads from cache only; no new writes.
*   `WRITE_ONLY`: Writes to cache but doesn’t read existing data.
*   `BYPASS`: Skips reading cache for this crawl (though it might still write if set up that way).

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-2-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-2-2)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-2-3) )`

**Additional flags**:

*   `bypass_cache=True` acts like `CacheMode.BYPASS`.
*   `disable_cache=True` acts like `CacheMode.DISABLED`.
*   `no_cache_read=True` acts like `CacheMode.WRITE_ONLY`.
*   `no_cache_write=True` acts like `CacheMode.READ_ONLY`.

* * *

3. Content Processing & Selection
---------------------------------

### 3.1 Text Processing

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-3-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-3-2)     word_count_threshold=10,   # Ignore text blocks <10 words [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-3-3)     only_text=False,           # If True, tries to remove non-text elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-3-4)     keep_data_attributes=False # Keep or discard data-* attributes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-3-5) )`

### 3.2 Content Selection

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-4-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-4-2)     css_selector=".main-content",  # Focus on .main-content region only [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-4-3)     excluded_tags=["form", "nav"], # Remove entire tag blocks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-4-4)     remove_forms=True,             # Specifically strip <form> elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-4-5)     remove_overlay_elements=True,  # Attempt to remove modals/popups [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-4-6) )`

### 3.3 Link Handling

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-5-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-5-2)     exclude_external_links=True,         # Remove external links from final content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-5-3)     exclude_social_media_links=True,     # Remove links to known social sites [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-5-4)     exclude_domains=["ads.example.com"], # Exclude links to these domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-5-5)     exclude_social_media_domains=["facebook.com","twitter.com"], # Extend the default list [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-5-6) )`

### 3.4 Media Filtering

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-6-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-6-2)     exclude_external_images=True  # Strip images from other domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-6-3) )`

* * *

4. Page Navigation & Timing
---------------------------

### 4.1 Basic Browser Flow

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-7-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-7-2)     wait_for="css:.dynamic-content", # Wait for .dynamic-content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-7-3)     delay_before_return_html=2.0,    # Wait 2s before capturing final HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-7-4)     page_timeout=60000,             # Navigation & script timeout (ms) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-7-5) )`

**Key Fields**:

*   `wait_for`:
*   `"css:selector"` or
*   `"js:() => boolean"`  
    e.g. `js:() => document.querySelectorAll('.item').length > 10`.
    
*   `mean_delay` & `max_range`: define random delays for `arun_many()` calls. 
    
*   `semaphore_count`: concurrency limit when crawling multiple URLs.

### 4.2 JavaScript Execution

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-8-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-8-2)     js_code=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-8-3)         "window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-8-4)         "document.querySelector('.load-more')?.click();" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-8-5)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-8-6)     js_only=False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-8-7) )`

*   `js_code` can be a single string or a list of strings. 
*   `js_only=True` means “I’m continuing in the same session with new JS steps, no new full navigation.”

### 4.3 Anti-Bot

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-9-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-9-2)     magic=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-9-3)     simulate_user=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-9-4)     override_navigator=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-9-5) )`

\- `magic=True` tries multiple stealth features.  - `simulate_user=True` mimics mouse movements or random delays.  - `override_navigator=True` fakes some navigator properties (like user agent checks).

* * *

5. Session Management
---------------------

**`session_id`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-10-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-10-2)     session_id="my_session123" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-10-3) )`

If re-used in subsequent `arun()` calls, the same tab/page context is continued (helpful for multi-step tasks or stateful browsing).

* * *

6. Screenshot, PDF & Media Options
----------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-11-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-11-2)     screenshot=True,             # Grab a screenshot as base64 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-11-3)     screenshot_wait_for=1.0,     # Wait 1s before capturing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-11-4)     pdf=True,                    # Also produce a PDF [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-11-5)     image_description_min_word_threshold=5,  # If analyzing alt text [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-11-6)     image_score_threshold=3,                # Filter out low-score images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-11-7) )`

**Where they appear**: - `result.screenshot` → Base64 screenshot string. - `result.pdf` → Byte array with PDF data.

* * *

7. Extraction Strategy
----------------------

**For advanced data extraction** (CSS/LLM-based), set `extraction_strategy`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-12-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-12-2)     extraction_strategy=my_css_or_llm_strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-12-3) )`

The extracted data will appear in `result.extracted_content`.

* * *

8. Comprehensive Example
------------------------

Below is a snippet combining many parameters:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-3) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-6)     # Example schema [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-8)         "name": "Articles", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-9)         "baseSelector": "article.post", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-11)             {"name": "title", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-12)             {"name": "link",  "selector": "a",  "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-13)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-14)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-16)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-17)         # Core [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-18)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-19)         cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-20)         check_robots_txt=True,   # Respect robots.txt rules [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-22)         # Content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-23)         word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-24)         css_selector="main.content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-25)         excluded_tags=["nav", "footer"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-26)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-28)         # Page & JS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-29)         js_code="document.querySelector('.show-more')?.click();", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-30)         wait_for="css:.loaded-block", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-31)         page_timeout=30000, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-33)         # Extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-34)         extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-36)         # Session [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-37)         session_id="persistent_session", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-39)         # Media [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-40)         screenshot=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-41)         pdf=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-43)         # Anti-bot [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-44)         simulate_user=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-45)         magic=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-46)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-48)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-49)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/posts", config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-50)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-51)             print("HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-52)             print("Extraction JSON:", result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-53)             if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-54)                 print("Screenshot length:", len(result.screenshot)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-55)             if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-56)                 print("PDF bytes length:", len(result.pdf)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-57)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-58)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-59) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-60) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun/#__codelineno-13-61)     asyncio.run(main())`

**What we covered**:

1. **Crawling** the main content region, ignoring external links.  2. Running **JavaScript** to click “.show-more”.  3. **Waiting** for “.loaded-block” to appear.  4. Generating a **screenshot** & **PDF** of the final page.  5. Extracting repeated “article.post” elements with a **CSS-based** extraction strategy.

* * *

9. Best Practices
-----------------

1. **Use `BrowserConfig` for global browser** settings (headless, user agent).  2. **Use `CrawlerRunConfig`** to handle the **specific** crawl needs: content filtering, caching, JS, screenshot, extraction, etc.  3. Keep your **parameters consistent** in run configs—especially if you’re part of a large codebase with multiple crawls.  4. **Limit** large concurrency (`semaphore_count`) if the site or your system can’t handle it.  5. For dynamic pages, set `js_code` or `scan_full_page` so you load all content.

* * *

10. Conclusion
--------------

All parameters that used to be direct arguments to `arun()` now belong in **`CrawlerRunConfig`**. This approach:

*   Makes code **clearer** and **more maintainable**. 
*   Minimizes confusion about which arguments affect global vs. per-crawl behavior. 
*   Allows you to create **reusable** config objects for different pages or tasks.

For a **full** reference, check out the [CrawlerRunConfig Docs](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
. 

Happy crawling with your **structured, flexible** config approach!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# CrawlResult - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

`CrawlResult` Reference
=======================

The **`CrawlResult`** class encapsulates everything returned after a single crawl operation. It provides the **raw or processed content**, details on links and media, plus optional metadata (like screenshots, PDFs, or extracted JSON).

**Location**: `crawl4ai/crawler/models.py` (for reference)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-1) class CrawlResult(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-2)     url: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-3)     html: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-4)     success: bool [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-5)     cleaned_html: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-6)     fit_html: Optional[str] = None  # Preprocessed HTML optimized for extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-7)     media: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-8)     links: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-9)     downloaded_files: Optional[List[str]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-10)     screenshot: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-11)     pdf : Optional[bytes] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-12)     mhtml: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-13)     markdown: Optional[Union[str, MarkdownGenerationResult]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-14)     extracted_content: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-15)     metadata: Optional[dict] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-16)     error_message: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-17)     session_id: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-18)     response_headers: Optional[dict] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-19)     status_code: Optional[int] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-20)     redirected_status_code: Optional[int] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-21)     ssl_certificate: Optional[SSLCertificate] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-22)     dispatch_result: Optional[DispatchResult] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-0-23)     ...`

Below is a **field-by-field** explanation and possible usage patterns.

* * *

1\. Basic Crawl Info
--------------------

### 1.1 **`url`** _(str)_

**What**: The final crawled URL (after any redirects).  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-1-1) print(result.url)  # e.g., "https://example.com/"`

### 1.2 **`success`** _(bool)_

**What**: `True` if the crawl pipeline ended without major errors; `False` otherwise.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-2-1) if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-2-2)     print(f"Crawl failed: {result.error_message}")`

### 1.3 **`status_code`** _(Optional\[int\])_

**What**: The page's HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404). When the page was reached via redirect, this is the status code of the **first** response in the redirect chain (e.g., 301 or 302). **Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-3-1) if result.status_code == 404: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-3-2)     print("Page not found!")`

### 1.4 **`redirected_status_code`** _(Optional\[int\])_

**What**: The HTTP status code of the **final** redirect destination. For a 302→200 redirect, `status_code` is 302 and `redirected_status_code` is 200. `None` for non-HTTP requests (raw HTML, local files). **Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-4-1) if result.status_code in (301, 302) and result.redirected_status_code == 200: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-4-2)     print(f"Redirected to {result.redirected_url} (OK)")`

### 1.5 **`error_message`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: If `success=False`, a textual description of the failure.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-5-1) if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-5-2)     print("Error:", result.error_message)`

### 1.5 **`session_id`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: The ID used for reusing a browser context across multiple calls.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-6-1) # If you used session_id="login_session" in CrawlerRunConfig, see it here: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-6-2) print("Session:", result.session_id)`

### 1.6 **`response_headers`** _(Optional\[dict\])_

**What**: Final HTTP response headers.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-7-1) if result.response_headers: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-7-2)     print("Server:", result.response_headers.get("Server", "Unknown"))`

### 1.7 **`ssl_certificate`** _(Optional\[SSLCertificate\])_

**What**: If `fetch_ssl_certificate=True` in your CrawlerRunConfig, **`result.ssl_certificate`** contains a [**`SSLCertificate`**](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/ssl-certificate/)
 object describing the site's certificate. You can export the cert in multiple formats (PEM/DER/JSON) or access its properties like `issuer`, `subject`, `valid_from`, `valid_until`, etc. **Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-8-1) if result.ssl_certificate: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-8-2)     print("Issuer:", result.ssl_certificate.issuer)`

* * *

2\. Raw / Cleaned Content
-------------------------

### 2.1 **`html`** _(str)_

**What**: The **original** unmodified HTML from the final page load.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-9-1) # Possibly large [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-9-2) print(len(result.html))`

### 2.2 **`cleaned_html`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: A sanitized HTML version—scripts, styles, or excluded tags are removed based on your `CrawlerRunConfig`.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-10-1) print(result.cleaned_html[:500])  # Show a snippet`

* * *

3\. Markdown Fields
-------------------

### 3.1 The Markdown Generation Approach

Crawl4AI can convert HTML→Markdown, optionally including:

*   **Raw** markdown
*   **Links as citations** (with a references section)
*   **Fit** markdown if a **content filter** is used (like Pruning or BM25)

**`MarkdownGenerationResult`** includes: - **`raw_markdown`** _(str)_: The full HTML→Markdown conversion.  
\- **`markdown_with_citations`** _(str)_: Same markdown, but with link references as academic-style citations.  
\- **`references_markdown`** _(str)_: The reference list or footnotes at the end.  
\- **`fit_markdown`** _(Optional\[str\])_: If content filtering (Pruning/BM25) was applied, the filtered "fit" text.  
\- **`fit_html`** _(Optional\[str\])_: The HTML that led to `fit_markdown`.

**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-11-1) if result.markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-11-2)     md_res = result.markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-11-3)     print("Raw MD:", md_res.raw_markdown[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-11-4)     print("Citations MD:", md_res.markdown_with_citations[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-11-5)     print("References:", md_res.references_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-11-6)     if md_res.fit_markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-11-7)         print("Pruned text:", md_res.fit_markdown[:300])`

### 3.2 **`markdown`** _(Optional\[Union\[str, MarkdownGenerationResult\]\])_

**What**: Holds the `MarkdownGenerationResult`.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-12-1) print(result.markdown.raw_markdown[:200]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-12-2) print(result.markdown.fit_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-12-3) print(result.markdown.fit_html)`

**Important**: "Fit" content (in `fit_markdown`/`fit_html`) exists in result.markdown, only if you used a **filter** (like **PruningContentFilter** or **BM25ContentFilter**) within a `MarkdownGenerationStrategy`.

* * *

4\. Media & Links
-----------------

### 4.1 **`media`** _(Dict\[str, List\[Dict\]\])_

**What**: Contains info about discovered images, videos, or audio. Typically keys: `"images"`, `"videos"`, `"audios"`.  
**Common Fields** in each item:

*   `src` _(str)_: Media URL
*   `alt` or `title` _(str)_: Descriptive text
*   `score` _(float)_: Relevance score if the crawler's heuristic found it "important"
*   `desc` or `description` _(Optional\[str\])_: Additional context extracted from surrounding text

**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-13-1) images = result.media.get("images", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-13-2) for img in images: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-13-3)     if img.get("score", 0) > 5: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-13-4)         print("High-value image:", img["src"])`

### 4.2 **`links`** _(Dict\[str, List\[Dict\]\])_

**What**: Holds internal and external link data. Usually two keys: `"internal"` and `"external"`.  
**Common Fields**:

*   `href` _(str)_: The link target
*   `text` _(str)_: Link text
*   `title` _(str)_: Title attribute
*   `context` _(str)_: Surrounding text snippet
*   `domain` _(str)_: If external, the domain

**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-14-1) for link in result.links["internal"]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-14-2)     print(f"Internal link to {link['href']} with text {link['text']}")`

* * *

5\. Additional Fields
---------------------

### 5.1 **`extracted_content`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: If you used **`extraction_strategy`** (CSS, LLM, etc.), the structured output (JSON).  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-15-1) if result.extracted_content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-15-2)     data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-15-3)     print(data)`

### 5.2 **`downloaded_files`** _(Optional\[List\[str\]\])_

**What**: If `accept_downloads=True` in your `BrowserConfig` + `downloads_path`, lists local file paths for downloaded items.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-16-1) if result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-16-2)     for file_path in result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-16-3)         print("Downloaded:", file_path)`

### 5.3 **`screenshot`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: Base64-encoded screenshot if `screenshot=True` in `CrawlerRunConfig`.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-17-1) import base64 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-17-2) if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-17-3)     with open("page.png", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-17-4)         f.write(base64.b64decode(result.screenshot))`

### 5.4 **`pdf`** _(Optional\[bytes\])_

**What**: Raw PDF bytes if `pdf=True` in `CrawlerRunConfig`.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-18-1) if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-18-2)     with open("page.pdf", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-18-3)         f.write(result.pdf)`

### 5.5 **`mhtml`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: MHTML snapshot of the page if `capture_mhtml=True` in `CrawlerRunConfig`. MHTML (MIME HTML) format preserves the entire web page with all its resources (CSS, images, scripts, etc.) in a single file.  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-19-1) if result.mhtml: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-19-2)     with open("page.mhtml", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-19-3)         f.write(result.mhtml)`

### 5.6 **`metadata`** _(Optional\[dict\])_

**What**: Page-level metadata if discovered (title, description, OG data, etc.).  
**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-20-1) if result.metadata: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-20-2)     print("Title:", result.metadata.get("title")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-20-3)     print("Author:", result.metadata.get("author"))`

* * *

6\. `dispatch_result` (optional)
--------------------------------

A `DispatchResult` object providing additional concurrency and resource usage information when crawling URLs in parallel (e.g., via `arun_many()` with custom dispatchers). It contains:

*   **`task_id`**: A unique identifier for the parallel task.
*   **`memory_usage`** (float): The memory (in MB) used at the time of completion.
*   **`peak_memory`** (float): The peak memory usage (in MB) recorded during the task's execution.
*   **`start_time`** / **`end_time`** (datetime): Time range for this crawling task.
*   **`error_message`** (str): Any dispatcher- or concurrency-related error encountered.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-21-1) # Example usage: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-21-2) for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-21-3)     if result.success and result.dispatch_result: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-21-4)         dr = result.dispatch_result [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-21-5)         print(f"URL: {result.url}, Task ID: {dr.task_id}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-21-6)         print(f"Memory: {dr.memory_usage:.1f} MB (Peak: {dr.peak_memory:.1f} MB)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-21-7)         print(f"Duration: {dr.end_time - dr.start_time}")`

> **Note**: This field is typically populated when using `arun_many(...)` alongside a **dispatcher** (e.g., `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` or `SemaphoreDispatcher`). If no concurrency or dispatcher is used, `dispatch_result` may remain `None`.

* * *

7\. Network Requests & Console Messages
---------------------------------------

When you enable network and console message capturing in `CrawlerRunConfig` using `capture_network_requests=True` and `capture_console_messages=True`, the `CrawlResult` will include these fields:

### 7.1 **`network_requests`** _(Optional\[List\[Dict\[str, Any\]\]\])_

**What**: A list of dictionaries containing information about all network requests, responses, and failures captured during the crawl. **Structure**: - Each item has an `event_type` field that can be `"request"`, `"response"`, or `"request_failed"`. - Request events include `url`, `method`, `headers`, `post_data`, `resource_type`, and `is_navigation_request`. - Response events include `url`, `status`, `status_text`, `headers`, and `request_timing`. - Failed request events include `url`, `method`, `resource_type`, and `failure_text`. - All events include a `timestamp` field.

**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-1) if result.network_requests: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-2)     # Count different types of events [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-3)     requests = [r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "request"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-4)     responses = [r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "response"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-5)     failures = [r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "request_failed"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-7)     print(f"Captured {len(requests)} requests, {len(responses)} responses, and {len(failures)} failures") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-9)     # Analyze API calls [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-10)     api_calls = [r for r in requests if "api" in r.get("url", "")] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-12)     # Identify failed resources [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-13)     for failure in failures: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-22-14)         print(f"Failed to load: {failure.get('url')} - {failure.get('failure_text')}")`

### 7.2 **`console_messages`** _(Optional\[List\[Dict\[str, Any\]\]\])_

**What**: A list of dictionaries containing all browser console messages captured during the crawl. **Structure**: - Each item has a `type` field indicating the message type (e.g., `"log"`, `"error"`, `"warning"`, etc.). - The `text` field contains the actual message text. - Some messages include `location` information (URL, line, column). - All messages include a `timestamp` field.

**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-1) if result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-2)     # Count messages by type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-3)     message_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-4)     for msg in result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-5)         msg_type = msg.get("type", "unknown") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-6)         message_types[msg_type] = message_types.get(msg_type, 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-8)     print(f"Message type counts: {message_types}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-10)     # Display errors (which are usually most important) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-11)     for msg in result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-12)         if msg.get("type") == "error": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-23-13)             print(f"Error: {msg.get('text')}")`

These fields provide deep visibility into the page's network activity and browser console, which is invaluable for debugging, security analysis, and understanding complex web applications.

For more details on network and console capturing, see the [Network & Console Capture documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/network-console-capture/)
.

* * *

8\. Example: Accessing Everything
---------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-1) async def handle_result(result: CrawlResult): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-2)     if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-3)         print("Crawl error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-4)         return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-6)     # Basic info [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-7)     print("Crawled URL:", result.url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-8)     print("Status code:", result.status_code) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-10)     # HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-11)     print("Original HTML size:", len(result.html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-12)     print("Cleaned HTML size:", len(result.cleaned_html or "")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-14)     # Markdown output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-15)     if result.markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-16)         print("Raw Markdown:", result.markdown.raw_markdown[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-17)         print("Citations Markdown:", result.markdown.markdown_with_citations[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-18)         if result.markdown.fit_markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-19)             print("Fit Markdown:", result.markdown.fit_markdown[:200]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-21)     # Media & Links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-22)     if "images" in result.media: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-23)         print("Image count:", len(result.media["images"])) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-24)     if "internal" in result.links: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-25)         print("Internal link count:", len(result.links["internal"])) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-27)     # Extraction strategy result [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-28)     if result.extracted_content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-29)         print("Structured data:", result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-31)     # Screenshot/PDF/MHTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-32)     if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-33)         print("Screenshot length:", len(result.screenshot)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-34)     if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-35)         print("PDF bytes length:", len(result.pdf)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-36)     if result.mhtml: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-37)         print("MHTML length:", len(result.mhtml)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-39)     # Network and console capturing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-40)     if result.network_requests: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-41)         print(f"Network requests captured: {len(result.network_requests)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-42)         # Analyze request types [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-43)         req_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-44)         for req in result.network_requests: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-45)             if "resource_type" in req: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-46)                 req_types[req["resource_type"]] = req_types.get(req["resource_type"], 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-47)         print(f"Resource types: {req_types}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-49)     if result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-50)         print(f"Console messages captured: {len(result.console_messages)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-51)         # Count by message type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-52)         msg_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-53)         for msg in result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-54)             msg_types[msg.get("type", "unknown")] = msg_types.get(msg.get("type", "unknown"), 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/#__codelineno-24-55)         print(f"Message types: {msg_types}")`

* * *

9\. Key Points & Future
-----------------------

1. **Deprecated legacy properties of CrawlResult**  
\- `markdown_v2` - Deprecated in v0.5. Just use `markdown`. It holds the `MarkdownGenerationResult` now! - `fit_markdown` and `fit_html` - Deprecated in v0.5. They can now be accessed via `MarkdownGenerationResult` in `result.markdown`. eg: `result.markdown.fit_markdown` and `result.markdown.fit_html`

2. **Fit Content**  
\- **`fit_markdown`** and **`fit_html`** appear in MarkdownGenerationResult, only if you used a content filter (like **PruningContentFilter** or **BM25ContentFilter**) inside your **MarkdownGenerationStrategy** or set them directly.  
\- If no filter is used, they remain `None`.

3. **References & Citations**  
\- If you enable link citations in your `DefaultMarkdownGenerator` (`options={"citations": True}`), you’ll see `markdown_with_citations` plus a **`references_markdown`** block. This helps large language models or academic-like referencing.

4. **Links & Media**  
\- `links["internal"]` and `links["external"]` group discovered anchors by domain.  
\- `media["images"]` / `["videos"]` / `["audios"]` store extracted media elements with optional scoring or context.

5. **Error Cases**  
\- If `success=False`, check `error_message` (e.g., timeouts, invalid URLs).  
\- `status_code` might be `None` if we failed before an HTTP response.

Use **`CrawlResult`** to glean all final outputs and feed them into your data pipelines, AI models, or archives. With the synergy of a properly configured **BrowserConfig** and **CrawlerRunConfig**, the crawler can produce robust, structured results here in **`CrawlResult`**.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Build - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

O**Prompt for AI Coding Assistant: Create an Interactive LLM Context Builder Page**

**Objective:**

Your task is to create an interactive HTML webpage with JavaScript functionality that allows users to select and combine different `crawl4ai` LLM context files into a single downloadable Markdown (`.md`) file. This tool will empower users to craft tailored context for their AI assistants based on their specific needs.

**Core Functionality:**

1.  **Display `crawl4ai` Components:** The page will list all available `crawl4ai` documentation components.
2.  **Select Context Types:** For each component, users can select which types of context they want to include:
    *   Memory (API facts)
    *   Reasoning (How-to/why)
    *   Examples (Code snippets) (All should be selected by default for each initially selected component).
3.  **Special "Aggregate" Contexts:** Include options for special, pre-combined contexts:
    *   "Vibe Coding" (a curated mix for general AI prompting)
    *   "All Library Context" (a comprehensive aggregation of all memory, reasoning, and examples for the entire library).
