4.  **Fetch and Concatenate:** When the user clicks a "Download Combined Context" button:
    *   The JavaScript will fetch the content of all selected Markdown files from the server (from a predefined folder, e.g., `/llmtxt/`).
    *   It will concatenate the content of these files into a single string.
5.  **Client-Side Download:** The concatenated content will be offered to the user as a download (e.g., `custom_crawl4ai_context.md`).

**Input/Assumptions:**

*   **Context Files Location:** All individual context Markdown files are located on the server in a publicly accessible folder named `llmtxt/`.
*   **File Naming Convention:** Files follow the pattern: `crawl4ai_{{component_name}}_[memory|reasoning|examples]_content.llm.md`.
    *   `{{component_name}}` can contain underscores (e.g., `deep_crawling`, `config_objects`).
    *   The special contexts will have names like `crawl4ai_vibe_content.llm.md` and `crawl4ai_all_content.llm.md`.
*   **Component List:** You will be provided with a list of `crawl4ai` components. For this implementation, use the following list:
    *   `core`
    *   `config_objects`
    *   `deep_crawling`
    *   `deployment` (covers Installation & Docker Deployment)
    *   `extraction` (covers Structured Data Extraction)
    *   `markdown` (covers Markdown Generation Algorithm)
    *   `pdf_processing`
    *   _(No separate "Vibe Coding" or "All Library Context" in this list, as they are special top-level selections)_

**Detailed UI/UX Requirements:**

1.  **Main Page Structure:**
    *   **Header:** "Crawl4AI Interactive LLM Context Builder"
    *   **Introduction:** Briefly explain the purpose of the tool (from the `USING_LLM_CONTEXTS.md` content you helped draft: "Supercharging Your AI Assistant...").
    *   **Selection Area:**
        *   **Special Aggregate Contexts (Radio Buttons or Prominent Checkboxes):**
            *   \[ \] "Vibe Coding Context" (`crawl4ai_vibe_content.llm.md`)
            *   \[ \] "All Library Context (Comprehensive)" (`crawl4ai_all_content.llm.md`)
            *   _Behavior:_ Selecting one of these might disable individual component selections (or vice-versa) to avoid redundancy, or simply add them to the list. Consider user experience here. A simple approach is that if an aggregate is selected, it's the _only_ thing downloaded.
        *   **Individual Component Selection (Table or List of Checkboxes):**
            *   A section titled "Select Individual Components & Context Types:"
            *   For each component in the provided list:
                *   A master checkbox for the component itself (e.g., `[ ] Core Functionality`). Selected by default.
                *   Nested checkboxes (indented or grouped) for context types, enabled only if the parent component is checked:
                    *   `[x] Memory (API Facts)`
                    *   `[x] Reasoning (How-to/Why)`
                    *   `[x] Examples (Code Snippets)` (These three sub-checkboxes should be selected by default if the parent component is selected).
    *   **Action Button:**
        *   A button: "Generate & Download Combined Context"
    *   **Status/Feedback Area:** (Optional, but good UX)
        *   Display messages like "Fetching files...", "Combining context...", "Download starting..." or error messages.

**Final Output:**

*   A single HTML file (e.g., `interactive_context_builder.html`).
*   Associated JavaScript code (can be inline within `<script>` tags or in a separate `.js` file).
*   Associated CSS code (can be inline within `<style>` tags or in a separate `.css` file).

This interactive tool will greatly enhance the user experience for `crawl4ai` developers looking to leverage your specialized LLM contexts. Please ensure the JavaScript is robust and provides good user feedback.

* * *

This prompt should give your AI coding assistant a very clear set of requirements and guidelines for building the interactive context builder. Remember to provide it with the list of components as mentioned in the "Input/Assumptions" section.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Strategies - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Extraction & Chunking Strategies API
====================================

This documentation covers the API reference for extraction and chunking strategies in Crawl4AI.

Extraction Strategies
---------------------

All extraction strategies inherit from the base `ExtractionStrategy` class and implement two key methods: - `extract(url: str, html: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]` - `run(url: str, sections: List[str]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]`

### LLMExtractionStrategy

Used for extracting structured data using Language Models.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-1) LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-2)     # Required Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-3)     provider: str = DEFAULT_PROVIDER,     # LLM provider (e.g., "ollama/llama2") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-4)     api_token: Optional[str] = None,      # API token [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-6)     # Extraction Configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-7)     instruction: str = None,              # Custom extraction instruction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-8)     schema: Dict = None,                  # Pydantic model schema for structured data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-9)     extraction_type: str = "block",       # "block" or "schema" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-11)     # Chunking Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-12)     chunk_token_threshold: int = 4000,    # Maximum tokens per chunk [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-13)     overlap_rate: float = 0.1,           # Overlap between chunks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-14)     word_token_rate: float = 0.75,       # Word to token conversion rate [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-15)     apply_chunking: bool = True,         # Enable/disable chunking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-17)     # API Configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-18)     base_url: str = None,                # Base URL for API [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-19)     extra_args: Dict = {},               # Additional provider arguments [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-20)     verbose: bool = False                # Enable verbose logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-0-21) )`

### RegexExtractionStrategy

Used for fast pattern-based extraction of common entities using regular expressions.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-1) RegexExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-2)     # Pattern Configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-3)     pattern: IntFlag = RegexExtractionStrategy.Nothing,  # Bit flags of built-in patterns to use [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-4)     custom: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,           # Custom pattern dictionary {label: regex} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-6)     # Input Format [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-7)     input_format: str = "fit_html",                    # "html", "markdown", "text" or "fit_html" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-10) # Built-in Patterns as Bit Flags [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-11) RegexExtractionStrategy.Email           # Email addresses [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-12) RegexExtractionStrategy.PhoneIntl       # International phone numbers  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-13) RegexExtractionStrategy.PhoneUS         # US-format phone numbers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-14) RegexExtractionStrategy.Url             # HTTP/HTTPS URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-15) RegexExtractionStrategy.IPv4            # IPv4 addresses [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-16) RegexExtractionStrategy.IPv6            # IPv6 addresses [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-17) RegexExtractionStrategy.Uuid            # UUIDs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-18) RegexExtractionStrategy.Currency        # Currency values (USD, EUR, etc) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-19) RegexExtractionStrategy.Percentage      # Percentage values [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-20) RegexExtractionStrategy.Number          # Numeric values [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-21) RegexExtractionStrategy.DateIso         # ISO format dates [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-22) RegexExtractionStrategy.DateUS          # US format dates [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-23) RegexExtractionStrategy.Time24h         # 24-hour format times [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-24) RegexExtractionStrategy.PostalUS        # US postal codes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-25) RegexExtractionStrategy.PostalUK        # UK postal codes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-26) RegexExtractionStrategy.HexColor        # HTML hex color codes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-27) RegexExtractionStrategy.TwitterHandle   # Twitter handles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-28) RegexExtractionStrategy.Hashtag         # Hashtags [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-29) RegexExtractionStrategy.MacAddr         # MAC addresses [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-30) RegexExtractionStrategy.Iban            # International bank account numbers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-31) RegexExtractionStrategy.CreditCard      # Credit card numbers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-1-32) RegexExtractionStrategy.All             # All available patterns`

### CosineStrategy

Used for content similarity-based extraction and clustering.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-1) CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-2)     # Content Filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-3)     semantic_filter: str = None,        # Topic/keyword filter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-4)     word_count_threshold: int = 10,     # Minimum words per cluster [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-5)     sim_threshold: float = 0.3,         # Similarity threshold [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-7)     # Clustering Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-8)     max_dist: float = 0.2,             # Maximum cluster distance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-9)     linkage_method: str = 'ward',       # Clustering method [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-10)     top_k: int = 3,                    # Top clusters to return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-12)     # Model Configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-13)     model_name: str = 'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2',  # Embedding model [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-15)     verbose: bool = False              # Enable verbose logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-2-16) )`

### JsonCssExtractionStrategy

Used for CSS selector-based structured data extraction.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-1) JsonCssExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-2)     schema: Dict[str, Any],    # Extraction schema [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-3)     verbose: bool = False      # Enable verbose logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-6) # Schema Structure [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-7) schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-8)     "name": str,              # Schema name [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-9)     "baseSelector": str,      # Base CSS selector [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-10)     "fields": [               # List of fields to extract [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-11)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-12)             "name": str,      # Field name [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-13)             "selector": str,  # CSS selector [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-14)             "type": str,     # Field type: "text", "attribute", "html", "regex" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-15)             "attribute": str, # For type="attribute" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-16)             "pattern": str,  # For type="regex" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-17)             "transform": str, # Optional: "lowercase", "uppercase", "strip" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-18)             "default": Any,   # Default value if extraction fails [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-19)             "source": str,   # Optional: navigate to sibling first, e.g. "+ tr" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-20)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-21)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-3-22) }`

Chunking Strategies
-------------------

All chunking strategies inherit from `ChunkingStrategy` and implement the `chunk(text: str) -> list` method.

### RegexChunking

Splits text based on regex patterns.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-4-1) RegexChunking( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-4-2)     patterns: List[str] = None  # Regex patterns for splitting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-4-3)                                # Default: [r'\n\n'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-4-4) )`

### SlidingWindowChunking

Creates overlapping chunks with a sliding window approach.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-5-1) SlidingWindowChunking( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-5-2)     window_size: int = 100,    # Window size in words [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-5-3)     step: int = 50             # Step size between windows [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-5-4) )`

### OverlappingWindowChunking

Creates chunks with specified overlap.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-6-1) OverlappingWindowChunking( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-6-2)     window_size: int = 1000,   # Chunk size in words [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-6-3)     overlap: int = 100         # Overlap size in words [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-6-4) )`

Usage Examples
--------------

### LLM Extraction

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-1) from pydantic import BaseModel [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-2) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-3) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-5) # Define schema [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-6) class Article(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-7)     title: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-8)     content: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-9)     author: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-11) # Create strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-12) strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-13)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="ollama/llama2"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-14)     schema=Article.schema(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-15)     instruction="Extract article details" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-16) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-18) # Use with crawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-19) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-20)     url="https://example.com/article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-21)     extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-22) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-24) # Access extracted data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-7-25) data = json.loads(result.extracted_content)`

### Regex Extraction

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, RegexExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-4) # Method 1: Use built-in patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-5) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-6)     pattern = RegexExtractionStrategy.Email | RegexExtractionStrategy.Url [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-9) # Method 2: Use custom patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-10) price_pattern = {"usd_price": r"\$\s?\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*(?:\.\d{2})?"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-11) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(custom=price_pattern) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-13) # Method 3: Generate pattern with LLM assistance (one-time) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-14) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-16) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-17)     # Get sample HTML first [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-18)     sample_result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/products") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-19)     html = sample_result.fit_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-21)     # Generate regex pattern once [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-22)     pattern = RegexExtractionStrategy.generate_pattern( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-23)         label="price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-24)         html=html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-25)         query="Product prices in USD format", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-26)         llm_config=LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-27)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-29)     # Save pattern for reuse [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-30)     import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-31)     with open("price_pattern.json", "w") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-32)         json.dump(pattern, f) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-34)     # Use pattern for extraction (no LLM calls) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-35)     strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(custom=pattern) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-36)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-37)         url="https://example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-38)         config=CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-39)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-41)     # Process results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-42)     data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-43)     for item in data: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-8-44)         print(f"{item['label']}: {item['value']}")`

### CSS Extraction

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-3) # Define schema [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-4) schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-5)     "name": "Product List", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-6)     "baseSelector": ".product-card", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-7)     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-8)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-9)             "name": "title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-10)             "selector": "h2.title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-11)             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-12)         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-13)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-14)             "name": "price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-15)             "selector": ".price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-16)             "type": "text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-17)             "transform": "strip" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-18)         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-19)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-20)             "name": "image", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-21)             "selector": "img", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-22)             "type": "attribute", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-23)             "attribute": "src" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-24)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-25)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-26) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-28) # Create and use strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-29) strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-30) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-31)     url="https://example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-32)     extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-9-33) )`

### Content Chunking

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-1) from crawl4ai.chunking_strategy import OverlappingWindowChunking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-2) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-4) # Create chunking strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-5) chunker = OverlappingWindowChunking( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-6)     window_size=500,  # 500 words per chunk [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-7)     overlap=50        # 50 words overlap [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-10) # Use with extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-11) strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-12)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="ollama/llama2"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-13)     chunking_strategy=chunker [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-14) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-16) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-17)     url="https://example.com/long-article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-18)     extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-10-19) )`

Best Practices
--------------

1.  **Choose the Right Strategy**
2.  Use `RegexExtractionStrategy` for common data types like emails, phones, URLs, dates
3.  Use `JsonCssExtractionStrategy` for well-structured HTML with consistent patterns
4.  Use `LLMExtractionStrategy` for complex, unstructured content requiring reasoning
5.  Use `CosineStrategy` for content similarity and clustering
    
6.  **Strategy Selection Guide**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-1) Is the target data a common type (email/phone/date/URL)?  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-2) → RegexExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-4) Does the page have consistent HTML structure? [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-5) → JsonCssExtractionStrategy or JsonXPathExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-7) Is the data semantically complex or unstructured? [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-8) → LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-10) Need to find content similar to a specific topic? [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-11-11) → CosineStrategy`
    
7.  **Optimize Chunking**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-12-1) # For long documents [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-12-2) strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-12-3)     chunk_token_threshold=2000,  # Smaller chunks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-12-4)     overlap_rate=0.1           # 10% overlap [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-12-5) )`
    
8.  **Combine Strategies for Best Performance**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-1) # First pass: Extract structure with CSS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-2) css_strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(product_schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-3) css_result = await crawler.arun(url, config=CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=css_strategy)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-4) product_data = json.loads(css_result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-6) # Second pass: Extract specific fields with regex [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-7) descriptions = [product["description"] for product in product_data] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-8) regex_strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-9)     pattern=RegexExtractionStrategy.Email | RegexExtractionStrategy.PhoneUS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-10)     custom={"dimension": r"\d+x\d+x\d+ (?:cm|in)"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-13) # Process descriptions with regex [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-14) for text in descriptions: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-13-15)     matches = regex_strategy.extract("", text)  # Direct extraction`
    
9.  **Handle Errors**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-1) try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-2)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-3)         url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-4)         extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-5)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-6)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-7)         content = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-8) except Exception as e: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-14-9)     print(f"Extraction failed: {e}")`
    
10.  **Monitor Performance**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-15-1) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-15-2)     verbose=True,  # Enable logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-15-3)     word_count_threshold=20,  # Filter short content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-15-4)     top_k=5  # Limit results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-15-5) )`
    
11.  **Cache Generated Patterns**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-1) # For RegexExtractionStrategy pattern generation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-3) from pathlib import Path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-5) cache_dir = Path("./pattern_cache") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-6) cache_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-7) pattern_file = cache_dir / "product_pattern.json" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-9) if pattern_file.exists(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-10)     with open(pattern_file) as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-11)         pattern = json.load(f) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-12) else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-13)     # Generate once with LLM [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-14)     pattern = RegexExtractionStrategy.generate_pattern(...) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-15)     with open(pattern_file, "w") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/strategies/#__codelineno-16-16)         json.dump(pattern, f)`
    

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# AdaptiveCrawler - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

AdaptiveCrawler
===============

The `AdaptiveCrawler` class implements intelligent web crawling that automatically determines when sufficient information has been gathered to answer a query. It uses a three-layer scoring system to evaluate coverage, consistency, and saturation.

Constructor
-----------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-0-1) AdaptiveCrawler( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-0-2)     crawler: AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-0-3)     config: Optional[AdaptiveConfig] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-0-4) )`

### Parameters

*   **crawler** (`AsyncWebCrawler`): The underlying web crawler instance to use for fetching pages
*   **config** (`Optional[AdaptiveConfig]`): Configuration settings for adaptive crawling behavior. If not provided, uses default settings.

Primary Method
--------------

### digest()

The main method that performs adaptive crawling starting from a URL with a specific query.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-1-1) async def digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-1-2)     start_url: str, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-1-3)     query: str, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-1-4)     resume_from: Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-1-5) ) -> CrawlState`

#### Parameters

*   **start\_url** (`str`): The starting URL for crawling
*   **query** (`str`): The search query that guides the crawling process
*   **resume\_from** (`Optional[Union[str, Path]]`): Path to a saved state file to resume from

#### Returns

*   **CrawlState**: The final crawl state containing all crawled URLs, knowledge base, and metrics

#### Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-2-1) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-2-2)     adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-2-3)     state = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-2-4)         start_url="https://docs.python.org", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-2-5)         query="async context managers" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-2-6)     )`

Properties
----------

### confidence

Current confidence score (0-1) indicating information sufficiency.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-3-1) @property [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-3-2) def confidence(self) -> float`

### coverage\_stats

Dictionary containing detailed coverage statistics.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-4-1) @property   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-4-2) def coverage_stats(self) -> Dict[str, float]`

Returns: - **coverage**: Query term coverage score - **consistency**: Information consistency score  
\- **saturation**: Content saturation score - **confidence**: Overall confidence score

### is\_sufficient

Boolean indicating whether sufficient information has been gathered.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-5-1) @property [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-5-2) def is_sufficient(self) -> bool`

### state

Access to the current crawl state.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-6-1) @property [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-6-2) def state(self) -> CrawlState`

Methods
-------

### get\_relevant\_content()

Retrieve the most relevant content from the knowledge base.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-7-1) def get_relevant_content( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-7-2)     self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-7-3)     top_k: int = 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-7-4) ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]`

#### Parameters

*   **top\_k** (`int`): Number of top relevant documents to return (default: 5)

#### Returns

List of dictionaries containing: - **url**: The URL of the page - **content**: The page content - **score**: Relevance score - **metadata**: Additional page metadata

### print\_stats()

Display crawl statistics in formatted output.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-8-1) def print_stats( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-8-2)     self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-8-3)     detailed: bool = False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-8-4) ) -> None`

#### Parameters

*   **detailed** (`bool`): If True, shows detailed metrics with colors. If False, shows summary table.

### export\_knowledge\_base()

Export the collected knowledge base to a JSONL file.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-9-1) def export_knowledge_base( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-9-2)     self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-9-3)     path: Union[str, Path] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-9-4) ) -> None`

#### Parameters

*   **path** (`Union[str, Path]`): Output file path for JSONL export

#### Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-10-1) adaptive.export_knowledge_base("my_knowledge.jsonl")`

### import\_knowledge\_base()

Import a previously exported knowledge base.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-11-1) def import_knowledge_base( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-11-2)     self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-11-3)     path: Union[str, Path] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-11-4) ) -> None`

#### Parameters

*   **path** (`Union[str, Path]`): Path to JSONL file to import

Configuration
-------------

The `AdaptiveConfig` class controls the behavior of adaptive crawling:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-1) @dataclass [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-2) class AdaptiveConfig: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-3)     confidence_threshold: float = 0.8      # Stop when confidence reaches this [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-4)     max_pages: int = 50                    # Maximum pages to crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-5)     top_k_links: int = 5                   # Links to follow per page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-6)     min_gain_threshold: float = 0.1        # Minimum expected gain to continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-7)     save_state: bool = False               # Auto-save crawl state [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-12-8)     state_path: Optional[str] = None       # Path for state persistence`

### Example with Custom Config

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-13-1) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-13-2)     confidence_threshold=0.7, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-13-3)     max_pages=20, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-13-4)     top_k_links=3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-13-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-13-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-13-7) adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler, config=config)`

Complete Example
----------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, AdaptiveCrawler, AdaptiveConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-5)     # Configure adaptive crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-6)     config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-7)         confidence_threshold=0.75, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-8)         max_pages=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-9)         save_state=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-10)         state_path="my_crawl.json" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-11)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-13)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-14)         adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-16)         # Start crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-17)         state = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-18)             start_url="https://example.com/docs", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-19)             query="authentication oauth2 jwt" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-20)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-22)         # Check results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-23)         print(f"Confidence achieved: {adaptive.confidence:.0%}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-24)         adaptive.print_stats() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-26)         # Get most relevant pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-27)         for page in adaptive.get_relevant_content(top_k=3): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-28)             print(f"- {page['url']} (score: {page['score']:.2f})") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-30)         # Export for later use [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-31)         adaptive.export_knowledge_base("auth_knowledge.jsonl") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-33) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/#__codelineno-14-34)     asyncio.run(main())`

See Also
--------

*   [digest() Method Reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/digest/)
    
*   [Adaptive Crawling Guide](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/)
    
*   [Advanced Adaptive Strategies](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/adaptive-strategies/)
    

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Supercharging Your AI Assistant: My Journey to Better LLM Contexts for crawl4ai - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Supercharging Your AI Assistant: My Journey to Better LLM Contexts for `crawl4ai`
=================================================================================

When I started diving deep into using AI coding assistants with my own libraries, particularly `crawl4ai`, I quickly realized that the common approach to providing context via a simple `llm.txt` or even a beefed-up `README.md` just wasn't cutting it. This document explains the problems I encountered and how I've tried to create a more effective system for `crawl4ai`, allowing you (and your AI assistant) to get precisely the right information.

My Frustration with Standard `llm.txt` Files
--------------------------------------------

My experience with generic `llm.txt` files for complex libraries like `crawl4ai` revealed several pain points:

1.  **Information Overload & Lost Focus:** I found that when I threw a massive, monolithic context file at an LLM, it often struggled. The sheer volume of information seemed to dilute its focus. If I asked a specific question about a niche feature, the LLM might get sidetracked by more prominent but currently irrelevant parts of the library. It felt like trying to find a single sentence in a thousand-page novel – the information was _there_, but not always accessible or prioritized correctly by the AI.
    
2.  **The "What" Without the "How" or "Why":** Most `llm.txt` files I encountered were essentially API dumps – a list of functions, classes, and parameters. This is the "what" of a library. But to truly use a library effectively, especially one as flexible as `crawl4ai`, you need the "how" (idiomatic usage patterns, best practices for common tasks) and the "why" (the design rationale behind certain features). Without this, I noticed my AI assistant would often generate syntactically correct but practically inefficient or non-idiomatic code. It was guessing the _intent_ and the _best way_ to use the library, and those guesses weren't always right.
    
3.  **No Guidance on "Thinking" Like an Expert:** A static list of facts doesn't teach an LLM the _art_ of using the library. It doesn't convey the trade-offs an experienced developer considers, the common pitfalls they've learned to avoid, or the clever ways to combine features to solve complex problems. I wanted my AI assistant to not just recall an API, but to help me _reason_ about the best way to build a solution with `crawl4ai`.
    

Inspiration: Selective Inclusion & Multi-Dimensional Understanding
------------------------------------------------------------------

I've always admired how libraries like Lodash or jQuery (in its modular days) allowed developers to pick and choose only the parts they needed, resulting in smaller, more focused bundles. This idea of modularity and selective inclusion resonated deeply with me as I thought about LLM context. Why force-feed an LLM the entire library's details when I'm only working on a specific component or task?

This led me to develop a new approach for `crawl4ai`: **multi-dimensional, modular contexts**.

Instead of one giant `llm.txt`, I've broken down the `crawl4ai` documentation into:

1.  **Logical Components:** Context is organized around the major functional areas of the library (e.g., Core, Data Extraction, Deep Crawling, Markdown Generation, etc.). This allows you to select context relevant only to the task at hand.
2.  **Three Dimensions of Context for Each Component:**
    *   **`_memory.md` (Foundational Memory):** This is the "what." It contains the precise, factual information about the component's public API, data structures, configuration objects, parameters, and method signatures. It's the detailed, unambiguous reference.
    *   **`_reasoning.md` (Reasoning & Problem-Solving Framework):** This is the "how" and "why." It includes design principles, common task workflows with decision guides, best practices, anti-patterns, illustrative code examples solving real problems, and explanations of trade-offs. It aims to guide the LLM in "thinking" like an expert `crawl4ai` user.
    *   **`_examples.md` (Practical Code Examples):** This is pure "show-me-the-code." It's a collection of runnable snippets demonstrating various ways to use the component's features and configurations, with minimal explanatory text. It’s for quickly seeing different patterns in action.

**The Goal:** My aim is to provide you with a flexible system. You can give your AI assistant: \* Just the **memory** files for quick API lookups. \* The **reasoning** files (perhaps with memory) for help designing solutions. \* The **examples** files for seeing practical implementations. \* A **combination** of these across one or more components tailored to your specific task. \* Or, for broader understanding, special aggregate contexts like the "Vibe Coding" context or the "All Library Context."

By providing these structured, multi-faceted contexts, I hope to significantly improve the quality and relevance of the assistance you get when using AI to code with `crawl4ai`. The following sections will guide you on how to select and use these different context files.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Installation 💻 - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Installation 💻
===============

Crawl4AI offers flexible installation options to suit various use cases. You can install it as a Python package, use it with Docker, or run it as a local server.

Option 1: Python Package Installation (Recommended)
---------------------------------------------------

Crawl4AI is now available on PyPI, making installation easier than ever. Choose the option that best fits your needs:

### Basic Installation

For basic web crawling and scraping tasks:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-0-1) pip install crawl4ai [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-0-2) playwright install # Install Playwright dependencies`

### Installation with PyTorch

For advanced text clustering (includes CosineSimilarity cluster strategy):

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install crawl4ai[torch]`

### Installation with Transformers

For text summarization and Hugging Face models:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-2-1) pip install crawl4ai[transformer]`

### Full Installation

For all features:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-3-1) pip install crawl4ai[all]`

### Development Installation

For contributors who plan to modify the source code:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-4-1) git clone https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai.git [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-4-2) cd crawl4ai [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-4-3) pip install -e ".[all]" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-4-4) playwright install # Install Playwright dependencies`

💡 After installation with "torch", "transformer", or "all" options, it's recommended to run the following CLI command to load the required models:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-5-1) crawl4ai-download-models`

This is optional but will boost the performance and speed of the crawler. You only need to do this once after installation.

Playwright Installation Note for Ubuntu
---------------------------------------

If you encounter issues with Playwright installation on Ubuntu, you may need to install additional dependencies:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-1) sudo apt-get install -y \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-2)     libwoff1 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-3)     libopus0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-4)     libwebp7 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-5)     libwebpdemux2 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-6)     libenchant-2-2 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-7)     libgudev-1.0-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-8)     libsecret-1-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-9)     libhyphen0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-10)     libgdk-pixbuf2.0-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-11)     libegl1 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-12)     libnotify4 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-13)     libxslt1.1 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-14)     libevent-2.1-7 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-15)     libgles2 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-16)     libxcomposite1 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-17)     libatk1.0-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-18)     libatk-bridge2.0-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-19)     libepoxy0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-20)     libgtk-3-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-21)     libharfbuzz-icu0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-22)     libgstreamer-gl1.0-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-23)     libgstreamer-plugins-bad1.0-0 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-24)     gstreamer1.0-plugins-good \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-25)     gstreamer1.0-plugins-bad \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-26)     libxt6 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-27)     libxaw7 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-28)     xvfb \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-29)     fonts-noto-color-emoji \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-30)     libfontconfig \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-31)     libfreetype6 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-32)     xfonts-cyrillic \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-33)     xfonts-scalable \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-34)     fonts-liberation \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-35)     fonts-ipafont-gothic \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-36)     fonts-wqy-zenhei \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-37)     fonts-tlwg-loma-otf \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-6-38)     fonts-freefont-ttf`

Option 2: Using Docker (Coming Soon)
------------------------------------

Docker support for Crawl4AI is currently in progress and will be available soon. This will allow you to run Crawl4AI in a containerized environment, ensuring consistency across different systems.

Option 3: Local Server Installation
-----------------------------------

For those who prefer to run Crawl4AI as a local server, instructions will be provided once the Docker implementation is complete.

Verifying Your Installation
---------------------------

After installation, you can verify that Crawl4AI is working correctly by running a simple Python script:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(verbose=True) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-6)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://www.example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-7)         print(result.markdown[:500])  # Print first 500 characters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-9) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/basic/installation/#__codelineno-7-10)     asyncio.run(main())`

This script should successfully crawl the example website and print the first 500 characters of the extracted content.

Getting Help
------------

If you encounter any issues during installation or usage, please check the [documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/)
 or raise an issue on the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/issues)
.

Happy crawling! 🕷️🤖

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Adaptive Crawling: Building Dynamic Knowledge That Grows on Demand - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Adaptive Crawling: Building Dynamic Knowledge That Grows on Demand
==================================================================

_Published on January 29, 2025 • 8 min read_

_By [unclecode](https://x.com/unclecode)
 • Follow me on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/unclecode)
 for more web scraping insights_

* * *

The Knowledge Capacitor
-----------------------

Imagine a capacitor that stores energy, releasing it precisely when needed. Now imagine that for information. That's Adaptive Crawling—a term I coined to describe a fundamentally different approach to web crawling. Instead of the brute force of traditional deep crawling, we build knowledge dynamically, growing it based on queries and circumstances, like a living organism responding to its environment.

This isn't just another crawling optimization. It's a paradigm shift from "crawl everything, hope for the best" to "crawl intelligently, know when to stop."

Why I Built This
----------------

I've watched too many startups burn through resources with a dangerous misconception: that LLMs make everything efficient. They don't. They make things _possible_, not necessarily _smart_. When you combine brute-force crawling with LLM processing, you're not just wasting time—you're hemorrhaging money on tokens, compute, and opportunity cost.

Consider this reality: - **Traditional deep crawling**: 500 pages → 50 useful → $15 in LLM tokens → 2 hours wasted - **Adaptive crawling**: 15 pages → 14 useful → $2 in tokens → 10 minutes → **7.5x cost reduction**

But it's not about crawling less. It's about crawling _right_.

The Information Theory Foundation
---------------------------------

\### 🧮 \*\*Pure Statistics, No Magic\*\* My first principle was crucial: start with classic statistical approaches. No embeddings. No LLMs. Just pure information theory:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-1) # Information gain calculation - the heart of adaptive crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-2) def calculate_information_gain(new_page, knowledge_base): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-3)     new_terms = extract_terms(new_page) - existing_terms(knowledge_base) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-4)     overlap = calculate_overlap(new_page, knowledge_base) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-6)     # High gain = many new terms + low overlap [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-7)     gain = len(new_terms) / (1 + overlap) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-0-8)     return gain`

This isn't regression to older methods—it's recognition that we've forgotten powerful, efficient solutions in our rush to apply LLMs everywhere.

The A\* of Web Crawling
-----------------------

Adaptive crawling implements what I call "information scenting"—like A\* pathfinding but for knowledge acquisition. Each link is evaluated not randomly, but by its probability of contributing meaningful information toward answering current and future queries.

🎯

**The Scenting Algorithm:**  
From available links, we select those with highest information gain. It's not about following every path—it's about following the _right_ paths. Like a bloodhound following the strongest scent to its target.

The Three Pillars of Intelligence
---------------------------------

### 1\. Coverage: The Breadth Sensor

Measures how well your knowledge spans the query space. Not just "do we have pages?" but "do we have the RIGHT pages?"

### 2\. Consistency: The Coherence Detector

Information from multiple sources should align. When pages agree, confidence rises. When they conflict, we need more data.

### 3\. Saturation: The Efficiency Guardian

The most crucial metric. When new pages stop adding information, we stop crawling. Simple. Powerful. Ignored by everyone else.

Real Impact: Time, Money, and Sanity
------------------------------------

Let me show you what this means for your bottom line:

### Building a Customer Support Knowledge Base

**Traditional Approach:**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-1-1) # Crawl entire documentation site [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-1-2) results = await crawler.crawl_bfs("https://docs.company.com", max_depth=5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-1-3) # Result: 1,200 pages, 18 hours, $150 in API costs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-1-4) # Useful content: ~100 pages scattered throughout`

**Adaptive Approach:**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-2-1) # Grow knowledge based on actual support queries [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-2-2) knowledge = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-2-3)     start_url="https://docs.company.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-2-4)     query="payment processing errors refund policies" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-2-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-2-6) # Result: 45 pages, 12 minutes, $8 in API costs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-2-7) # Useful content: 42 pages, all relevant`

**Savings: 93% time reduction, 95% cost reduction, 100% more sanity**

The Dynamic Growth Pattern
--------------------------

Knowledge grows like crystals in a supersaturated solution

Add a query (seed), and relevant information crystallizes around it.  
Change the query, and the knowledge structure adapts.

This is the beauty of adaptive crawling: your knowledge base becomes a living entity that grows based on actual needs, not hypothetical completeness.

Why "Adaptive"?
---------------

I specifically chose "Adaptive" because it captures the essence: the system adapts to what it finds. Dense technical documentation might need 20 pages for confidence. A simple FAQ might need just 5. The crawler doesn't follow a recipe—it reads the room and adjusts.

This is my term, my concept, and I have extensive plans for its evolution.

The Progressive Roadmap
-----------------------

This is just the beginning. My roadmap for Adaptive Crawling:

### Phase 1 (Current): Statistical Foundation

*   Pure information theory approach
*   No dependencies on expensive models
*   Proven efficiency gains

### Phase 2 (Now Available): Embedding Enhancement

*   Semantic understanding layered onto statistical base
*   Still efficient, now even smarter
*   Optional, not required

### Phase 3 (Future): LLM Integration

*   LLMs for complex reasoning tasks only
*   Used surgically, not wastefully
*   Always with statistical foundation underneath

The Efficiency Revolution
-------------------------

\### 💰 \*\*The Economics of Intelligence\*\* For a typical SaaS documentation crawl: \*\*Traditional Deep Crawling:\*\* - Pages crawled: 1,000 - Useful pages: 80 - Time spent: 3 hours - LLM tokens used: 2.5M - Cost: $75 - Efficiency: 8% \*\*Adaptive Crawling:\*\* - Pages crawled: 95 - Useful pages: 88 - Time spent: 15 minutes - LLM tokens used: 200K - Cost: $6 - Efficiency: 93% \*\*That's not optimization. That's transformation.\*\*

Missing the Forest for the Trees
--------------------------------

The startup world has a dangerous blind spot. We're so enamored with LLMs that we forget: just because you CAN process everything with an LLM doesn't mean you SHOULD.

Classic NLP and statistical methods can: - Filter irrelevant content before it reaches LLMs - Identify patterns without expensive inference - Make intelligent decisions in microseconds - Scale without breaking the bank

Adaptive crawling proves this. It uses battle-tested information theory to make smart decisions BEFORE expensive processing.

Your Knowledge, On Demand
-------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-1) # Monday: Customer asks about authentication [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-2) auth_knowledge = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-3)     "https://docs.api.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-4)     "oauth jwt authentication" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-7) # Tuesday: They ask about rate limiting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-8) # The crawler adapts, builds on existing knowledge [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-9) rate_limit_knowledge = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-10)     "https://docs.api.com",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-11)     "rate limiting throttling quotas" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-12) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-3-14) # Your knowledge base grows intelligently, not indiscriminately`

The Competitive Edge
--------------------

Companies using adaptive crawling will have: - **90% lower crawling costs** - **Knowledge bases that actually answer questions** - **Update cycles in minutes, not days** - **Happy customers who find answers fast** - **Engineers who sleep at night**

Those still using brute force? They'll wonder why their infrastructure costs keep rising while their customers keep complaining.

The Embedding Evolution (Now Available!)
----------------------------------------

\### 🧠 \*\*Semantic Understanding Without the Cost\*\* The embedding strategy brings semantic intelligence while maintaining efficiency:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-1) # Statistical strategy - great for exact terms [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-2) config_statistical = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-3)     strategy="statistical"  # Default [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-6) # Embedding strategy - understands concepts [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-7) config_embedding = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-8)     strategy="embedding", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-9)     embedding_model="sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-10)     n_query_variations=10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-4-11) )`

\*\*The magic\*\*: It automatically expands your query into semantic variations, maps the coverage space, and identifies gaps to fill intelligently.

### Real-World Comparison

\*\*Query\*\*: "authentication oauth" \*\*Statistical Strategy\*\*: - Searches for exact terms - 12 pages crawled - 78% confidence - Fast but literal

\*\*Embedding Strategy\*\*: - Understands "auth", "login", "SSO" - 8 pages crawled - 92% confidence - Semantic comprehension

### Detecting Irrelevance

One killer feature: the embedding strategy knows when to give up:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-1) # Crawling Python docs with a cooking query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-2) result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-3)     start_url="https://docs.python.org/3/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-4)     query="how to make spaghetti carbonara" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-7) # System detects irrelevance and stops [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-8) # Confidence: 5% (below threshold) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-9) # Pages crawled: 2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-5-10) # Stopped reason: "below_minimum_relevance_threshold"`

No more crawling hundreds of pages hoping to find something that doesn't exist!

Try It Yourself
---------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, AdaptiveCrawler, AdaptiveConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-4)     # Choose your strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-5)     config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-6)         strategy="embedding",  # or "statistical" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-7)         embedding_min_confidence_threshold=0.1  # Stop if irrelevant [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-10)     adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-12)     # Watch intelligence at work [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-13)     result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-14)         start_url="https://your-docs.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-15)         query="your users' actual questions" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-16)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-18)     # See the efficiency [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-19)     adaptive.print_stats() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-20)     print(f"Found {adaptive.confidence:.0%} of needed information") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-21)     print(f"In just {len(result.crawled_urls)} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/#__codelineno-6-22)     print(f"Saving you {1000 - len(result.crawled_urls)} unnecessary crawls")`

A Personal Note
---------------

I created Adaptive Crawling because I was tired of watching smart people make inefficient choices. We have incredibly powerful statistical tools that we've forgotten in our rush toward LLMs. This is my attempt to bring balance back to the Force.

This is not just a feature. It's a philosophy: **Grow knowledge on demand. Stop when you have enough. Save time, money, and computational resources for what really matters.**

The Future is Adaptive
----------------------

Traditional Crawling: Drinking from a firehose  
Adaptive Crawling: Sipping exactly what you need

The future of web crawling isn't about processing more data.  
It's about processing the _right_ data.

Join me in making web crawling intelligent, efficient, and actually useful. Because in the age of information overload, the winners won't be those who collect the most data—they'll be those who collect the _right_ data.

* * *

_Adaptive Crawling is now part of Crawl4AI. [Get started with the documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/)
 or [dive into the mathematical framework](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/PROGRESSIVE_CRAWLING.md)
. For updates on my work in information theory and efficient AI, follow me on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/unclecode)
._

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Dockerize hooks - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Introducing Event Streams and Interactive Hooks in Crawl4AI
-----------------------------------------------------------

![event-driven-crawl](https://res.cloudinary.com/kidocode/image/upload/t_400x400/v1734344008/15bb8bbb-83ac-43ac-962d-3feb3e0c3bbf_2_tjmr4n.webp)

In the near future, I’m planning to enhance Crawl4AI’s capabilities by introducing an event stream mechanism that will give clients deeper, real-time insights into the crawling process. Today, hooks are a powerful feature at the code level—they let developers define custom logic at key points in the crawl. However, when using Crawl4AI as a service (e.g., through a Dockerized API), there isn’t an easy way to interact with these hooks at runtime.

**What’s Changing?**

I’m working on a solution that will allow the crawler to emit a continuous stream of events, updating clients on the current crawling stage, encountered pages, and any decision points. This event stream could be exposed over a standardized protocol like Server-Sent Events (SSE) or WebSockets, enabling clients to “subscribe” and listen as the crawler works.

**Interactivity Through Process IDs**

A key part of this new design is the concept of a unique process ID for each crawl session. Imagine you’re listening to an event stream that informs you: - The crawler just hit a certain page  
\- It triggered a hook and is now pausing for instructions

With the event stream in place, you can send a follow-up request back to the server—referencing the unique process ID—to provide extra data, instructions, or parameters. This might include selecting which links to follow next, adjusting extraction strategies, or providing authentication tokens for a protected API. Once the crawler receives these instructions, it resumes execution with the updated context.
