# 🚀 Crawl4AI v0.7.0: The Adaptive Intelligence Update - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

🚀 Crawl4AI v0.7.0: The Adaptive Intelligence Update
====================================================

_January 28, 2025 • 10 min read_

* * *

Today I'm releasing Crawl4AI v0.7.0—the Adaptive Intelligence Update. This release introduces fundamental improvements in how Crawl4AI handles modern web complexity through adaptive learning, intelligent content discovery, and advanced extraction capabilities.

🎯 What's New at a Glance
-------------------------

*   **Adaptive Crawling**: Your crawler now learns and adapts to website patterns
*   **Virtual Scroll Support**: Complete content extraction from infinite scroll pages
*   **Link Preview with Intelligent Scoring**: Intelligent link analysis and prioritization
*   **Async URL Seeder**: Discover thousands of URLs in seconds with intelligent filtering
*   **Performance Optimizations**: Significant speed and memory improvements

🧠 Adaptive Crawling: Intelligence Through Pattern Learning
-----------------------------------------------------------

**The Problem:** Websites change. Class names shift. IDs disappear. Your carefully crafted selectors break at 3 AM, and you wake up to empty datasets and angry stakeholders.

**My Solution:** I implemented an adaptive learning system that observes patterns, builds confidence scores, and adjusts extraction strategies on the fly. It's like having a junior developer who gets better at their job with every page they scrape.

### Technical Deep-Dive

The Adaptive Crawler maintains a persistent state for each domain, tracking: - Pattern success rates - Selector stability over time  
\- Content structure variations - Extraction confidence scores

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, AdaptiveCrawler, AdaptiveConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-6)     # Configure adaptive crawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-7)     config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-8)         strategy="statistical",  # or "embedding" for semantic understanding [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-9)         max_pages=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-10)         confidence_threshold=0.7,  # Stop at 70% confidence [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-11)         top_k_links=3,  # Follow top 3 links per page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-12)         min_gain_threshold=0.05  # Need 5% information gain to continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-15)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(verbose=False) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-16)         adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-18)         print("Starting adaptive crawl about Python decorators...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-19)         result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-20)             start_url="https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-21)             query="python decorators functions wrapping" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-22)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-24)         print(f"\n✅ Crawling Complete!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-25)         print(f"• Confidence Level: {adaptive.confidence:.0%}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-26)         print(f"• Pages Crawled: {len(result.crawled_urls)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-27)         print(f"• Knowledge Base: {len(adaptive.state.knowledge_base)} documents") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-29)         # Get most relevant content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-30)         relevant = adaptive.get_relevant_content(top_k=3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-31)         print(f"\nMost Relevant Pages:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-32)         for i, page in enumerate(relevant, 1): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-33)             print(f"{i}. {page['url']} (relevance: {page['score']:.2%})") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-0-35) asyncio.run(main())`

**Expected Real-World Impact:** - **News Aggregation**: Maintain 95%+ extraction accuracy even as news sites update their templates - **E-commerce Monitoring**: Track product changes across hundreds of stores without constant maintenance - **Research Data Collection**: Build robust academic datasets that survive website redesigns - **Reduced Maintenance**: Cut selector update time by 80% for frequently-changing sites

🌊 Virtual Scroll: Complete Content Capture
-------------------------------------------

**The Problem:** Modern web apps only render what's visible. Scroll down, new content appears, old content vanishes into the void. Traditional crawlers capture that first viewport and miss 90% of the content. It's like reading only the first page of every book.

**My Solution:** I built Virtual Scroll support that mimics human browsing behavior, capturing content as it loads and preserving it before the browser's garbage collector strikes.

### Implementation Details

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import VirtualScrollConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-3) # For social media feeds (Twitter/X style) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-4) twitter_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-5)     container_selector="[data-testid='primaryColumn']", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-6)     scroll_count=20,                    # Number of scrolls [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-7)     scroll_by="container_height",       # Smart scrolling by container size [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-8)     wait_after_scroll=1.0              # Let content load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-11) # For e-commerce product grids (Instagram style) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-12) grid_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-13)     container_selector="main .product-grid", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-14)     scroll_count=30, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-15)     scroll_by=800,                     # Fixed pixel scrolling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-16)     wait_after_scroll=1.5              # Images need time [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-17) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-19) # For news feeds with lazy loading [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-20) news_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-21)     container_selector=".article-feed", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-22)     scroll_count=50, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-23)     scroll_by="page_height",           # Viewport-based scrolling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-24)     wait_after_scroll=0.5              # Wait for content to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-25) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-27) # Use it in your crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-28) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-29)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-30)         "https://twitter.com/trending", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-31)         config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-32)             virtual_scroll_config=twitter_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-33)             # Combine with other features [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-34)             extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-35)                 "tweets": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-36)                     "selector": "[data-testid='tweet']", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-37)                     "fields": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-38)                         "text": {"selector": "[data-testid='tweetText']", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-39)                         "likes": {"selector": "[data-testid='like']", "type": "text"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-40)                     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-41)                 } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-42)             }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-43)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-44)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-45) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-1-46)     print(f"Captured {len(result.extracted_content['tweets'])} tweets")`

**Key Capabilities:** - **DOM Recycling Awareness**: Detects and handles virtual DOM element recycling - **Smart Scroll Physics**: Three modes - container height, page height, or fixed pixels - **Content Preservation**: Captures content before it's destroyed - **Intelligent Stopping**: Stops when no new content appears - **Memory Efficient**: Streams content instead of holding everything in memory

**Expected Real-World Impact:** - **Social Media Analysis**: Capture entire Twitter threads with hundreds of replies, not just top 10 - **E-commerce Scraping**: Extract 500+ products from infinite scroll catalogs vs. 20-50 with traditional methods  
\- **News Aggregation**: Get all articles from modern news sites, not just above-the-fold content - **Research Applications**: Complete data extraction from academic databases using virtual pagination

🔗 Link Preview: Intelligent Link Analysis and Scoring
------------------------------------------------------

**The Problem:** You crawl a page and get 200 links. Which ones matter? Which lead to the content you actually want? Traditional crawlers force you to follow everything or build complex filters.

**My Solution:** I implemented a three-layer scoring system that analyzes links like a human would—considering their position, context, and relevance to your goals.

### Intelligent Link Analysis and Scoring

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode, AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-3) from crawl4ai.adaptive_crawler import LinkPreviewConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-6)     # Configure intelligent link analysis [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-7)     link_config = LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-8)         include_internal=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-9)         include_external=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-10)         max_links=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-11)         concurrency=5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-12)         query="python tutorial",  # For contextual scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-13)         score_threshold=0.3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-14)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-16)     # Use in your crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-18)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-19)             "https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-20)             config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-21)                 link_preview_config=link_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-22)                 score_links=True,  # Enable intrinsic scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-23)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-24)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-25)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-27)         # Access scored and sorted links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-28)         if result.success and result.links: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-29)             for link in result.links.get("internal", []): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-30)                 text = link.get('text', 'No text')[:40] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-31)                 print( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-32)                     text, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-33)                     f"{link.get('intrinsic_score', 0):.1f}/10" if link.get('intrinsic_score') is not None else "0.0/10", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-34)                     f"{link.get('contextual_score', 0):.2f}/1" if link.get('contextual_score') is not None else "0.00/1", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-35)                     f"{link.get('total_score', 0):.3f}" if link.get('total_score') is not None else "0.000" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-36)                 ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-2-38) asyncio.run(main())`

**Scoring Components:**

1.  **Intrinsic Score**: Based on link quality indicators
2.  Position on page (navigation, content, footer)
3.  Link attributes (rel, title, class names)
4.  Anchor text quality and length
5.  URL structure and depth
    
6.  **Contextual Score**: Relevance to your query using BM25 algorithm
    
7.  Keyword matching in link text and title
8.  Meta description analysis
9.  Content preview scoring
    
10.  **Total Score**: Combined score for final ranking
    

**Expected Real-World Impact:** - **Research Efficiency**: Find relevant papers 10x faster by following only high-score links - **Competitive Analysis**: Automatically identify important pages on competitor sites - **Content Discovery**: Build topic-focused crawlers that stay on track - **SEO Audits**: Identify and prioritize high-value internal linking opportunities

🎣 Async URL Seeder: Automated URL Discovery at Scale
-----------------------------------------------------

**The Problem:** You want to crawl an entire domain but only have the homepage. Or worse, you want specific content types across thousands of pages. Manual URL discovery? That's a job for machines, not humans.

**My Solution:** I built Async URL Seeder—a turbocharged URL discovery engine that combines multiple sources with intelligent filtering and relevance scoring.

### Technical Architecture

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncUrlSeeder, SeedingConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-5)     async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-6)         # Discover Python tutorial URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-7)         config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-8)             source="sitemap",  # Use sitemap [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-9)             pattern="*python*",  # URL pattern filter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-10)             extract_head=True,  # Get metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-11)             query="python tutorial",  # For relevance scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-12)             scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-13)             score_threshold=0.2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-14)             max_urls=10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-15)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-17)         print("Discovering Python async tutorial URLs...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-18)         urls = await seeder.urls("https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-20)         print(f"\n✅ Found {len(urls)} relevant URLs:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-21)         for i, url_info in enumerate(urls[:5], 1): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-22)             print(f"\n{i}. {url_info['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-23)             if url_info.get('relevance_score'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-24)                 print(f"   Relevance: {url_info['relevance_score']:.3f}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-25)             if url_info.get('head_data', {}).get('title'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-26)                 print(f"   Title: {url_info['head_data']['title'][:60]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-3-28) asyncio.run(main())`

**Discovery Methods:** - **Sitemap Mining**: Parses robots.txt and all linked sitemaps - **Common Crawl**: Queries the Common Crawl index for historical URLs - **Intelligent Crawling**: Follows links with smart depth control - **Pattern Analysis**: Learns URL structures and generates variations

**Expected Real-World Impact:** - **Migration Projects**: Discover 10,000+ URLs from legacy sites in under 60 seconds - **Market Research**: Map entire competitor ecosystems automatically - **Academic Research**: Build comprehensive datasets without manual URL collection - **SEO Audits**: Find every indexable page with content scoring - **Content Archival**: Ensure no content is left behind during site migrations

⚡ Performance Optimizations
---------------------------

This release includes significant performance improvements through optimized resource handling, better concurrency management, and reduced memory footprint.

### What We Optimized

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-1) # Optimized crawling with v0.7.0 improvements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-2) results = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-3) for url in urls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-4)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-5)         url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-6)         config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-7)             # Performance optimizations [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-8)             wait_until="domcontentloaded",  # Faster than networkidle [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-9)             cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED    # Enable caching [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-10)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-11)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-4-12)     results.append(result)`

**Performance Gains:** - **Startup Time**: 70% faster browser initialization - **Page Loading**: 40% reduction with smart resource blocking - **Extraction**: 3x faster with compiled CSS selectors - **Memory Usage**: 60% reduction with streaming processing - **Concurrent Crawls**: Handle 5x more parallel requests

🔧 Important Changes
--------------------

### Breaking Changes

*   `link_extractor` renamed to `link_preview` (better reflects functionality)
*   Minimum Python version now 3.9
*   `CrawlerConfig` split into `CrawlerRunConfig` and `BrowserConfig`

### Migration Guide

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-1) # Old (v0.6.x) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-2) from crawl4ai import CrawlerConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-3) config = CrawlerConfig(timeout=30000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-5) # New (v0.7.0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-6) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-7) browser_config = BrowserConfig(timeout=30000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-5-8) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS)`

🤖 Coming Soon: Intelligent Web Automation
------------------------------------------

I'm currently working on bringing advanced automation capabilities to Crawl4AI. This includes:

*   **Crawl Agents**: Autonomous crawlers that understand your goals and adapt their strategies
*   **Auto JS Generation**: Automatic JavaScript code generation for complex interactions
*   **Smart Form Handling**: Intelligent form detection and filling
*   **Context-Aware Actions**: Crawlers that understand page context and make decisions

These features are under active development and will revolutionize how we approach web automation. Stay tuned!

🚀 Get Started
--------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.0/#__codelineno-6-1) pip install crawl4ai==0.7.0`

Check out the [updated documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/)
.

Questions? Issues? I'm always listening: - GitHub: [github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai)
 - Discord: [discord.gg/crawl4ai](https://discord.gg/jP8KfhDhyN)
 - Twitter: [@unclecode](https://x.com/unclecode)

Happy crawling! 🕷️

* * *

_P.S. If you're using Crawl4AI in production, I'd love to hear about it. Your use cases inspire the next features._

* * *

\> Feedback

# Crawl4AI v0.7.8: Stability & Bug Fix Release - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl4AI v0.7.8: Stability & Bug Fix Release
============================================

_December 2025_

* * *

I'm releasing Crawl4AI v0.7.8—a focused stability release that addresses 11 bugs reported by the community. While there are no new features in this release, these fixes resolve important issues affecting Docker deployments, LLM extraction, URL handling, and dependency compatibility.

What's Fixed at a Glance
------------------------

*   **Docker API**: Fixed ContentRelevanceFilter deserialization, ProxyConfig serialization, and cache folder permissions
*   **LLM Extraction**: Configurable rate limiter backoff, HTML input format support, and proper URL handling for raw HTML
*   **URL Handling**: Correct relative URL resolution after JavaScript redirects
*   **Dependencies**: Replaced deprecated PyPDF2 with pypdf, Pydantic v2 ConfigDict compatibility
*   **AdaptiveCrawler**: Fixed query expansion to actually use LLM instead of hardcoded mock data

Bug Fixes
---------

### Docker & API Fixes

#### ContentRelevanceFilter Deserialization (#1642)

**The Problem:** When sending deep crawl requests to the Docker API with `ContentRelevanceFilter`, the server failed to deserialize the filter, causing requests to fail.

**The Fix:** I added `ContentRelevanceFilter` to the public exports and enhanced the deserialization logic with dynamic imports.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-1) # This now works correctly in Docker API [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-2) import httpx [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-4) request = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-5)     "urls": ["https://docs.example.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-6)     "crawler_config": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-7)         "deep_crawl_strategy": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-8)             "type": "BFSDeepCrawlStrategy", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-9)             "max_depth": 2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-10)             "filter_chain": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-11)                 { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-12)                     "type": "ContentRelevanceFilter", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-13)                     "query": "API documentation", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-14)                     "threshold": 0.3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-15)                 } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-16)             ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-17)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-18)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-19) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-21) async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-22)     response = await client.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json=request) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-0-23)     # Previously failed, now works!`

#### ProxyConfig JSON Serialization (#1629)

**The Problem:** `BrowserConfig.to_dict()` failed when `proxy_config` was set because `ProxyConfig` wasn't being serialized to a dictionary.

**The Fix:** `ProxyConfig.to_dict()` is now called during serialization.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai.async_configs import ProxyConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-4) proxy = ProxyConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-5)     server="http://proxy.example.com:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-6)     username="user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-7)     password="pass" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-10) config = BrowserConfig(headless=True, proxy_config=proxy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-12) # Previously raised TypeError, now works [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-13) config_dict = config.to_dict() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-1-14) json.dumps(config_dict)  # Valid JSON`

#### Docker Cache Folder Permissions (#1638)

**The Problem:** The `.cache` folder in the Docker image had incorrect permissions, causing crawling to fail when caching was enabled.

**The Fix:** Corrected ownership and permissions during image build.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-2-1) # Cache now works correctly in Docker [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-2-2) docker run -d -p 11235:11235 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-2-3)     --shm-size=1g \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-2-4)     -v ./my-cache:/app/.cache \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-2-5)     unclecode/crawl4ai:0.7.8`

* * *

### LLM & Extraction Fixes

#### Configurable Rate Limiter Backoff (#1269)

**The Problem:** The LLM rate limiting backoff parameters were hardcoded, making it impossible to adjust retry behavior for different API rate limits.

**The Fix:** `LLMConfig` now accepts three new parameters for complete control over retry behavior.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-3) # Default behavior (unchanged) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-4) default_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-5) # backoff_base_delay=2, backoff_max_attempts=3, backoff_exponential_factor=2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-7) # Custom configuration for APIs with strict rate limits [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-8) custom_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-9)     provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-10)     backoff_base_delay=5,           # Wait 5 seconds on first retry [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-11)     backoff_max_attempts=5,          # Try up to 5 times [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-12)     backoff_exponential_factor=3     # Multiply delay by 3 each attempt [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-13) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-3-15) # Retry sequence: 5s -> 15s -> 45s -> 135s -> 405s`

#### LLM Strategy HTML Input Support (#1178)

**The Problem:** `LLMExtractionStrategy` always sent markdown to the LLM, but some extraction tasks work better with HTML structure preserved.

**The Fix:** Added `input_format` parameter supporting `"markdown"`, `"html"`, `"fit_markdown"`, `"cleaned_html"`, and `"fit_html"`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-1) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-3) # Default: markdown input (unchanged) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-4) markdown_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-5)     llm_config=LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-6)     instruction="Extract product information" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-9) # NEW: HTML input - preserves table/list structure [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-10) html_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-11)     llm_config=LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-12)     instruction="Extract the data table preserving structure", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-13)     input_format="html" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-14) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-16) # NEW: Filtered markdown - only relevant content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-17) fit_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-18)     llm_config=LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-19)     instruction="Summarize the main content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-20)     input_format="fit_markdown" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-4-21) )`

#### Raw HTML URL Variable (#1116)

**The Problem:** When using `url="raw:<html>..."`, the entire HTML content was being passed to extraction strategies as the URL parameter, polluting LLM prompts.

**The Fix:** The URL is now correctly set to `"Raw HTML"` for raw HTML inputs.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-3) html = "<html><body><h1>Test</h1></body></html>" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-5) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-6)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-7)         url=f"raw:{html}", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-8)         config=CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=my_strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-5-10)     # extraction_strategy receives url="Raw HTML" instead of the HTML blob`

* * *

### URL Handling Fix

#### Relative URLs After Redirects (#1268)

**The Problem:** When JavaScript caused a page redirect, relative links were resolved against the original URL instead of the final URL.

**The Fix:** `redirected_url` now captures the actual page URL after all JavaScript execution completes.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-4)     # Page at /old-page redirects via JS to /new-page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-5)     result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com/old-page") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-7)     # BEFORE: redirected_url = "https://example.com/old-page" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-8)     # AFTER:  redirected_url = "https://example.com/new-page" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-10)     # Links are now correctly resolved against the final URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-11)     for link in result.links['internal']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-6-12)         print(link['href'])  # Relative links resolved correctly`

* * *

### Dependency & Compatibility Fixes

#### PyPDF2 Replaced with pypdf (#1412)

**The Problem:** PyPDF2 was deprecated in 2022 and is no longer maintained.

**The Fix:** Replaced with the actively maintained `pypdf` library.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-7-1) # Installation (unchanged) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-7-2) pip install crawl4ai[pdf] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-7-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-7-4) # The PDF processor now uses pypdf internally [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-7-5) # No code changes required - API remains the same`

#### Pydantic v2 ConfigDict Compatibility (#678)

**The Problem:** Using the deprecated `class Config` syntax caused deprecation warnings with Pydantic v2.

**The Fix:** Migrated to `model_config = ConfigDict(...)` syntax.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-8-1) # No more deprecation warnings when importing crawl4ai models [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai.models import CrawlResult [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-8-3) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-8-5) # All models are now Pydantic v2 compatible`

* * *

### AdaptiveCrawler Fix

#### Query Expansion Using LLM (#1621)

**The Problem:** The `EmbeddingStrategy` in AdaptiveCrawler had commented-out LLM code and was using hardcoded mock query variations instead.

**The Fix:** Uncommented and activated the LLM call for actual query expansion.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-9-1) # AdaptiveCrawler query expansion now actually uses the LLM [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-9-2) # Instead of hardcoded variations like: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-9-3) # variations = {'queries': ['what are the best vegetables...']} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-9-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-9-5) # The LLM generates relevant query variations based on your actual query`

* * *

### Code Formatting Fix

#### Import Statement Formatting (#1181)

**The Problem:** When extracting code from web pages, import statements were sometimes concatenated without proper line separation.

**The Fix:** Import statements now maintain proper newline separation.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-10-1) # BEFORE: "import osimport sysfrom pathlib import Path" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-10-2) # AFTER: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-10-3) # import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-10-4) # import sys [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-10-5) # from pathlib import Path`

* * *

Breaking Changes
----------------

**None!** This release is fully backward compatible.

*   All existing code continues to work without modification
*   New parameters have sensible defaults matching previous behavior
*   No API changes to existing functionality

* * *

Upgrade Instructions
--------------------

### Python Package

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-11-1) pip install --upgrade crawl4ai [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-11-2) # or [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-11-3) pip install crawl4ai==0.7.8`

### Docker

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-12-1) # Pull the latest version [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-12-2) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:0.7.8 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-12-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-12-4) # Run [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-12-5) docker run -d -p 11235:11235 --shm-size=1g unclecode/crawl4ai:0.7.8`

* * *

Verification
------------

Run the verification tests to confirm all fixes are working:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.8/#__codelineno-13-1) python docs/releases_review/demo_v0.7.8.py`

This runs actual tests that verify each bug fix is properly implemented.

* * *

Acknowledgments
---------------

Thank you to everyone who reported these issues and provided detailed reproduction steps. Your bug reports make Crawl4AI better for everyone.

Issues fixed: #1642, #1638, #1629, #1621, #1412, #1269, #1268, #1181, #1178, #1116, #678

* * *

Support & Resources
-------------------

*   **Documentation**: [docs.crawl4ai.com](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/)
    
*   **GitHub**: [github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai)
    
*   **Discord**: [discord.gg/crawl4ai](https://discord.gg/jP8KfhDhyN)
    
*   **Twitter**: [@unclecode](https://x.com/unclecode)
    

* * *

**This stability release ensures Crawl4AI works reliably across Docker deployments, LLM extraction workflows, and various edge cases. Thank you for your continued support and feedback!**

**Happy crawling!**

_\- unclecode_

* * *

\> Feedback
