# Installation - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Installation & Setup (2023 Edition)
===================================

1\. Basic Installation
----------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-0-1) pip install crawl4ai`

This installs the **core** Crawl4AI library along with essential dependencies. **No** advanced features (like transformers or PyTorch) are included yet.

2\. Initial Setup & Diagnostics
-------------------------------

### 2.1 Run the Setup Command

After installing, call:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-1-1) crawl4ai-setup`

**What does it do?** - Installs or updates required browser dependencies for both regular and undetected modes - Performs OS-level checks (e.g., missing libs on Linux) - Confirms your environment is ready to crawl

### 2.2 Diagnostics

Optionally, you can run **diagnostics** to confirm everything is functioning:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-2-1) crawl4ai-doctor`

This command attempts to: - Check Python version compatibility - Verify Playwright installation - Inspect environment variables or library conflicts

If any issues arise, follow its suggestions (e.g., installing additional system packages) and re-run `crawl4ai-setup`.

* * *

3\. Verifying Installation: A Simple Crawl (Skip this step if you already run `crawl4ai-doctor`)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below is a minimal Python script demonstrating a **basic** crawl. It uses our new **`BrowserConfig`** and **`CrawlerRunConfig`** for clarity, though no custom settings are passed in this example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-6)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-7)             url="https://www.example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-8)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-9)         print(result.markdown[:300])  # Show the first 300 characters of extracted text [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-11) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-3-12)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Expected** outcome: - A headless browser session loads `example.com` - Crawl4AI returns ~300 characters of markdown.  
If errors occur, rerun `crawl4ai-doctor` or manually ensure Playwright is installed correctly.

* * *

4\. Advanced Installation (Optional)
------------------------------------

**Warning**: Only install these **if you truly need them**. They bring in larger dependencies, including big models, which can increase disk usage and memory load significantly.

### 4.1 Torch, Transformers, or All

*   **Text Clustering (Torch)**  
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-4-1) pip install crawl4ai[torch] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-4-2) crawl4ai-setup`
    
    Installs PyTorch-based features (e.g., cosine similarity or advanced semantic chunking).
    
*   **Transformers**  
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-5-1) pip install crawl4ai[transformer] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-5-2) crawl4ai-setup`
    
    Adds Hugging Face-based summarization or generation strategies.
    
*   **All Features**  
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-6-1) pip install crawl4ai[all] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-6-2) crawl4ai-setup`
    

#### (Optional) Pre-Fetching Models

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-7-1) crawl4ai-download-models`

This step caches large models locally (if needed). **Only do this** if your workflow requires them.

* * *

5\. Docker (Experimental)
-------------------------

We provide a **temporary** Docker approach for testing. **It’s not stable and may break** with future releases. We plan a major Docker revamp in a future stable version, 2025 Q1. If you still want to try:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-8-1) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:basic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/installation/#__codelineno-8-2) docker run -p 11235:11235 unclecode/crawl4ai:basic`

You can then make POST requests to `http://localhost:11235/crawl` to perform crawls. **Production usage** is discouraged until our new Docker approach is ready (planned in Jan or Feb 2025).

* * *

6\. Local Server Mode (Legacy)
------------------------------

Some older docs mention running Crawl4AI as a local server. This approach has been **partially replaced** by the new Docker-based prototype and upcoming stable server release. You can experiment, but expect major changes. Official local server instructions will arrive once the new Docker architecture is finalized.

* * *

Summary
-------

1. **Install** with `pip install crawl4ai` and run `crawl4ai-setup`. 2. **Diagnose** with `crawl4ai-doctor` if you see errors. 3. **Verify** by crawling `example.com` with minimal `BrowserConfig` + `CrawlerRunConfig`. 4. **Advanced** features (Torch, Transformers) are **optional**—avoid them if you don’t need them (they significantly increase resource usage). 5. **Docker** is **experimental**—use at your own risk until the stable version is released. 6. **Local server** references in older docs are largely deprecated; a new solution is in progress.

**Got questions?** Check [GitHub issues](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/issues)
 for updates or ask the community!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Command Line Interface - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl4AI CLI Guide
==================

Table of Contents
-----------------

*   [Installation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#installation)
    
*   [Basic Usage](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#basic-usage)
    
*   [Configuration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#configuration)
    
*   [Browser Configuration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#browser-configuration)
    
*   [Crawler Configuration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#crawler-configuration)
    
*   [Extraction Configuration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#extraction-configuration)
    
*   [Content Filtering](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#content-filtering)
    
*   [Advanced Features](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#advanced-features)
    
*   [LLM Q&A](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#llm-qa)
    
*   [Structured Data Extraction](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#structured-data-extraction)
    
*   [Content Filtering](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#content-filtering-1)
    
*   [Output Formats](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#output-formats)
    
*   [Examples](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#examples)
    
*   [Configuration Reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#configuration-reference)
    
*   [Best Practices & Tips](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#best-practices--tips)
    

Installation
------------

The Crawl4AI CLI will be installed automatically when you install the library.

Basic Usage
-----------

The Crawl4AI CLI (`crwl`) provides a simple interface to the Crawl4AI library:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-1) # Basic crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-2) crwl https://example.com [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-4) # Get markdown output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-5) crwl https://example.com -o markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-7) # Verbose JSON output with cache bypass [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-8) crwl https://example.com -o json -v --bypass-cache [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-10) # See usage examples [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-0-11) crwl --example`

Quick Example of Advanced Usage
-------------------------------

If you clone the repository and run the following command, you will receive the content of the page in JSON format according to a JSON-CSS schema:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-1-1) crwl "https://www.infoq.com/ai-ml-data-eng/" -e docs/examples/cli/extract_css.yml -s docs/examples/cli/css_schema.json -o json;`

Configuration
-------------

### Browser Configuration

Browser settings can be configured via YAML file or command line parameters:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-2-1) # browser.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-2-2) headless: true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-2-3) viewport_width: 1280 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-2-4) user_agent_mode: "random" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-2-5) verbose: true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-2-6) ignore_https_errors: true`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-3-1) # Using config file [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-3-2) crwl https://example.com -B browser.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-3-4) # Using direct parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-3-5) crwl https://example.com -b "headless=true,viewport_width=1280,user_agent_mode=random"`

### Crawler Configuration

Control crawling behavior:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-1) # crawler.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-2) cache_mode: "bypass" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-3) wait_until: "networkidle" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-4) page_timeout: 30000 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-5) delay_before_return_html: 0.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-6) word_count_threshold: 100 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-7) scan_full_page: true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-8) scroll_delay: 0.3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-9) process_iframes: false [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-10) remove_overlay_elements: true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-11) magic: true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-4-12) verbose: true`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-5-1) # Using config file [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-5-2) crwl https://example.com -C crawler.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-5-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-5-4) # Using direct parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-5-5) crwl https://example.com -c "css_selector=#main,delay_before_return_html=2,scan_full_page=true"`

### Extraction Configuration

Two types of extraction are supported:

1.  CSS/XPath-based extraction:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-6-1) # extract_css.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-6-2) type: "json-css" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-6-3) params: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-6-4)   verbose: true`
    

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-1) // css_schema.json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-2) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-3)   "name": "ArticleExtractor", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-4)   "baseSelector": ".article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-5)   "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-6)     { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-7)       "name": "title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-8)       "selector": "h1.title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-9)       "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-10)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-11)     { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-12)       "name": "link", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-13)       "selector": "a.read-more", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-14)       "type": "attribute", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-15)       "attribute": "href" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-16)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-17)   ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-7-18) }`

1.  LLM-based extraction:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-1) # extract_llm.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-2) type: "llm" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-3) provider: "openai/gpt-4" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-4) instruction: "Extract all articles with their titles and links" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-5) api_token: "your-token" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-6) params: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-7)   temperature: 0.3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-8-8)   max_tokens: 1000`
    

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-1) // llm_schema.json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-2) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-3)   "title": "Article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-4)   "type": "object", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-5)   "properties": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-6)     "title": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-7)       "type": "string", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-8)       "description": "The title of the article" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-9)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-10)     "link": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-11)       "type": "string", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-12)       "description": "URL to the full article" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-13)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-14)   } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-9-15) }`

Advanced Features
-----------------

### LLM Q&A

Ask questions about crawled content:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-1) # Simple question [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-2) crwl https://example.com -q "What is the main topic discussed?" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-4) # View content then ask questions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-5) crwl https://example.com -o markdown  # See content first [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-6) crwl https://example.com -q "Summarize the key points" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-7) crwl https://example.com -q "What are the conclusions?" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-9) # Combined with advanced crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-10) crwl https://example.com \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-11)     -B browser.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-12)     -c "css_selector=article,scan_full_page=true" \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-10-13)     -q "What are the pros and cons mentioned?"`

First-time setup: - Prompts for LLM provider and API token - Saves configuration in `~/.crawl4ai/global.yml` - Supports various providers (openai/gpt-4, anthropic/claude-3-sonnet, etc.) - For case of `ollama` you do not need to provide API token. - See [LiteLLM Providers](https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/providers)
 for full list

### Structured Data Extraction

Extract structured data using CSS selectors:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-11-1) crwl https://example.com \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-11-2)     -e extract_css.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-11-3)     -s css_schema.json \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-11-4)     -o json`

Or using LLM-based extraction:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-12-1) crwl https://example.com \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-12-2)     -e extract_llm.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-12-3)     -s llm_schema.json \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-12-4)     -o json`

### Content Filtering

Filter content for relevance:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-1) # filter_bm25.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-2) type: "bm25" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-3) query: "target content" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-4) threshold: 1.0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-6) # filter_pruning.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-7) type: "pruning" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-8) query: "focus topic" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-13-9) threshold: 0.48`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-14-1) crwl https://example.com -f filter_bm25.yml -o markdown-fit`

Output Formats
--------------

*   `all` - Full crawl result including metadata
*   `json` - Extracted structured data (when using extraction)
*   `markdown` / `md` - Raw markdown output
*   `markdown-fit` / `md-fit` - Filtered markdown for better readability

Complete Examples
-----------------

1.  Basic Extraction:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-15-1) crwl https://example.com \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-15-2)     -B browser.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-15-3)     -C crawler.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-15-4)     -o json`
    
2.  Structured Data Extraction:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-16-1) crwl https://example.com \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-16-2)     -e extract_css.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-16-3)     -s css_schema.json \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-16-4)     -o json \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-16-5)     -v`
    
3.  LLM Extraction with Filtering:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-17-1) crwl https://example.com \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-17-2)     -B browser.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-17-3)     -e extract_llm.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-17-4)     -s llm_schema.json \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-17-5)     -f filter_bm25.yml \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-17-6)     -o json`
    
4.  Interactive Q&A:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-18-1) # First crawl and view [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-18-2) crwl https://example.com -o markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-18-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-18-4) # Then ask questions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-18-5) crwl https://example.com -q "What are the main points?" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/cli/#__codelineno-18-6) crwl https://example.com -q "Summarize the conclusions"`
    

Best Practices & Tips
---------------------

1.  **Configuration Management**:
2.  Keep common configurations in YAML files
3.  Use CLI parameters for quick overrides
4.  Store sensitive data (API tokens) in `~/.crawl4ai/global.yml`
    
5.  **Performance Optimization**:
    
6.  Use `--bypass-cache` for fresh content
7.  Enable `scan_full_page` for infinite scroll pages
8.  Adjust `delay_before_return_html` for dynamic content
    
9.  **Content Extraction**:
    
10.  Use CSS extraction for structured content
11.  Use LLM extraction for unstructured content
12.  Combine with filters for focused results
    
13.  **Q&A Workflow**:
    
14.  View content first with `-o markdown`
15.  Ask specific questions
16.  Use broader context with appropriate selectors

Recap
-----

The Crawl4AI CLI provides: - Flexible configuration via files and parameters - Multiple extraction strategies (CSS, XPath, LLM) - Content filtering and optimization - Interactive Q&A capabilities - Various output formats

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# 🚀 Crawl4AI v0.7.5: The Docker Hooks & Security Update - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

🚀 Crawl4AI v0.7.5: The Docker Hooks & Security Update
======================================================

_September 29, 2025 • 8 min read_

* * *

Today I'm releasing Crawl4AI v0.7.5—focused on extensibility and security. This update introduces the Docker Hooks System for pipeline customization, enhanced LLM integration, and important security improvements.

🎯 What's New at a Glance
-------------------------

*   **Docker Hooks System**: Custom Python functions at key pipeline points with function-based API
*   **Function-Based Hooks**: New `hooks_to_string()` utility with Docker client auto-conversion
*   **Enhanced LLM Integration**: Custom providers with temperature control
*   **HTTPS Preservation**: Secure internal link handling
*   **Bug Fixes**: Resolved multiple community-reported issues
*   **Improved Docker Error Handling**: Better debugging and reliability

🔧 Docker Hooks System: Pipeline Customization
----------------------------------------------

Every scraping project needs custom logic—authentication, performance optimization, content processing. Traditional solutions require forking or complex workarounds. Docker Hooks let you inject custom Python functions at 8 key points in the crawling pipeline.

### Real Example: Authentication & Performance

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-1) import requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-3) # Real working hooks for httpbin.org [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-4) hooks_config = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-5)     "on_page_context_created": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-6) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-7)     print("Hook: Setting up page context") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-8)     # Block images to speed up crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-9)     await context.route("**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,webp}", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-10)     print("Hook: Images blocked") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-11)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-12) """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-14)     "before_retrieve_html": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-15) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-16)     print("Hook: Before retrieving HTML") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-17)     # Scroll to bottom to load lazy content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-18)     await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-19)     await page.wait_for_timeout(1000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-20)     print("Hook: Scrolled to bottom") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-21)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-22) """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-24)     "before_goto": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-25) async def hook(page, context, url, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-26)     print(f"Hook: About to navigate to {url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-27)     # Add custom headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-28)     await page.set_extra_http_headers({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-29)         'X-Test-Header': 'crawl4ai-hooks-test' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-30)     }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-31)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-32) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-33) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-35) # Test with Docker API [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-36) payload = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-37)     "urls": ["https://httpbin.org/html"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-38)     "hooks": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-39)         "code": hooks_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-40)         "timeout": 30 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-41)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-42) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-43) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-44) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json=payload) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-45) result = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-46) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-47) if result.get('success'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-48)     print("✅ Hooks executed successfully!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-0-49)     print(f"Content length: {len(result.get('markdown', ''))} characters")`

**Available Hook Points:** - `on_browser_created`: Browser setup - `on_page_context_created`: Page context configuration - `before_goto`: Pre-navigation setup - `after_goto`: Post-navigation processing - `on_user_agent_updated`: User agent changes - `on_execution_started`: Crawl initialization - `before_retrieve_html`: Pre-extraction processing - `before_return_html`: Final HTML processing

### Function-Based Hooks API

Writing hooks as strings works, but lacks IDE support and type checking. v0.7.5 introduces a function-based approach with automatic conversion!

**Option 1: Using the `hooks_to_string()` Utility**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import hooks_to_string [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-2) import requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-4) # Define hooks as regular Python functions (with full IDE support!) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-5) async def on_page_context_created(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-6)     """Block images to speed up crawling""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-7)     await context.route("**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,webp}", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-8)     await page.set_viewport_size({"width": 1920, "height": 1080}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-9)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-11) async def before_goto(page, context, url, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-12)     """Add custom headers""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-13)     await page.set_extra_http_headers({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-14)         'X-Crawl4AI': 'v0.7.5', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-15)         'X-Custom-Header': 'my-value' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-16)     }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-17)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-19) # Convert functions to strings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-20) hooks_code = hooks_to_string({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-21)     "on_page_context_created": on_page_context_created, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-22)     "before_goto": before_goto [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-23) }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-25) # Use with REST API [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-26) payload = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-27)     "urls": ["https://httpbin.org/html"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-28)     "hooks": {"code": hooks_code, "timeout": 30} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-29) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-1-30) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json=payload)`

**Option 2: Docker Client with Automatic Conversion (Recommended!)**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai.docker_client import Crawl4aiDockerClient [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-3) # Define hooks as functions (same as above) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-4) async def on_page_context_created(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-5)     await context.route("**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,webp}", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-6)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-8) async def before_retrieve_html(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-9)     # Scroll to load lazy content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-10)     await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-11)     await page.wait_for_timeout(1000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-12)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-14) # Use Docker client - conversion happens automatically! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-15) client = Crawl4aiDockerClient(base_url="http://localhost:11235") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-17) results = await client.crawl( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-18)     urls=["https://httpbin.org/html"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-19)     hooks={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-20)         "on_page_context_created": on_page_context_created, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-21)         "before_retrieve_html": before_retrieve_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-22)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-23)     hooks_timeout=30 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-24) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-26) if results and results.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-2-27)     print(f"✅ Hooks executed! HTML length: {len(results.html)}")`

**Benefits of Function-Based Hooks:** - ✅ Full IDE support (autocomplete, syntax highlighting) - ✅ Type checking and linting - ✅ Easier to test and debug - ✅ Reusable across projects - ✅ Automatic conversion in Docker client - ✅ No breaking changes - string hooks still work!

🤖 Enhanced LLM Integration
---------------------------

Enhanced LLM integration with custom providers, temperature control, and base URL configuration.

### Multi-Provider Support

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai.extraction_strategy import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-4) # Test with different providers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-5) async def test_llm_providers(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-6)     # OpenAI with custom temperature [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-7)     openai_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-8)         provider="gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-9)         api_token="your-api-token", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-10)         temperature=0.7,  # New in v0.7.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-11)         instruction="Summarize this page in one sentence" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-14)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-15)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-16)             "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-17)             config=CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=openai_strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-18)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-20)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-21)             print("✅ LLM extraction completed") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-22)             print(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-24) # Docker API with enhanced LLM config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-25) llm_payload = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-26)     "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-27)     "f": "llm", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-28)     "q": "Summarize this page in one sentence.", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-29)     "provider": "gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-30)     "temperature": 0.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-31) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-3-33) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/md", json=llm_payload)`

**New Features:** - Custom `temperature` parameter for creativity control - `base_url` for custom API endpoints - Multi-provider environment variable support - Docker API integration

🔒 HTTPS Preservation
---------------------

**The Problem:** Modern web apps require HTTPS everywhere. When crawlers downgrade internal links from HTTPS to HTTP, authentication breaks and security warnings appear.

**Solution:** HTTPS preservation maintains secure protocols throughout crawling.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, FilterChain, URLPatternFilter, BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-3) async def test_https_preservation(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-4)     # Enable HTTPS preservation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-5)     url_filter = URLPatternFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-6)         patterns=["^(https:\/\/)?quotes\.toscrape\.com(\/.*)?$"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-7)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-9)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-10)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-11)         preserve_https_for_internal_links=True,  # New in v0.7.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-12)         deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-13)             max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-14)             max_pages=5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-15)             filter_chain=FilterChain([url_filter]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-16)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-17)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-19)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-20)         async for result in await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-21)             url="https://quotes.toscrape.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-22)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-23)         ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-24)             # All internal links maintain HTTPS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-25)             internal_links = [link['href'] for link in result.links['internal']] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-26)             https_links = [link for link in internal_links if link.startswith('https://')] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-28)             print(f"HTTPS links preserved: {len(https_links)}/{len(internal_links)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-29)             for link in https_links[:3]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-4-30)                 print(f"  → {link}")`

🛠️ Bug Fixes and Improvements
------------------------------

### Major Fixes

*   **URL Processing**: Fixed '+' sign preservation in query parameters (#1332)
*   **Proxy Configuration**: Enhanced proxy string parsing (old `proxy` parameter deprecated)
*   **Docker Error Handling**: Comprehensive error messages with status codes
*   **Memory Management**: Fixed leaks in long-running sessions
*   **JWT Authentication**: Fixed Docker JWT validation issues (#1442)
*   **Playwright Stealth**: Fixed stealth features for Playwright integration (#1481)
*   **API Configuration**: Fixed config handling to prevent overriding user-provided settings (#1505)
*   **Docker Filter Serialization**: Resolved JSON encoding errors in deep crawl strategy (#1419)
*   **LLM Provider Support**: Fixed custom LLM provider integration for adaptive crawler (#1291)
*   **Performance Issues**: Resolved backoff strategy failures and timeout handling (#989)

### Community-Reported Issues Fixed

This release addresses multiple issues reported by the community through GitHub issues and Discord discussions: - Fixed browser configuration reference errors - Resolved dependency conflicts with cssselect - Improved error messaging for failed authentications - Enhanced compatibility with various proxy configurations - Fixed edge cases in URL normalization

### Configuration Updates

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-1) # Old proxy config (deprecated) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-2) # browser_config = BrowserConfig(proxy="http://proxy:8080") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-4) # New enhanced proxy config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-5) browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-6)     proxy_config={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-7)         "server": "http://proxy:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-8)         "username": "optional-user", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-9)         "password": "optional-pass" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-10)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-5-11) )`

🔄 Breaking Changes
-------------------

1.  **Python 3.10+ Required**: Upgrade from Python 3.9
2.  **Proxy Parameter Deprecated**: Use new `proxy_config` structure
3.  **New Dependency**: Added `cssselect` for better CSS handling

🚀 Get Started
--------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-6-1) # Install latest version [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-6-2) pip install crawl4ai==0.7.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-6-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-6-4) # Docker deployment [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-6-5) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:latest [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-6-6) docker run -p 11235:11235 unclecode/crawl4ai:latest`

**Try the Demo:**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-7-1) # Run working examples [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.5/#__codelineno-7-2) python docs/releases_review/demo_v0.7.5.py`

**Resources:** - 📖 Documentation: [docs.crawl4ai.com](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/)
 - 🐙 GitHub: [github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai)
 - 💬 Discord: [discord.gg/crawl4ai](https://discord.gg/jP8KfhDhyN)
 - 🐦 Twitter: [@unclecode](https://x.com/unclecode)
