# Simple Crawling - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Simple Crawling
===============

This guide covers the basics of web crawling with Crawl4AI. You'll learn how to set up a crawler, make your first request, and understand the response.

Basic Usage
-----------

Set up a simple crawl using `BrowserConfig` and `CrawlerRunConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-6)     browser_config = BrowserConfig()  # Default browser configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-7)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig()   # Default crawl run configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-11)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-12)             config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-14)         print(result.markdown)  # Print clean markdown content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-16) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-0-17)     asyncio.run(main())`

Understanding the Response
--------------------------

The `arun()` method returns a `CrawlResult` object with several useful properties. Here's a quick overview (see [CrawlResult](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result/)
 for complete details):

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-2)     markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-3)         content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.6), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-4)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-5)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-8) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-9)     url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-10)     config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-13) # Different content formats [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-14) print(result.html)         # Raw HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-15) print(result.cleaned_html) # Cleaned HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-16) print(result.markdown.raw_markdown) # Raw markdown from cleaned html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-17) print(result.markdown.fit_markdown) # Most relevant content in markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-19) # Check success status [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-20) print(result.success)      # True if crawl succeeded [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-21) print(result.status_code)  # HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-23) # Access extracted media and links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-24) print(result.media)        # Dictionary of found media (images, videos, audio) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-1-25) print(result.links)        # Dictionary of internal and external links`

Adding Basic Options
--------------------

Customize your crawl using `CrawlerRunConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-2)     word_count_threshold=10,        # Minimum words per content block [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-3)     exclude_external_links=True,    # Remove external links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-4)     remove_overlay_elements=True,   # Remove popups/modals [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-5)     process_iframes=True           # Process iframe content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-8) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-9)     url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-10)     config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-2-11) )`

Handling Errors
---------------

Always check if the crawl was successful:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-3-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-3-2) result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-3-4) if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-3-5)     print(f"Crawl failed: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-3-6)     print(f"Status code: {result.status_code}")`

Logging and Debugging
---------------------

Enable verbose logging in `BrowserConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-4-1) browser_config = BrowserConfig(verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-4-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-4-4)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-4-5)     result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=run_config)`

Complete Example
----------------

Here's a more comprehensive example demonstrating common usage patterns:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-3) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-6)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-7)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-8)         # Content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-9)         word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-10)         excluded_tags=['form', 'header'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-11)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-13)         # Content processing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-14)         process_iframes=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-15)         remove_overlay_elements=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-17)         # Cache control [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-18)         cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED  # Use cache if available [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-19)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-21)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-22)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-23)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-24)             config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-25)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-27)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-28)             # Print clean content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-29)             print("Content:", result.markdown[:500])  # First 500 chars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-31)             # Process images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-32)             for image in result.media["images"]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-33)                 print(f"Found image: {image['src']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-35)             # Process links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-36)             for link in result.links["internal"]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-37)                 print(f"Internal link: {link['href']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-39)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-40)             print(f"Crawl failed: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-42) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/#__codelineno-5-43)     asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Crawler Result - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl Result and Output
=======================

When you call `arun()` on a page, Crawl4AI returns a **`CrawlResult`** object containing everything you might need—raw HTML, a cleaned version, optional screenshots or PDFs, structured extraction results, and more. This document explains those fields and how they map to different output types.

* * *

1\. The `CrawlResult` Model
---------------------------

Below is the core schema. Each field captures a different aspect of the crawl’s result:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-1) class MarkdownGenerationResult(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-2)     raw_markdown: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-3)     markdown_with_citations: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-4)     references_markdown: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-5)     fit_markdown: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-6)     fit_html: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-8) class CrawlResult(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-9)     url: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-10)     html: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-11)     fit_html: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-12)     success: bool [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-13)     cleaned_html: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-14)     media: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-15)     links: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-16)     downloaded_files: Optional[List[str]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-17)     js_execution_result: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-18)     screenshot: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-19)     pdf: Optional[bytes] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-20)     mhtml: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-21)     markdown: Optional[Union[str, MarkdownGenerationResult]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-22)     extracted_content: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-23)     metadata: Optional[dict] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-24)     error_message: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-25)     session_id: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-26)     response_headers: Optional[dict] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-27)     status_code: Optional[int] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-28)     ssl_certificate: Optional[SSLCertificate] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-29)     dispatch_result: Optional[DispatchResult] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-30)     redirected_url: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-31)     redirected_status_code: Optional[int] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-32)     network_requests: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-33)     console_messages: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-34)     tables: List[Dict] = Field(default_factory=list) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-36)     class Config: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-0-37)         arbitrary_types_allowed = True`

### Table: Key Fields in `CrawlResult`

| Field (Name & Type) | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **url (`str`)** | The final or actual URL crawled (in case of redirects). |
| **html (`str`)** | Original, unmodified page HTML. Good for debugging or custom processing. |
| **fit\_html (`Optional[str]`)** | Preprocessed HTML optimized for extraction and content filtering. |
| **success (`bool`)** | `True` if the crawl completed without major errors, else `False`. |
| **cleaned\_html (`Optional[str]`)** | Sanitized HTML with scripts/styles removed; can exclude tags if configured via `excluded_tags` etc. |
| **media (`Dict[str, List[Dict]]`)** | Extracted media info (images, audio, etc.), each with attributes like `src`, `alt`, `score`, etc. |
| **links (`Dict[str, List[Dict]]`)** | Extracted link data, split by `internal` and `external`. Each link usually has `href`, `text`, etc. |
| **downloaded\_files (`Optional[List[str]]`)** | If `accept_downloads=True` in `BrowserConfig`, this lists the filepaths of saved downloads. |
| **js\_execution\_result (`Optional[Dict[str, Any]]`)** | Results from JavaScript execution during crawling. |
| **screenshot (`Optional[str]`)** | Screenshot of the page (base64-encoded) if `screenshot=True`. |
| **pdf (`Optional[bytes]`)** | PDF of the page if `pdf=True`. |
| **mhtml (`Optional[str]`)** | MHTML snapshot of the page if `capture_mhtml=True`. Contains the full page with all resources. |
| **markdown (`Optional[str or MarkdownGenerationResult]`)** | It holds a `MarkdownGenerationResult`. Over time, this will be consolidated into `markdown`. The generator can provide raw markdown, citations, references, and optionally `fit_markdown`. |
| **extracted\_content (`Optional[str]`)** | The output of a structured extraction (CSS/LLM-based) stored as JSON string or other text. |
| **metadata (`Optional[dict]`)** | Additional info about the crawl or extracted data. |
| **error\_message (`Optional[str]`)** | If `success=False`, contains a short description of what went wrong. |
| **session\_id (`Optional[str]`)** | The ID of the session used for multi-page or persistent crawling. |
| **response\_headers (`Optional[dict]`)** | HTTP response headers, if captured. |
| **status\_code (`Optional[int]`)** | HTTP status code (e.g., 200 for OK). |
| **ssl\_certificate (`Optional[SSLCertificate]`)** | SSL certificate info if `fetch_ssl_certificate=True`. |
| **dispatch\_result (`Optional[DispatchResult]`)** | Additional concurrency and resource usage information when crawling URLs in parallel. |
| **redirected\_url (`Optional[str]`)** | The URL after any redirects (different from `url` which is the final URL). |
| **redirected\_status\_code (`Optional[int]`)** | HTTP status code of the final redirect destination (e.g., 200). `None` for non-HTTP requests (raw HTML, local files). |
| **network\_requests (`Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]]`)** | List of network requests, responses, and failures captured during the crawl if `capture_network_requests=True`. |
| **console\_messages (`Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]]`)** | List of browser console messages captured during the crawl if `capture_console_messages=True`. |
| **tables (`List[Dict]`)** | Table data extracted from HTML tables with structure `[{headers, rows, caption, summary}]`. |

* * *

2\. HTML Variants
-----------------

### `html`: Raw HTML

Crawl4AI preserves the exact HTML as `result.html`. Useful for:

*   Debugging page issues or checking the original content.
*   Performing your own specialized parse if needed.

### `cleaned_html`: Sanitized

If you specify any cleanup or exclusion parameters in `CrawlerRunConfig` (like `excluded_tags`, `remove_forms`, etc.), you’ll see the result here:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-1-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-1-2)     excluded_tags=["form", "header", "footer"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-1-3)     keep_data_attributes=False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-1-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-1-5) result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-1-6) print(result.cleaned_html)  # Freed of forms, header, footer, data-* attributes`

* * *

3\. Markdown Generation
-----------------------

### 3.1 `markdown`

*   **`markdown`**: The current location for detailed markdown output, returning a **`MarkdownGenerationResult`** object.
*   **`markdown_v2`**: Deprecated since v0.5.

**`MarkdownGenerationResult`** Fields:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **raw\_markdown** | The basic HTML→Markdown conversion. |
| **markdown\_with\_citations** | Markdown including inline citations that reference links at the end. |
| **references\_markdown** | The references/citations themselves (if `citations=True`). |
| **fit\_markdown** | The filtered/“fit” markdown if a content filter was used. |
| **fit\_html** | The filtered HTML that generated `fit_markdown`. |

### 3.2 Basic Example with a Markdown Generator

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-4) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-5)     markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-6)         options={"citations": True, "body_width": 80}  # e.g. pass html2text style options [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-7)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-9) result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-11) md_res = result.markdown  # or eventually 'result.markdown' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-12) print(md_res.raw_markdown[:500]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-13) print(md_res.markdown_with_citations) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-2-14) print(md_res.references_markdown)`

**Note**: If you use a filter like `PruningContentFilter`, you’ll get `fit_markdown` and `fit_html` as well.

* * *

4\. Structured Extraction: `extracted_content`
----------------------------------------------

If you run a JSON-based extraction strategy (CSS, XPath, LLM, etc.), the structured data is **not** stored in `markdown`—it’s placed in **`result.extracted_content`** as a JSON string (or sometimes plain text).

### Example: CSS Extraction with `raw://` HTML

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-8)         "name": "Example Items", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-9)         "baseSelector": "div.item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-11)             {"name": "title", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-12)             {"name": "link", "selector": "a", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-13)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-14)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-15)     raw_html = "<div class='item'><h2>Item 1</h2><a href='https://example.com/item1'>Link 1</a></div>" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-18)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-19)             url="raw://" + raw_html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-20)             config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-21)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-22)                 extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-23)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-24)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-25)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-26)         print(data) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-28) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-3-29)     asyncio.run(main())`

Here: - `url="raw://..."` passes the HTML content directly, no network requests.  
\- The **CSS** extraction strategy populates `result.extracted_content` with the JSON array `[{"title": "...", "link": "..."}]`.

* * *

5\. More Fields: Links, Media, Tables and More
----------------------------------------------

### 5.1 `links`

A dictionary, typically with `"internal"` and `"external"` lists. Each entry might have `href`, `text`, `title`, etc. This is automatically captured if you haven’t disabled link extraction.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-4-1) print(result.links["internal"][:3])  # Show first 3 internal links`

### 5.2 `media`

Similarly, a dictionary with `"images"`, `"audio"`, `"video"`, etc. Each item could include `src`, `alt`, `score`, and more, if your crawler is set to gather them.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-5-1) images = result.media.get("images", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-5-2) for img in images: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-5-3)     print("Image URL:", img["src"], "Alt:", img.get("alt"))`

### 5.3 `tables`

The `tables` field contains structured data extracted from HTML tables found on the crawled page. Tables are analyzed based on various criteria to determine if they are actual data tables (as opposed to layout tables), including:

*   Presence of thead and tbody sections
*   Use of th elements for headers
*   Column consistency
*   Text density
*   And other factors

Tables that score above the threshold (default: 7) are extracted and stored in result.tables.

### Accessing Table data:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-6)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-7)             url="https://www.w3schools.com/html/html_tables.asp", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-8)             config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-9)                 table_score_threshold=7  # Minimum score for table detection [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-10)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-11)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-13)         if result.success and result.tables: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-14)             print(f"Found {len(result.tables)} tables") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-16)             for i, table in enumerate(result.tables): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-17)                 print(f"\nTable {i+1}:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-18)                 print(f"Caption: {table.get('caption', 'No caption')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-19)                 print(f"Headers: {table['headers']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-20)                 print(f"Rows: {len(table['rows'])}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-22)                 # Print first few rows as example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-23)                 for j, row in enumerate(table['rows'][:3]): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-24)                     print(f"  Row {j+1}: {row}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-26) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-6-27)     asyncio.run(main())`

### Configuring Table Extraction:

You can adjust the sensitivity of the table detection algorithm with:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-7-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-7-2)     table_score_threshold=5  # Lower value = more tables detected (default: 7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-7-3) )`

Each extracted table contains:

*   `headers`: Column header names
*   `rows`: List of rows, each containing cell values
*   `caption`: Table caption text (if available)
*   `summary`: Table summary attribute (if specified)

### Table Extraction Tips

*   Not all HTML tables are extracted - only those detected as "data tables" vs. layout tables.
*   Tables with inconsistent cell counts, nested tables, or those used purely for layout may be skipped.
*   If you're missing tables, try adjusting the `table_score_threshold` to a lower value (default is 7).

The table detection algorithm scores tables based on features like consistent columns, presence of headers, text density, and more. Tables scoring above the threshold are considered data tables worth extracting.

### 5.4 `screenshot`, `pdf`, and `mhtml`

If you set `screenshot=True`, `pdf=True`, or `capture_mhtml=True` in **`CrawlerRunConfig`**, then:

*   `result.screenshot` contains a base64-encoded PNG string.
*   `result.pdf` contains raw PDF bytes (you can write them to a file).
*   `result.mhtml` contains the MHTML snapshot of the page as a string (you can write it to a .mhtml file).

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-1) # Save the PDF [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-2) with open("page.pdf", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-3)     f.write(result.pdf) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-5) # Save the MHTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-6) if result.mhtml: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-7)     with open("page.mhtml", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-8-8)         f.write(result.mhtml)`

The MHTML (MIME HTML) format is particularly useful as it captures the entire web page including all of its resources (CSS, images, scripts, etc.) in a single file, making it perfect for archiving or offline viewing.

### 5.5 `ssl_certificate`

If `fetch_ssl_certificate=True`, `result.ssl_certificate` holds details about the site’s SSL cert, such as issuer, validity dates, etc.

* * *

6\. Accessing These Fields
--------------------------

After you run:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-9-1) result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=some_config)`

Check any field:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-1) if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-2)     print(result.status_code, result.response_headers) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-3)     print("Links found:", len(result.links.get("internal", []))) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-4)     if result.markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-5)         print("Markdown snippet:", result.markdown.raw_markdown[:200]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-6)     if result.extracted_content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-7)         print("Structured JSON:", result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-8) else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/crawler-result/#__codelineno-10-9)     print("Error:", result.error_message)`

**Deprecation**: Since v0.5 `result.markdown_v2`, `result.fit_html`,`result.fit_markdown` are deprecated. Use `result.markdown` instead! It holds `MarkdownGenerationResult`, which includes `fit_html` and `fit_markdown` as it's properties.

* * *

7\. Next Steps
--------------

*   **Markdown Generation**: Dive deeper into how to configure `DefaultMarkdownGenerator` and various filters.
*   **Content Filtering**: Learn how to use `BM25ContentFilter` and `PruningContentFilter`.
*   **Session & Hooks**: If you want to manipulate the page or preserve state across multiple `arun()` calls, see the hooking or session docs.
*   **LLM Extraction**: For complex or unstructured content requiring AI-driven parsing, check the LLM-based strategies doc.

**Enjoy** exploring all that `CrawlResult` offers—whether you need raw HTML, sanitized output, markdown, or fully structured data, Crawl4AI has you covered!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Fit Markdown - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Fit Markdown with Pruning & BM25
================================

**Fit Markdown** is a specialized **filtered** version of your page’s markdown, focusing on the most relevant content. By default, Crawl4AI converts the entire HTML into a broad **raw\_markdown**. With fit markdown, we apply a **content filter** algorithm (e.g., **Pruning** or **BM25**) to remove or rank low-value sections—such as repetitive sidebars, shallow text blocks, or irrelevancies—leaving a concise textual “core.”

* * *

1\. How “Fit Markdown” Works
----------------------------

### 1.1 The `content_filter`

In **`CrawlerRunConfig`**, you can specify a **`content_filter`** to shape how content is pruned or ranked before final markdown generation. A filter’s logic is applied **before** or **during** the HTML→Markdown process, producing:

*   **`result.markdown.raw_markdown`** (unfiltered)
*   **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`** (filtered or “fit” version)
*   **`result.markdown.fit_html`** (the corresponding HTML snippet that produced `fit_markdown`)

### 1.2 Common Filters

1. **PruningContentFilter** – Scores each node by text density, link density, and tag importance, discarding those below a threshold.  
2. **BM25ContentFilter** – Focuses on textual relevance using BM25 ranking, especially useful if you have a specific user query (e.g., “machine learning” or “food nutrition”).

* * *

2\. PruningContentFilter
------------------------

**Pruning** discards less relevant nodes based on **text density, link density, and tag importance**. It’s a heuristic-based approach—if certain sections appear too “thin” or too “spammy,” they’re pruned.

### 2.1 Usage Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-4) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-7)     # Step 1: Create a pruning filter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-8)     prune_filter = PruningContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-9)         # Lower → more content retained, higher → more content pruned [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-10)         threshold=0.45,            [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-11)         # "fixed" or "dynamic" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-12)         threshold_type="dynamic",   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-13)         # Ignore nodes with <5 words [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-14)         min_word_threshold=5       [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-17)     # Step 2: Insert it into a Markdown Generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-18)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=prune_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-20)     # Step 3: Pass it to CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-21)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-22)         markdown_generator=md_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-23)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-25)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-26)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-27)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-28)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-29)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-31)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-32)             # 'fit_markdown' is your pruned content, focusing on "denser" text [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-33)             print("Raw Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-34)             print("Fit Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.fit_markdown)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-35)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-36)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-38) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-0-39)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 2.2 Key Parameters

*   **`min_word_threshold`** (int): If a block has fewer words than this, it’s pruned.
*   **`threshold_type`** (str):
*   `"fixed"` → each node must exceed `threshold` (0–1).
*   `"dynamic"` → node scoring adjusts according to tag type, text/link density, etc.
*   **`threshold`** (float, default ~0.48): The base or “anchor” cutoff.

**Algorithmic Factors**:

*   **Text density** – Encourages blocks that have a higher ratio of text to overall content.
*   **Link density** – Penalizes sections that are mostly links.
*   **Tag importance** – e.g., an `<article>` or `<p>` might be more important than a `<div>`.
*   **Structural context** – If a node is deeply nested or in a suspected sidebar, it might be deprioritized.

* * *

3\. BM25ContentFilter
---------------------

**BM25** is a classical text ranking algorithm often used in search engines. If you have a **user query** or rely on page metadata to derive a query, BM25 can identify which text chunks best match that query.

### 3.1 Usage Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-3) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import BM25ContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-4) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-7)     # 1) A BM25 filter with a user query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-8)     bm25_filter = BM25ContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-9)         user_query="startup fundraising tips", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-10)         # Adjust for stricter or looser results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-11)         bm25_threshold=1.2   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-14)     # 2) Insert into a Markdown Generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-15)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=bm25_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-17)     # 3) Pass to crawler config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-18)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-19)         markdown_generator=md_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-20)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-22)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-23)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-24)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-25)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-26)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-27)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-28)             print("Fit Markdown (BM25 query-based):") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-29)             print(result.markdown.fit_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-30)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-31)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-33) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-1-34)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 3.2 Parameters

*   **`user_query`** (str, optional): E.g. `"machine learning"`. If blank, the filter tries to glean a query from page metadata.
*   **`bm25_threshold`** (float, default 1.0):
*   Higher → fewer chunks but more relevant.
*   Lower → more inclusive.

> In more advanced scenarios, you might see parameters like `language`, `case_sensitive`, or `priority_tags` to refine how text is tokenized or weighted.

* * *

4\. Accessing the “Fit” Output
------------------------------

After the crawl, your “fit” content is found in **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`**.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-2-1) fit_md = result.markdown.fit_markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-2-2) fit_html = result.markdown.fit_html`

If the content filter is **BM25**, you might see additional logic or references in `fit_markdown` that highlight relevant segments. If it’s **Pruning**, the text is typically well-cleaned but not necessarily matched to a query.

* * *

5\. Code Patterns Recap
-----------------------

### 5.1 Pruning

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-3-1) prune_filter = PruningContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-3-2)     threshold=0.5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-3-3)     threshold_type="fixed", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-3-4)     min_word_threshold=10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-3-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-3-6) md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=prune_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-3-7) config = CrawlerRunConfig(markdown_generator=md_generator)`

### 5.2 BM25

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-4-1) bm25_filter = BM25ContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-4-2)     user_query="health benefits fruit", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-4-3)     bm25_threshold=1.2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-4-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-4-5) md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=bm25_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-4-6) config = CrawlerRunConfig(markdown_generator=md_generator)`

* * *

6\. Combining with “word\_count\_threshold” & Exclusions
--------------------------------------------------------

Remember you can also specify:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-2)     word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-3)     excluded_tags=["nav", "footer", "header"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-4)     exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-5)     markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-6)         content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-7)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-5-8) )`

Thus, **multi-level** filtering occurs:

1.  The crawler’s `excluded_tags` are removed from the HTML first.
2.  The content filter (Pruning, BM25, or custom) prunes or ranks the remaining text blocks.
3.  The final “fit” content is generated in `result.markdown.fit_markdown`.

* * *

7\. Custom Filters
------------------

If you need a different approach (like a specialized ML model or site-specific heuristics), you can create a new class inheriting from `RelevantContentFilter` and implement `filter_content(html)`. Then inject it into your **markdown generator**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-6-1) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import RelevantContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-6-3) class MyCustomFilter(RelevantContentFilter): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-6-4)     def filter_content(self, html, min_word_threshold=None): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-6-5)         # parse HTML, implement custom logic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/fit-markdown/#__codelineno-6-6)         return [block for block in ... if ... some condition...]`

**Steps**:

1.  Subclass `RelevantContentFilter`.
2.  Implement `filter_content(...)`.
3.  Use it in your `DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=MyCustomFilter(...))`.

* * *

8\. Final Thoughts
------------------

**Fit Markdown** is a crucial feature for:

*   **Summaries**: Quickly get the important text from a cluttered page.
*   **Search**: Combine with **BM25** to produce content relevant to a query.
*   **AI Pipelines**: Filter out boilerplate so LLM-based extraction or summarization runs on denser text.

**Key Points**: - **PruningContentFilter**: Great if you just want the “meatiest” text without a user query.  
\- **BM25ContentFilter**: Perfect for query-based extraction or searching.  
\- Combine with **`excluded_tags`, `exclude_external_links`, `word_count_threshold`** to refine your final “fit” text.  
\- Fit markdown ends up in **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`**; eventually **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`** in future versions.

With these tools, you can **zero in** on the text that truly matters, ignoring spammy or boilerplate content, and produce a concise, relevant “fit markdown” for your AI or data pipelines. Happy pruning and searching!

*   Last Updated: 2025-01-01

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Code Examples - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Code Examples
=============

This page provides a comprehensive list of example scripts that demonstrate various features and capabilities of Crawl4AI. Each example is designed to showcase specific functionality, making it easier for you to understand how to implement these features in your own projects.

Getting Started Examples
------------------------

| Example | Description | Link |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Hello World | A simple introductory example demonstrating basic usage of AsyncWebCrawler with JavaScript execution and content filtering. | [View Code](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/docs/examples/hello_world.py) |
| Quickstart | A comprehensive collection of examples showcasing various features including basic crawling, content cleaning, link analysis, JavaScript execution, CSS selectors, media handling, custom hooks, proxy configuration, screenshots, and multiple extraction strategies. | [View Code](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/docs/examples/quickstart.py) |
| Quickstart Set 1 | Basic examples for getting started with Crawl4AI. | [View Code](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/docs/examples/quickstart_examples_set_1.py) |
| Quickstart Set 2 | More advanced examples for working with Crawl4AI. | [View Code](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/docs/examples/quickstart_examples_set_2.py) |

Running the Examples
--------------------

To run any of these examples, you'll need to have Crawl4AI installed:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/examples/#__codelineno-0-1) pip install crawl4ai`

Then, you can run an example script like this:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/examples/#__codelineno-1-1) python -m docs.examples.hello_world`

For examples that require additional dependencies or environment variables, refer to the comments at the top of each file.

Some examples may require: - API keys (for LLM-based examples) - Docker setup (for Docker-related examples) - Additional dependencies (specified in the example files)

Contributing New Examples
-------------------------

If you've created an interesting example that demonstrates a unique use case or feature of Crawl4AI, we encourage you to contribute it to our examples collection. Please see our [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/CONTRIBUTORS.md)
 for more information.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Chunking - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Chunking Strategies
===================

Chunking strategies are critical for dividing large texts into manageable parts, enabling effective content processing and extraction. These strategies are foundational in cosine similarity-based extraction techniques, which allow users to retrieve only the most relevant chunks of content for a given query. Additionally, they facilitate direct integration into RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems for structured and scalable workflows.

### Why Use Chunking?

1. **Cosine Similarity and Query Relevance**: Prepares chunks for semantic similarity analysis. 2. **RAG System Integration**: Seamlessly processes and stores chunks for retrieval. 3. **Structured Processing**: Allows for diverse segmentation methods, such as sentence-based, topic-based, or windowed approaches.

### Methods of Chunking

#### 1\. Regex-Based Chunking

Splits text based on regular expression patterns, useful for coarse segmentation.

**Code Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-1) class RegexChunking: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-2)     def __init__(self, patterns=None): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-3)         self.patterns = patterns or [r'\n\n']  # Default pattern for paragraphs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-5)     def chunk(self, text): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-6)         paragraphs = [text] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-7)         for pattern in self.patterns: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-8)             paragraphs = [seg for p in paragraphs for seg in re.split(pattern, p)] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-9)         return paragraphs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-11) # Example Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-12) text = """This is the first paragraph. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-14) This is the second paragraph.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-15) chunker = RegexChunking() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-0-16) print(chunker.chunk(text))`

#### 2\. Sentence-Based Chunking

Divides text into sentences using NLP tools, ideal for extracting meaningful statements.

**Code Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-1) from nltk.tokenize import sent_tokenize [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-3) class NlpSentenceChunking: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-4)     def chunk(self, text): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-5)         sentences = sent_tokenize(text) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-6)         return [sentence.strip() for sentence in sentences] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-8) # Example Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-9) text = "This is sentence one. This is sentence two." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-10) chunker = NlpSentenceChunking() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-1-11) print(chunker.chunk(text))`

#### 3\. Topic-Based Segmentation

Uses algorithms like TextTiling to create topic-coherent chunks.

**Code Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-1) from nltk.tokenize import TextTilingTokenizer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-3) class TopicSegmentationChunking: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-4)     def __init__(self): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-5)         self.tokenizer = TextTilingTokenizer() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-7)     def chunk(self, text): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-8)         return self.tokenizer.tokenize(text) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-10) # Example Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-11) text = """This is an introduction. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-12) This is a detailed discussion on the topic.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-13) chunker = TopicSegmentationChunking() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-2-14) print(chunker.chunk(text))`

#### 4\. Fixed-Length Word Chunking

Segments text into chunks of a fixed word count.

**Code Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-1) class FixedLengthWordChunking: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-2)     def __init__(self, chunk_size=100): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-3)         self.chunk_size = chunk_size [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-5)     def chunk(self, text): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-6)         words = text.split() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-7)         return [' '.join(words[i:i + self.chunk_size]) for i in range(0, len(words), self.chunk_size)] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-9) # Example Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-10) text = "This is a long text with many words to be chunked into fixed sizes." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-11) chunker = FixedLengthWordChunking(chunk_size=5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-3-12) print(chunker.chunk(text))`

#### 5\. Sliding Window Chunking

Generates overlapping chunks for better contextual coherence.

**Code Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-1) class SlidingWindowChunking: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-2)     def __init__(self, window_size=100, step=50): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-3)         self.window_size = window_size [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-4)         self.step = step [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-6)     def chunk(self, text): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-7)         words = text.split() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-8)         chunks = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-9)         for i in range(0, len(words) - self.window_size + 1, self.step): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-10)             chunks.append(' '.join(words[i:i + self.window_size])) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-11)         return chunks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-13) # Example Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-14) text = "This is a long text to demonstrate sliding window chunking." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-15) chunker = SlidingWindowChunking(window_size=5, step=2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-4-16) print(chunker.chunk(text))`

### Combining Chunking with Cosine Similarity

To enhance the relevance of extracted content, chunking strategies can be paired with cosine similarity techniques. Here’s an example workflow:

**Code Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-1) from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-2) from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-4) class CosineSimilarityExtractor: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-5)     def __init__(self, query): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-6)         self.query = query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-7)         self.vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-9)     def find_relevant_chunks(self, chunks): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-10)         vectors = self.vectorizer.fit_transform([self.query] + chunks) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-11)         similarities = cosine_similarity(vectors[0:1], vectors[1:]).flatten() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-12)         return [(chunks[i], similarities[i]) for i in range(len(chunks))] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-14) # Example Workflow [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-15) text = """This is a sample document. It has multiple sentences.  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-16) We are testing chunking and similarity.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-18) chunker = SlidingWindowChunking(window_size=5, step=3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-19) chunks = chunker.chunk(text) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-20) query = "testing chunking" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-21) extractor = CosineSimilarityExtractor(query) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-22) relevant_chunks = extractor.find_relevant_chunks(chunks) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/chunking/#__codelineno-5-24) print(relevant_chunks)`

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Local Files & Raw HTML - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Prefix-Based Input Handling in Crawl4AI
=======================================

This guide will walk you through using the Crawl4AI library to crawl web pages, local HTML files, and raw HTML strings. We'll demonstrate these capabilities using a Wikipedia page as an example.

Crawling a Web URL
------------------

To crawl a live web page, provide the URL starting with `http://` or `https://`, using a `CrawlerRunConfig` object:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CacheMode, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-4) async def crawl_web(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-6)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-7)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-8)             url="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/apple",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-9)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-10)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-11)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-12)             print("Markdown Content:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-13)             print(result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-14)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-15)             print(f"Failed to crawl: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-0-17) asyncio.run(crawl_web())`

Crawling a Local HTML File
--------------------------

To crawl a local HTML file, prefix the file path with `file://`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CacheMode, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-4) async def crawl_local_file(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-5)     local_file_path = "/path/to/apple.html"  # Replace with your file path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-6)     file_url = f"file://{local_file_path}" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-7)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-10)         result = await crawler.arun(url=file_url, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-11)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-12)             print("Markdown Content from Local File:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-13)             print(result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-14)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-15)             print(f"Failed to crawl local file: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-1-17) asyncio.run(crawl_local_file())`

Crawling Raw HTML Content
-------------------------

To crawl raw HTML content, prefix the HTML string with `raw:`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-3) from crawl4ai.async_configs import CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-5) async def crawl_raw_html(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-6)     raw_html = "<html><body><h1>Hello, World!</h1></body></html>" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-7)     raw_html_url = f"raw:{raw_html}" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-8)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(bypass_cache=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-11)         result = await crawler.arun(url=raw_html_url, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-12)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-13)             print("Markdown Content from Raw HTML:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-14)             print(result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-15)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-16)             print(f"Failed to crawl raw HTML: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-2-18) asyncio.run(crawl_raw_html())`

* * *

Complete Example
================

Below is a comprehensive script that:

1.  Crawls the Wikipedia page for "Apple."
2.  Saves the HTML content to a local file (`apple.html`).
3.  Crawls the local HTML file and verifies the markdown length matches the original crawl.
4.  Crawls the raw HTML content from the saved file and verifies consistency.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-1) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-2) import sys [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-4) from pathlib import Path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-5) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CacheMode, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-7) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-8)     wikipedia_url = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/apple" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-9)     script_dir = Path(__file__).parent [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-10)     html_file_path = script_dir / "apple.html" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-12)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-13)         # Step 1: Crawl the Web URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-14)         print("\n=== Step 1: Crawling the Wikipedia URL ===") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-15)         web_config = CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-16)         result = await crawler.arun(url=wikipedia_url, config=web_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-18)         if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-19)             print(f"Failed to crawl {wikipedia_url}: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-20)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-22)         with open(html_file_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-23)             f.write(result.html) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-24)         web_crawl_length = len(result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-25)         print(f"Length of markdown from web crawl: {web_crawl_length}\n") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-27)         # Step 2: Crawl from the Local HTML File [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-28)         print("=== Step 2: Crawling from the Local HTML File ===") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-29)         file_url = f"file://{html_file_path.resolve()}" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-30)         file_config = CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-31)         local_result = await crawler.arun(url=file_url, config=file_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-33)         if not local_result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-34)             print(f"Failed to crawl local file {file_url}: {local_result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-35)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-36) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-37)         local_crawl_length = len(local_result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-38)         assert web_crawl_length == local_crawl_length, "Markdown length mismatch" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-39)         print("✅ Markdown length matches between web and local file crawl.\n") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-41)         # Step 3: Crawl Using Raw HTML Content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-42)         print("=== Step 3: Crawling Using Raw HTML Content ===") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-43)         with open(html_file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-44)             raw_html_content = f.read() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-45)         raw_html_url = f"raw:{raw_html_content}" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-46)         raw_config = CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-47)         raw_result = await crawler.arun(url=raw_html_url, config=raw_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-49)         if not raw_result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-50)             print(f"Failed to crawl raw HTML content: {raw_result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-51)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-52) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-53)         raw_crawl_length = len(raw_result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-54)         assert web_crawl_length == raw_crawl_length, "Markdown length mismatch" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-55)         print("✅ Markdown length matches between web and raw HTML crawl.\n") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-56) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-57)         print("All tests passed successfully!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-58)     if html_file_path.exists(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-59)         os.remove(html_file_path) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-60) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-61) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/local-files/#__codelineno-3-62)     asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

Conclusion
==========

With the unified `url` parameter and prefix-based handling in **Crawl4AI**, you can seamlessly handle web URLs, local HTML files, and raw HTML content. Use `CrawlerRunConfig` for flexible and consistent configuration in all scenarios.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Browser, Crawler & LLM Config - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Browser, Crawler & LLM Configuration (Quick Overview)
=====================================================

Crawl4AI's flexibility stems from two key classes:

1.  **`BrowserConfig`** – Dictates **how** the browser is launched and behaves (e.g., headless or visible, proxy, user agent).
2.  **`CrawlerRunConfig`** – Dictates **how** each **crawl** operates (e.g., caching, extraction, timeouts, JavaScript code to run, etc.).
3.  **`LLMConfig`** - Dictates **how** LLM providers are configured. (model, api token, base url, temperature etc.)

In most examples, you create **one** `BrowserConfig` for the entire crawler session, then pass a **fresh** or re-used `CrawlerRunConfig` whenever you call `arun()`. This tutorial shows the most commonly used parameters. If you need advanced or rarely used fields, see the [Configuration Parameters](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
.

* * *

1\. BrowserConfig Essentials
----------------------------
