`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-1) class BrowserConfig: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-2)     def __init__( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-3)         browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-4)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-5)         browser_mode="dedicated", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-6)         use_managed_browser=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-7)         cdp_url=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-8)         debugging_port=9222, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-9)         host="localhost", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-10)         proxy_config=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-11)         viewport_width=1080, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-12)         viewport_height=600, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-13)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-14)         use_persistent_context=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-15)         user_data_dir=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-16)         cookies=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-17)         headers=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-18)         user_agent=( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-19)             # "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.15; rv:109.0) AppleWebKit/537.36 " [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-20)             # "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 " [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-21)             # "(KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/116.0.5845.187 Safari/604.1 Edg/117.0.2045.47" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-22)             "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/116.0.0.0 Safari/537.36" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-23)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-24)         user_agent_mode="", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-25)         text_mode=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-26)         light_mode=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-27)         extra_args=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-28)         enable_stealth=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-29)         # ... other advanced parameters omitted here [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-30)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-0-31)         ...`

### Key Fields to Note

1.⠀**`browser_type`**  
\- Options: `"chromium"`, `"firefox"`, or `"webkit"`.  
\- Defaults to `"chromium"`.  
\- If you need a different engine, specify it here.

2.⠀**`headless`**  
\- `True`: Runs the browser in headless mode (invisible browser).  
\- `False`: Runs the browser in visible mode, which helps with debugging.

3.⠀**`browser_mode`**  
\- Determines how the browser should be initialized: - `"dedicated"` (default): Creates a new browser instance each time - `"builtin"`: Uses the builtin CDP browser running in background - `"custom"`: Uses explicit CDP settings provided in `cdp_url` - `"docker"`: Runs browser in Docker container with isolation

4.⠀**`use_managed_browser`** & **`cdp_url`**  
\- `use_managed_browser=True`: Launch browser using Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) for advanced control - `cdp_url`: URL for CDP endpoint (e.g., `"ws://localhost:9222/devtools/browser/"`) - Automatically set based on `browser_mode`

5.⠀**`debugging_port`** & **`host`**  
\- `debugging_port`: Port for browser debugging protocol (default: 9222) - `host`: Host for browser connection (default: "localhost")

6.⠀**`proxy_config`**  
\- A `ProxyConfig` object or dictionary with fields like:  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-1-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-1-2)     "server": "http://proxy.example.com:8080",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-1-3)     "username": "...",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-1-4)     "password": "..." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-1-5) }`

\- Leave as `None` if a proxy is not required.

7.⠀**`viewport_width` & `viewport_height`** - The initial window size. - Some sites behave differently with smaller or bigger viewports.

8.⠀**`device_scale_factor`** - Controls the device pixel ratio (DPR) for rendering. Default is `1.0`. - Set to `2.0` for Retina-quality screenshots (e.g., a 1920×1080 viewport produces 3840×2160 images). - Higher values increase screenshot size and rendering time proportionally.

9.⠀**`verbose`**  
\- If `True`, prints extra logs.  
\- Handy for debugging.

9.⠀**`use_persistent_context`**  
\- If `True`, uses a **persistent** browser profile, storing cookies/local storage across runs.  
\- Typically also set `user_data_dir` to point to a folder.

10.⠀**`cookies`** & **`headers`**  
\- If you want to start with specific cookies or add universal HTTP headers to the browser context, set them here.  
\- E.g. `cookies=[{"name": "session", "value": "abc123", "domain": "example.com"}]`.

11.⠀**`user_agent`** & **`user_agent_mode`**  
\- `user_agent`: Custom User-Agent string. If `None`, a default is used.  
\- `user_agent_mode`: Set to `"random"` for randomization (helps fight bot detection).

12.⠀**`text_mode`** & **`light_mode`**  
\- `text_mode=True` disables images, possibly speeding up text-only crawls.  
\- `light_mode=True` turns off certain background features for performance.

13.⠀**`extra_args`**  
\- Additional flags for the underlying browser.  
\- E.g. `["--disable-extensions"]`.

14.⠀**`enable_stealth`**  
\- If `True`, enables stealth mode using playwright-stealth.  
\- Modifies browser fingerprints to avoid basic bot detection.  
\- Default is `False`. Recommended for sites with bot protection.

### Helper Methods

Both configuration classes provide a `clone()` method to create modified copies:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-1) # Create a base browser config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-2) base_browser = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-3)     browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-4)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-5)     text_mode=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-8) # Create a visible browser config for debugging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-9) debug_browser = base_browser.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-10)     headless=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-11)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-2-12) )`

### Class-Level Defaults

Both `BrowserConfig` and `CrawlerRunConfig` support **class-level default overrides** via `set_defaults()`. This is useful in server/cloud deployments where every config instance needs the same base settings — set them once at startup instead of repeating at every call site.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-3) # At application startup — one time [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-4) BrowserConfig.set_defaults( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-5)     cache_cdp_connection=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-6)     cdp_close_delay=0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-7)     create_isolated_context=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-9) CrawlerRunConfig.set_defaults(verbose=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-11) # Every new instance automatically inherits those defaults [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-12) cfg = BrowserConfig(cdp_url="ws://localhost:9222") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-13) # → cache_cdp_connection=True, cdp_close_delay=0, create_isolated_context=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-15) # Explicit values still win [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-16) cfg = BrowserConfig(cdp_url="ws://localhost:9222", cache_cdp_connection=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-3-17) # → cache_cdp_connection=False (explicit overrides the class default)`

**Available methods** (on both `BrowserConfig` and `CrawlerRunConfig`):

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `set_defaults(**kwargs)` | Set class-level defaults. Invalid parameter names raise `ValueError`. |
| `get_defaults()` | Return a copy of the current class-level defaults. |
| `reset_defaults()` | Clear all class-level defaults. |
| `reset_defaults("param1", "param2")` | Clear only the named defaults. |

> **Note:** Class defaults are independent per class — `BrowserConfig.set_defaults()` does not affect `CrawlerRunConfig`, and vice versa. Defaults are stored in memory and apply for the lifetime of the process.

**Minimal Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-3) browser_conf = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-4)     browser_type="firefox", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-5)     headless=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-6)     text_mode=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-9) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_conf) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-10)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-4-11)     print(result.markdown[:300])`

* * *

2\. CrawlerRunConfig Essentials
-------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-1) class CrawlerRunConfig: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-2)     def __init__( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-3)         word_count_threshold=200, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-4)         extraction_strategy=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-5)         chunking_strategy=RegexChunking(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-6)         markdown_generator=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-7)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-8)         js_code=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-9)         c4a_script=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-10)         wait_for=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-11)         screenshot=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-12)         pdf=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-13)         capture_mhtml=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-14)         # Location and Identity Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-15)         locale=None,            # e.g. "en-US", "fr-FR" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-16)         timezone_id=None,       # e.g. "America/New_York" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-17)         geolocation=None,       # GeolocationConfig object [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-18)         # Proxy Configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-19)         proxy_config=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-20)         proxy_rotation_strategy=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-21)         # Page Interaction Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-22)         scan_full_page=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-23)         scroll_delay=0.2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-24)         wait_until="domcontentloaded", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-25)         page_timeout=60000, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-26)         delay_before_return_html=0.1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-27)         # URL Matching Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-28)         url_matcher=None,       # For URL-specific configurations [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-29)         match_mode=MatchMode.OR, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-30)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-31)         stream=False,  # Enable streaming for arun_many() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-32)         # ... other advanced parameters omitted [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-33)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-5-34)         ...`

### Key Fields to Note

1.⠀**`word_count_threshold`**:  
\- The minimum word count before a block is considered.  
\- If your site has lots of short paragraphs or items, you can lower it.

2.⠀**`extraction_strategy`**:  
\- Where you plug in JSON-based extraction (CSS, LLM, etc.).  
\- If `None`, no structured extraction is done (only raw/cleaned HTML + markdown).

3.⠀**`chunking_strategy`**:  
\- Strategy to chunk content before extraction.  
\- Defaults to `RegexChunking()`. Can be customized for different chunking approaches.

4.⠀**`markdown_generator`**:  
\- E.g., `DefaultMarkdownGenerator(...)`, controlling how HTML→Markdown conversion is done.  
\- If `None`, a default approach is used.

5.⠀**`cache_mode`**:  
\- Controls caching behavior (`ENABLED`, `BYPASS`, `DISABLED`, etc.).  
\- Defaults to `CacheMode.BYPASS`.

6.⠀**`js_code`**, **`js_code_before_wait`**, & **`c4a_script`**: - `js_code`: JavaScript to run **after** `wait_for` completes — on the fully-loaded page. - `js_code_before_wait`: JavaScript to run **before** `wait_for` — for triggering loading that `wait_for` then checks. - `c4a_script`: C4A script that compiles to JavaScript. - Great for "Load More" buttons or user interactions.

7.⠀**`wait_for`**:  
\- A CSS or JS expression to wait for before extracting content.  
\- Common usage: `wait_for="css:.main-loaded"` or `wait_for="js:() => window.loaded === true"`.

8.⠀**`flatten_shadow_dom`**: - If `True`, flattens Shadow DOM content into the light DOM before HTML capture. - Essential for sites built with Web Components (Stencil, Lit, Shoelace, etc.). - Also force-opens closed shadow roots. See [Flattening Shadow DOM](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#31-flattening-shadow-dom)
.

9.⠀**`screenshot`**, **`pdf`**, & **`capture_mhtml`**: - If `True`, captures a screenshot, PDF, or MHTML snapshot after the page is fully loaded. - The results go to `result.screenshot` (base64), `result.pdf` (bytes), or `result.mhtml` (string). - Use `force_viewport_screenshot=True` to capture only the visible viewport instead of the full page. This is faster and produces smaller images when you don't need a full-page screenshot.

9.⠀**Location Parameters**:  
\- **`locale`**: Browser's locale (e.g., `"en-US"`, `"fr-FR"`) for language preferences - **`timezone_id`**: Browser's timezone (e.g., `"America/New_York"`, `"Europe/Paris"`) - **`geolocation`**: GPS coordinates via `GeolocationConfig(latitude=48.8566, longitude=2.3522)` - See [Identity Based Crawling](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/identity-based-crawling/#7-locale-timezone-and-geolocation-control)

10.⠀**Proxy Configuration**: - **`proxy_config`**: Single `ProxyConfig` or `list[ProxyConfig]` — proxies tried in order. Pass a list for automatic escalation. - **`proxy_rotation_strategy`**: Strategy for rotating proxies during crawls

11.⠀**Anti-Bot Retry & Fallback** (see [Anti-Bot & Fallback](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/anti-bot-and-fallback/)
): - **`max_retries`**: Number of retry rounds when blocking is detected (default: 0). Each round tries all proxies in `proxy_config`. - **`fallback_fetch_function`**: Async function called as last resort — takes URL, returns raw HTML

12.⠀**Page Interaction Parameters**: - **`scan_full_page`**: If `True`, scroll through the entire page to load all content - **`wait_until`**: Condition to wait for when navigating (e.g., "domcontentloaded", "networkidle") - **`page_timeout`**: Timeout in milliseconds for page operations (default: 60000) - **`delay_before_return_html`**: Delay in seconds before retrieving final HTML.

13.⠀**`url_matcher`** & **`match_mode`**:  
\- Enable URL-specific configurations when used with `arun_many()`. - Set `url_matcher` to patterns (glob, function, or list) to match specific URLs. - Use `match_mode` (OR/AND) to control how multiple patterns combine. - See [URL-Specific Configurations](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/arun_many/#url-specific-configurations)
 for examples.

13.⠀**`verbose`**:  
\- Logs additional runtime details.  
\- Overlaps with the browser's verbosity if also set to `True` in `BrowserConfig`.

14.⠀**`stream`**:  
\- If `True`, enables streaming mode for `arun_many()` to process URLs as they complete. - Allows handling results incrementally instead of waiting for all URLs to finish.

### Helper Methods

The `clone()` method is particularly useful for creating variations of your crawler configuration:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-1) # Create a base configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-2) base_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-3)     cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-4)     word_count_threshold=200, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-5)     wait_until="networkidle" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-8) # Create variations for different use cases [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-9) stream_config = base_config.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-10)     stream=True,  # Enable streaming mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-11)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-12) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-14) debug_config = base_config.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-15)     page_timeout=120000,  # Longer timeout for debugging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-16)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-6-17) )`

The `clone()` method: - Creates a new instance with all the same settings - Updates only the specified parameters - Leaves the original configuration unchanged - Perfect for creating variations without repeating all parameters

* * *

3\. LLMConfig Essentials
------------------------

### Key fields to note

1.⠀**`provider`**:  
\- Which LLM provider to use. - Possible values are `"ollama/llama3","groq/llama3-70b-8192","groq/llama3-8b-8192", "openai/gpt-4o-mini" ,"openai/gpt-4o","openai/o1-mini","openai/o1-preview","openai/o3-mini","openai/o3-mini-high","anthropic/claude-3-haiku-20240307","anthropic/claude-3-opus-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-sonnet-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620","gemini/gemini-pro","gemini/gemini-1.5-pro","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-exp","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-lite-preview-02-05","deepseek/deepseek-chat"`  
_(default: `"openai/gpt-4o-mini"`)_

2.⠀**`api_token`**:  
\- Optional. When not provided explicitly, api\_token will be read from environment variables based on provider. For example: If a gemini model is passed as provider then,`"GEMINI_API_KEY"` will be read from environment variables  
\- API token of LLM provider  
eg: `api_token = "gsk_1ClHGGJ7Lpn4WGybR7vNWGdyb3FY7zXEw3SCiy0BAVM9lL8CQv"` - Environment variable - use with prefix "env:"  
eg:`api_token = "env: GROQ_API_KEY"`

3.⠀**`base_url`**:  
\- If your provider has a custom endpoint

4.⠀**Retry/backoff controls** _(optional)_:  
\- `backoff_base_delay` _(default `2` seconds)_ – base delay inserted before the first retry when the provider returns a rate-limit response.  
\- `backoff_max_attempts` _(default `3`)_ – total number of attempts (initial call plus retries) before the request is surfaced as an error.  
\- `backoff_exponential_factor` _(default `2`)_ – growth rate for the retry delay (`delay = base_delay * factor^attempt`).  
\- These values are forwarded to the shared `perform_completion_with_backoff` helper, ensuring every strategy that consumes your `LLMConfig` honors the same throttling policy.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-7-1) llm_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-7-2)     provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-7-3)     api_token=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-7-4)     backoff_base_delay=1, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-7-5)     backoff_max_attempts=5, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-7-6)     backoff_exponential_factor=3, #optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-7-7) )`

4\. Putting It All Together
---------------------------

In a typical scenario, you define **one** `BrowserConfig` for your crawler session, then create **one or more** `CrawlerRunConfig` & `LLMConfig` depending on each call's needs:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode, LLMConfig, LLMContentFilter, DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-3) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-6)     # 1) Browser config: headless, bigger viewport, no proxy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-7)     browser_conf = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-8)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-9)         viewport_width=1280, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-10)         viewport_height=720 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-11)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-13)     # 2) Example extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-14)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-15)         "name": "Articles", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-16)         "baseSelector": "div.article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-17)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-18)             {"name": "title", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-19)             {"name": "link", "selector": "a", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-20)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-21)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-22)     extraction = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-24)     # 3) Example LLM content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-26)     gemini_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-27)         provider="gemini/gemini-1.5-pro",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-28)         api_token = "env:GEMINI_API_TOKEN" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-29)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-31)     # Initialize LLM filter with specific instruction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-32)     filter = LLMContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-33)         llm_config=gemini_config,  # or your preferred provider [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-34)         instruction=""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-35)         Focus on extracting the core educational content. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-36)         Include: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-37)         - Key concepts and explanations [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-38)         - Important code examples [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-39)         - Essential technical details [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-40)         Exclude: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-41)         - Navigation elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-42)         - Sidebars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-43)         - Footer content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-44)         Format the output as clean markdown with proper code blocks and headers. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-45)         """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-46)         chunk_token_threshold=500,  # Adjust based on your needs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-47)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-48)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-50)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-51)         content_filter=filter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-52)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-53)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-54) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-55)     # 4) Crawler run config: skip cache, use extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-56)     run_conf = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-57)         markdown_generator=md_generator, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-58)         extraction_strategy=extraction, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-59)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-60)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-61) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-62)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_conf) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-63)         # 4) Execute the crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-64)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com/news", config=run_conf) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-65) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-66)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-67)             print("Extracted content:", result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-68)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-69)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-70) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-71) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/browser-crawler-config/#__codelineno-8-72)     asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

5\. Next Steps
--------------

For a **detailed list** of available parameters (including advanced ones), see:

*   [BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig & LLMConfig Reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
    

You can explore topics like:

*   **Custom Hooks & Auth** (Inject JavaScript or handle login forms).
*   **Session Management** (Re-use pages, preserve state across multiple calls).
*   **Magic Mode** or **Identity-based Crawling** (Fight bot detection by simulating user behavior).
*   **Advanced Caching** (Fine-tune read/write cache modes).

* * *

6\. Conclusion
--------------

**BrowserConfig**, **CrawlerRunConfig** and **LLMConfig** give you straightforward ways to define:

*   **Which** browser to launch, how it should run, and any proxy or user agent needs.
*   **How** each crawl should behave—caching, timeouts, JavaScript code, extraction strategies, etc.
*   **Which** LLM provider to use, api token, temperature and base url for custom endpoints

Use them together for **clear, maintainable** code, and when you need more specialized behavior, check out the advanced parameters in the [reference docs](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
. Happy crawling!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Adaptive Crawling - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Adaptive Web Crawling
=====================

Introduction
------------

Traditional web crawlers follow predetermined patterns, crawling pages blindly without knowing when they've gathered enough information. **Adaptive Crawling** changes this paradigm by introducing intelligence into the crawling process.

Think of it like research: when you're looking for information, you don't read every book in the library. You stop when you've found sufficient information to answer your question. That's exactly what Adaptive Crawling does for web scraping.

Key Concepts
------------

### The Problem It Solves

When crawling websites for specific information, you face two challenges: 1. **Under-crawling**: Stopping too early and missing crucial information 2. **Over-crawling**: Wasting resources by crawling irrelevant pages

Adaptive Crawling solves both by using a three-layer scoring system that determines when you have "enough" information.

### How It Works

The AdaptiveCrawler uses three metrics to measure information sufficiency:

*   **Coverage**: How well your collected pages cover the query terms
*   **Consistency**: Whether the information is coherent across pages
*   **Saturation**: Detecting when new pages aren't adding new information

When these metrics indicate sufficient information has been gathered, crawling stops automatically.

Quick Start
-----------

### Basic Usage

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, AdaptiveCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-3) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-4)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-5)         # Create an adaptive crawler (config is optional) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-6)         adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-8)         # Start crawling with a query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-9)         result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-10)             start_url="https://docs.python.org/3/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-11)             query="async context managers" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-12)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-14)         # View statistics [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-15)         adaptive.print_stats() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-17)         # Get the most relevant content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-18)         relevant_pages = adaptive.get_relevant_content(top_k=5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-19)         for page in relevant_pages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-0-20)             print(f"- {page['url']} (score: {page['score']:.2f})")`

### Configuration Options

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import AdaptiveConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-3) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-4)     confidence_threshold=0.8,    # Stop when 80% confident (default: 0.7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-5)     max_pages=30,               # Maximum pages to crawl (default: 20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-6)     top_k_links=5,              # Links to follow per page (default: 3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-7)     min_gain_threshold=0.05     # Minimum expected gain to continue (default: 0.1) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-1-10) adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler, config)`

Crawling Strategies
-------------------

Adaptive Crawling supports two distinct strategies for determining information sufficiency:

### Statistical Strategy (Default)

The statistical strategy uses pure information theory and term-based analysis:

*   **Fast and efficient** - No API calls or model loading
*   **Term-based coverage** - Analyzes query term presence and distribution
*   **No external dependencies** - Works offline
*   **Best for**: Well-defined queries with specific terminology

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-2-1) # Default configuration uses statistical strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-2-2) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-2-3)     strategy="statistical",  # This is the default [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-2-4)     confidence_threshold=0.8 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-2-5) )`

### Embedding Strategy

The embedding strategy uses semantic embeddings for deeper understanding:

*   **Semantic understanding** - Captures meaning beyond exact term matches
*   **Query expansion** - Automatically generates query variations
*   **Gap-driven selection** - Identifies semantic gaps in knowledge
*   **Validation-based stopping** - Uses held-out queries to validate coverage
*   **Best for**: Complex queries, ambiguous topics, conceptual understanding

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-1) # Configure embedding strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-2) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-3)     strategy="embedding", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-4)     embedding_model="sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",  # Default [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-5)     n_query_variations=10,  # Generate 10 query variations [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-6)     embedding_min_confidence_threshold=0.1  # Stop if completely irrelevant [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-9) # With custom LLM provider for query expansion (recommended) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-10) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-12) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-13)     strategy="embedding", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-14)     embedding_llm_config=LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-15)         provider='openai/text-embedding-3-small', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-16)         api_token='your-api-key', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-17)         temperature=0.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-18)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-19) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-21) # Alternative: Dictionary format (backward compatible) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-22) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-23)     strategy="embedding", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-24)     embedding_llm_config={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-25)         'provider': 'openai/text-embedding-3-small', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-26)         'api_token': 'your-api-key' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-27)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-3-28) )`

### Strategy Comparison

| Feature | Statistical | Embedding |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Speed** | Very fast | Moderate (API calls) |
| **Cost** | Free | Depends on provider |
| **Accuracy** | Good for exact terms | Excellent for concepts |
| **Dependencies** | None | Embedding model/API |
| **Query Understanding** | Literal | Semantic |
| **Best Use Case** | Technical docs, specific terms | Research, broad topics |

### Embedding Strategy Configuration

The embedding strategy offers fine-tuned control through several parameters:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-1) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-2)     strategy="embedding", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-4)     # Model configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-5)     embedding_model="sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-6)     embedding_llm_config=None,  # Use for API-based embeddings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-8)     # Query expansion [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-9)     n_query_variations=10,  # Number of query variations to generate [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-11)     # Coverage parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-12)     embedding_coverage_radius=0.2,  # Distance threshold for coverage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-13)     embedding_k_exp=3.0,  # Exponential decay factor (higher = stricter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-15)     # Stopping criteria [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-16)     embedding_min_relative_improvement=0.1,  # Min improvement to continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-17)     embedding_validation_min_score=0.3,  # Min validation score [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-18)     embedding_min_confidence_threshold=0.1,  # Below this = irrelevant [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-20)     # Link selection [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-21)     embedding_overlap_threshold=0.85,  # Similarity for deduplication [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-23)     # Display confidence mapping [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-24)     embedding_quality_min_confidence=0.7,  # Min displayed confidence [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-25)     embedding_quality_max_confidence=0.95  # Max displayed confidence [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-4-26) )`

### Handling Irrelevant Queries

The embedding strategy can detect when a query is completely unrelated to the content:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-1) # This will stop quickly with low confidence [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-2) result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-3)     start_url="https://docs.python.org/3/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-4)     query="how to cook pasta"  # Irrelevant to Python docs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-7) # Check if query was irrelevant [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-8) if result.metrics.get('is_irrelevant', False): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-5-9)     print("Query is unrelated to the content!")`

When to Use Adaptive Crawling
-----------------------------

### Perfect For:

*   **Research Tasks**: Finding comprehensive information about a topic
*   **Question Answering**: Gathering sufficient context to answer specific queries
*   **Knowledge Base Building**: Creating focused datasets for AI/ML applications
*   **Competitive Intelligence**: Collecting complete information about specific products/features

### Not Recommended For:

*   **Full Site Archiving**: When you need every page regardless of content
*   **Structured Data Extraction**: When targeting specific, known page patterns
*   **Real-time Monitoring**: When you need continuous updates

Understanding the Output
------------------------

### Confidence Score

The confidence score (0-1) indicates how sufficient the gathered information is: - **0.0-0.3**: Insufficient information, needs more crawling - **0.3-0.6**: Partial information, may answer basic queries - **0.6-0.7**: Good coverage, can answer most queries - **0.7-1.0**: Excellent coverage, comprehensive information

### Statistics Display

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-6-1) adaptive.print_stats(detailed=False)  # Summary table [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-6-2) adaptive.print_stats(detailed=True)   # Detailed metrics`

The summary shows: - Pages crawled vs. confidence achieved - Coverage, consistency, and saturation scores - Crawling efficiency metrics

Persistence and Resumption
--------------------------

### Saving Progress

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-7-1) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-7-2)     save_state=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-7-3)     state_path="my_crawl_state.json" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-7-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-7-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-7-6) # Crawl will auto-save progress [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-7-7) result = await adaptive.digest(start_url, query)`

### Resuming a Crawl

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-8-1) # Resume from saved state [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-8-2) result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-8-3)     start_url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-8-4)     query, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-8-5)     resume_from="my_crawl_state.json" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-8-6) )`

### Exporting Knowledge Base

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-9-1) # Export collected pages to JSONL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-9-2) adaptive.export_knowledge_base("knowledge_base.jsonl") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-9-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-9-4) # Import into another session [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-9-5) new_adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-9-6) new_adaptive.import_knowledge_base("knowledge_base.jsonl")`

Best Practices
--------------

### 1\. Query Formulation

*   Use specific, descriptive queries
*   Include key terms you expect to find
*   Avoid overly broad queries

### 2\. Threshold Tuning

*   Start with default (0.7) for general use
*   Lower to 0.5-0.6 for exploratory crawling
*   Raise to 0.8+ for exhaustive coverage

### 3\. Performance Optimization

*   Use appropriate `max_pages` limits
*   Adjust `top_k_links` based on site structure
*   Enable caching for repeat crawls

### 4\. Link Selection

*   The crawler prioritizes links based on:
*   Relevance to query
*   Expected information gain
*   URL structure and depth

Examples
--------

### Research Assistant

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-1) # Gather information about a programming concept [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-2) result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-3)     start_url="https://realpython.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-4)     query="python decorators implementation patterns" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-7) # Get the most relevant excerpts [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-8) for doc in adaptive.get_relevant_content(top_k=3): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-9)     print(f"\nFrom: {doc['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-10)     print(f"Relevance: {doc['score']:.2%}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-10-11)     print(doc['content'][:500] + "...")`

### Knowledge Base Builder

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-1) # Build a focused knowledge base about machine learning [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-2) queries = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-3)     "supervised learning algorithms", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-4)     "neural network architectures",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-5)     "model evaluation metrics" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-6) ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-8) for query in queries: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-9)     await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-10)         start_url="https://scikit-learn.org/stable/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-11)         query=query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-14) # Export combined knowledge base [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-11-15) adaptive.export_knowledge_base("ml_knowledge.jsonl")`

### API Documentation Crawler

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-1) # Intelligently crawl API documentation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-2) config = AdaptiveConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-3)     confidence_threshold=0.85,  # Higher threshold for completeness [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-4)     max_pages=30 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-7) adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-8) result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-9)     start_url="https://api.example.com/docs", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-10)     query="authentication endpoints rate limits" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/#__codelineno-12-11) )`

Next Steps
----------

*   Learn about [Advanced Adaptive Strategies](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/adaptive-strategies/)
    
*   Explore the [AdaptiveCrawler API Reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/adaptive-crawler/)
    
*   See more [Examples](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/tree/main/docs/examples/adaptive_crawling)
    

FAQ
---

**Q: How is this different from traditional crawling?** A: Traditional crawling follows fixed patterns (BFS/DFS). Adaptive crawling makes intelligent decisions about which links to follow and when to stop based on information gain.

**Q: Can I use this with JavaScript-heavy sites?** A: Yes! AdaptiveCrawler inherits all capabilities from AsyncWebCrawler, including JavaScript execution.

**Q: How does it handle large websites?** A: The algorithm naturally limits crawling to relevant sections. Use `max_pages` as a safety limit.

**Q: Can I customize the scoring algorithms?** A: Advanced users can implement custom strategies. See [Adaptive Strategies](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/adaptive-strategies/)
.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Clustering Strategies - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Cosine Strategy
===============

The Cosine Strategy in Crawl4AI uses similarity-based clustering to identify and extract relevant content sections from web pages. This strategy is particularly useful when you need to find and extract content based on semantic similarity rather than structural patterns.

How It Works
------------

The Cosine Strategy: 1. Breaks down page content into meaningful chunks 2. Converts text into vector representations 3. Calculates similarity between chunks 4. Clusters similar content together 5. Ranks and filters content based on relevance

Basic Usage
-----------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-1) from crawl4ai import CosineStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-3) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-4)     semantic_filter="product reviews",    # Target content type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-5)     word_count_threshold=10,             # Minimum words per cluster [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-6)     sim_threshold=0.3                    # Similarity threshold [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-9) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-10)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-11)         url="https://example.com/reviews", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-12)         extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-0-15)     content = result.extracted_content`

Configuration Options
---------------------

### Core Parameters

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-1) CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-2)     # Content Filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-3)     semantic_filter: str = None,       # Keywords/topic for content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-4)     word_count_threshold: int = 10,    # Minimum words per cluster [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-5)     sim_threshold: float = 0.3,        # Similarity threshold (0.0 to 1.0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-7)     # Clustering Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-8)     max_dist: float = 0.2,            # Maximum distance for clustering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-9)     linkage_method: str = 'ward',      # Clustering linkage method [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-10)     top_k: int = 3,                   # Number of top categories to extract [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-12)     # Model Configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-13)     model_name: str = 'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2',  # Embedding model [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-15)     verbose: bool = False             # Enable logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-1-16) )`

### Parameter Details

1. **semantic\_filter** - Sets the target topic or content type - Use keywords relevant to your desired content - Example: "technical specifications", "user reviews", "pricing information"

2. **sim\_threshold** - Controls how similar content must be to be grouped together - Higher values (e.g., 0.8) mean stricter matching - Lower values (e.g., 0.3) allow more variation

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-2-1) # Strict matching [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-2-2) strategy = CosineStrategy(sim_threshold=0.8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-2-4) # Loose matching [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-2-5) strategy = CosineStrategy(sim_threshold=0.3)`

3. **word\_count\_threshold** - Filters out short content blocks - Helps eliminate noise and irrelevant content

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-3-1) # Only consider substantial paragraphs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-3-2) strategy = CosineStrategy(word_count_threshold=50)`

4. **top\_k** - Number of top content clusters to return - Higher values return more diverse content

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-4-1) # Get top 5 most relevant content clusters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-4-2) strategy = CosineStrategy(top_k=5)`

Use Cases
---------

### 1\. Article Content Extraction

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-1) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-2)     semantic_filter="main article content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-3)     word_count_threshold=100,  # Longer blocks for articles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-4)     top_k=1                   # Usually want single main content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-7) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-8)     url="https://example.com/blog/post", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-9)     extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-5-10) )`

### 2\. Product Review Analysis

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-6-1) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-6-2)     semantic_filter="customer reviews and ratings", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-6-3)     word_count_threshold=20,   # Reviews can be shorter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-6-4)     top_k=10,                 # Get multiple reviews [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-6-5)     sim_threshold=0.4         # Allow variety in review content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-6-6) )`

### 3\. Technical Documentation

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-7-1) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-7-2)     semantic_filter="technical specifications documentation", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-7-3)     word_count_threshold=30, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-7-4)     sim_threshold=0.6,        # Stricter matching for technical content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-7-5)     max_dist=0.3             # Allow related technical sections [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-7-6) )`

Advanced Features
-----------------

### Custom Clustering

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-8-1) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-8-2)     linkage_method='complete',  # Alternative clustering method [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-8-3)     max_dist=0.4,              # Larger clusters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-8-4)     model_name='sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2'  # Multilingual support [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-8-5) )`

### Content Filtering Pipeline

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-1) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-2)     semantic_filter="pricing plans features", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-3)     word_count_threshold=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-4)     sim_threshold=0.5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-5)     top_k=3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-8) async def extract_pricing_features(url: str): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-11)             url=url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-12)             extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-15)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-16)             content = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-17)             return { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-18)                 'pricing_features': content, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-19)                 'clusters': len(content), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-20)                 'similarity_scores': [item['score'] for item in content] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-9-21)             }`

Best Practices
--------------

1. **Adjust Thresholds Iteratively** - Start with default values - Adjust based on results - Monitor clustering quality

2. **Choose Appropriate Word Count Thresholds** - Higher for articles (100+) - Lower for reviews/comments (20+) - Medium for product descriptions (50+)

3. **Optimize Performance**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-10-1) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-10-2)     word_count_threshold=10,  # Filter early [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-10-3)     top_k=5,                 # Limit results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-10-4)     verbose=True             # Monitor performance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-10-5) )`

4. **Handle Different Content Types**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-11-1) # For mixed content pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-11-2) strategy = CosineStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-11-3)     semantic_filter="product features", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-11-4)     sim_threshold=0.4,      # More flexible matching [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-11-5)     max_dist=0.3,          # Larger clusters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-11-6)     top_k=3                # Multiple relevant sections [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-11-7) )`

Error Handling
--------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-1) try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-2)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-3)         url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-4)         extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-5)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-7)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-8)         content = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-9)         if not content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-10)             print("No relevant content found") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-11)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-12)         print(f"Extraction failed: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-14) except Exception as e: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/clustring-strategies/#__codelineno-12-15)     print(f"Error during extraction: {str(e)}")`

The Cosine Strategy is particularly effective when: - Content structure is inconsistent - You need semantic understanding - You want to find similar content blocks - Structure-based extraction (CSS/XPath) isn't reliable

It works well with other strategies and can be used as a pre-processing step for LLM-based extraction.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Page Interaction - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Page Interaction
================

Crawl4AI provides powerful features for interacting with **dynamic** webpages, handling JavaScript execution, waiting for conditions, and managing multi-step flows. By combining **js\_code**, **wait\_for**, and certain **CrawlerRunConfig** parameters, you can:

1.  Click “Load More” buttons
2.  Fill forms and submit them
3.  Wait for elements or data to appear
4.  Reuse sessions across multiple steps

Below is a quick overview of how to do it.

* * *

1\. JavaScript Execution
------------------------

### Basic Execution

**`js_code`** in **`CrawlerRunConfig`** accepts either a single JS string or a list of JS snippets. It runs **after** `wait_for` and `delay_before_return_html` — so the page is fully loaded when your code executes.

**Example**: We'll scroll to the bottom of the page, then optionally click a "Load More" button.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-5)     # Single JS command [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-6)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-7)         js_code="window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-11)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-12)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com",  # Example site [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-13)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-15)         print("Crawled length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-17)     # Multiple commands [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-18)     js_commands = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-19)         "window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-20)         # 'More' link on Hacker News [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-21)         "document.querySelector('a.morelink')?.click();",   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-22)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-23)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(js_code=js_commands) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-25)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-26)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-27)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com",  # Another pass [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-28)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-29)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-30)         print("After scroll+click, length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-32) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-0-33)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Relevant `CrawlerRunConfig` params**: - **`js_code`**: JavaScript to run **after** `wait_for` and `delay_before_return_html` complete. Runs on the fully-loaded page. - **`js_code_before_wait`**: JavaScript to run **before** `wait_for`. Use when you need to trigger loading that `wait_for` then checks. - **`js_only`**: If set to `True` on subsequent calls, indicates we're continuing an existing session without a new full navigation. - **`session_id`**: If you want to keep the same page across multiple calls, specify an ID.

### Execution Order

Understanding when your JavaScript runs relative to other pipeline steps:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-1-1) 1. Page navigation (page.goto) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-1-2) 2. js_code_before_wait     ← triggers loading / clicks tabs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-1-3) 3. wait_for                ← waits for content to appear [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-1-4) 4. delay_before_return_html ← extra safety margin [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-1-5) 5. js_code                 ← runs on the fully-loaded page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-1-6) 6. flatten_shadow_dom      ← if enabled [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-1-7) 7. page.content()          ← HTML capture`

If you need JS to trigger something and then wait for the result, use `js_code_before_wait` + `wait_for`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-2-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-2-2)     # Click a tab first [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-2-3)     js_code_before_wait="document.querySelector('#specs-tab')?.click();", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-2-4)     # Then wait for the tab content to appear [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-2-5)     wait_for="css:#specs-panel .content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-2-6) )`

* * *

2\. Wait Conditions
-------------------

### 2.1 CSS-Based Waiting

Sometimes, you just want to wait for a specific element to appear. For example:
