`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-6)         # Wait for at least 30 items on Hacker News [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-7)         wait_for="css:.athing:nth-child(30)"   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-11)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-12)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-14)         print("We have at least 30 items loaded!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-15)         # Rough check [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-16)         print("Total items in HTML:", result.cleaned_html.count("athing"))   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-18) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-3-19)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key param**: - **`wait_for="css:..."`**: Tells the crawler to wait until that CSS selector is present.

### 2.2 JavaScript-Based Waiting

For more complex conditions (e.g., waiting for content length to exceed a threshold), prefix `js:`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-4-1) wait_condition = """() => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-4-2)     const items = document.querySelectorAll('.athing'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-4-3)     return items.length > 50;  // Wait for at least 51 items [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-4-4) }""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-4-6) config = CrawlerRunConfig(wait_for=f"js:{wait_condition}")`

**Behind the Scenes**: Crawl4AI keeps polling the JS function until it returns `true` or a timeout occurs.

* * *

3\. Handling Dynamic Content
----------------------------

Many modern sites require **multiple steps**: scrolling, clicking “Load More,” or updating via JavaScript. Below are typical patterns.

### 3.1 Load More Example (Hacker News “More” Link)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-5)     # Step 1: Load initial Hacker News page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-6)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-7)         wait_for="css:.athing:nth-child(30)"  # Wait for 30 items [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-11)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-12)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-14)         print("Initial items loaded.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-16)         # Step 2: Let's scroll and click the "More" link [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-17)         load_more_js = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-18)             "window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-19)             # The "More" link at page bottom [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-20)             "document.querySelector('a.morelink')?.click();"   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-21)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-23)         next_page_conf = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-24)             js_code=load_more_js, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-25)             wait_for="""js:() => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-26)                 return document.querySelectorAll('.athing').length > 30; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-27)             }""", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-28)             # Mark that we do not re-navigate, but run JS in the same session: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-29)             js_only=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-30)             session_id="hn_session" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-31)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-33)         # Re-use the same crawler session [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-34)         result2 = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-35)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com",  # same URL but continuing session [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-36)             config=next_page_conf [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-37)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-38)         total_items = result2.cleaned_html.count("athing") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-39)         print("Items after load-more:", total_items) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-41) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-5-42)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key params**: - **`session_id="hn_session"`**: Keep the same page across multiple calls to `arun()`. - **`js_only=True`**: We’re not performing a full reload, just applying JS in the existing page. - **`wait_for`** with `js:`: Wait for item count to grow beyond 30.

* * *

### 3.2 Form Interaction

If the site has a search or login form, you can fill fields and submit them with **`js_code`**. For instance, if GitHub had a local search form:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-1) js_form_interaction = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-2) document.querySelector('#your-search').value = 'TypeScript commits'; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-3) document.querySelector('form').submit(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-4) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-6) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-7)     js_code=js_form_interaction, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-8)     wait_for="css:.commit" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-6-10) result = await crawler.arun(url="https://github.com/search", config=config)`

**In reality**: Replace IDs or classes with the real site’s form selectors.

* * *

4\. Timing Control
------------------

1. **`page_timeout`** (ms): Overall page load or script execution time limit.  
2. **`delay_before_return_html`** (seconds): Wait an extra moment before capturing the final HTML.  
3. **`mean_delay`** & **`max_range`**: If you call `arun_many()` with multiple URLs, these add a random pause between each request.

**Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-7-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-7-2)     page_timeout=60000,  # 60s limit [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-7-3)     delay_before_return_html=2.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-7-4) )`

* * *

5\. Multi-Step Interaction Example
----------------------------------

Below is a simplified script that does multiple “Load More” clicks on GitHub’s TypeScript commits page. It **re-uses** the same session to accumulate new commits each time. The code includes the relevant **`CrawlerRunConfig`** parameters you’d rely on.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-4) async def multi_page_commits(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-5)     browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-6)         headless=False,  # Visible for demonstration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-7)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-9)     session_id = "github_ts_commits" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-11)     base_wait = """js:() => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-12)         const commits = document.querySelectorAll('li.Box-sc-g0xbh4-0 h4'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-13)         return commits.length > 0; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-14)     }""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-16)     # Step 1: Load initial commits [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-17)     config1 = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-18)         wait_for=base_wait, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-19)         session_id=session_id, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-20)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-21)         # Not using js_only yet since it's our first load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-22)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-24)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-25)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-26)             url="https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/commits/main", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-27)             config=config1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-28)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-29)         print("Initial commits loaded. Count:", result.cleaned_html.count("commit")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-31)         # Step 2: For subsequent pages, we run JS to click 'Next Page' if it exists [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-32)         js_next_page = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-33)         const selector = 'a[data-testid="pagination-next-button"]'; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-34)         const button = document.querySelector(selector); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-35)         if (button) button.click(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-36)         """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-38)         # Wait until new commits appear [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-39)         wait_for_more = """js:() => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-40)             const commits = document.querySelectorAll('li.Box-sc-g0xbh4-0 h4'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-41)             if (!window.firstCommit && commits.length>0) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-42)                 window.firstCommit = commits[0].textContent; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-43)                 return false; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-44)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-45)             // If top commit changes, we have new commits [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-46)             const topNow = commits[0]?.textContent.trim(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-47)             return topNow && topNow !== window.firstCommit; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-48)         }""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-50)         for page in range(2):  # let's do 2 more "Next" pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-51)             config_next = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-52)                 session_id=session_id, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-53)                 js_code=js_next_page, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-54)                 wait_for=wait_for_more, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-55)                 js_only=True,       # We're continuing from the open tab [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-56)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-57)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-58)             result2 = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-59)                 url="https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/commits/main", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-60)                 config=config_next [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-61)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-62)             print(f"Page {page+2} commits count:", result2.cleaned_html.count("commit")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-63) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-64)         # Optionally kill session [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-65)         await crawler.crawler_strategy.kill_session(session_id) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-66) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-67) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-68)     await multi_page_commits() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-69) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-70) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-8-71)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key Points**:

*   **`session_id`**: Keep the same page open.
*   **`js_code`** + **`wait_for`** + **`js_only=True`**: We do partial refreshes, waiting for new commits to appear.
*   **`cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS`** ensures we always see fresh data each step.

* * *

6\. Combine Interaction with Extraction
---------------------------------------

Once dynamic content is loaded, you can attach an **`extraction_strategy`** (like `JsonCssExtractionStrategy` or `LLMExtractionStrategy`). For example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-3) schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-4)     "name": "Commits", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-5)     "baseSelector": "li.Box-sc-g0xbh4-0", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-6)     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-7)         {"name": "title", "selector": "h4.markdown-title", "type": "text"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-8)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-9) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-10) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-11)     session_id="ts_commits_session", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-12)     js_code=js_next_page, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-13)     wait_for=wait_for_more, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-14)     extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-9-15) )`

When done, check `result.extracted_content` for the JSON.

* * *

7\. Shadow DOM Flattening
-------------------------

Sites built with **Web Components** (Stencil, Lit, Shoelace, etc.) render content inside Shadow DOM — an encapsulated sub-tree that is invisible to normal page serialization. Set `flatten_shadow_dom=True` to extract it:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-10-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-10-2)     flatten_shadow_dom=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-10-3)     wait_until="load", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-10-4)     delay_before_return_html=3.0,  # give components time to hydrate [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-10-5) )`

This walks all shadow trees, resolves `<slot>` projections, and produces flat HTML. It also force-opens closed shadow roots via an init script. For details and a full example, see [Flattening Shadow DOM](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#31-flattening-shadow-dom)
 and [`shadow_dom_crawling.py`](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/docs/examples/shadow_dom_crawling.py)
.

* * *

8\. Relevant `CrawlerRunConfig` Parameters
------------------------------------------

Below are the key interaction-related parameters in `CrawlerRunConfig`. For a full list, see [Configuration Parameters](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
.

*   **`js_code`**: JavaScript to run after `wait_for` + `delay_before_return_html`, on the fully-loaded page.
*   **`js_code_before_wait`**: JavaScript to run before `wait_for`. For triggering loading that `wait_for` then checks.
*   **`js_only`**: If `True`, no new page navigation—only JS in the existing session.
*   **`wait_for`**: CSS (`"css:..."`) or JS (`"js:..."`) expression to wait for.
*   **`session_id`**: Reuse the same page across calls.
*   **`cache_mode`**: Whether to read/write from the cache or bypass.
*   **`flatten_shadow_dom`**: Flatten Shadow DOM content into the light DOM before capture.
*   **`process_iframes`**: Inline iframe content into the main document.
*   **`remove_overlay_elements`**: Remove certain popups automatically.
*   **`remove_consent_popups`**: Remove GDPR/cookie consent popups from known CMP providers (OneTrust, Cookiebot, Didomi, etc.).
*   **`simulate_user`, `override_navigator`, `magic`**: Anti-bot or "human-like" interactions.

* * *

9\. Conclusion
--------------

Crawl4AI's **page interaction** features let you:

1. **Execute JavaScript** for scrolling, clicks, or form filling.  
2. **Wait** for CSS or custom JS conditions before capturing data.  
3. **Handle** multi-step flows (like “Load More”) with partial reloads or persistent sessions.  
4\. **Flatten Shadow DOM** on Web Component sites to extract hidden content. 5. Combine with **structured extraction** for dynamic sites.

With these tools, you can scrape modern, interactive webpages confidently. For advanced hooking, user simulation, or in-depth config, check the [API reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
 or related advanced docs. Happy scripting!

* * *

10\. Virtual Scrolling
----------------------

For sites that use **virtual scrolling** (where content is replaced rather than appended as you scroll, like Twitter or Instagram), Crawl4AI provides a dedicated `VirtualScrollConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, VirtualScrollConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-3) async def crawl_twitter_timeline(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-4)     # Configure virtual scroll for Twitter-like feeds [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-5)     virtual_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-6)         container_selector="[data-testid='primaryColumn']",  # Twitter's main column [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-7)         scroll_count=30,                # Scroll 30 times [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-8)         scroll_by="container_height",   # Scroll by container height each time [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-9)         wait_after_scroll=1.0          # Wait 1 second after each scroll [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-12)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-13)         virtual_scroll_config=virtual_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-14)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-16)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-17)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-18)             url="https://twitter.com/search?q=AI", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-19)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-20)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/page-interaction/#__codelineno-11-21)         # result.html now contains ALL tweets from the virtual scroll`

### Virtual Scroll vs JavaScript Scrolling

| Feature | Virtual Scroll | JS Code Scrolling |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Use Case** | Content replaced during scroll | Content appended or simple scroll |
| **Configuration** | `VirtualScrollConfig` object | `js_code` with scroll commands |
| **Automatic Merging** | Yes - merges all unique content | No - captures final state only |
| **Best For** | Twitter, Instagram, virtual tables | Traditional pages, load more buttons |

For detailed examples and configuration options, see the [Virtual Scroll documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/virtual-scroll/)
.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Content Selection - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Content Selection
=================

Crawl4AI provides multiple ways to **select**, **filter**, and **refine** the content from your crawls. Whether you need to target a specific CSS region, exclude entire tags, filter out external links, or remove certain domains and images, **`CrawlerRunConfig`** offers a wide range of parameters.

Below, we show how to configure these parameters and combine them for precise control.

* * *

1\. CSS-Based Selection
-----------------------

There are two ways to select content from a page: using `css_selector` or the more flexible `target_elements`.

### 1.1 Using `css_selector`

A straightforward way to **limit** your crawl results to a certain region of the page is **`css_selector`** in **`CrawlerRunConfig`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-6)         # e.g., first 30 items from Hacker News [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-7)         css_selector=".athing:nth-child(-n+30)"   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-11)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com/newest",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-12)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-14)         print("Partial HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-16) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-0-17)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Result**: Only elements matching that selector remain in `result.cleaned_html`.

### 1.2 Using `target_elements`

The `target_elements` parameter provides more flexibility by allowing you to target **multiple elements** for content extraction while preserving the entire page context for other features:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-6)         # Target article body and sidebar, but not other content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-7)         target_elements=["article.main-content", "aside.sidebar"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-11)             url="https://example.com/blog-post",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-12)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-14)         print("Markdown focused on target elements") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-15)         print("Links from entire page still available:", len(result.links.get("internal", []))) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-17) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-1-18)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key difference**: With `target_elements`, the markdown generation and structural data extraction focus on those elements, but other page elements (like links, images, and tables) are still extracted from the entire page. This gives you fine-grained control over what appears in your markdown content while preserving full page context for link analysis and media collection.

* * *

2\. Content Filtering & Exclusions
----------------------------------

### 2.1 Basic Overview

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-2)     # Content thresholds [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-3)     word_count_threshold=10,        # Minimum words per block [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-5)     # Tag exclusions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-6)     excluded_tags=['form', 'header', 'footer', 'nav'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-8)     # Link filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-9)     exclude_external_links=True,     [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-10)     exclude_social_media_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-11)     # Block entire domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-12)     exclude_domains=["adtrackers.com", "spammynews.org"],     [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-13)     exclude_social_media_domains=["facebook.com", "twitter.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-15)     # Media filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-16)     exclude_external_images=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-2-17) )`

**Explanation**:

*   **`word_count_threshold`**: Ignores text blocks under X words. Helps skip trivial blocks like short nav or disclaimers.
*   **`excluded_tags`**: Removes entire tags (`<form>`, `<header>`, `<footer>`, etc.).
*   **Link Filtering**:
*   `exclude_external_links`: Strips out external links and may remove them from `result.links`.
*   `exclude_social_media_links`: Removes links pointing to known social media domains.
*   `exclude_domains`: A custom list of domains to block if discovered in links.
*   `exclude_social_media_domains`: A curated list (override or add to it) for social media sites.
*   **Media Filtering**:
*   `exclude_external_images`: Discards images not hosted on the same domain as the main page (or its subdomains).

By default in case you set `exclude_social_media_links=True`, the following social media domains are excluded:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-1) [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-2)     'facebook.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-3)     'twitter.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-4)     'x.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-5)     'linkedin.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-6)     'instagram.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-7)     'pinterest.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-8)     'tiktok.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-9)     'snapchat.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-10)     'reddit.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-3-11) ]`

### 2.2 Example Usage

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-6)         css_selector="main.content",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-7)         word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-8)         excluded_tags=["nav", "footer"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-9)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-10)         exclude_social_media_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-11)         exclude_domains=["ads.com", "spammytrackers.net"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-12)         exclude_external_images=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-13)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-14)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-16)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-17)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://news.ycombinator.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-18)         print("Cleaned HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-20) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-4-21)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Note**: If these parameters remove too much, reduce or disable them accordingly.

* * *

3\. Handling Iframes
--------------------

Some sites embed content in `<iframe>` tags. If you want that inline:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-5-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-5-2)     # Merge iframe content into the final output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-5-3)     process_iframes=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-5-4)     remove_overlay_elements=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-5-5)     # Remove GDPR/cookie consent popups (OneTrust, Cookiebot, etc.) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-5-6)     remove_consent_popups=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-5-7) )`

**Usage**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-6)         process_iframes=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-7)         remove_overlay_elements=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-11)             url="https://example.org/iframe-demo",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-12)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-14)         print("Iframe-merged length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-16) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-6-17)     asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

3.1 Flattening Shadow DOM
-------------------------

Sites built with **Web Components** (Stencil, Lit, Shoelace, Angular Elements, etc.) render content inside [Shadow DOM](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Web_components/Using_shadow_DOM)
 — an encapsulated sub-tree that is invisible to normal page serialization. The browser renders it on screen, but `page.content()` never includes it.

Set `flatten_shadow_dom=True` to walk all shadow trees, resolve `<slot>` projections, and produce a single flat HTML document:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-7-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-7-2)     # Flatten shadow DOM into the main document [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-7-3)     flatten_shadow_dom=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-7-4)     # Give web components time to hydrate [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-7-5)     wait_until="load", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-7-6)     delay_before_return_html=3.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-7-7) )`

**Full example** — crawling a product page where specs live inside shadow roots:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-6)         flatten_shadow_dom=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-7)         wait_until="load", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-8)         delay_before_return_html=3.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-11)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-12)             url="https://store.boschrexroth.com/en/us/p/hydraulic-cylinder-r900999011", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-13)             config=config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-15)         # Without flatten_shadow_dom: ~1 KB of markdown (breadcrumbs only) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-16)         # With flatten_shadow_dom:   ~33 KB (full product specs, downloads, etc.) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-17)         print(len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-19) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-8-20)     asyncio.run(main())`

When `flatten_shadow_dom=True` is set, Crawl4AI also injects an init script that force-opens **closed** shadow roots (by patching `Element.prototype.attachShadow`), so even components that use `mode: 'closed'` become accessible.

> **Tip**: Web components need JavaScript to run before they render content (a process called _hydration_). Use `wait_until="load"` and a `delay_before_return_html` of 2–5 seconds to ensure components are fully hydrated before flattening.

For a complete runnable example, see [`shadow_dom_crawling.py`](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/docs/examples/shadow_dom_crawling.py)
.

* * *

4\. Structured Extraction Examples
----------------------------------

You can combine content selection with a more advanced extraction strategy. For instance, a **CSS-based** or **LLM-based** extraction strategy can run on the filtered HTML.

### 4.1 Pattern-Based with `JsonCssExtractionStrategy`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-7)     # Minimal schema for repeated items [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-8)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-9)         "name": "News Items", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-10)         "baseSelector": "tr.athing", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-11)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-12)             {"name": "title", "selector": "span.titleline a", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-13)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-14)                 "name": "link",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-15)                 "selector": "span.titleline a",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-16)                 "type": "attribute",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-17)                 "attribute": "href" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-18)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-19)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-20)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-22)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-23)         # Content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-24)         excluded_tags=["form", "header"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-25)         exclude_domains=["adsite.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-27)         # CSS selection or entire page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-28)         css_selector="table.itemlist", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-30)         # No caching for demonstration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-31)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-33)         # Extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-34)         extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-35)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-36) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-37)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-38)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-39)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com/newest",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-40)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-41)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-42)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-43)         print("Sample extracted item:", data[:1])  # Show first item [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-44) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-45) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-9-46)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 4.2 LLM-Based Extraction

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-3) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-4) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-5) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-7) class ArticleData(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-8)     headline: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-9)     summary: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-11) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-12)     llm_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-13)         llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4",api_token="sk-YOUR_API_KEY") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-14)         schema=ArticleData.schema(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-15)         extraction_type="schema", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-16)         instruction="Extract 'headline' and a short 'summary' from the content." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-17)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-19)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-20)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-21)         word_count_threshold=20, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-22)         extraction_strategy=llm_strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-23)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-25)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-26)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://news.ycombinator.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-27)         article = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-28)         print(article) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-30) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-10-31)     asyncio.run(main())`

Here, the crawler:

*   Filters out external links (`exclude_external_links=True`).
*   Ignores very short text blocks (`word_count_threshold=20`).
*   Passes the final HTML to your LLM strategy for an AI-driven parse.

* * *

5\. Comprehensive Example
-------------------------

Below is a short function that unifies **CSS selection**, **exclusion** logic, and a pattern-based extraction, demonstrating how you can fine-tune your final data:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-6) async def extract_main_articles(url: str): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-8)         "name": "ArticleBlock", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-9)         "baseSelector": "div.article-block", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-11)             {"name": "headline", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-12)             {"name": "summary", "selector": ".summary", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-13)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-14)                 "name": "metadata", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-15)                 "type": "nested", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-16)                 "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-17)                     {"name": "author", "selector": ".author", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-18)                     {"name": "date", "selector": ".date", "type": "text"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-19)                 ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-20)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-21)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-22)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-24)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-25)         # Keep only #main-content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-26)         css_selector="#main-content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-28)         # Filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-29)         word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-30)         excluded_tags=["nav", "footer"],   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-31)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-32)         exclude_domains=["somebadsite.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-33)         exclude_external_images=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-35)         # Extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-36)         extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-38)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-39)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-41)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-42)         result = await crawler.arun(url=url, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-43)         if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-44)             print(f"Error: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-45)             return None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-46)         return json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-48) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-49)     articles = await extract_main_articles("https://news.ycombinator.com/newest") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-50)     if articles: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-51)         print("Extracted Articles:", articles[:2])  # Show first 2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-52) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-53) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-11-54)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Why This Works**: - **CSS** scoping with `#main-content`.  
\- Multiple **exclude\_** parameters to remove domains, external images, etc.  
\- A **JsonCssExtractionStrategy** to parse repeated article blocks.

* * *

6\. Scraping Modes
------------------

Crawl4AI uses `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy` (LXML-based) as the default scraping strategy for HTML content processing. This strategy offers excellent performance, especially for large HTML documents.

**Note:** For backward compatibility, `WebScrapingStrategy` is still available as an alias for `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-3) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-4)     # Default configuration already uses LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-7)     # Or explicitly specify it if desired [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-8)     config_explicit = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-9)         scraping_strategy=LXMLWebScrapingStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-12)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-13)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-14)             url="https://example.com",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-15)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-12-16)         )`

You can also create your own custom scraping strategy by inheriting from `ContentScrapingStrategy`. The strategy must return a `ScrapingResult` object with the following structure:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-1) from crawl4ai import ContentScrapingStrategy, ScrapingResult, MediaItem, Media, Link, Links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-3) class CustomScrapingStrategy(ContentScrapingStrategy): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-4)     def scrap(self, url: str, html: str, **kwargs) -> ScrapingResult: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-5)         # Implement your custom scraping logic here [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-6)         return ScrapingResult( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-7)             cleaned_html="<html>...</html>",  # Cleaned HTML content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-8)             success=True,                     # Whether scraping was successful [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-9)             media=Media( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-10)                 images=[                      # List of images found [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-11)                     MediaItem( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-12)                         src="https://example.com/image.jpg", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-13)                         alt="Image description", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-14)                         desc="Surrounding text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-15)                         score=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-16)                         type="image", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-17)                         group_id=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-18)                         format="jpg", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-19)                         width=800 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-20)                     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-21)                 ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-22)                 videos=[],                    # List of videos (same structure as images) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-23)                 audios=[]                     # List of audio files (same structure as images) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-24)             ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-25)             links=Links( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-26)                 internal=[                    # List of internal links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-27)                     Link( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-28)                         href="https://example.com/page", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-29)                         text="Link text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-30)                         title="Link title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-31)                         base_domain="example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-32)                     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-33)                 ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-34)                 external=[]                   # List of external links (same structure) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-35)             ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-36)             metadata={                        # Additional metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-37)                 "title": "Page Title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-38)                 "description": "Page description" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-39)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-40)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-42)     async def ascrap(self, url: str, html: str, **kwargs) -> ScrapingResult: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-43)         # For simple cases, you can use the sync version [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-13-44)         return await asyncio.to_thread(self.scrap, url, html, **kwargs)`

### Performance Considerations

The LXML strategy provides excellent performance, particularly when processing large HTML documents, offering up to 10-20x faster processing compared to BeautifulSoup-based approaches.

Benefits of LXML strategy: - Fast processing of large HTML documents (especially >100KB) - Efficient memory usage - Good handling of well-formed HTML - Robust table detection and extraction

### Backward Compatibility

For users upgrading from earlier versions: - `WebScrapingStrategy` is now an alias for `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy` - Existing code using `WebScrapingStrategy` will continue to work without modification - No changes are required to your existing code

* * *

7\. Combining CSS Selection Methods
-----------------------------------

You can combine `css_selector` and `target_elements` in powerful ways to achieve fine-grained control over your output:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-5)     # Target specific content but preserve page context [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-6)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-7)         # Focus markdown on main content and sidebar [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-8)         target_elements=["#main-content", ".sidebar"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-10)         # Global filters applied to entire page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-11)         excluded_tags=["nav", "footer", "header"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-12)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-14)         # Use basic content thresholds [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-15)         word_count_threshold=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-17)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-18)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-20)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-21)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-22)             url="https://example.com/article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-23)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-24)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-26)         print(f"Content focuses on specific elements, but all links still analyzed") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-27)         print(f"Internal links: {len(result.links.get('internal', []))}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-28)         print(f"External links: {len(result.links.get('external', []))}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-30) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/content-selection/#__codelineno-14-31)     asyncio.run(main())`

This approach gives you the best of both worlds: - Markdown generation and content extraction focus on the elements you care about - Links, images and other page data still give you the full context of the page - Content filtering still applies globally

8\. Conclusion
--------------

By mixing **target\_elements** or **css\_selector** scoping, **content filtering** parameters, and advanced **extraction strategies**, you can precisely **choose** which data to keep. Key parameters in **`CrawlerRunConfig`** for content selection include:

1.  **`target_elements`** – Array of CSS selectors to focus markdown generation and data extraction, while preserving full page context for links and media.
2.  **`css_selector`** – Basic scoping to an element or region for all extraction processes.
3.  **`word_count_threshold`** – Skip short blocks.
4.  **`excluded_tags`** – Remove entire HTML tags.
5.  **`exclude_external_links`**, **`exclude_social_media_links`**, **`exclude_domains`** – Filter out unwanted links or domains.
6.  **`exclude_external_images`** – Remove images from external sources.
7.  **`process_iframes`** – Merge iframe content if needed.

Combine these with structured extraction (CSS, LLM-based, or others) to build powerful crawls that yield exactly the content you want, from raw or cleaned HTML up to sophisticated JSON structures. For more detail, see [Configuration Reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/)
. Enjoy curating your data to the max!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# WebScrapingStrategy Migration Guide - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

WebScrapingStrategy Migration Guide
===================================

Overview
--------

Crawl4AI has simplified its content scraping architecture. The BeautifulSoup-based `WebScrapingStrategy` has been deprecated in favor of the faster LXML-based implementation. However, **no action is required** - your existing code will continue to work.

What Changed?
-------------

1.  **`WebScrapingStrategy` is now an alias** for `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy`
2.  **The BeautifulSoup implementation has been removed** (~1000 lines of redundant code)
3.  **`LXMLWebScrapingStrategy` inherits directly** from `ContentScrapingStrategy`
4.  **Performance remains optimal** with LXML as the sole implementation

Backward Compatibility
----------------------

**Your existing code continues to work without any changes:**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-0-1) # This still works perfectly [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, WebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-0-4) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-0-5)     scraping_strategy=WebScrapingStrategy()  # Works as before [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-0-6) )`

Migration Options
-----------------

You have three options:

### Option 1: Do Nothing (Recommended)

Your code will continue to work. `WebScrapingStrategy` is permanently aliased to `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy`.

### Option 2: Update Imports (Optional)

For clarity, you can update your imports:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-1-1) # Old (still works) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import WebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-1-3) strategy = WebScrapingStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-1-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-1-5) # New (more explicit) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-1-6) from crawl4ai import LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-1-7) strategy = LXMLWebScrapingStrategy()`

### Option 3: Use Default Configuration

Since `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy` is the default, you can omit the strategy parameter:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-2-1) # Simplest approach - uses LXMLWebScrapingStrategy by default [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-2-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig()`

Type Hints
----------

If you use type hints, both work:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai import WebScrapingStrategy, LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-3) def process_with_strategy(strategy: WebScrapingStrategy) -> None: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-4)     # Works with both WebScrapingStrategy and LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-5)     pass [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-7) # Both are valid [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-8) process_with_strategy(WebScrapingStrategy()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-3-9) process_with_strategy(LXMLWebScrapingStrategy())`

Subclassing
-----------

If you've subclassed `WebScrapingStrategy`, it continues to work:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-4-1) class MyCustomStrategy(WebScrapingStrategy): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-4-2)     def __init__(self): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-4-3)         super().__init__() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/webscraping-strategy-migration/#__codelineno-4-4)         # Your custom code`

Performance Benefits
--------------------

By consolidating to LXML: - **10-20x faster** HTML parsing for large documents - **Lower memory usage** - **Consistent behavior** across all use cases - **Simplified maintenance** and bug fixes

Summary
-------

This change simplifies Crawl4AI's internals while maintaining 100% backward compatibility. Your existing code continues to work, and you get better performance automatically.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# 🚀 Crawl4AI v0.7.7: The Self-Hosting & Monitoring Update - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

🚀 Crawl4AI v0.7.7: The Self-Hosting & Monitoring Update
========================================================

_November 14, 2025 • 10 min read_

* * *

Today I'm releasing Crawl4AI v0.7.7—the Self-Hosting & Monitoring Update. This release transforms Crawl4AI Docker from a simple containerized crawler into a complete self-hosting platform with enterprise-grade real-time monitoring, full operational transparency, and production-ready observability.

🎯 What's New at a Glance
-------------------------

*   **📊 Real-time Monitoring Dashboard**: Interactive web UI with live system metrics and browser pool status
*   **🔌 Comprehensive Monitor API**: Complete REST API for programmatic access to all monitoring data
*   **⚡ WebSocket Streaming**: Real-time updates every 2 seconds for custom dashboards
*   **🎮 Control Actions**: Manual browser management (kill, restart, cleanup)
*   **🔥 Smart Browser Pool**: 3-tier architecture (permanent/hot/cold) with automatic promotion
*   **🧹 Janitor Cleanup System**: Automatic resource management with event logging
*   **📈 Production Metrics**: 6 critical metrics for operational excellence
*   **🏭 Integration Ready**: Prometheus, alerting, and log aggregation examples
*   **🐛 Critical Bug Fixes**: Async LLM extraction, DFS crawling, viewport config, and more

📊 Real-time Monitoring Dashboard: Complete Visibility
------------------------------------------------------

**The Problem:** Running Crawl4AI in Docker was like flying blind. Users had no visibility into what was happening inside the container—memory usage, active requests, browser pools, or errors. Troubleshooting required checking logs, and there was no way to monitor performance or manually intervene when issues occurred.

**My Solution:** I built a complete real-time monitoring system with an interactive dashboard, comprehensive REST API, WebSocket streaming, and manual control actions. Now you have full transparency and control over your crawling infrastructure.

### The Self-Hosting Value Proposition

Before v0.7.7, Docker was just a containerized crawler. After v0.7.7, it's a complete self-hosting platform that gives you:

*   **🔒 Data Privacy**: Your data never leaves your infrastructure
*   **💰 Cost Control**: No per-request pricing or rate limits
*   **🎯 Full Customization**: Complete control over configurations and strategies
*   **📊 Complete Transparency**: Real-time visibility into every aspect
*   **⚡ Performance**: Direct access without network overhead
*   **🛡️ Enterprise Security**: Keep workflows behind your firewall

### Interactive Monitoring Dashboard

Access the dashboard at `http://localhost:11235/dashboard` to see:

*   **System Health Overview**: CPU, memory, network, and uptime in real-time
*   **Live Request Tracking**: Active and completed requests with full details
*   **Browser Pool Management**: Interactive table with permanent/hot/cold browsers
*   **Janitor Events Log**: Automatic cleanup activities
*   **Error Monitoring**: Full context error logs
