The dashboard updates every 2 seconds via WebSocket, giving you live visibility into your crawling operations.

🔌 Monitor API: Programmatic Access
-----------------------------------

**The Problem:** Monitoring dashboards are great for humans, but automation and integration require programmatic access.

**My Solution:** A comprehensive REST API that exposes all monitoring data for integration with your existing infrastructure.

### System Health Endpoint

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-1) import httpx [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-4) async def monitor_system_health(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-5)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-6)         response = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/health") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-7)         health = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-9)         print(f"Container Metrics:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-10)         print(f"  CPU: {health['container']['cpu_percent']:.1f}%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-11)         print(f"  Memory: {health['container']['memory_percent']:.1f}%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-12)         print(f"  Uptime: {health['container']['uptime_seconds']}s") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-14)         print(f"\nBrowser Pool:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-15)         print(f"  Permanent: {health['pool']['permanent']['active']} active") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-16)         print(f"  Hot Pool: {health['pool']['hot']['count']} browsers") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-17)         print(f"  Cold Pool: {health['pool']['cold']['count']} browsers") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-19)         print(f"\nStatistics:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-20)         print(f"  Total Requests: {health['stats']['total_requests']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-21)         print(f"  Success Rate: {health['stats']['success_rate_percent']:.1f}%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-22)         print(f"  Avg Latency: {health['stats']['avg_latency_ms']:.0f}ms") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-0-24) asyncio.run(monitor_system_health())`

### Request Tracking

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-1) async def track_requests(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-3)         response = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/requests") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-4)         requests_data = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-6)         print(f"Active Requests: {len(requests_data['active'])}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-7)         print(f"Completed Requests: {len(requests_data['completed'])}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-9)         # See details of recent requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-10)         for req in requests_data['completed'][:5]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-11)             status_icon = "✅" if req['success'] else "❌" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-1-12)             print(f"{status_icon} {req['endpoint']} - {req['latency_ms']:.0f}ms")`

### Browser Pool Management

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-1) async def monitor_browser_pool(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-3)         response = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/browsers") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-4)         browsers = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-6)         print(f"Pool Summary:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-7)         print(f"  Total Browsers: {browsers['summary']['total_count']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-8)         print(f"  Total Memory: {browsers['summary']['total_memory_mb']} MB") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-9)         print(f"  Reuse Rate: {browsers['summary']['reuse_rate_percent']:.1f}%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-11)         # List all browsers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-12)         for browser in browsers['permanent']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-13)             print(f"🔥 Permanent: {browser['browser_id'][:8]}... | " [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-14)                   f"Requests: {browser['request_count']} | " [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-2-15)                   f"Memory: {browser['memory_mb']:.0f} MB")`

### Endpoint Performance Statistics

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-1) async def get_endpoint_stats(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-3)         response = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/endpoints/stats") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-4)         stats = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-6)         print("Endpoint Analytics:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-7)         for endpoint, data in stats.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-8)             print(f"  {endpoint}:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-9)             print(f"    Requests: {data['count']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-10)             print(f"    Avg Latency: {data['avg_latency_ms']:.0f}ms") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-3-11)             print(f"    Success Rate: {data['success_rate_percent']:.1f}%")`

### Complete API Reference

The Monitor API includes these endpoints:

*   `GET /monitor/health` - System health with pool statistics
*   `GET /monitor/requests` - Active and completed request tracking
*   `GET /monitor/browsers` - Browser pool details and efficiency
*   `GET /monitor/endpoints/stats` - Per-endpoint performance analytics
*   `GET /monitor/timeline?minutes=5` - Time-series data for charts
*   `GET /monitor/logs/janitor?limit=10` - Cleanup activity logs
*   `GET /monitor/logs/errors?limit=10` - Error logs with context
*   `POST /monitor/actions/cleanup` - Force immediate cleanup
*   `POST /monitor/actions/kill_browser` - Kill specific browser
*   `POST /monitor/actions/restart_browser` - Restart browser
*   `POST /monitor/stats/reset` - Reset accumulated statistics

⚡ WebSocket Streaming: Real-time Updates
----------------------------------------

**The Problem:** Polling the API every few seconds wastes resources and adds latency. Real-time dashboards need instant updates.

**My Solution:** WebSocket streaming with 2-second update intervals for building custom real-time dashboards.

### WebSocket Integration Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-1) import websockets [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-5) async def monitor_realtime(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-6)     uri = "ws://localhost:11235/monitor/ws" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-8)     async with websockets.connect(uri) as websocket: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-9)         print("Connected to real-time monitoring stream") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-11)         while True: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-12)             # Receive update every 2 seconds [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-13)             data = await websocket.recv() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-14)             update = json.loads(data) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-16)             # Access all monitoring data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-17)             print(f"\n--- Update at {update['timestamp']} ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-18)             print(f"Memory: {update['health']['container']['memory_percent']:.1f}%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-19)             print(f"Active Requests: {len(update['requests']['active'])}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-20)             print(f"Total Browsers: {update['browsers']['summary']['total_count']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-22)             if update['errors']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-23)                 print(f"⚠️  Recent Errors: {len(update['errors'])}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-4-25) asyncio.run(monitor_realtime())`

**Expected Real-World Impact:** - **Custom Dashboards**: Build tailored monitoring UIs for your team - **Real-time Alerting**: Trigger alerts instantly when metrics exceed thresholds - **Integration**: Feed live data into monitoring tools like Grafana - **Automation**: React to events in real-time without polling

🔥 Smart Browser Pool: 3-Tier Architecture
------------------------------------------

**The Problem:** Creating a new browser for every request is slow and memory-intensive. Traditional browser pools are static and inefficient.

**My Solution:** A smart 3-tier browser pool that automatically adapts to usage patterns.

### How It Works

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-1) import httpx [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-3) async def demonstrate_browser_pool(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-4)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-5)         # Request 1-3: Default config → Uses permanent browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-6)         print("Phase 1: Using permanent browser") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-7)         for i in range(3): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-8)             await client.post( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-9)                 "http://localhost:11235/crawl", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-10)                 json={"urls": [f"https://httpbin.org/html?req={i}"]} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-11)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-12)             print(f"  Request {i+1}: Reused permanent browser") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-14)         # Request 4-6: Custom viewport → Cold pool (first use) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-15)         print("\nPhase 2: Custom config creates cold pool browser") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-16)         viewport_config = {"viewport": {"width": 1280, "height": 720}} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-17)         for i in range(4): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-18)             await client.post( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-19)                 "http://localhost:11235/crawl", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-20)                 json={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-21)                     "urls": [f"https://httpbin.org/json?v={i}"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-22)                     "browser_config": viewport_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-23)                 } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-24)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-25)             if i < 2: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-26)                 print(f"  Request {i+1}: Cold pool browser") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-27)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-28)                 print(f"  Request {i+1}: Promoted to hot pool! (after 3 uses)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-30)         # Check pool status [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-31)         response = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/browsers") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-32)         browsers = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-34)         print(f"\nPool Status:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-35)         print(f"  Permanent: {len(browsers['permanent'])} (always active)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-36)         print(f"  Hot: {len(browsers['hot'])} (frequently used configs)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-37)         print(f"  Cold: {len(browsers['cold'])} (on-demand)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-38)         print(f"  Reuse Rate: {browsers['summary']['reuse_rate_percent']:.1f}%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-39) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-5-40) asyncio.run(demonstrate_browser_pool())`

**Pool Tiers:**

*   **🔥 Permanent Browser**: Always-on, default configuration, instant response
*   **♨️ Hot Pool**: Browsers promoted after 3+ uses, kept warm for quick access
*   **❄️ Cold Pool**: On-demand browsers for variant configs, cleaned up when idle

**Expected Real-World Impact:** - **Memory Efficiency**: 10x reduction in memory usage vs creating browsers per request - **Performance**: Instant access to frequently-used configurations - **Automatic Optimization**: Pool adapts to your usage patterns - **Resource Management**: Janitor automatically cleans up idle browsers

🧹 Janitor System: Automatic Cleanup
------------------------------------

**The Problem:** Long-running crawlers accumulate idle browsers and consume memory over time.

**My Solution:** An automatic janitor system that monitors and cleans up idle resources.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-1) async def monitor_janitor_activity(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-3)         response = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/logs/janitor?limit=5") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-4)         logs = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-6)         print("Recent Cleanup Activities:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-7)         for log in logs: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-8)             print(f"  {log['timestamp']}: {log['message']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-10) # Example output: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-11) # 2025-11-14 10:30:00: Cleaned up 2 cold pool browsers (idle > 5min) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-12) # 2025-11-14 10:25:00: Browser reuse rate: 85.3% [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-6-13) # 2025-11-14 10:20:00: Hot pool browser promoted (10 requests)`

🎮 Control Actions: Manual Management
-------------------------------------

**The Problem:** Sometimes you need to manually intervene—kill a stuck browser, force cleanup, or restart resources.

**My Solution:** Manual control actions via the API for operational troubleshooting.

### Force Cleanup

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-1) async def force_cleanup(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-3)         response = await client.post("http://localhost:11235/monitor/actions/cleanup") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-4)         result = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-6)         print(f"Cleanup completed:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-7)         print(f"  Browsers cleaned: {result.get('cleaned_count', 0)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-7-8)         print(f"  Memory freed: {result.get('memory_freed_mb', 0):.1f} MB")`

### Kill Specific Browser

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-1) async def kill_stuck_browser(browser_id: str): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-3)         response = await client.post( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-4)             "http://localhost:11235/monitor/actions/kill_browser", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-5)             json={"browser_id": browser_id} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-6)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-8)         if response.status_code == 200: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-8-9)             print(f"✅ Browser {browser_id} killed successfully")`

### Reset Statistics

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-9-1) async def reset_stats(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-9-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-9-3)         response = await client.post("http://localhost:11235/monitor/stats/reset") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-9-4)         print("📊 Statistics reset for fresh monitoring")`

📈 Production Integration Patterns
----------------------------------

### Prometheus Integration

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-1) # Export metrics for Prometheus scraping [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-2) async def export_prometheus_metrics(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-3)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-4)         health = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/health") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-5)         data = health.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-7)         # Export in Prometheus format [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-8)         metrics = f""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-9) # HELP crawl4ai_memory_usage_percent Memory usage percentage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-10) # TYPE crawl4ai_memory_usage_percent gauge [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-11) crawl4ai_memory_usage_percent {data['container']['memory_percent']} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-13) # HELP crawl4ai_request_success_rate Request success rate [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-14) # TYPE crawl4ai_request_success_rate gauge [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-15) crawl4ai_request_success_rate {data['stats']['success_rate_percent']} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-17) # HELP crawl4ai_browser_pool_count Total browsers in pool [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-18) # TYPE crawl4ai_browser_pool_count gauge [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-19) crawl4ai_browser_pool_count {data['pool']['permanent']['active'] + data['pool']['hot']['count'] + data['pool']['cold']['count']} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-20) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-10-21)         return metrics`

### Alerting Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-1) async def check_alerts(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-2)     async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-3)         health = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/health") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-4)         data = health.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-6)         # Memory alert [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-7)         if data['container']['memory_percent'] > 80: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-8)             print("🚨 ALERT: Memory usage above 80%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-9)             # Trigger cleanup [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-10)             await client.post("http://localhost:11235/monitor/actions/cleanup") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-12)         # Success rate alert [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-13)         if data['stats']['success_rate_percent'] < 90: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-14)             print("🚨 ALERT: Success rate below 90%") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-15)             # Check error logs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-16)             errors = await client.get("http://localhost:11235/monitor/logs/errors") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-17)             print(f"Recent errors: {len(errors.json())}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-19)         # Latency alert [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-20)         if data['stats']['avg_latency_ms'] > 5000: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-11-21)             print("🚨 ALERT: Average latency above 5s")`

### Key Metrics to Track

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-1) CRITICAL_METRICS = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-2)     "memory_usage": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-3)         "current": "container.memory_percent", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-4)         "target": "<80%", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-5)         "alert_threshold": ">80%", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-6)         "action": "Force cleanup or scale" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-7)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-8)     "success_rate": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-9)         "current": "stats.success_rate_percent", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-10)         "target": ">95%", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-11)         "alert_threshold": "<90%", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-12)         "action": "Check error logs" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-13)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-14)     "avg_latency": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-15)         "current": "stats.avg_latency_ms", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-16)         "target": "<2000ms", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-17)         "alert_threshold": ">5000ms", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-18)         "action": "Investigate slow requests" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-19)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-20)     "browser_reuse_rate": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-21)         "current": "browsers.summary.reuse_rate_percent", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-22)         "target": ">80%", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-23)         "alert_threshold": "<60%", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-24)         "action": "Check pool configuration" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-25)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-26)     "total_browsers": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-27)         "current": "browsers.summary.total_count", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-28)         "target": "<15", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-29)         "alert_threshold": ">20", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-30)         "action": "Check for browser leaks" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-31)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-32)     "error_frequency": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-33)         "current": "len(errors)", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-34)         "target": "<5/hour", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-35)         "alert_threshold": ">10/hour", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-36)         "action": "Review error patterns" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-37)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-12-38) }`

🐛 Critical Bug Fixes
---------------------

This release includes significant bug fixes that improve stability and performance:

### Async LLM Extraction (#1590)

**The Problem:** LLM extraction was blocking async execution, causing URLs to be processed sequentially instead of in parallel (issue #1055).

**The Fix:** Resolved the blocking issue to enable true parallel processing for LLM extraction.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-1) # Before v0.7.7: Sequential processing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-2) # After v0.7.7: True parallel processing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-4) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-5)     urls = ["url1", "url2", "url3", "url4"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-7)     # Now processes truly in parallel with LLM extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-8)     results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-9)         urls, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-10)         config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-11)             extraction_strategy=LLMExtractionStrategy(...) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-12)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-13-14)     # 4x faster for parallel LLM extraction!`

**Expected Impact:** Major performance improvement for batch LLM extraction workflows.

### DFS Deep Crawling (#1607)

**The Problem:** DFS (Depth-First Search) deep crawl strategy had implementation issues.

**The Fix:** Enhanced DFSDeepCrawlStrategy with proper seen URL tracking and improved documentation.

### Browser & Crawler Config Documentation (#1609)

**The Problem:** Documentation didn't match the actual `async_configs.py` implementation.

**The Fix:** Updated all configuration documentation to accurately reflect the current implementation.

### Sitemap Seeder (#1598)

**The Problem:** Sitemap parsing and URL normalization issues in AsyncUrlSeeder (issue #1559).

**The Fix:** Added comprehensive tests and fixes for sitemap namespace parsing and URL normalization.

### Remove Overlay Elements (#1529)

**The Problem:** The `remove_overlay_elements` functionality wasn't working (issue #1396).

**The Fix:** Fixed by properly calling the injected JavaScript function.

### Viewport Configuration (#1495)

**The Problem:** Viewport configuration wasn't working in managed browsers (issue #1490).

**The Fix:** Added proper viewport size configuration support for browser launch.

### Managed Browser CDP Timing (#1528)

**The Problem:** CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) endpoint verification had timing issues causing connection failures (issue #1445).

**The Fix:** Added exponential backoff for CDP endpoint verification to handle timing variations.

### Security Updates

*   **pyOpenSSL**: Updated from >=24.3.0 to >=25.3.0 to address security vulnerability
*   Added verification tests for the security update

### Docker Fixes

*   **Port Standardization**: Fixed inconsistent port usage (11234 vs 11235) - now standardized to 11235
*   **LLM Environment**: Fixed LLM API key handling for multi-provider support (PR #1537)
*   **Error Handling**: Improved Docker API error messages with comprehensive status codes
*   **Serialization**: Fixed `fit_html` property serialization in `/crawl` and `/crawl/stream` endpoints

### Other Important Fixes

*   **arun\_many Returns**: Fixed function to always return a list, even on exception (PR #1530)
*   **Webhook Serialization**: Properly serialize Pydantic HttpUrl in webhook config
*   **LLMConfig Documentation**: Fixed casing and variable name consistency (issue #1551)
*   **Python Version**: Dropped Python 3.9 support, now requires Python >=3.10

📊 Expected Real-World Impact
-----------------------------

### For DevOps & Infrastructure Teams

*   **Full Visibility**: Know exactly what's happening inside your crawling infrastructure
*   **Proactive Monitoring**: Catch issues before they become problems
*   **Resource Optimization**: Identify memory leaks and performance bottlenecks
*   **Operational Control**: Manual intervention when automated systems need help

### For Production Deployments

*   **Enterprise Observability**: Prometheus, Grafana, and alerting integration
*   **Debugging**: Real-time logs and error tracking
*   **Capacity Planning**: Historical metrics for scaling decisions
*   **SLA Monitoring**: Track success rates and latency against targets

### For Development Teams

*   **Local Monitoring**: Understand crawler behavior during development
*   **Performance Testing**: Measure impact of configuration changes
*   **Troubleshooting**: Quickly identify and fix issues
*   **Learning**: See exactly how the browser pool works

🔄 Breaking Changes
-------------------

**None!** This release is fully backward compatible.

*   All existing Docker configurations continue to work
*   No API changes to existing endpoints
*   Monitoring is additive functionality
*   No migration required

🚀 Upgrade Instructions
-----------------------

### Docker

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-1) # Pull the latest version [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-2) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:0.7.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-4) # Or use the latest tag [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-5) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:latest [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-7) # Run with monitoring enabled (default) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-8) docker run -d \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-9)   -p 11235:11235 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-10)   --shm-size=1g \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-11)   --name crawl4ai \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-12)   unclecode/crawl4ai:0.7.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-14) # Access the monitoring dashboard [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-14-15) open http://localhost:11235/dashboard`

### Python Package

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-15-1) # Upgrade to latest version [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-15-2) pip install --upgrade crawl4ai [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-15-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-15-4) # Or install specific version [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-15-5) pip install crawl4ai==0.7.7`

🎬 Try the Demo
---------------

Run the comprehensive demo that showcases all monitoring features:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.7.7/#__codelineno-16-1) python docs/releases_review/demo_v0.7.7.py`

**The demo includes:** 1. System health overview with live metrics 2. Request tracking with active/completed monitoring 3. Browser pool management (permanent/hot/cold) 4. Complete Monitor API endpoint examples 5. WebSocket streaming demonstration 6. Control actions (cleanup, kill, restart) 7. Production metrics and alerting patterns 8. Self-hosting value proposition

📚 Documentation
----------------

### New Documentation

*   **[Self-Hosting Guide](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/)
    ** - Complete self-hosting documentation with monitoring
*   **Demo Script**: `docs/releases_review/demo_v0.7.7.py` - Working examples

### Updated Documentation

*   **Docker Deployment** → **Self-Hosting** (renamed for better positioning)
*   Added comprehensive monitoring sections
*   Production integration patterns
*   WebSocket streaming examples

💡 Pro Tips
-----------

1.  **Start with the dashboard** - Visit `/dashboard` to get familiar with the monitoring system
2.  **Track the 6 key metrics** - Memory, success rate, latency, reuse rate, browser count, errors
3.  **Set up alerting early** - Use the Monitor API to build alerts before issues occur
4.  **Monitor browser pool efficiency** - Aim for >80% reuse rate for optimal performance
5.  **Use WebSocket for custom dashboards** - Build tailored monitoring UIs for your team
6.  **Leverage Prometheus integration** - Export metrics for long-term storage and analysis
7.  **Check janitor logs** - Understand automatic cleanup patterns
8.  **Use control actions judiciously** - Manual interventions are for exceptional cases

🙏 Acknowledgments
------------------

Thank you to our community for the feedback, bug reports, and feature requests that shaped this release. Special thanks to everyone who contributed to the issues that were fixed in this version.

The monitoring system was built based on real user needs for production deployments, and your input made it comprehensive and practical.
