This setting is crucial: if the LLM instructions rely on HTML tags, pick `"html"`. If you prefer a text-based approach, pick `"markdown"`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-1) LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-2)     # ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-3)     input_format="html",  # Instead of "markdown" or "fit_markdown" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-4) )`

* * *

8\. Token Usage & Show Usage
----------------------------

To keep track of tokens and cost, each chunk is processed with an LLM call. We record usage in:

*   **`usages`** (list): token usage per chunk or call.
*   **`total_usage`**: sum of all chunk calls.
*   **`show_usage()`**: prints a usage report (if the provider returns usage data).

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-1) llm_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy(...) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-2) # ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-3) llm_strategy.show_usage() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-4) # e.g. “Total usage: 1241 tokens across 2 chunk calls”`

If your model provider doesn't return usage info, these fields might be partial or empty.

> **Tip:** `JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema()` also supports token usage tracking via an optional `usage` parameter. See [Token Usage Tracking in Schema Generation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#token-usage-tracking)
>  for details.

* * *

9\. Example: Building a Knowledge Graph
---------------------------------------

Below is a snippet combining **`LLMExtractionStrategy`** with a Pydantic schema for a knowledge graph. Notice how we pass an **`instruction`** telling the model what to parse.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-1) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-4) from typing import List [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-5) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-6) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-7) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-9) class Entity(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-10)     name: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-11)     description: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-13) class Relationship(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-14)     entity1: Entity [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-15)     entity2: Entity [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-16)     description: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-17)     relation_type: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-19) class KnowledgeGraph(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-20)     entities: List[Entity] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-21)     relationships: List[Relationship] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-23) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-24)     # LLM extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-25)     llm_strat = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-26)         llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4", api_token=os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY')), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-27)         schema=KnowledgeGraph.model_json_schema(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-28)         extraction_type="schema", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-29)         instruction="Extract entities and relationships from the content. Return valid JSON.", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-30)         chunk_token_threshold=1400, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-31)         apply_chunking=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-32)         input_format="html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-33)         extra_args={"temperature": 0.1, "max_tokens": 1500} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-34)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-36)     crawl_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-37)         extraction_strategy=llm_strat, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-38)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-39)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-41)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=BrowserConfig(headless=True)) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-42)         # Example page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-43)         url = "https://www.nbcnews.com/business" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-44)         result = await crawler.arun(url=url, config=crawl_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-45) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-46)         print("--- LLM RAW RESPONSE ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-47)         print(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-48)         print("--- END LLM RAW RESPONSE ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-50)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-51)             with open("kb_result.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-52)                 f.write(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-53)             llm_strat.show_usage() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-54)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-55)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-56) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-57) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-58)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Key Observations**:

*   **`extraction_type="schema"`** ensures we get JSON fitting our `KnowledgeGraph`.
*   **`input_format="html"`** means we feed HTML to the model.
*   **`instruction`** guides the model to output a structured knowledge graph.

* * *

10\. Best Practices & Caveats
-----------------------------

1. **Cost & Latency**: LLM calls can be slow or expensive. Consider chunking or smaller coverage if you only need partial data.  
2. **Model Token Limits**: If your page + instruction exceed the context window, chunking is essential.  
3. **Instruction Engineering**: Well-crafted instructions can drastically improve output reliability.  
4. **Schema Strictness**: `"schema"` extraction tries to parse the model output as JSON. If the model returns invalid JSON, partial extraction might happen, or you might get an error.  
5. **Parallel vs. Serial**: The library can process multiple chunks in parallel, but you must watch out for rate limits on certain providers.  
6. **Check Output**: Sometimes, an LLM might omit fields or produce extraneous text. You may want to post-validate with Pydantic or do additional cleanup.

* * *

11\. Conclusion
---------------

**LLM-based extraction** in Crawl4AI is **provider-agnostic**, letting you choose from hundreds of models via LiteLLM. It’s perfect for **semantically complex** tasks or generating advanced structures like knowledge graphs. However, it’s **slower** and potentially costlier than schema-based approaches. Keep these tips in mind:

*   Put your LLM strategy **in `CrawlerRunConfig`**.
*   Use **`input_format`** to pick which form (markdown, HTML, fit\_markdown) the LLM sees.
*   Tweak **`chunk_token_threshold`**, **`overlap_rate`**, and **`apply_chunking`** to handle large content efficiently.
*   Monitor token usage with `show_usage()`.

If your site’s data is consistent or repetitive, consider [`JsonCssExtractionStrategy`](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/)
 first for speed and simplicity. But if you need an **AI-driven** approach, `LLMExtractionStrategy` offers a flexible, multi-provider solution for extracting structured JSON from any website.

**Next Steps**:

1. **Experiment with Different Providers**  
\- Try switching the `provider` (e.g., `"ollama/llama2"`, `"openai/gpt-4o"`, etc.) to see differences in speed, accuracy, or cost.  
\- Pass different `extra_args` like `temperature`, `top_p`, and `max_tokens` to fine-tune your results.

2. **Performance Tuning**  
\- If pages are large, tweak `chunk_token_threshold`, `overlap_rate`, or `apply_chunking` to optimize throughput.  
\- Check the usage logs with `show_usage()` to keep an eye on token consumption and identify potential bottlenecks.

3. **Validate Outputs**  
\- If using `extraction_type="schema"`, parse the LLM’s JSON with a Pydantic model for a final validation step.  
\- Log or handle any parse errors gracefully, especially if the model occasionally returns malformed JSON.

4. **Explore Hooks & Automation**  
\- Integrate LLM extraction with [hooks](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/hooks-auth/)
 for complex pre/post-processing.  
\- Use a multi-step pipeline: crawl, filter, LLM-extract, then store or index results for further analysis.

**Last Updated**: 2025-01-01

* * *

That’s it for **Extracting JSON (LLM)**—now you can harness AI to parse, classify, or reorganize data on the web. Happy crawling!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Deep Crawling - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Deep Crawling
=============

One of Crawl4AI's most powerful features is its ability to perform **configurable deep crawling** that can explore websites beyond a single page. With fine-tuned control over crawl depth, domain boundaries, and content filtering, Crawl4AI gives you the tools to extract precisely the content you need.

In this tutorial, you'll learn:

1.  How to set up a **Basic Deep Crawler** with BFS strategy
2.  Understanding the difference between **streamed and non-streamed** output
3.  Implementing **filters and scorers** to target specific content
4.  Creating **advanced filtering chains** for sophisticated crawls
5.  Using **BestFirstCrawling** for intelligent exploration prioritization
6.  **Crash recovery** for long-running production crawls
7.  **Prefetch mode** for fast URL discovery

> **Prerequisites**  
> \- You’ve completed or read [AsyncWebCrawler Basics](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/)
>  to understand how to run a simple crawl.  
> \- You know how to configure `CrawlerRunConfig`.

* * *

1\. Quick Example
-----------------

Here's a minimal code snippet that implements a basic deep crawl using the **BFSDeepCrawlStrategy**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-4) from crawl4ai.content_scraping_strategy import LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-7)     # Configure a 2-level deep crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-8)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-9)         deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-10)             max_depth=2,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-11)             include_external=False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-12)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-13)         scraping_strategy=LXMLWebScrapingStrategy(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-14)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-18)         results = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-20)         print(f"Crawled {len(results)} pages in total") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-22)         # Access individual results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-23)         for result in results[:3]:  # Show first 3 results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-24)             print(f"URL: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-25)             print(f"Depth: {result.metadata.get('depth', 0)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-27) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-0-28)     asyncio.run(main())`

**What's happening?**  
\- `BFSDeepCrawlStrategy(max_depth=2, include_external=False)` instructs Crawl4AI to: - Crawl the starting page (depth 0) plus 2 more levels - Stay within the same domain (don't follow external links) - Each result contains metadata like the crawl depth - Results are returned as a list after all crawling is complete

* * *

2\. Understanding Deep Crawling Strategy Options
------------------------------------------------

### 2.1 BFSDeepCrawlStrategy (Breadth-First Search)

The **BFSDeepCrawlStrategy** uses a breadth-first approach, exploring all links at one depth before moving deeper:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-3) # Basic configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-4) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-5)     max_depth=2,               # Crawl initial page + 2 levels deep [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-6)     include_external=False,    # Stay within the same domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-7)     max_pages=50,              # Maximum number of pages to crawl (optional) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-8)     score_threshold=0.3,       # Minimum score for URLs to be crawled (optional) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-1-9) )`

**Key parameters:** - **`max_depth`**: Number of levels to crawl beyond the starting page - **`include_external`**: Whether to follow links to other domains - **`max_pages`**: Maximum number of pages to crawl (default: infinite) - **`score_threshold`**: Minimum score for URLs to be crawled (default: -inf) - **`filter_chain`**: FilterChain instance for URL filtering - **`url_scorer`**: Scorer instance for evaluating URLs

### 2.2 DFSDeepCrawlStrategy (Depth-First Search)

The **DFSDeepCrawlStrategy** uses a depth-first approach, explores as far down a branch as possible before backtracking.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import DFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-3) # Basic configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-4) strategy = DFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-5)     max_depth=2,               # Crawl initial page + 2 levels deep [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-6)     include_external=False,    # Stay within the same domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-7)     max_pages=30,              # Maximum number of pages to crawl (optional) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-8)     score_threshold=0.5,       # Minimum score for URLs to be crawled (optional) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-2-9) )`

**Key parameters:** - **`max_depth`**: Number of levels to crawl beyond the starting page - **`include_external`**: Whether to follow links to other domains - **`max_pages`**: Maximum number of pages to crawl (default: infinite) - **`score_threshold`**: Minimum score for URLs to be crawled (default: -inf) - **`filter_chain`**: FilterChain instance for URL filtering - **`url_scorer`**: Scorer instance for evaluating URLs

### 2.3 BestFirstCrawlingStrategy (⭐️ - Recommended Deep crawl strategy)

For more intelligent crawling, use **BestFirstCrawlingStrategy** with scorers to prioritize the most relevant pages:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BestFirstCrawlingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.scorers import KeywordRelevanceScorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-4) # Create a scorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-5) scorer = KeywordRelevanceScorer( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-6)     keywords=["crawl", "example", "async", "configuration"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-7)     weight=0.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-10) # Configure the strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-11) strategy = BestFirstCrawlingStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-12)     max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-13)     include_external=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-14)     url_scorer=scorer, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-15)     max_pages=25,              # Maximum number of pages to crawl (optional) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-3-16) )`

This crawling approach: - Evaluates each discovered URL based on scorer criteria - Visits higher-scoring pages first - Helps focus crawl resources on the most relevant content - Can limit total pages crawled with `max_pages` - Does not need `score_threshold` as it naturally prioritizes by score

* * *

3\. Streaming vs. Non-Streaming Results
---------------------------------------

Crawl4AI can return results in two modes:

### 3.1 Non-Streaming Mode (Default)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-2)     deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy(max_depth=1), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-3)     stream=False  # Default behavior [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-6) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-7)     # Wait for ALL results to be collected before returning [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-8)     results = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-10)     for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-4-11)         process_result(result)`

**When to use non-streaming mode:** - You need the complete dataset before processing - You're performing batch operations on all results together - Crawl time isn't a critical factor

### 3.2 Streaming Mode

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-2)     deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy(max_depth=1), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-3)     stream=True  # Enable streaming [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-6) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-7)     # Returns an async iterator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-8)     async for result in await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-9)         # Process each result as it becomes available [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-5-10)         process_result(result)`

**Benefits of streaming mode:** - Process results immediately as they're discovered - Start working with early results while crawling continues - Better for real-time applications or progressive display - Reduces memory pressure when handling many pages

* * *

4\. Filtering Content with Filter Chains
----------------------------------------

Filters help you narrow down which pages to crawl. Combine multiple filters using **FilterChain** for powerful targeting.

### 4.1 Basic URL Pattern Filter

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.filters import FilterChain, URLPatternFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-3) # Only follow URLs containing "blog" or "docs" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-4) url_filter = URLPatternFilter(patterns=["*blog*", "*docs*"]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-6) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-7)     deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-8)         max_depth=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-9)         filter_chain=FilterChain([url_filter]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-6-11) )`

### 4.2 Combining Multiple Filters

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.filters import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-2)     FilterChain, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-3)     URLPatternFilter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-4)     DomainFilter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-5)     ContentTypeFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-8) # Create a chain of filters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-9) filter_chain = FilterChain([ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-10)     # Only follow URLs with specific patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-11)     URLPatternFilter(patterns=["*guide*", "*tutorial*"]), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-13)     # Only crawl specific domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-14)     DomainFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-15)         allowed_domains=["docs.example.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-16)         blocked_domains=["old.docs.example.com"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-17)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-19)     # Only include specific content types [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-20)     ContentTypeFilter(allowed_types=["text/html"]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-21) ]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-23) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-24)     deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-25)         max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-26)         filter_chain=filter_chain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-27)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-7-28) )`

### 4.3 Available Filter Types

Crawl4AI includes several specialized filters:

*   **`URLPatternFilter`**: Matches URL patterns using wildcard syntax
*   **`DomainFilter`**: Controls which domains to include or exclude
*   **`ContentTypeFilter`**: Filters based on HTTP Content-Type
*   **`ContentRelevanceFilter`**: Uses similarity to a text query
*   **`SEOFilter`**: Evaluates SEO elements (meta tags, headers, etc.)

* * *

5\. Using Scorers for Prioritized Crawling
------------------------------------------

Scorers assign priority values to discovered URLs, helping the crawler focus on the most relevant content first.

### 5.1 KeywordRelevanceScorer

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.scorers import KeywordRelevanceScorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BestFirstCrawlingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-4) # Create a keyword relevance scorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-5) keyword_scorer = KeywordRelevanceScorer( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-6)     keywords=["crawl", "example", "async", "configuration"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-7)     weight=0.7  # Importance of this scorer (0.0 to 1.0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-10) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-11)     deep_crawl_strategy=BestFirstCrawlingStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-12)         max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-13)         url_scorer=keyword_scorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-14)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-15)     stream=True  # Recommended with BestFirstCrawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-16) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-18) # Results will come in order of relevance score [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-19) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-20)     async for result in await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-21)         score = result.metadata.get("score", 0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-8-22)         print(f"Score: {score:.2f} | {result.url}")`

**How scorers work:** - Evaluate each discovered URL before crawling - Calculate relevance based on various signals - Help the crawler make intelligent choices about traversal order

* * *

6\. Advanced Filtering Techniques
---------------------------------

### 6.1 SEO Filter for Quality Assessment

The **SEOFilter** helps you identify pages with strong SEO characteristics:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.filters import FilterChain, SEOFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-3) # Create an SEO filter that looks for specific keywords in page metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-4) seo_filter = SEOFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-5)     threshold=0.5,  # Minimum score (0.0 to 1.0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-6)     keywords=["tutorial", "guide", "documentation"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-9) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-10)     deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-11)         max_depth=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-12)         filter_chain=FilterChain([seo_filter]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-9-14) )`

### 6.2 Content Relevance Filter

The **ContentRelevanceFilter** analyzes the actual content of pages:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.filters import FilterChain, ContentRelevanceFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-3) # Create a content relevance filter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-4) relevance_filter = ContentRelevanceFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-5)     query="Web crawling and data extraction with Python", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-6)     threshold=0.7  # Minimum similarity score (0.0 to 1.0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-9) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-10)     deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-11)         max_depth=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-12)         filter_chain=FilterChain([relevance_filter]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-10-14) )`

This filter: - Measures semantic similarity between query and page content - It's a BM25-based relevance filter using head section content

* * *

7\. Building a Complete Advanced Crawler
----------------------------------------

This example combines multiple techniques for a sophisticated crawl:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-3) from crawl4ai.content_scraping_strategy import LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-4) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BestFirstCrawlingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-5) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.filters import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-6)     FilterChain, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-7)     DomainFilter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-8)     URLPatternFilter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-9)     ContentTypeFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-11) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling.scorers import KeywordRelevanceScorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-13) async def run_advanced_crawler(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-14)     # Create a sophisticated filter chain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-15)     filter_chain = FilterChain([ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-16)         # Domain boundaries [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-17)         DomainFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-18)             allowed_domains=["docs.example.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-19)             blocked_domains=["old.docs.example.com"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-20)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-22)         # URL patterns to include [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-23)         URLPatternFilter(patterns=["*guide*", "*tutorial*", "*blog*"]), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-25)         # Content type filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-26)         ContentTypeFilter(allowed_types=["text/html"]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-27)     ]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-29)     # Create a relevance scorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-30)     keyword_scorer = KeywordRelevanceScorer( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-31)         keywords=["crawl", "example", "async", "configuration"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-32)         weight=0.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-33)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-35)     # Set up the configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-36)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-37)         deep_crawl_strategy=BestFirstCrawlingStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-38)             max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-39)             include_external=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-40)             filter_chain=filter_chain, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-41)             url_scorer=keyword_scorer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-42)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-43)         scraping_strategy=LXMLWebScrapingStrategy(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-44)         stream=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-45)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-46)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-48)     # Execute the crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-49)     results = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-50)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-51)         async for result in await crawler.arun("https://docs.example.com", config=config): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-52)             results.append(result) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-53)             score = result.metadata.get("score", 0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-54)             depth = result.metadata.get("depth", 0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-55)             print(f"Depth: {depth} | Score: {score:.2f} | {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-56) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-57)     # Analyze the results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-58)     print(f"Crawled {len(results)} high-value pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-59)     print(f"Average score: {sum(r.metadata.get('score', 0) for r in results) / len(results):.2f}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-60) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-61)     # Group by depth [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-62)     depth_counts = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-63)     for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-64)         depth = result.metadata.get("depth", 0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-65)         depth_counts[depth] = depth_counts.get(depth, 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-66) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-67)     print("Pages crawled by depth:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-68)     for depth, count in sorted(depth_counts.items()): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-69)         print(f"  Depth {depth}: {count} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-70) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-71) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-11-72)     asyncio.run(run_advanced_crawler())`

* * *

8\. Limiting and Controlling Crawl Size
---------------------------------------

### 8.1 Using max\_pages

You can limit the total number of pages crawled with the `max_pages` parameter:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-12-1) # Limit to exactly 20 pages regardless of depth [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-12-2) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-12-3)     max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-12-4)     max_pages=20 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-12-5) )`

This feature is useful for: - Controlling API costs - Setting predictable execution times - Focusing on the most important content - Testing crawl configurations before full execution

### 8.2 Using score\_threshold

For BFS and DFS strategies, you can set a minimum score threshold to only crawl high-quality pages:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-13-1) # Only follow links with scores above 0.4 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-13-2) strategy = DFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-13-3)     max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-13-4)     url_scorer=KeywordRelevanceScorer(keywords=["api", "guide", "reference"]), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-13-5)     score_threshold=0.4  # Skip URLs with scores below this value [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-13-6) )`

Note that for BestFirstCrawlingStrategy, score\_threshold is not needed since pages are already processed in order of highest score first.

9\. Common Pitfalls & Tips
--------------------------

1.**Set realistic limits.** Be cautious with `max_depth` values > 3, which can exponentially increase crawl size. Use `max_pages` to set hard limits.

2.**Don't neglect the scoring component.** BestFirstCrawling works best with well-tuned scorers. Experiment with keyword weights for optimal prioritization.

3.**Be a good web citizen.** Respect robots.txt. (disabled by default)

4.**Handle page errors gracefully.** Not all pages will be accessible. Check `result.status` when processing results.

5.**Balance breadth vs. depth.** Choose your strategy wisely - BFS for comprehensive coverage, DFS for deep exploration, BestFirst for focused relevance-based crawling.

6.**Preserve HTTPS for security.** If crawling HTTPS sites that redirect to HTTP, use `preserve_https_for_internal_links=True` to maintain secure connections:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-14-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-14-2)     deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy(max_depth=2), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-14-3)     preserve_https_for_internal_links=True  # Keep HTTPS even if server redirects to HTTP [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-14-4) )`

This is especially useful for security-conscious crawling or when dealing with sites that support both protocols.

* * *

10\. Crash Recovery for Long-Running Crawls
-------------------------------------------

For production deployments, especially in cloud environments where instances can be terminated unexpectedly, Crawl4AI provides built-in crash recovery support for all deep crawl strategies.

### 10.1 Enabling State Persistence

All deep crawl strategies (BFS, DFS, Best-First) support two optional parameters:

*   **`resume_state`**: Pass a previously saved state to resume from a checkpoint
*   **`on_state_change`**: Async callback fired after each URL is processed

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-4) # Callback to save state after each URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-5) async def save_state_to_redis(state: dict): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-6)     await redis.set("crawl_state", json.dumps(state)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-8) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-9)     max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-10)     on_state_change=save_state_to_redis,  # Called after each URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-15-11) )`

### 10.2 State Structure

The state dictionary is JSON-serializable and contains:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-16-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-16-2)     "strategy_type": "bfs",  # or "dfs", "best_first" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-16-3)     "visited": ["url1", "url2", ...],  # Already crawled URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-16-4)     "pending": [{"url": "...", "parent_url": "..."}],  # Queue/stack [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-16-5)     "depths": {"url1": 0, "url2": 1},  # Depth tracking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-16-6)     "pages_crawled": 42  # Counter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-16-7) }`

### 10.3 Resuming from a Checkpoint

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-3) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-5) # Load saved state (e.g., from Redis, database, or file) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-6) saved_state = json.loads(await redis.get("crawl_state")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-8) # Resume crawling from where we left off [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-9) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-10)     max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-11)     resume_state=saved_state,  # Continue from checkpoint [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-12)     on_state_change=save_state_to_redis,  # Keep saving progress [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-13) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-15) config = CrawlerRunConfig(deep_crawl_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-17) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-18)     # Will skip already-visited URLs and continue from pending queue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-17-19)     results = await crawler.arun(start_url, config=config)`

### 10.4 Manual State Export

You can export the last captured state using `export_state()`. Note that this requires `on_state_change` to be set (state is captured in the callback):

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-3) captured_state = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-5) async def capture_state(state: dict): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-6)     global captured_state [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-7)     captured_state = state [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-9) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-10)     max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-11)     on_state_change=capture_state,  # Required for state capture [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-12) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-13) config = CrawlerRunConfig(deep_crawl_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-15) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-16)     results = await crawler.arun(start_url, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-18) # Get the last captured state [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-19) state = strategy.export_state() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-20) if state: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-21)     # Save to your preferred storage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-22)     with open("crawl_checkpoint.json", "w") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-18-23)         json.dump(state, f)`

### 10.5 Complete Example: Redis-Based Recovery

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-3) import redis.asyncio as redis [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-4) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-5) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-7) REDIS_KEY = "crawl4ai:crawl_state" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-9) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-10)     redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-12)     # Check for existing state [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-13)     saved_state = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-14)     existing = await redis_client.get(REDIS_KEY) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-15)     if existing: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-16)         saved_state = json.loads(existing) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-17)         print(f"Resuming from checkpoint: {saved_state['pages_crawled']} pages already crawled") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-19)     # State persistence callback [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-20)     async def persist_state(state: dict): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-21)         await redis_client.set(REDIS_KEY, json.dumps(state)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-23)     # Create strategy with recovery support [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-24)     strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-25)         max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-26)         max_pages=100, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-27)         resume_state=saved_state, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-28)         on_state_change=persist_state, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-29)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-31)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(deep_crawl_strategy=strategy, stream=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-33)     try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-34)         async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-35)             async for result in await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-36)                 print(f"Crawled: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-37)     except Exception as e: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-38)         print(f"Crawl interrupted: {e}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-39)         print("State saved - restart to resume") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-40)     finally: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-41)         await redis_client.close() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-43) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-19-44)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 10.6 Zero Overhead

When `resume_state=None` and `on_state_change=None` (the defaults), there is no performance impact. State tracking only activates when you enable these features.

* * *

11\. Cancellation Support for Deep Crawls
-----------------------------------------

For production environments like cloud platforms, you often need to stop a running crawl mid-execution—whether the user changed their mind, specified the wrong URL, or wants to control costs. Crawl4AI provides built-in cancellation support for all deep crawl strategies.

### 11.1 Two Ways to Cancel

**Option A: Callback-based cancellation** (recommended for external systems)

Use `should_cancel` to check an external source (Redis, database, API) before each URL:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-3) async def check_if_cancelled(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-4)     # Check Redis, database, or any external source [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-5)     job = await redis.get(f"job:{job_id}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-6)     return job.get("status") == "cancelled" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-8) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-9)     max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-10)     max_pages=1000, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-11)     should_cancel=check_if_cancelled,  # Called before each URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-20-12) )`

**Option B: Direct cancellation** (for in-process control)

Call `cancel()` directly on the strategy instance:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-21-1) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy(max_depth=3, max_pages=1000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-21-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-21-3) # In another coroutine or thread: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-21-4) strategy.cancel()  # Thread-safe, stops before next URL`

### 11.2 Checking Cancellation Status

Use the `cancelled` property to check if a crawl was cancelled:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-22-1) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-22-2)     results = await crawler.arun(url, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-22-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-22-4) if strategy.cancelled: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-22-5)     print(f"Crawl was cancelled after {len(results)} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-22-6) else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-22-7)     print(f"Crawl completed with {len(results)} pages")`

### 11.3 State Notifications Include Cancelled Flag

When using `on_state_change`, the state dictionary includes a `cancelled` field:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-1) async def handle_state(state: dict): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-2)     if state.get("cancelled"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-3)         print("Crawl was cancelled!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-4)         print(f"Crawled {state['pages_crawled']} pages before cancellation") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-5)     # Save state for potential resume [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-6)     await redis.set("crawl_state", json.dumps(state)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-8) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-9)     max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-10)     should_cancel=check_cancelled, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-11)     on_state_change=handle_state, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-23-12) )`

### 11.4 Key Behaviors

| Scenario | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| Cancel before first URL | Returns empty results, `cancelled=True` |
| Cancel during crawl | Completes current URL, then stops |
| Callback raises exception | Logged as warning, crawl continues (fail-open) |
| Strategy reuse after cancel | Works normally (cancel flag auto-resets) |
| Sync callback function | Supported (auto-detected and handled) |

### 11.5 Complete Example: Cloud Platform Job Cancellation

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-3) import redis.asyncio as redis [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-4) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-5) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-7) async def run_cancellable_crawl(job_id: str, start_url: str): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-8)     redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-10)     # Check external cancellation source [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-11)     async def check_cancelled(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-12)         status = await redis_client.get(f"job:{job_id}:status") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-13)         return status == b"cancelled" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-15)     # Save progress for monitoring and recovery [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-16)     async def save_progress(state: dict): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-17)         await redis_client.set( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-18)             f"job:{job_id}:state", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-19)             json.dumps(state) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-20)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-21)         # Update job progress [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-22)         await redis_client.set( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-23)             f"job:{job_id}:pages_crawled", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-24)             state["pages_crawled"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-25)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-27)     strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-28)         max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-29)         max_pages=500, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-30)         should_cancel=check_cancelled, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-31)         on_state_change=save_progress, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-32)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-34)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-35)         deep_crawl_strategy=strategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-36)         stream=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-37)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-39)     results = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-40)     try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-41)         async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-42)             async for result in await crawler.arun(start_url, config=config): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-43)                 results.append(result) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-44)                 print(f"Crawled: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-45)     finally: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-46)         # Report final status [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-47)         if strategy.cancelled: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-48)             await redis_client.set(f"job:{job_id}:status", "cancelled") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-49)             print(f"Job cancelled after {len(results)} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-50)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-51)             await redis_client.set(f"job:{job_id}:status", "completed") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-52)             print(f"Job completed with {len(results)} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-53) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-54)         await redis_client.close() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-55) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-56)     return results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-57) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-58) # Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-59) # asyncio.run(run_cancellable_crawl("job-123", "https://example.com")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-60) # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-61) # To cancel from another process: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-24-62) # redis_client.set("job:job-123:status", "cancelled")`

### 11.6 Supported Strategies

Cancellation works identically across all deep crawl strategies:

*   **BFSDeepCrawlStrategy** - Breadth-first search
*   **DFSDeepCrawlStrategy** - Depth-first search
*   **BestFirstCrawlingStrategy** - Priority-based crawling

All strategies support: - `should_cancel` callback parameter - `cancel()` method - `cancelled` property

* * *

12\. Prefetch Mode for Fast URL Discovery
-----------------------------------------

When you need to quickly discover URLs without full page processing, use **prefetch mode**. This is ideal for two-phase crawling where you first map the site, then selectively process specific pages.

### 12.1 Enabling Prefetch Mode

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-3) config = CrawlerRunConfig(prefetch=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-5) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-6)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-8)     # Result contains only HTML and links - no markdown, no extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-9)     print(f"Found {len(result.links['internal'])} internal links") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-25-10)     print(f"Found {len(result.links['external'])} external links")`

### 12.2 What Gets Skipped

Prefetch mode uses a fast path that bypasses heavy processing:

| Processing Step | Normal Mode | Prefetch Mode |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fetch HTML | ✅   | ✅   |
| Extract links | ✅   | ✅ (fast `quick_extract_links()`) |
| Generate markdown | ✅   | ❌ Skipped |
| Content scraping | ✅   | ❌ Skipped |
| Media extraction | ✅   | ❌ Skipped |
| LLM extraction | ✅   | ❌ Skipped |

### 12.3 Performance Benefit

*   **Normal mode**: Full pipeline (~2-5 seconds per page)
*   **Prefetch mode**: HTML + links only (~200-500ms per page)

This makes prefetch mode **5-10x faster** for URL discovery.

### 12.4 Two-Phase Crawling Pattern

The most common use case is two-phase crawling:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-4) async def two_phase_crawl(start_url: str): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-6)         # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-7)         # Phase 1: Fast discovery (prefetch mode) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-8)         # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-9)         prefetch_config = CrawlerRunConfig(prefetch=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-10)         discovery = await crawler.arun(start_url, config=prefetch_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-12)         all_urls = [link["href"] for link in discovery.links.get("internal", [])] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-13)         print(f"Discovered {len(all_urls)} URLs") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-15)         # Filter to URLs you care about [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-16)         blog_urls = [url for url in all_urls if "/blog/" in url] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-17)         print(f"Found {len(blog_urls)} blog posts to process") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-19)         # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-20)         # Phase 2: Full processing on selected URLs only [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-21)         # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-22)         full_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-23)             # Your normal extraction settings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-24)             word_count_threshold=100, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-25)             remove_overlay_elements=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-26)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-28)         results = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-29)         for url in blog_urls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-30)             result = await crawler.arun(url, config=full_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-31)             if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-32)                 results.append(result) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-33)                 print(f"Processed: {url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-35)         return results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-36) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-37) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-38)     results = asyncio.run(two_phase_crawl("https://example.com")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/#__codelineno-26-39)     print(f"Fully processed {len(results)} pages")`

### 12.5 Use Cases

*   **Site mapping**: Quickly discover all URLs before deciding what to process
*   **Link validation**: Check which pages exist without heavy processing
*   **Selective deep crawl**: Prefetch to find URLs, filter by pattern, then full crawl
*   **Crawl planning**: Estimate crawl size before committing resources

* * *

13\. Summary & Next Steps
-------------------------

In this **Deep Crawling with Crawl4AI** tutorial, you learned to:

*   Configure **BFSDeepCrawlStrategy**, **DFSDeepCrawlStrategy**, and **BestFirstCrawlingStrategy**
*   Process results in streaming or non-streaming mode
*   Apply filters to target specific content
*   Use scorers to prioritize the most relevant pages
*   Limit crawls with `max_pages` and `score_threshold` parameters
*   Build a complete advanced crawler with combined techniques
*   **Implement crash recovery** with `resume_state` and `on_state_change` for production deployments
*   **Cancel running crawls** with `should_cancel` callback or `cancel()` method for cloud platform job management
*   **Use prefetch mode** for fast URL discovery and two-phase crawling

With these tools, you can efficiently extract structured data from websites at scale, focusing precisely on the content you need for your specific use case.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Link & Media - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Link & Media
============

In this tutorial, you’ll learn how to:

1.  Extract links (internal, external) from crawled pages
2.  Filter or exclude specific domains (e.g., social media or custom domains)
3.  Access and ma### 3.2 Excluding Images

#### Excluding External Images

If you're dealing with heavy pages or want to skip third-party images (advertisements, for example), you can turn on:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-0-1) crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-0-2)     exclude_external_images=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

This setting attempts to discard images from outside the primary domain, keeping only those from the site you're crawling.

#### Excluding All Images

If you want to completely remove all images from the page to maximize performance and reduce memory usage, use:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-1-1) crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-1-2)     exclude_all_images=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-1-3) )`

This setting removes all images very early in the processing pipeline, which significantly improves memory efficiency and processing speed. This is particularly useful when: - You don't need image data in your results - You're crawling image-heavy pages that cause memory issues - You want to focus only on text content - You need to maximize crawling speeddata (especially images) in the crawl result  
4\. Configure your crawler to exclude or prioritize certain images

> **Prerequisites**  
> \- You have completed or are familiar with the [AsyncWebCrawler Basics](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/simple-crawling/)
>  tutorial.  
> \- You can run Crawl4AI in your environment (Playwright, Python, etc.).

* * *

Below is a revised version of the **Link Extraction** and **Media Extraction** sections that includes example data structures showing how links and media items are stored in `CrawlResult`. Feel free to adjust any field names or descriptions to match your actual output.

* * *

1\. Link Extraction
-------------------

### 1.1 `result.links`

When you call `arun()` or `arun_many()` on a URL, Crawl4AI automatically extracts links and stores them in the `links` field of `CrawlResult`. By default, the crawler tries to distinguish **internal** links (same domain) from **external** links (different domains).

**Basic Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-4)     result = await crawler.arun("https://www.example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-5)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-6)         internal_links = result.links.get("internal", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-7)         external_links = result.links.get("external", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-8)         print(f"Found {len(internal_links)} internal links.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-9)         print(f"Found {len(internal_links)} external links.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-10)         print(f"Found {len(result.media)} media items.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-12)         # Each link is typically a dictionary with fields like: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-13)         # { "href": "...", "text": "...", "title": "...", "base_domain": "..." } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-14)         if internal_links: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-15)             print("Sample Internal Link:", internal_links[0]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-16)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-2-17)         print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message)`
