**Structure Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-1) result.links = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-2)   "internal": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-3)     { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-4)       "href": "https://kidocode.com/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-5)       "text": "", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-6)       "title": "", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-7)       "base_domain": "kidocode.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-8)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-9)     { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-10)       "href": "https://kidocode.com/degrees/technology", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-11)       "text": "Technology Degree", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-12)       "title": "KidoCode Tech Program", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-13)       "base_domain": "kidocode.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-14)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-15)     # ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-16)   ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-17)   "external": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-18)     # possibly other links leading to third-party sites [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-19)   ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-3-20) }`

*   **`href`**: The raw hyperlink URL.
*   **`text`**: The link text (if any) within the `<a>` tag.
*   **`title`**: The `title` attribute of the link (if present).
*   **`base_domain`**: The domain extracted from `href`. Helpful for filtering or grouping by domain.

* * *

2\. Advanced Link Head Extraction & Scoring
-------------------------------------------

Ever wanted to not just extract links, but also get the actual content (title, description, metadata) from those linked pages? And score them for relevance? This is exactly what Link Head Extraction does - it fetches the `<head>` section from each discovered link and scores them using multiple algorithms.

### 2.1 Why Link Head Extraction?

When you crawl a page, you get hundreds of links. But which ones are actually valuable? Link Head Extraction solves this by:

1.  **Fetching head content** from each link (title, description, meta tags)
2.  **Scoring links intrinsically** based on URL quality, text relevance, and context
3.  **Scoring links contextually** using BM25 algorithm when you provide a search query
4.  **Combining scores intelligently** to give you a final relevance ranking

### 2.2 Complete Working Example

Here's a full example you can copy, paste, and run immediately:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-3) from crawl4ai import LinkPreviewConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-5) async def extract_link_heads_example(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-6)     """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-7)     Complete example showing link head extraction with scoring. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-8)     This will crawl a documentation site and extract head content from internal links. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-9)     """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-11)     # Configure link head extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-12)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-13)         # Enable link head extraction with detailed configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-14)         link_preview_config=LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-15)             include_internal=True,           # Extract from internal links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-16)             include_external=False,          # Skip external links for this example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-17)             max_links=10,                   # Limit to 10 links for demo [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-18)             concurrency=5,                  # Process 5 links simultaneously [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-19)             timeout=10,                     # 10 second timeout per link [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-20)             query="API documentation guide", # Query for contextual scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-21)             score_threshold=0.3,            # Only include links scoring above 0.3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-22)             verbose=True                    # Show detailed progress [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-23)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-24)         # Enable intrinsic scoring (URL quality, text relevance) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-25)         score_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-26)         # Keep output clean [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-27)         only_text=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-28)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-29)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-31)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-32)         # Crawl a documentation site (great for testing) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-33)         result = await crawler.arun("https://docs.python.org/3/", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-35)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-36)             print(f"✅ Successfully crawled: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-37)             print(f"📄 Page title: {result.metadata.get('title', 'No title')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-39)             # Access links (now enhanced with head data and scores) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-40)             internal_links = result.links.get("internal", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-41)             external_links = result.links.get("external", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-43)             print(f"\n🔗 Found {len(internal_links)} internal links") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-44)             print(f"🌍 Found {len(external_links)} external links") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-45) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-46)             # Count links with head data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-47)             links_with_head = [link for link in internal_links  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-48)                              if link.get("head_data") is not None] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-49)             print(f"🧠 Links with head data extracted: {len(links_with_head)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-50) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-51)             # Show the top 3 scoring links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-52)             print(f"\n🏆 Top 3 Links with Full Scoring:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-53)             for i, link in enumerate(links_with_head[:3]): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-54)                 print(f"\n{i+1}. {link['href']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-55)                 print(f"   Link Text: '{link.get('text', 'No text')[:50]}...'") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-56) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-57)                 # Show all three score types [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-58)                 intrinsic = link.get('intrinsic_score') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-59)                 contextual = link.get('contextual_score')  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-60)                 total = link.get('total_score') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-61) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-62)                 if intrinsic is not None: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-63)                     print(f"   📊 Intrinsic Score: {intrinsic:.2f}/10.0 (URL quality & context)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-64)                 if contextual is not None: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-65)                     print(f"   🎯 Contextual Score: {contextual:.3f} (BM25 relevance to query)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-66)                 if total is not None: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-67)                     print(f"   ⭐ Total Score: {total:.3f} (combined final score)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-68) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-69)                 # Show extracted head data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-70)                 head_data = link.get("head_data", {}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-71)                 if head_data: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-72)                     title = head_data.get("title", "No title") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-73)                     description = head_data.get("meta", {}).get("description", "No description") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-74) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-75)                     print(f"   📰 Title: {title[:60]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-76)                     if description: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-77)                         print(f"   📝 Description: {description[:80]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-78) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-79)                     # Show extraction status [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-80)                     status = link.get("head_extraction_status", "unknown") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-81)                     print(f"   ✅ Extraction Status: {status}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-82)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-83)             print(f"❌ Crawl failed: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-84) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-85) # Run the example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-86) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-4-87)     asyncio.run(extract_link_heads_example())`

**Expected Output:**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-1) ✅ Successfully crawled: https://docs.python.org/3/ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-2) 📄 Page title: 3.13.5 Documentation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-3) 🔗 Found 53 internal links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-4) 🌍 Found 1 external links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-5) 🧠 Links with head data extracted: 10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-7) 🏆 Top 3 Links with Full Scoring: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-9) 1. https://docs.python.org/3.15/ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-10)    Link Text: 'Python 3.15 (in development)...' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-11)    📊 Intrinsic Score: 4.17/10.0 (URL quality & context) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-12)    🎯 Contextual Score: 1.000 (BM25 relevance to query) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-13)    ⭐ Total Score: 5.917 (combined final score) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-14)    📰 Title: 3.15.0a0 Documentation... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-15)    📝 Description: The official Python documentation... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-5-16)    ✅ Extraction Status: valid`

### 2.3 Configuration Deep Dive

The `LinkPreviewConfig` class supports these options:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-1) from crawl4ai import LinkPreviewConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-3) link_preview_config = LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-4)     # BASIC SETTINGS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-5)     verbose=True,                    # Show detailed logs (recommended for learning) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-7)     # LINK FILTERING [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-8)     include_internal=True,           # Include same-domain links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-9)     include_external=True,           # Include different-domain links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-10)     max_links=50,                   # Maximum links to process (prevents overload) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-12)     # PATTERN FILTERING [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-13)     include_patterns=[               # Only process links matching these patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-14)         "*/docs/*",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-15)         "*/api/*",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-16)         "*/reference/*" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-17)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-18)     exclude_patterns=[               # Skip links matching these patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-19)         "*/login*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-20)         "*/admin*" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-21)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-23)     # PERFORMANCE SETTINGS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-24)     concurrency=10,                  # How many links to process simultaneously [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-25)     timeout=5,                      # Seconds to wait per link [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-27)     # RELEVANCE SCORING [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-28)     query="machine learning API",    # Query for BM25 contextual scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-29)     score_threshold=0.3,            # Only include links above this score [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-6-30) )`

### 2.4 Understanding the Three Score Types

Each extracted link gets three different scores:

#### 1\. **Intrinsic Score (0-10)** - URL and Content Quality

Based on URL structure, link text quality, and page context:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-1) # High intrinsic score indicators: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-2) # ✅ Clean URL structure (docs.python.org/api/reference) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-3) # ✅ Meaningful link text ("API Reference Guide") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-4) # ✅ Relevant to page context [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-5) # ✅ Not buried deep in navigation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-7) # Low intrinsic score indicators: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-8) # ❌ Random URLs (site.com/x7f9g2h) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-9) # ❌ No link text or generic text ("Click here") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-7-10) # ❌ Unrelated to page content`

#### 2\. **Contextual Score (0-1)** - BM25 Relevance to Query

Only available when you provide a `query`. Uses BM25 algorithm against head content:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-8-1) # Example: query = "machine learning tutorial" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-8-2) # High contextual score: Link to "Complete Machine Learning Guide" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-8-3) # Low contextual score: Link to "Privacy Policy"`

#### 3\. **Total Score** - Smart Combination

Intelligently combines intrinsic and contextual scores with fallbacks:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-9-1) # When both scores available: (intrinsic * 0.3) + (contextual * 0.7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-9-2) # When only intrinsic: uses intrinsic score [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-9-3) # When only contextual: uses contextual score [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-9-4) # When neither: not calculated`

### 2.5 Practical Use Cases

#### Use Case 1: Research Assistant

Find the most relevant documentation pages:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-1) async def research_assistant(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-2)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-3)         link_preview_config=LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-4)             include_internal=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-5)             include_external=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-6)             include_patterns=["*/docs/*", "*/tutorial/*", "*/guide/*"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-7)             query="machine learning neural networks", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-8)             max_links=20, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-9)             score_threshold=0.5,  # Only high-relevance links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-10)             verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-11)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-12)         score_links=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-15)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-16)         result = await crawler.arun("https://scikit-learn.org/", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-18)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-19)             # Get high-scoring links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-20)             good_links = [link for link in result.links.get("internal", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-21)                          if link.get("total_score", 0) > 0.7] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-23)             print(f"🎯 Found {len(good_links)} highly relevant links:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-24)             for link in good_links[:5]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-25)                 print(f"⭐ {link['total_score']:.3f} - {link['href']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-10-26)                 print(f"   {link.get('head_data', {}).get('title', 'No title')}")`

#### Use Case 2: Content Discovery

Find all API endpoints and references:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-1) async def api_discovery(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-2)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-3)         link_preview_config=LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-4)             include_internal=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-5)             include_patterns=["*/api/*", "*/reference/*"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-6)             exclude_patterns=["*/deprecated/*"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-7)             max_links=100, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-8)             concurrency=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-9)             verbose=False  # Clean output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-10)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-11)         score_links=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-14)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-15)         result = await crawler.arun("https://docs.example-api.com/", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-17)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-18)             api_links = result.links.get("internal", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-20)             # Group by endpoint type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-21)             endpoints = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-22)             for link in api_links: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-23)                 if link.get("head_data"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-24)                     title = link["head_data"].get("title", "") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-25)                     if "GET" in title: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-26)                         endpoints.setdefault("GET", []).append(link) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-27)                     elif "POST" in title: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-28)                         endpoints.setdefault("POST", []).append(link) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-30)             for method, links in endpoints.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-31)                 print(f"\n{method} Endpoints ({len(links)}):") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-32)                 for link in links[:3]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-11-33)                     print(f"  • {link['href']}")`

#### Use Case 3: Link Quality Analysis

Analyze website structure and content quality:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-1) async def quality_analysis(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-2)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-3)         link_preview_config=LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-4)             include_internal=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-5)             max_links=200, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-6)             concurrency=20, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-7)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-8)         score_links=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-11)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-12)         result = await crawler.arun("https://your-website.com/", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-14)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-15)             links = result.links.get("internal", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-17)             # Analyze intrinsic scores [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-18)             scores = [link.get('intrinsic_score', 0) for link in links] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-19)             avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores) if scores else 0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-21)             print(f"📊 Link Quality Analysis:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-22)             print(f"   Average intrinsic score: {avg_score:.2f}/10.0") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-23)             print(f"   High quality links (>7.0): {len([s for s in scores if s > 7.0])}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-24)             print(f"   Low quality links (<3.0): {len([s for s in scores if s < 3.0])}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-26)             # Find problematic links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-27)             bad_links = [link for link in links  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-28)                         if link.get('intrinsic_score', 0) < 2.0] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-30)             if bad_links: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-31)                 print(f"\n⚠️  Links needing attention:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-32)                 for link in bad_links[:5]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-12-33)                     print(f"   {link['href']} (score: {link.get('intrinsic_score', 0):.1f})")`

### 2.6 Performance Tips

1.  **Start Small**: Begin with `max_links: 10` to understand the feature
2.  **Use Patterns**: Filter with `include_patterns` to focus on relevant sections
3.  **Adjust Concurrency**: Higher concurrency = faster but more resource usage
4.  **Set Timeouts**: Use `timeout: 5` to prevent hanging on slow sites
5.  **Use Score Thresholds**: Filter out low-quality links with `score_threshold`

### 2.7 Troubleshooting

**No head data extracted?**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-13-1) # Check your configuration: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-13-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-13-3)     link_preview_config=LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-13-4)         verbose=True   # ← Enable to see what's happening [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-13-5)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-13-6) )`

**Scores showing as None?**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-14-1) # Make sure scoring is enabled: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-14-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-14-3)     score_links=True,  # ← Enable intrinsic scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-14-4)     link_preview_config=LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-14-5)         query="your search terms"  # ← For contextual scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-14-6)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-14-7) )`

**Process taking too long?**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-15-1) # Optimize performance: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-15-2) link_preview_config = LinkPreviewConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-15-3)     max_links=20,      # ← Reduce number [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-15-4)     concurrency=10,    # ← Increase parallelism [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-15-5)     timeout=3,         # ← Shorter timeout [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-15-6)     include_patterns=["*/important/*"]  # ← Focus on key areas [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-15-7) )`

* * *

3\. Domain Filtering
--------------------

Some websites contain hundreds of third-party or affiliate links. You can filter out certain domains at **crawl time** by configuring the crawler. The most relevant parameters in `CrawlerRunConfig` are:

*   **`exclude_external_links`**: If `True`, discard any link pointing outside the root domain.
*   **`exclude_social_media_domains`**: Provide a list of social media platforms (e.g., `["facebook.com", "twitter.com"]`) to exclude from your crawl.
*   **`exclude_social_media_links`**: If `True`, automatically skip known social platforms.
*   **`exclude_domains`**: Provide a list of custom domains you want to exclude (e.g., `["spammyads.com", "tracker.net"]`).

### 3.1 Example: Excluding External & Social Media Links

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-5)     crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-6)         exclude_external_links=True,          # No links outside primary domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-7)         exclude_social_media_links=True       # Skip recognized social media domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-11)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-12)             "https://www.example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-13)             config=crawler_cfg [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-15)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-16)             print("[OK] Crawled:", result.url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-17)             print("Internal links count:", len(result.links.get("internal", []))) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-18)             print("External links count:", len(result.links.get("external", [])))   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-19)             # Likely zero external links in this scenario [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-20)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-21)             print("[ERROR]", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-23) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-16-24)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 3.2 Example: Excluding Specific Domains

If you want to let external links in, but specifically exclude a domain (e.g., `suspiciousads.com`), do this:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-17-1) crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-17-2)     exclude_domains=["suspiciousads.com"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-17-3) )`

This approach is handy when you still want external links but need to block certain sites you consider spammy.

* * *

4\. Media Extraction
--------------------

### 4.1 Accessing `result.media`

By default, Crawl4AI collects images, audio and video URLs it finds on the page. These are stored in `result.media`, a dictionary keyed by media type (e.g., `images`, `videos`, `audio`). **Note: Tables have been moved from `result.media["tables"]` to the new `result.tables` format for better organization and direct access.**

**Basic Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-1) if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-2)     # Get images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-3)     images_info = result.media.get("images", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-4)     print(f"Found {len(images_info)} images in total.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-5)     for i, img in enumerate(images_info[:3]):  # Inspect just the first 3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-6)         print(f"[Image {i}] URL: {img['src']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-7)         print(f"           Alt text: {img.get('alt', '')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-8)         print(f"           Score: {img.get('score')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-18-9)         print(f"           Description: {img.get('desc', '')}\n")`

**Structure Example**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-1) result.media = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-2)   "images": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-3)     { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-4)       "src": "https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/.../Group%2089.svg", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-5)       "alt": "coding school for kids", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-6)       "desc": "Trial Class Degrees degrees All Degrees AI Degree Technology ...", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-7)       "score": 3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-8)       "type": "image", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-9)       "group_id": 0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-10)       "format": None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-11)       "width": None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-12)       "height": None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-13)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-14)     # ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-15)   ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-16)   "videos": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-17)     # Similar structure but with video-specific fields [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-18)   ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-19)   "audio": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-20)     # Similar structure but with audio-specific fields [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-21)   ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-19-22) }`

Depending on your Crawl4AI version or scraping strategy, these dictionaries can include fields like:

*   **`src`**: The media URL (e.g., image source)
*   **`alt`**: The alt text for images (if present)
*   **`desc`**: A snippet of nearby text or a short description (optional)
*   **`score`**: A heuristic relevance score if you’re using content-scoring features
*   **`width`**, **`height`**: If the crawler detects dimensions for the image/video
*   **`type`**: Usually `"image"`, `"video"`, or `"audio"`
*   **`group_id`**: If you’re grouping related media items, the crawler might assign an ID

With these details, you can easily filter out or focus on certain images (for instance, ignoring images with very low scores or a different domain), or gather metadata for analytics.

### 4.2 Excluding External Images

If you’re dealing with heavy pages or want to skip third-party images (advertisements, for example), you can turn on:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-20-1) crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-20-2)     exclude_external_images=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-20-3) )`

This setting attempts to discard images from outside the primary domain, keeping only those from the site you’re crawling.

### 4.3 Additional Media Config

*   **`screenshot`**: Set to `True` if you want a full-page screenshot stored as `base64` in `result.screenshot`.
*   **`pdf`**: Set to `True` if you want a PDF version of the page in `result.pdf`.
*   **`capture_mhtml`**: Set to `True` if you want an MHTML snapshot of the page in `result.mhtml`. This format preserves the entire web page with all its resources (CSS, images, scripts) in a single file, making it perfect for archiving or offline viewing.
*   **`wait_for_images`**: If `True`, attempts to wait until images are fully loaded before final extraction.

#### Example: Capturing Page as MHTML

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-5)     crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-6)         capture_mhtml=True  # Enable MHTML capture [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-7)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-10)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=crawler_cfg) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-12)         if result.success and result.mhtml: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-13)             # Save the MHTML snapshot to a file [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-14)             with open("example.mhtml", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-15)                 f.write(result.mhtml) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-16)             print("MHTML snapshot saved to example.mhtml") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-17)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-18)             print("Failed to capture MHTML:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-20) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-21-21)     asyncio.run(main())`

The MHTML format is particularly useful because: - It captures the complete page state including all resources - It can be opened in most modern browsers for offline viewing - It preserves the page exactly as it appeared during crawling - It's a single file, making it easy to store and transfer

* * *

5\. Putting It All Together: Link & Media Filtering
---------------------------------------------------

Here’s a combined example demonstrating how to filter out external links, skip certain domains, and exclude external images:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-5)     # Suppose we want to keep only internal links, remove certain domains,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-6)     # and discard external images from the final crawl data. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-7)     crawler_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-8)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-9)         exclude_domains=["spammyads.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-10)         exclude_social_media_links=True,   # skip Twitter, Facebook, etc. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-11)         exclude_external_images=True,      # keep only images from main domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-12)         wait_for_images=True,             # ensure images are loaded [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-13)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-14)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-16)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-17)         result = await crawler.arun("https://www.example.com", config=crawler_cfg) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-19)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-20)             print("[OK] Crawled:", result.url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-22)             # 1. Links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-23)             in_links = result.links.get("internal", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-24)             ext_links = result.links.get("external", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-25)             print("Internal link count:", len(in_links)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-26)             print("External link count:", len(ext_links))  # should be zero with exclude_external_links=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-28)             # 2. Images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-29)             images = result.media.get("images", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-30)             print("Images found:", len(images)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-32)             # Let's see a snippet of these images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-33)             for i, img in enumerate(images[:3]): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-34)                 print(f"  - {img['src']} (alt={img.get('alt','')}, score={img.get('score','N/A')})") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-35)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-36)             print("[ERROR] Failed to crawl. Reason:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-38) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/link-media/#__codelineno-22-39)     asyncio.run(main())`

* * *

6\. Common Pitfalls & Tips
--------------------------

1. **Conflicting Flags**:  
\- `exclude_external_links=True` but then also specifying `exclude_social_media_links=True` is typically fine, but understand that the first setting already discards _all_ external links. The second becomes somewhat redundant.  
\- `exclude_external_images=True` but want to keep some external images? Currently no partial domain-based setting for images, so you might need a custom approach or hook logic.

2. **Relevancy Scores**:  
\- If your version of Crawl4AI or your scraping strategy includes an `img["score"]`, it’s typically a heuristic based on size, position, or content analysis. Evaluate carefully if you rely on it.

3. **Performance**:  
\- Excluding certain domains or external images can speed up your crawl, especially for large, media-heavy pages.  
\- If you want a “full” link map, do _not_ exclude them. Instead, you can post-filter in your own code.

4. **Social Media Lists**:  
\- `exclude_social_media_links=True` typically references an internal list of known social domains like Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, etc. If you need to add or remove from that list, look for library settings or a local config file (depending on your version).

* * *

**That’s it for Link & Media Analysis!** You’re now equipped to filter out unwanted sites and zero in on the images and videos that matter for your project.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Quick Start - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Getting Started with Crawl4AI
=============================

Welcome to **Crawl4AI**, an open-source LLM-friendly Web Crawler & Scraper. In this tutorial, you’ll:

1.  Run your **first crawl** using minimal configuration.
2.  Generate **Markdown** output (and learn how it’s influenced by content filters).
3.  Experiment with a simple **CSS-based extraction** strategy.
4.  See a glimpse of **LLM-based extraction** (including open-source and closed-source model options).
5.  Crawl a **dynamic** page that loads content via JavaScript.

* * *

1\. Introduction
----------------

Crawl4AI provides:

*   An asynchronous crawler, **`AsyncWebCrawler`**.
*   Configurable browser and run settings via **`BrowserConfig`** and **`CrawlerRunConfig`**.
*   Automatic HTML-to-Markdown conversion via **`DefaultMarkdownGenerator`** (supports optional filters).
*   Multiple extraction strategies (LLM-based or “traditional” CSS/XPath-based).

By the end of this guide, you’ll have performed a basic crawl, generated Markdown, tried out two extraction strategies, and crawled a dynamic page that uses “Load More” buttons or JavaScript updates.

* * *

2\. Your First Crawl
--------------------

Here’s a minimal Python script that creates an **`AsyncWebCrawler`**, fetches a webpage, and prints the first 300 characters of its Markdown output:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-6)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-7)         print(result.markdown[:300])  # Print first 300 chars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-9) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-0-10)     asyncio.run(main())`

**What’s happening?** - **`AsyncWebCrawler`** launches a headless browser (Chromium by default). - It fetches `https://example.com`. - Crawl4AI automatically converts the HTML into Markdown.

You now have a simple, working crawl!

* * *

3\. Basic Configuration (Light Introduction)
--------------------------------------------

Crawl4AI’s crawler can be heavily customized using two main classes:

1. **`BrowserConfig`**: Controls browser behavior (headless or full UI, user agent, JavaScript toggles, etc.).  
2. **`CrawlerRunConfig`**: Controls how each crawl runs (caching, extraction, timeouts, hooking, etc.).

Below is an example with minimal usage:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-5)     browser_conf = BrowserConfig(headless=True)  # or False to see the browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-6)     run_conf = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-7)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_conf) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-11)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-12)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-13)             config=run_conf [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-15)         print(result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-17) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-1-18)     asyncio.run(main())`

> IMPORTANT: By default cache mode is set to `CacheMode.BYPASS` to have fresh content. Set `CacheMode.ENABLED` to enable caching.

We’ll explore more advanced config in later tutorials (like enabling proxies, PDF output, multi-tab sessions, etc.). For now, just note how you pass these objects to manage crawling.

* * *

4\. Generating Markdown Output
------------------------------

By default, Crawl4AI automatically generates Markdown from each crawled page. However, the exact output depends on whether you specify a **markdown generator** or **content filter**.

*   **`result.markdown`**:  
    The direct HTML-to-Markdown conversion.
*   **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`**:  
    The same content after applying any configured **content filter** (e.g., `PruningContentFilter`).

### Example: Using a Filter with `DefaultMarkdownGenerator`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-3) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-5) md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-6)     content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.4, threshold_type="fixed") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-9) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-10)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-11)     markdown_generator=md_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-12) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-14) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-15)     result = await crawler.arun("https://news.ycombinator.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-16)     print("Raw Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-2-17)     print("Fit Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.fit_markdown))`

**Note**: If you do **not** specify a content filter or markdown generator, you’ll typically see only the raw Markdown. `PruningContentFilter` may adds around `50ms` in processing time. We’ll dive deeper into these strategies in a dedicated **Markdown Generation** tutorial.

* * *

5\. Simple Data Extraction (CSS-based)
--------------------------------------

Crawl4AI can also extract structured data (JSON) using CSS or XPath selectors. Below is a minimal CSS-based example:

> **New!** Crawl4AI now provides a powerful utility to automatically generate extraction schemas using LLM. This is a one-time cost that gives you a reusable schema for fast, LLM-free extractions:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-4) # Generate a schema (one-time cost) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-5) html = "<div class='product'><h2>Gaming Laptop</h2><span class='price'>$999.99</span></div>" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-7) # Using OpenAI (requires API token) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-8) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-9)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-10)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o",api_token="your-openai-token")  # Required for OpenAI [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-13) # Or using Ollama (open source, no token needed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-14) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-15)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-16)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="ollama/llama3.3", api_token=None)  # Not needed for Ollama [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-17) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-19) # Use the schema for fast, repeated extractions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-3-20) strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema)`

For a complete guide on schema generation and advanced usage, see [No-LLM Extraction Strategies](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/)
.

Here's a basic extraction example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-8)         "name": "Example Items", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-9)         "baseSelector": "div.item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-11)             {"name": "title", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-12)             {"name": "link", "selector": "a", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-13)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-14)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-16)     raw_html = "<div class='item'><h2>Item 1</h2><a href='https://example.com/item1'>Link 1</a></div>" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-18)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-19)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-20)             url="raw://" + raw_html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-21)             config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-22)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-23)                 extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-24)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-25)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-26)         # The JSON output is stored in 'extracted_content' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-27)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-28)         print(data) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-30) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-4-31)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Why is this helpful?** - Great for repetitive page structures (e.g., item listings, articles). - No AI usage or costs. - The crawler returns a JSON string you can parse or store.

> Tips: You can pass raw HTML to the crawler instead of a URL. To do so, prefix the HTML with `raw://`.

* * *

6\. Simple Data Extraction (LLM-based)
--------------------------------------

For more complex or irregular pages, a language model can parse text intelligently into a structure you define. Crawl4AI supports **open-source** or **closed-source** providers:

*   **Open-Source Models** (e.g., `ollama/llama3.3`, `no_token`)
*   **OpenAI Models** (e.g., `openai/gpt-4`, requires `api_token`)
*   Or any provider supported by the underlying library

Below is an example using **open-source** style (no token) and closed-source:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-1) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-4) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-5) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-6) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-8) class OpenAIModelFee(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-9)     model_name: str = Field(..., description="Name of the OpenAI model.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-10)     input_fee: str = Field(..., description="Fee for input token for the OpenAI model.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-11)     output_fee: str = Field( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-12)         ..., description="Fee for output token for the OpenAI model." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-15) async def extract_structured_data_using_llm( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-16)     provider: str, api_token: str = None, extra_headers: Dict[str, str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-17) ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-18)     print(f"\n--- Extracting Structured Data with {provider} ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-20)     if api_token is None and provider != "ollama": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-21)         print(f"API token is required for {provider}. Skipping this example.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-22)         return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-24)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-26)     extra_args = {"temperature": 0, "top_p": 0.9, "max_tokens": 2000} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-27)     if extra_headers: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-28)         extra_args["extra_headers"] = extra_headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-30)     crawler_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-31)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-32)         word_count_threshold=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-33)         page_timeout=80000, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-34)         extraction_strategy=LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-35)             llm_config = LLMConfig(provider=provider,api_token=api_token), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-36)             schema=OpenAIModelFee.model_json_schema(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-37)             extraction_type="schema", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-38)             instruction="""From the crawled content, extract all mentioned model names along with their fees for input and output tokens.  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-39)             Do not miss any models in the entire content.""", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-40)             extra_args=extra_args, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-41)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-42)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-43) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-44)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-45)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-46)             url="https://openai.com/api/pricing/", config=crawler_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-47)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-48)         print(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-50) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-51) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-52)     asyncio.run( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-53)         extract_structured_data_using_llm( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-54)             provider="openai/gpt-4o", api_token=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-55)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-5-56)     )`

**What’s happening?** - We define a Pydantic schema (`PricingInfo`) describing the fields we want. - The LLM extraction strategy uses that schema and your instructions to transform raw text into structured JSON. - Depending on the **provider** and **api\_token**, you can use local models or a remote API.

* * *

7\. Adaptive Crawling (New!)
----------------------------

Crawl4AI now includes intelligent adaptive crawling that automatically determines when sufficient information has been gathered. Here's a quick example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, AdaptiveCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-4) async def adaptive_example(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-6)         adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-8)         # Start adaptive crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-9)         result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-10)             start_url="https://docs.python.org/3/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-11)             query="async context managers" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-12)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-14)         # View results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-15)         adaptive.print_stats() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-16)         print(f"Crawled {len(result.crawled_urls)} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-17)         print(f"Achieved {adaptive.confidence:.0%} confidence") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-19) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-6-20)     asyncio.run(adaptive_example())`

**What's special about adaptive crawling?** - **Automatic stopping**: Stops when sufficient information is gathered - **Intelligent link selection**: Follows only relevant links - **Confidence scoring**: Know how complete your information is

[Learn more about Adaptive Crawling →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/adaptive-crawling/)

* * *

8\. Multi-URL Concurrency (Preview)
-----------------------------------

If you need to crawl multiple URLs in **parallel**, you can use `arun_many()`. By default, Crawl4AI employs a **MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher**, automatically adjusting concurrency based on system resources. Here’s a quick glimpse:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-4) async def quick_parallel_example(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-5)     urls = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-6)         "https://example.com/page1", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-7)         "https://example.com/page2", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-8)         "https://example.com/page3" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-9)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-11)     run_conf = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-12)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-13)         stream=True  # Enable streaming mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-14)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-16)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-17)         # Stream results as they complete [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-18)         async for result in await crawler.arun_many(urls, config=run_conf): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-19)             if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-20)                 print(f"[OK] {result.url}, length: {len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-21)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-22)                 print(f"[ERROR] {result.url} => {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-24)         # Or get all results at once (default behavior) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-25)         run_conf = run_conf.clone(stream=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-26)         results = await crawler.arun_many(urls, config=run_conf) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-27)         for res in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-28)             if res.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-29)                 print(f"[OK] {res.url}, length: {len(res.markdown.raw_markdown)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-30)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-31)                 print(f"[ERROR] {res.url} => {res.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-33) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-7-34)     asyncio.run(quick_parallel_example())`

The example above shows two ways to handle multiple URLs: 1. **Streaming mode** (`stream=True`): Process results as they become available using `async for` 2. **Batch mode** (`stream=False`): Wait for all results to complete

For more advanced concurrency (e.g., a **semaphore-based** approach, **adaptive memory usage throttling**, or customized rate limiting), see [Advanced Multi-URL Crawling](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling/)
.

* * *

8\. Dynamic Content Example
---------------------------

Some sites require multiple “page clicks” or dynamic JavaScript updates. Below is an example showing how to **click** a “Next Page” button and wait for new commits to load on GitHub, using **`BrowserConfig`** and **`CrawlerRunConfig`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-3) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-5) async def extract_structured_data_using_css_extractor(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-6)     print("\n--- Using JsonCssExtractionStrategy for Fast Structured Output ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-8)         "name": "KidoCode Courses", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-9)         "baseSelector": "section.charge-methodology .w-tab-content > div", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-11)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-12)                 "name": "section_title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-13)                 "selector": "h3.heading-50", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-14)                 "type": "text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-15)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-16)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-17)                 "name": "section_description", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-18)                 "selector": ".charge-content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-19)                 "type": "text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-20)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-21)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-22)                 "name": "course_name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-23)                 "selector": ".text-block-93", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-24)                 "type": "text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-25)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-26)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-27)                 "name": "course_description", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-28)                 "selector": ".course-content-text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-29)                 "type": "text", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-30)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-31)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-32)                 "name": "course_icon", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-33)                 "selector": ".image-92", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-34)                 "type": "attribute", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-35)                 "attribute": "src", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-36)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart/#__codelineno-8-37)         ],
