`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-7-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-7-2)     check_robots_txt=True,  # Enable robots.txt compliance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-7-3)     user_agent="MyBot/1.0"  # Identify your crawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-7-4) )`

3\. **LLMConfig** - Setting up LLM providers
============================================

LLMConfig is useful to pass LLM provider config to strategies and functions that rely on LLMs to do extraction, filtering, schema generation etc. Currently it can be used in the following -

1.  LLMExtractionStrategy
2.  LLMContentFilter
3.  JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate\_schema
4.  JsonXPathExtractionStrategy.generate\_schema

3.1 Parameters
--------------

| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **`provider`** | `"ollama/llama3","groq/llama3-70b-8192","groq/llama3-8b-8192", "openai/gpt-4o-mini" ,"openai/gpt-4o","openai/o1-mini","openai/o1-preview","openai/o3-mini","openai/o3-mini-high","anthropic/claude-3-haiku-20240307","anthropic/claude-3-opus-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-sonnet-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620","gemini/gemini-pro","gemini/gemini-1.5-pro","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-exp","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-lite-preview-02-05","deepseek/deepseek-chat"`  <br>_(default: `"openai/gpt-4o-mini"`)_ | Which LLM provider to use. |
| **`api_token`** | 1.Optional. When not provided explicitly, api\_token will be read from environment variables based on provider. For example: If a gemini model is passed as provider then,`"GEMINI_API_KEY"` will be read from environment variables  <br>2\. API token of LLM provider  <br>eg: `api_token = "gsk_1ClHGGJ7Lpn4WGybR7vNWGdyb3FY7zXEw3SCiy0BAVM9lL8CQv"`  <br>3\. Environment variable - use with prefix "env:"  <br>eg:`api_token = "env: GROQ_API_KEY"` | API token to use for the given provider |
| **`base_url`** | Optional. Custom API endpoint | If your provider has a custom endpoint |
| **`backoff_base_delay`** | Optional. `int` _(default: `2`)_ | Seconds to wait before the first retry when the provider throttles a request. |
| **`backoff_max_attempts`** | Optional. `int` _(default: `3`)_ | Total tries (initial call + retries) before surfacing an error. |
| **`backoff_exponential_factor`** | Optional. `int` _(default: `2`)_ | Multiplier that increases the wait time for each retry (`delay = base_delay * factor^attempt`). |

3.2 Example Usage
-----------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-8-1) llm_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-8-2)     provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-8-3)     api_token=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-8-4)     backoff_base_delay=1, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-8-5)     backoff_max_attempts=5, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-8-6)     backoff_exponential_factor=3, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-8-7) )`

4\. Putting It All Together
---------------------------

*   **Use** `BrowserConfig` for **global** browser settings: engine, headless, proxy, user agent.
*   **Use** `CrawlerRunConfig` for each crawl’s **context**: how to filter content, handle caching, wait for dynamic elements, or run JS.
*   **Pass** both configs to `AsyncWebCrawler` (the `BrowserConfig`) and then to `arun()` (the `CrawlerRunConfig`).
*   **Use** `LLMConfig` for LLM provider configurations that can be used across all extraction, filtering, schema generation tasks. Can be used in - `LLMExtractionStrategy`, `LLMContentFilter`, `JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema` & `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy.generate_schema`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-1) # Create a modified copy with the clone() method [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-2) stream_cfg = run_cfg.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-3)     stream=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-4)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-7) # Or set project-wide defaults once at startup [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-8) BrowserConfig.set_defaults(headless=True, text_mode=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters/#__codelineno-9-9) CrawlerRunConfig.set_defaults(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS)`

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# LLM-Free Strategies - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Extracting JSON (No LLM)
========================

One of Crawl4AI's **most powerful** features is extracting **structured JSON** from websites **without** relying on large language models. Crawl4AI offers several strategies for LLM-free extraction:

1.  **Schema-based extraction** with CSS or XPath selectors via `JsonCssExtractionStrategy` and `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`
2.  **Regular expression extraction** with `RegexExtractionStrategy` for fast pattern matching

These approaches let you extract data instantly—even from complex or nested HTML structures—without the cost, latency, or environmental impact of an LLM.

**Why avoid LLM for basic extractions?**

1.  **Faster & Cheaper**: No API calls or GPU overhead.
2.  **Lower Carbon Footprint**: LLM inference can be energy-intensive. Pattern-based extraction is practically carbon-free.
3.  **Precise & Repeatable**: CSS/XPath selectors and regex patterns do exactly what you specify. LLM outputs can vary or hallucinate.
4.  **Scales Readily**: For thousands of pages, pattern-based extraction runs quickly and in parallel.

Below, we'll explore how to craft these schemas and use them with **JsonCssExtractionStrategy** (or **JsonXPathExtractionStrategy** if you prefer XPath). We'll also highlight advanced features like **nested fields** and **base element attributes**.

* * *

1\. Intro to Schema-Based Extraction
------------------------------------

A schema defines:

1.  A **base selector** that identifies each "container" element on the page (e.g., a product row, a blog post card).
2.  **Fields** describing which CSS/XPath selectors to use for each piece of data you want to capture (text, attribute, HTML block, etc.).
3.  **Nested** or **list** types for repeated or hierarchical structures.

For example, if you have a list of products, each one might have a name, price, reviews, and "related products." This approach is faster and more reliable than an LLM for consistent, structured pages.

* * *

2\. Simple Example: Crypto Prices
---------------------------------

Let's begin with a **simple** schema-based extraction using the `JsonCssExtractionStrategy`. Below is a snippet that extracts cryptocurrency prices from a site (similar to the legacy Coinbase example). Notice we **don't** call any LLM:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-6) async def extract_crypto_prices(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-7)     # 1. Define a simple extraction schema [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-8)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-9)         "name": "Crypto Prices", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-10)         "baseSelector": "div.crypto-row",    # Repeated elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-11)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-12)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-13)                 "name": "coin_name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-14)                 "selector": "h2.coin-name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-15)                 "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-16)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-17)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-18)                 "name": "price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-19)                 "selector": "span.coin-price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-20)                 "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-21)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-22)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-23)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-25)     # 2. Create the extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-26)     extraction_strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema, verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-28)     # 3. Set up your crawler config (if needed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-29)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-30)         # e.g., pass js_code or wait_for if the page is dynamic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-31)         # wait_for="css:.crypto-row:nth-child(20)" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-32)         cache_mode = CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-33)         extraction_strategy=extraction_strategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-34)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-36)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(verbose=True) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-37)         # 4. Run the crawl and extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-38)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-39)             url="https://example.com/crypto-prices", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-41)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-42)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-43) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-44)         if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-45)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-46)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-48)         # 5. Parse the extracted JSON [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-49)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-50)         print(f"Extracted {len(data)} coin entries") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-51)         print(json.dumps(data[0], indent=2) if data else "No data found") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-52) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-0-53) asyncio.run(extract_crypto_prices())`

**Highlights**:

*   **`baseSelector`**: Tells us where each "item" (crypto row) is.
*   **`fields`**: Two fields (`coin_name`, `price`) using simple CSS selectors.
*   Each field defines a **`type`** (e.g., `text`, `attribute`, `html`, `regex`, etc.).
*   Optional keys: **`transform`**, **`default`**, **`attribute`**, **`pattern`**, and **`source`** (for sibling data — see [Extracting Sibling Data](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#sibling-data)
    ).

No LLM is needed, and the performance is **near-instant** for hundreds or thousands of items.

* * *

### **XPath Example with `raw://` HTML**

Below is a short example demonstrating **XPath** extraction plus the **`raw://`** scheme. We'll pass a **dummy HTML** directly (no network request) and define the extraction strategy in `CrawlerRunConfig`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-4) from crawl4ai import JsonXPathExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-6) async def extract_crypto_prices_xpath(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-7)     # 1. Minimal dummy HTML with some repeating rows [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-8)     dummy_html = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-9)     <html> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-10)       <body> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-11)         <div class='crypto-row'> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-12)           <h2 class='coin-name'>Bitcoin</h2> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-13)           <span class='coin-price'>$28,000</span> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-14)         </div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-15)         <div class='crypto-row'> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-16)           <h2 class='coin-name'>Ethereum</h2> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-17)           <span class='coin-price'>$1,800</span> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-18)         </div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-19)       </body> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-20)     </html> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-21)     """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-23)     # 2. Define the JSON schema (XPath version) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-24)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-25)         "name": "Crypto Prices via XPath", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-26)         "baseSelector": "//div[@class='crypto-row']", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-27)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-28)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-29)                 "name": "coin_name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-30)                 "selector": ".//h2[@class='coin-name']", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-31)                 "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-32)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-33)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-34)                 "name": "price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-35)                 "selector": ".//span[@class='coin-price']", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-36)                 "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-37)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-38)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-39)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-41)     # 3. Place the strategy in the CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-42)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-43)         extraction_strategy=JsonXPathExtractionStrategy(schema, verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-44)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-45) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-46)     # 4. Use raw:// scheme to pass dummy_html directly [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-47)     raw_url = f"raw://{dummy_html}" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-49)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(verbose=True) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-50)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-51)             url=raw_url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-52)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-53)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-54) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-55)         if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-56)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-57)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-58) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-59)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-60)         print(f"Extracted {len(data)} coin rows") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-61)         if data: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-62)             print("First item:", data[0]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-63) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-1-64) asyncio.run(extract_crypto_prices_xpath())`

**Key Points**:

1.  **`JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`** is used instead of `JsonCssExtractionStrategy`.
2.  **`baseSelector`** and each field's `"selector"` use **XPath** instead of CSS.
3.  **`raw://`** lets us pass `dummy_html` with no real network request—handy for local testing.
4.  Everything (including the extraction strategy) is in **`CrawlerRunConfig`**.

That's how you keep the config self-contained, illustrate **XPath** usage, and demonstrate the **raw** scheme for direct HTML input—all while avoiding the old approach of passing `extraction_strategy` directly to `arun()`.

* * *

3\. Advanced Schema & Nested Structures
---------------------------------------

Real sites often have **nested** or repeated data—like categories containing products, which themselves have a list of reviews or features. For that, we can define **nested** or **list** (and even **nested\_list**) fields.

### Sample E-Commerce HTML

We have a **sample e-commerce** HTML file on GitHub (example):

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-2-1) https://gist.githubusercontent.com/githubusercontent/2d7b8ba3cd8ab6cf3c8da771ddb36878/raw/1ae2f90c6861ce7dd84cc50d3df9920dee5e1fd2/sample_ecommerce.html`

This snippet includes categories, products, features, reviews, and related items. Let's see how to define a schema that fully captures that structure **without LLM**.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-1) schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-2)     "name": "E-commerce Product Catalog", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-3)     "baseSelector": "div.category", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-4)     # (1) We can define optional baseFields if we want to extract attributes  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-5)     # from the category container [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-6)     "baseFields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-7)         {"name": "data_cat_id", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "data-cat-id"},  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-8)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-9)     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-10)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-11)             "name": "category_name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-12)             "selector": "h2.category-name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-13)             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-14)         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-15)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-16)             "name": "products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-17)             "selector": "div.product", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-18)             "type": "nested_list",    # repeated sub-objects [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-19)             "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-20)                 { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-21)                     "name": "name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-22)                     "selector": "h3.product-name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-23)                     "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-24)                 }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-25)                 { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-26)                     "name": "price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-27)                     "selector": "p.product-price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-28)                     "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-29)                 }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-30)                 { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-31)                     "name": "details", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-32)                     "selector": "div.product-details", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-33)                     "type": "nested",  # single sub-object [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-34)                     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-35)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-36)                             "name": "brand", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-37)                             "selector": "span.brand", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-38)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-39)                         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-40)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-41)                             "name": "model", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-42)                             "selector": "span.model", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-43)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-44)                         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-45)                     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-46)                 }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-47)                 { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-48)                     "name": "features", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-49)                     "selector": "ul.product-features li", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-50)                     "type": "list", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-51)                     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-52)                         {"name": "feature", "type": "text"}  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-53)                     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-54)                 }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-55)                 { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-56)                     "name": "reviews", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-57)                     "selector": "div.review", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-58)                     "type": "nested_list", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-59)                     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-60)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-61)                             "name": "reviewer",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-62)                             "selector": "span.reviewer",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-63)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-64)                         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-65)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-66)                             "name": "rating",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-67)                             "selector": "span.rating",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-68)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-69)                         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-70)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-71)                             "name": "comment",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-72)                             "selector": "p.review-text",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-73)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-74)                         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-75)                     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-76)                 }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-77)                 { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-78)                     "name": "related_products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-79)                     "selector": "ul.related-products li", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-80)                     "type": "list", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-81)                     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-82)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-83)                             "name": "name",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-84)                             "selector": "span.related-name",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-85)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-86)                         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-87)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-88)                             "name": "price",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-89)                             "selector": "span.related-price",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-90)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-91)                         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-92)                     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-93)                 } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-94)             ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-95)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-96)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-3-97) }`

Key Takeaways:

*   **Nested vs. List**:
*   **`type: "nested"`** means a **single** sub-object (like `details`).
*   **`type: "list"`** means multiple items that are **simple** dictionaries or single text fields.
*   **`type: "nested_list"`** means repeated **complex** objects (like `products` or `reviews`).
*   **Base Fields**: We can extract **attributes** from the container element via `"baseFields"`. For instance, `"data_cat_id"` might be `data-cat-id="elect123"`.
*   **Transforms**: We can also define a `transform` if we want to lower/upper case, strip whitespace, or even run a custom function.

### Running the Extraction

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-6) ecommerce_schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-7)     # ... the advanced schema from above ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-8) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-10) async def extract_ecommerce_data(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-11)     strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(ecommerce_schema, verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-13)     config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-15)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(verbose=True) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-16)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-17)             url="https://gist.githubusercontent.com/githubusercontent/2d7b8ba3cd8ab6cf3c8da771ddb36878/raw/1ae2f90c6861ce7dd84cc50d3df9920dee5e1fd2/sample_ecommerce.html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-18)             extraction_strategy=strategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-19)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-20)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-22)         if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-23)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-24)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-26)         # Parse the JSON output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-27)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-28)         print(json.dumps(data, indent=2) if data else "No data found.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-4-30) asyncio.run(extract_ecommerce_data())`

If all goes well, you get a **structured** JSON array with each "category," containing an array of `products`. Each product includes `details`, `features`, `reviews`, etc. All of that **without** an LLM.

* * *

4\. RegexExtractionStrategy - Fast Pattern-Based Extraction
-----------------------------------------------------------

Crawl4AI now offers a powerful new zero-LLM extraction strategy: `RegexExtractionStrategy`. This strategy provides lightning-fast extraction of common data types like emails, phone numbers, URLs, dates, and more using pre-compiled regular expressions.

### Key Features

*   **Zero LLM Dependency**: Extracts data without any AI model calls
*   **Blazing Fast**: Uses pre-compiled regex patterns for maximum performance
*   **Built-in Patterns**: Includes ready-to-use patterns for common data types
*   **Custom Patterns**: Add your own regex patterns for domain-specific extraction
*   **LLM-Assisted Pattern Generation**: Optionally use an LLM once to generate optimized patterns, then reuse them without further LLM calls

### Simple Example: Extracting Common Entities

The easiest way to start is by using the built-in pattern catalog:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-3) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-4)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-5)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-6)     RegexExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-9) async def extract_with_regex(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-10)     # Create a strategy using built-in patterns for URLs and currencies [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-11)     strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-12)         pattern = RegexExtractionStrategy.Url | RegexExtractionStrategy.Currency [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-15)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-18)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-19)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-20)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-21)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-23)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-24)             data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-25)             for item in data[:5]:  # Show first 5 matches [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-26)                 print(f"{item['label']}: {item['value']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-27)             print(f"Total matches: {len(data)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-5-29) asyncio.run(extract_with_regex())`

### Available Built-in Patterns

`RegexExtractionStrategy` provides these common patterns as IntFlag attributes for easy combining:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-1) # Use individual patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-2) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(pattern=RegexExtractionStrategy.Email) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-4) # Combine multiple patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-5) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-6)     pattern = ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-7)         RegexExtractionStrategy.Email |  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-8)         RegexExtractionStrategy.PhoneUS |  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-9)         RegexExtractionStrategy.Url [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-13) # Use all available patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-6-14) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(pattern=RegexExtractionStrategy.All)`

Available patterns include: - `Email` - Email addresses - `PhoneIntl` - International phone numbers - `PhoneUS` - US-format phone numbers - `Url` - HTTP/HTTPS URLs - `IPv4` - IPv4 addresses - `IPv6` - IPv6 addresses - `Uuid` - UUIDs - `Currency` - Currency values (USD, EUR, etc.) - `Percentage` - Percentage values - `Number` - Numeric values - `DateIso` - ISO format dates - `DateUS` - US format dates - `Time24h` - 24-hour format times - `PostalUS` - US postal codes - `PostalUK` - UK postal codes - `HexColor` - HTML hex color codes - `TwitterHandle` - Twitter handles - `Hashtag` - Hashtags - `MacAddr` - MAC addresses - `Iban` - International bank account numbers - `CreditCard` - Credit card numbers

### Custom Pattern Example

For more targeted extraction, you can provide custom patterns:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-3) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-4)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-5)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-6)     RegexExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-9) async def extract_prices(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-10)     # Define a custom pattern for US Dollar prices [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-11)     price_pattern = {"usd_price": r"\$\s?\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*(?:\.\d{2})?"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-13)     # Create strategy with custom pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-14)     strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(custom=price_pattern) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-15)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-18)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-19)             url="https://www.example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-20)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-21)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-23)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-24)             data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-25)             for item in data: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-26)                 print(f"Found price: {item['value']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-7-28) asyncio.run(extract_prices())`

### LLM-Assisted Pattern Generation

For complex or site-specific patterns, you can use an LLM once to generate an optimized pattern, then save and reuse it without further LLM calls:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-3) from pathlib import Path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-4) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-5)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-6)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-7)     RegexExtractionStrategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-8)     LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-11) async def extract_with_generated_pattern(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-12)     cache_dir = Path("./pattern_cache") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-13)     cache_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-14)     pattern_file = cache_dir / "price_pattern.json" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-16)     # 1. Generate or load pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-17)     if pattern_file.exists(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-18)         pattern = json.load(pattern_file.open()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-19)         print(f"Using cached pattern: {pattern}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-20)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-21)         print("Generating pattern via LLM...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-23)         # Configure LLM [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-24)         llm_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-25)             provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-26)             api_token="env:OPENAI_API_KEY", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-27)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-29)         # Get sample HTML for context [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-30)         async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-31)             result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/products") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-32)             html = result.fit_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-34)         # Generate pattern (one-time LLM usage) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-35)         pattern = RegexExtractionStrategy.generate_pattern( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-36)             label="price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-37)             html=html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-38)             query="Product prices in USD format", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-39)             llm_config=llm_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-40)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-42)         # Cache pattern for future use [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-43)         json.dump(pattern, pattern_file.open("w"), indent=2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-44) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-45)     # 2. Use pattern for extraction (no LLM calls) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-46)     strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(custom=pattern) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-47)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-49)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-50)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-51)             url="https://example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-52)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-53)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-54) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-55)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-56)             data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-57)             for item in data[:10]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-58)                 print(f"Extracted: {item['value']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-59)             print(f"Total matches: {len(data)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-60) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-8-61) asyncio.run(extract_with_generated_pattern())`

This pattern allows you to: 1. Use an LLM once to generate a highly optimized regex for your specific site 2. Save the pattern to disk for reuse 3. Extract data using only regex (no further LLM calls) in production

### Extraction Results Format

The `RegexExtractionStrategy` returns results in a consistent format:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-1) [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-2)   { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-3)     "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-4)     "label": "email", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-5)     "value": "contact@example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-6)     "span": [145, 163] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-7)   }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-8)   { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-9)     "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-10)     "label": "url", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-11)     "value": "https://support.example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-12)     "span": [210, 235] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-13)   } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-9-14) ]`

Each match includes: - `url`: The source URL - `label`: The pattern name that matched (e.g., "email", "phone\_us") - `value`: The extracted text - `span`: The start and end positions in the source content

* * *

5\. Why "No LLM" Is Often Better
--------------------------------

1.  **Zero Hallucination**: Pattern-based extraction doesn't guess text. It either finds it or not.
2.  **Guaranteed Structure**: The same schema or regex yields consistent JSON across many pages, so your downstream pipeline can rely on stable keys.
3.  **Speed**: LLM-based extraction can be 10–1000x slower for large-scale crawling.
4.  **Scalable**: Adding or updating a field is a matter of adjusting the schema or regex, not re-tuning a model.

**When might you consider an LLM?** Possibly if the site is extremely unstructured or you want AI summarization. But always try a schema or regex approach first for repeated or consistent data patterns.

* * *

6\. Base Element Attributes & Additional Fields
-----------------------------------------------

It's easy to **extract attributes** (like `href`, `src`, or `data-xxx`) from your base or nested elements using:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-10-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-10-2)   "name": "href", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-10-3)   "type": "attribute", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-10-4)   "attribute": "href", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-10-5)   "default": null [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-10-6) }`

You can define them in **`baseFields`** (extracted from the main container element) or in each field's sub-lists. This is especially helpful if you need an item's link or ID stored in the parent `<div>`.

* * *

7\. Putting It All Together: Larger Example
-------------------------------------------

Consider a blog site. We have a schema that extracts the **URL** from each post card (via `baseFields` with an `"attribute": "href"`), plus the title, date, summary, and author:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-1) schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-2)   "name": "Blog Posts", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-3)   "baseSelector": "a.blog-post-card", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-4)   "baseFields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-5)     {"name": "post_url", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-6)   ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-7)   "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-8)     {"name": "title", "selector": "h2.post-title", "type": "text", "default": "No Title"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-9)     {"name": "date", "selector": "time.post-date", "type": "text", "default": ""}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-10)     {"name": "summary", "selector": "p.post-summary", "type": "text", "default": ""}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-11)     {"name": "author", "selector": "span.post-author", "type": "text", "default": ""} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-12)   ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-11-13) }`

Then run with `JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema)` to get an array of blog post objects, each with `"post_url"`, `"title"`, `"date"`, `"summary"`, `"author"`.

* * *

8\. Extracting Sibling Data with `source`
-----------------------------------------

Some websites split a single logical item across **sibling elements** rather than nesting everything inside one container. A classic example is Hacker News, where each submission spans two adjacent `<tr>` rows:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-1) <tr class="athing submission">  <!-- rank, title, url --> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-2)   <td><span class="rank">1.</span></td> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-3)   <td><span class="titleline"><a href="https://example.com">Example Title</a></span></td> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-4) </tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-5) <tr>                             <!-- score, author, comments (sibling!) --> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-6)   <td class="subtext"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-7)     <span class="score">100 points</span> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-8)     <a class="hnuser">johndoe</a> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-9)   </td> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-12-10) </tr>`

Normally, field selectors only search **descendants** of the base element — siblings are unreachable. The `source` field key solves this by navigating to a sibling element before running the selector.

### Syntax

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-13-1) "source": "+ <selector>"`

*   **`+ tr`** — next sibling `<tr>`
*   **`+ div.details`** — next sibling `<div>` with class `details`
*   **`+ .subtext`** — next sibling with class `subtext`

### Example: Hacker News

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-1) schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-2)     "name": "HN Submissions", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-3)     "baseSelector": "tr.athing.submission", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-4)     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-5)         {"name": "rank", "selector": "span.rank", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-6)         {"name": "title", "selector": "span.titleline a", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-7)         {"name": "url", "selector": "span.titleline a", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-8)         {"name": "score", "selector": "span.score", "type": "text", "source": "+ tr"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-9)         {"name": "author", "selector": "a.hnuser", "type": "text", "source": "+ tr"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-10)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-11) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-14-13) strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema)`

The `score` and `author` fields first navigate to the next sibling `<tr>`, then run their selectors inside that element. Fields without `source` work as before — searching descendants of the base element.

`source` works with all field types (`text`, `attribute`, `nested`, `list`, etc.) and with both `JsonCssExtractionStrategy` and `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`. If the sibling isn't found, the field returns its `default` value.

* * *

9\. Tips & Best Practices
-------------------------

1.  **Inspect the DOM** in Chrome DevTools or Firefox's Inspector to find stable selectors.
2.  **Start Simple**: Verify you can extract a single field. Then add complexity like nested objects or lists.
3.  **Test** your schema on partial HTML or a test page before a big crawl.
4.  **Combine with JS Execution** if the site loads content dynamically. You can pass `js_code` or `wait_for` in `CrawlerRunConfig`.
5.  **Look at Logs** when `verbose=True`: if your selectors are off or your schema is malformed, it'll often show warnings.
6.  **Use baseFields** if you need attributes from the container element (e.g., `href`, `data-id`), especially for the "parent" item.
7.  **Performance**: For large pages, make sure your selectors are as narrow as possible.
8.  **Consider Using Regex First**: For simple data types like emails, URLs, and dates, `RegexExtractionStrategy` is often the fastest approach.

* * *

10\. Schema Generation Utility
------------------------------

While manually crafting schemas is powerful and precise, Crawl4AI now offers a convenient utility to **automatically generate** extraction schemas using LLM. This is particularly useful when:

1.  You're dealing with a new website structure and want a quick starting point
2.  You need to extract complex nested data structures
3.  You want to avoid the learning curve of CSS/XPath selector syntax

### Using the Schema Generator

The schema generator is available as a static method on both `JsonCssExtractionStrategy` and `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`. You can choose between OpenAI's GPT-4 or the open-source Ollama for schema generation:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy, JsonXPathExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-2) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-4) # Sample HTML with product information [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-5) html = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-6) <div class="product-card"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-7)     <h2 class="title">Gaming Laptop</h2> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-8)     <div class="price">$999.99</div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-9)     <div class="specs"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-10)         <ul> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-11)             <li>16GB RAM</li> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-12)             <li>1TB SSD</li> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-13)         </ul> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-14)     </div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-15) </div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-16) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-18) # Option 1: Using OpenAI (requires API token) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-19) css_schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-20)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-21)     schema_type="css", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-22)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o",api_token="your-openai-token") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-23) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-25) # Option 2: Using Ollama (open source, no token needed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-26) xpath_schema = JsonXPathExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-27)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-28)     schema_type="xpath", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-29)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="ollama/llama3.3", api_token=None)  # Not needed for Ollama [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-30) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-32) # Use the generated schema for fast, repeated extractions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-15-33) strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(css_schema)`

### Schema Validation

By default, `generate_schema` **validates** the generated schema against the HTML to ensure that it actually extracts the data you expect. If the schema doesn't produce results, it automatically refines the selectors before returning.

You can control this with the `validate` parameter:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-1) # Default: validated (recommended) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-2) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-3)     url="https://news.ycombinator.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-4)     query="Extract each story: title, url, score, author", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-7) # Skip validation if you want raw LLM output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-8) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-9)     url="https://news.ycombinator.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-10)     query="Extract each story: title, url, score, author", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-11)     validate=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-16-12) )`

The generator also understands sibling layouts — for sites like Hacker News where data is split across sibling elements, it will automatically use the [`source` field](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#sibling-data)
 to reach sibling data.

### Token Usage Tracking

`generate_schema` may make multiple LLM calls internally (field inference, schema generation, validation retries). To track the total token consumption across all of these calls, pass a `TokenUsage` accumulator:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-2) from crawl4ai.models import TokenUsage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-4) usage = TokenUsage() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-6) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-7)     url="https://news.ycombinator.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-8)     query="Extract each story: title, url, score, author", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-9)     usage=usage, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-12) print(f"Prompt tokens:     {usage.prompt_tokens}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-13) print(f"Completion tokens: {usage.completion_tokens}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-17-14) print(f"Total tokens:      {usage.total_tokens}")`

The `usage` parameter is optional — omitting it changes nothing (fully backward-compatible). You can also reuse the same accumulator across multiple calls to get a grand total:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-18-1) usage = TokenUsage() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-18-2) schema1 = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema(url=url1, query=q1, usage=usage) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-18-3) schema2 = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema(url=url2, query=q2, usage=usage) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-18-4) print(f"Grand total: {usage.total_tokens} tokens")`

Both `generate_schema` (sync) and `agenerate_schema` (async) support the `usage` parameter.

### LLM Provider Options

1.  **OpenAI GPT-4 (`openai/gpt4o`)**
2.  Default provider
3.  Requires an API token
4.  Generally provides more accurate schemas
5.  Set via environment variable: `OPENAI_API_KEY`
    
6.  **Ollama (`ollama/llama3.3`)**
    
7.  Open source alternative
8.  No API token required
9.  Self-hosted option
10.  Good for development and testing

### Benefits of Schema Generation

1.  **One-Time Cost**: While schema generation uses LLM, it's a one-time cost. The generated schema can be reused for unlimited extractions without further LLM calls.
2.  **Smart Pattern Recognition**: The LLM analyzes the HTML structure and identifies common patterns, often producing more robust selectors than manual attempts.
3.  **Automatic Nesting**: Complex nested structures are automatically detected and properly represented in the schema.
4.  **Learning Tool**: The generated schemas serve as excellent examples for learning how to write your own schemas.

### Best Practices

1.  **Review Generated Schemas**: While the generator is smart, always review and test the generated schema before using it in production.
2.  **Provide Representative HTML**: The better your sample HTML represents the overall structure, the more accurate the generated schema will be.
3.  **Consider Both CSS and XPath**: Try both schema types and choose the one that works best for your specific case.
4.  **Cache Generated Schemas**: Since generation uses LLM, save successful schemas for reuse.
5.  **API Token Security**: Never hardcode API tokens. Use environment variables or secure configuration management.
6.  **Choose Provider Wisely**:
7.  Use OpenAI for production-quality schemas
8.  Use Ollama for development, testing, or when you need a self-hosted solution

### Multi-Sample Schema Generation

When scraping multiple pages with varying DOM structures (e.g., product pages where table rows appear in different positions), single-sample schema generation may produce **fragile selectors** like `tr:nth-child(6)` that break on other pages.

**The Problem:**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-19-1) Page A: Manufacturer is in row 6  → selector: tr:nth-child(6) td a [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-19-2) Page B: Manufacturer is in row 5  → selector FAILS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-19-3) Page C: Manufacturer is in row 7  → selector FAILS`

**The Solution:** Provide multiple HTML samples so the LLM identifies stable patterns that work across all pages.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-3) # Collect HTML samples from different pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-4) html_sample_1 = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-5) <table class="specs"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-6)   <tr><td>Brand</td><td>Apple</td></tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-7)   <tr><td>Manufacturer</td><td><a href="/m/apple">Apple Inc</a></td></tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-8) </table> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-9) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-11) html_sample_2 = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-12) <table class="specs"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-13)   <tr><td>Manufacturer</td><td><a href="/m/samsung">Samsung</a></td></tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-14)   <tr><td>Brand</td><td>Galaxy</td></tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-15) </table> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-16) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-18) html_sample_3 = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-19) <table class="specs"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-20)   <tr><td>Model</td><td>Pixel 8</td></tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-21)   <tr><td>Brand</td><td>Google</td></tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-22)   <tr><td>Manufacturer</td><td><a href="/m/google">Google LLC</a></td></tr> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-23) </table> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-24) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-26) # Combine samples with labels [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-27) combined_html = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-28) ## HTML Sample 1 (Product A): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-29) ```html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-20-30) """ + html_sample_1 + """`

HTML Sample 2 (Product B):
--------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-21-1) """ + html_sample_2 + """`

HTML Sample 3 (Product C):
--------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/extraction/no-llm-strategies/#__codelineno-22-1) """ + html_sample_3 + """`

"""

Provide instructions for stable selectors
=========================================

query = """ IMPORTANT: I'm providing 3 HTML samples from different product pages. The manufacturer field appears in different row positions across pages. Generate selectors using stable attributes like href patterns (e.g., a\[href\*='/m/'\]) instead of fragile positional selectors like nth-child(). Extract: manufacturer name and link. """

Generate schema with multi-sample awareness
===========================================

schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate\_schema( html=combined\_html, query=query, schema\_type="CSS", llm\_config=LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o", api\_token="your-token") )

The generated schema will use stable selectors like:
====================================================

a\[href\*="/m/"\] instead of tr:nth-child(6) td a
=================================================

print(schema) \`\`\`

**Key Points for Multi-Sample Queries:**

1.  **Format samples clearly** - Use markdown headers and code blocks to separate samples
2.  **State the number of samples** - "I'm providing 3 HTML samples..."
3.  **Explain the variation** - "...the manufacturer field appears in different row positions"
