4.  **Request stable selectors** - "Use href patterns, data attributes, or class names instead of nth-child"

**Stable vs Fragile Selectors:**

| Fragile (single sample) | Stable (multi-sample) |
| --- | --- |
| `tr:nth-child(6) td a` | `a[href*="/m/"]` |
| `div:nth-child(3) .price` | `.price, [data-price]` |
| `ul li:first-child` | `li[data-featured="true"]` |

This approach lets you generate schemas once that work reliably across hundreds of similar pages with varying structures.

* * *

11\. Conclusion
---------------

With Crawl4AI's LLM-free extraction strategies - `JsonCssExtractionStrategy`, `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`, and now `RegexExtractionStrategy` - you can build powerful pipelines that:

*   Scrape any consistent site for structured data.
*   Support nested objects, repeating lists, or pattern-based extraction.
*   Scale to thousands of pages quickly and reliably.

**Choosing the Right Strategy**:

*   Use **`RegexExtractionStrategy`** for fast extraction of common data types like emails, phones, URLs, dates, etc.
*   Use **`JsonCssExtractionStrategy`** or **`JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`** for structured data with clear HTML patterns
*   If you need both: first extract structured data with JSON strategies, then use regex on specific fields

**Remember**: For repeated, structured data, you don't need to pay for or wait on an LLM. Well-crafted schemas and regex patterns get you the data faster, cleaner, and cheaper—**the real power** of Crawl4AI.

**Last Updated**: 2025-05-02

* * *

That's it for **Extracting JSON (No LLM)**! You've seen how schema-based approaches (either CSS or XPath) and regex patterns can handle everything from simple lists to deeply nested product catalogs—instantly, with minimal overhead. Enjoy building robust scrapers that produce consistent, structured JSON for your data pipelines!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Crawl4AI v0.8.0 Release Notes - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl4AI v0.8.0 Release Notes
=============================

**Release Date**: January 2026 **Previous Version**: v0.7.6 **Status**: Release Candidate

* * *

Highlights
----------

*   **Critical Security Fixes** for Docker API deployment
*   **11 New Features** including crash recovery, prefetch mode, and proxy improvements
*   **Breaking Changes** - see migration guide below

* * *

Breaking Changes
----------------

### 1\. Docker API: Hooks Disabled by Default

**What changed**: Hooks are now disabled by default on the Docker API.

**Why**: Security fix for Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability.

**Who is affected**: Users of the Docker API who use the `hooks` parameter in `/crawl` requests.

**Migration**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-0-1) # To re-enable hooks (only if you trust all API users): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-0-2) export CRAWL4AI_HOOKS_ENABLED=true`

### 2\. Docker API: file:// URLs Blocked

**What changed**: The endpoints `/execute_js`, `/screenshot`, `/pdf`, and `/html` now reject `file://` URLs.

**Why**: Security fix for Local File Inclusion (LFI) vulnerability.

**Who is affected**: Users who were reading local files via the Docker API.

**Migration**: Use the Python library directly for local file processing:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-1-1) # Instead of API call with file:// URL, use library: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-1-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-1-4)     result = await crawler.arun(url="file:///path/to/file.html")`

* * *

Security Fixes
--------------

### Critical: Remote Code Execution via Hooks (CVE Pending)

**Severity**: CRITICAL (CVSS 10.0) **Affected**: Docker API deployment (all versions before v0.8.0) **Vector**: `POST /crawl` with malicious `hooks` parameter

**Details**: The `__import__` builtin was available in hook code, allowing attackers to import `os`, `subprocess`, etc. and execute arbitrary commands.

**Fix**: 1. Removed `__import__` from allowed builtins 2. Hooks disabled by default (`CRAWL4AI_HOOKS_ENABLED=false`)

### High: Local File Inclusion via file:// URLs (CVE Pending)

**Severity**: HIGH (CVSS 8.6) **Affected**: Docker API deployment (all versions before v0.8.0) **Vector**: `POST /execute_js` (and other endpoints) with `file:///etc/passwd`

**Details**: API endpoints accepted `file://` URLs, allowing attackers to read arbitrary files from the server.

**Fix**: URL scheme validation now only allows `http://`, `https://`, and `raw:` URLs.

### Credits

Discovered by **Neo by ProjectDiscovery** ([projectdiscovery.io](https://projectdiscovery.io/)
) - December 2025

* * *

New Features
------------

### 1\. init\_scripts Support for BrowserConfig

Pre-page-load JavaScript injection for stealth evasions.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-2-1) config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-2-2)     init_scripts=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-2-3)         "Object.defineProperty(navigator, 'webdriver', {get: () => false})" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-2-4)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-2-5) )`

### 2\. CDP Connection Improvements

*   WebSocket URL support (`ws://`, `wss://`)
*   Proper cleanup with `cdp_cleanup_on_close=True`
*   Browser reuse across multiple connections

### 3\. Crash Recovery for Deep Crawl Strategies

All deep crawl strategies (BFS, DFS, Best-First) now support crash recovery:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-3-3) strategy = BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-3-4)     max_depth=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-3-5)     resume_state=saved_state,  # Resume from checkpoint [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-3-6)     on_state_change=save_callback  # Persist state in real-time [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-3-7) )`

### 4\. PDF and MHTML for raw:/file:// URLs

Generate PDFs and MHTML from cached HTML content.

### 5\. Screenshots for raw:/file:// URLs

Render cached HTML and capture screenshots.

### 6\. base\_url Parameter for CrawlerRunConfig

Proper URL resolution for raw: HTML processing:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-4-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig(base_url='https://example.com') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-4-2) result = await crawler.arun(url='raw:{html}', config=config)`

### 7\. Prefetch Mode for Two-Phase Deep Crawling

Fast link extraction without full page processing:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-5-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig(prefetch=True)`

### 8\. Proxy Rotation and Configuration

Enhanced proxy rotation with sticky sessions support.

### 9\. Proxy Support for HTTP Strategy

Non-browser crawler now supports proxies.

### 10\. Browser Pipeline for raw:/file:// URLs

New `process_in_browser` parameter for browser operations on local content:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-6-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-6-2)     process_in_browser=True,  # Force browser processing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-6-3)     screenshot=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-6-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-6-5) result = await crawler.arun(url='raw:<html>...</html>', config=config)`

### 11\. Smart TTL Cache for Sitemap URL Seeder

Intelligent cache invalidation for sitemaps:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-7-1) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-7-2)     cache_ttl_hours=24, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-7-3)     validate_sitemap_lastmod=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-7-4) )`

* * *

Bug Fixes
---------

### raw: URL Parsing Truncates at # Character

**Problem**: CSS color codes like `#eee` were being truncated.

**Before**: `raw:body{background:#eee}` → `body{background:` **After**: `raw:body{background:#eee}` → `body{background:#eee}`

### Caching System Improvements

Various fixes to cache validation and persistence.

* * *

Documentation Updates
---------------------

*   Multi-sample schema generation documentation
*   URL seeder smart TTL cache parameters
*   Security documentation (SECURITY.md)

* * *

Upgrade Guide
-------------

### From v0.7.x to v0.8.0

1.  **Update the package**:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-8-1) pip install --upgrade crawl4ai`
    
2.  **Docker API users**:
    
3.  Hooks are now disabled by default
4.  If you need hooks: `export CRAWL4AI_HOOKS_ENABLED=true`
5.  `file://` URLs no longer work on API (use library directly)
    
6.  **Review security settings**:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-9-1) # config.yml - recommended for production [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-9-2) security: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-9-3)   enabled: true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/v0.8.0/#__codelineno-9-4)   jwt_enabled: true`
    
7.  **Test your integration** before deploying to production
    

### Breaking Change Checklist

*   \[ \] Check if you use `hooks` parameter in API calls
*   \[ \] Check if you use `file://` URLs via the API
*   \[ \] Update environment variables if needed
*   \[ \] Review security configuration

* * *

Full Changelog
--------------

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/CHANGELOG.md)
 for complete version history.

* * *

Contributors
------------

Thanks to all contributors who made this release possible.

Special thanks to **Neo by ProjectDiscovery** for responsible security disclosure.

* * *

_For questions or issues, please open a [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/issues)
._

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# URL Seeding - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

URL Seeding: The Smart Way to Crawl at Scale
============================================

Why URL Seeding?
----------------

Web crawling comes in different flavors, each with its own strengths. Let's understand when to use URL seeding versus deep crawling.

### Deep Crawling: Real-Time Discovery

Deep crawling is perfect when you need: - **Fresh, real-time data** - discovering pages as they're created - **Dynamic exploration** - following links based on content - **Selective extraction** - stopping when you find what you need

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-1) # Deep crawling example: Explore a website dynamically [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-4) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-6) async def deep_crawl_example(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-7)     # Configure a 2-level deep crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-8)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-9)         deep_crawl_strategy=BFSDeepCrawlStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-10)             max_depth=2,           # Crawl 2 levels deep [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-11)             include_external=False, # Stay within domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-12)             max_pages=50           # Limit for efficiency [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-13)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-14)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-18)         # Start crawling and follow links dynamically [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-19)         results = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-21)         print(f"Discovered and crawled {len(results)} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-22)         for result in results[:3]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-23)             print(f"Found: {result.url} at depth {result.metadata.get('depth', 0)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-0-25) asyncio.run(deep_crawl_example())`

### URL Seeding: Bulk Discovery

URL seeding shines when you want: - **Comprehensive coverage** - get thousands of URLs in seconds - **Bulk processing** - filter before crawling - **Resource efficiency** - know exactly what you'll crawl

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-1) # URL seeding example: Analyze all documentation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncUrlSeeder, SeedingConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-4) seeder = AsyncUrlSeeder() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-5) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-6)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-7)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-8)     pattern="*/docs/*" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-11) # Get ALL documentation URLs instantly [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-12) urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-1-13) # 1000+ URLs discovered in seconds!`

### The Trade-offs

| Aspect | Deep Crawling | URL Seeding |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Coverage** | Discovers pages dynamically | Gets most existing URLs instantly |
| **Freshness** | Finds brand new pages | May miss very recent pages |
| **Speed** | Slower, page by page | Extremely fast bulk discovery |
| **Resource Usage** | Higher - crawls to discover | Lower - discovers then crawls |
| **Control** | Can stop mid-process | Pre-filters before crawling |

### When to Use Each

**Choose Deep Crawling when:** - You need the absolute latest content - You're searching for specific information - The site structure is unknown or dynamic - You want to stop as soon as you find what you need

**Choose URL Seeding when:** - You need to analyze large portions of a site - You want to filter URLs before crawling - You're doing comparative analysis - You need to optimize resource usage

The magic happens when you understand both approaches and choose the right tool for your task. Sometimes, you might even combine them - use URL seeding for bulk discovery, then deep crawl specific sections for the latest updates.

Your First URL Seeding Adventure
--------------------------------

Let's see the magic in action. We'll discover blog posts about Python, filter for tutorials, and crawl only those pages.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncUrlSeeder, AsyncWebCrawler, SeedingConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-4) async def smart_blog_crawler(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-5)     # Step 1: Create our URL discoverer [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-6)     seeder = AsyncUrlSeeder() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-8)     # Step 2: Configure discovery - let's find all blog posts [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-9)     config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-10)         source="sitemap+cc",      # Use the website's sitemap+cc [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-11)         pattern="*/courses/*",    # Only courses related posts [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-12)         extract_head=True,          # Get page metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-13)         max_urls=100               # Limit for this example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-14)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-16)     # Step 3: Discover URLs from the Python blog [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-17)     print("🔍 Discovering course posts...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-18)     urls = await seeder.urls("realpython.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-19)     print(f"✅ Found {len(urls)} course posts") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-21)     # Step 4: Filter for Python tutorials (using metadata!) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-22)     tutorials = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-23)         url for url in urls  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-24)         if url["status"] == "valid" and  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-25)         any(keyword in str(url["head_data"]).lower()  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-26)             for keyword in ["tutorial", "guide", "how to"]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-27)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-28)     print(f"📚 Filtered to {len(tutorials)} tutorials") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-30)     # Step 5: Show what we found [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-31)     print("\n🎯 Found these tutorials:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-32)     for tutorial in tutorials[:5]:  # First 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-33)         title = tutorial["head_data"].get("title", "No title") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-34)         print(f"  - {title}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-35)         print(f"    {tutorial['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-36) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-37)     # Step 6: Now crawl ONLY these relevant pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-38)     print("\n🚀 Crawling tutorials...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-39)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-40)         config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-41)             only_text=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-42)             word_count_threshold=300,  # Only substantial articles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-43)             stream=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-44)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-45) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-46)         # Extract URLs and crawl them [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-47)         tutorial_urls = [t["url"] for t in tutorials[:10]] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-48)         results = await crawler.arun_many(tutorial_urls, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-50)         successful = 0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-51)         async for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-52)             if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-53)                 successful += 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-54)                 print(f"✓ Crawled: {result.url[:60]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-55) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-56)         print(f"\n✨ Successfully crawled {successful} tutorials!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-57) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-58) # Run it! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-2-59) asyncio.run(smart_blog_crawler())`

**What just happened?**

1.  We discovered all blog URLs from the sitemap+cc
2.  We filtered using metadata (no crawling needed!)
3.  We crawled only the relevant tutorials
4.  We saved tons of time and bandwidth

This is the power of URL seeding - you see everything before you crawl anything.

Understanding the URL Seeder
----------------------------

Now that you've seen the magic, let's understand how it works.

### Basic Usage

Creating a URL seeder is simple:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncUrlSeeder [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-3) # Method 1: Manual cleanup [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-4) seeder = AsyncUrlSeeder() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-5) try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-6)     config = SeedingConfig(source="sitemap") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-7)     urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-8) finally: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-9)     await seeder.close() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-11) # Method 2: Context manager (recommended) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-12) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-13)     config = SeedingConfig(source="sitemap") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-14)     urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-3-15)     # Automatically cleaned up on exit`

The seeder can discover URLs from two powerful sources:

#### 1\. Sitemaps (Fastest)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-4-1) # Discover from sitemap [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-4-2) config = SeedingConfig(source="sitemap") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-4-3) urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config)`

Sitemaps are XML files that websites create specifically to list all their URLs. It's like getting a menu at a restaurant - everything is listed upfront.

**Sitemap Index Support**: For large websites like TechCrunch that use sitemap indexes (a sitemap of sitemaps), the seeder automatically detects and processes all sub-sitemaps in parallel:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-1) <!-- Example sitemap index --> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-2) <sitemapindex> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-3)   <sitemap> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-4)     <loc>https://techcrunch.com/sitemap-1.xml</loc> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-5)   </sitemap> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-6)   <sitemap> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-7)     <loc>https://techcrunch.com/sitemap-2.xml</loc> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-8)   </sitemap> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-9)   <!-- ... more sitemaps ... --> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-5-10) </sitemapindex>`

The seeder handles this transparently - you'll get all URLs from all sub-sitemaps automatically!

#### 2\. Common Crawl (Most Comprehensive)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-6-1) # Discover from Common Crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-6-2) config = SeedingConfig(source="cc") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-6-3) urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config)`

Common Crawl is a massive public dataset that regularly crawls the entire web. It's like having access to a pre-built index of the internet.

#### 3\. Both Sources (Maximum Coverage)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-7-1) # Use both sources [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-7-2) config = SeedingConfig(source="sitemap+cc") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-7-3) urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config)`

### Configuration Magic: SeedingConfig

The `SeedingConfig` object is your control panel. Here's everything you can configure:

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `source` | str | "sitemap+cc" | URL source: "cc" (Common Crawl), "sitemap", or "sitemap+cc" |
| `pattern` | str | "\*" | URL pattern filter (e.g., "_/blog/_", "\*.html") |
| `extract_head` | bool | False | Extract metadata from page `<head>` |
| `live_check` | bool | False | Verify URLs are accessible |
| `max_urls` | int | \-1 | Maximum URLs to return (-1 = unlimited) |
| `concurrency` | int | 10  | Parallel workers for fetching |
| `hits_per_sec` | int | 5   | Rate limit for requests |
| `force` | bool | False | Bypass cache, fetch fresh data |
| `verbose` | bool | False | Show detailed progress |
| `query` | str | None | Search query for BM25 scoring |
| `scoring_method` | str | None | Scoring method (currently "bm25") |
| `score_threshold` | float | None | Minimum score to include URL |
| `filter_nonsense_urls` | bool | True | Filter out utility URLs (robots.txt, etc.) |
| `cache_ttl_hours` | int | 24  | Hours before sitemap cache expires (0 = no TTL) |
| `validate_sitemap_lastmod` | bool | True | Check sitemap's lastmod and refetch if newer |

#### Pattern Matching Examples

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-1) # Match all blog posts [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-2) config = SeedingConfig(pattern="*/blog/*") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-4) # Match only HTML files [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-5) config = SeedingConfig(pattern="*.html") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-7) # Match product pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-8) config = SeedingConfig(pattern="*/product/*") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-10) # Match everything except admin pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-11) config = SeedingConfig(pattern="*") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-8-12) # Then filter: urls = [u for u in urls if "/admin/" not in u["url"]]`

### URL Validation: Live Checking

Sometimes you need to know if URLs are actually accessible. That's where live checking comes in:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-1) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-2)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-3)     live_check=True,  # Verify each URL is accessible [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-4)     concurrency=20    # Check 20 URLs in parallel [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-6) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-7)     urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-9) # Now you can filter by status [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-10) live_urls = [u for u in urls if u["status"] == "valid"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-11) dead_urls = [u for u in urls if u["status"] == "not_valid"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-13) print(f"Live URLs: {len(live_urls)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-9-14) print(f"Dead URLs: {len(dead_urls)}")`

**When to use live checking:** - Before a large crawling operation - When working with older sitemaps - When data freshness is critical

**When to skip it:** - Quick explorations - When you trust the source - When speed is more important than accuracy

### The Power of Metadata: Head Extraction

This is where URL seeding gets really powerful. Instead of crawling entire pages, you can extract just the metadata:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-1) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-2)     extract_head=True  # Extract metadata from <head> section [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-3) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-4) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-5)     urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-7) # Now each URL has rich metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-8) for url in urls[:3]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-9)     print(f"\nURL: {url['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-10)     print(f"Title: {url['head_data'].get('title')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-12)     meta = url['head_data'].get('meta', {}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-13)     print(f"Description: {meta.get('description')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-14)     print(f"Keywords: {meta.get('keywords')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-16)     # Even Open Graph data! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-10-17)     print(f"OG Image: {meta.get('og:image')}")`

#### What Can We Extract?

The head extraction gives you a treasure trove of information:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-1) # Example of extracted head_data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-2) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-3)     "title": "10 Python Tips for Beginners", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-4)     "charset": "utf-8", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-5)     "lang": "en", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-6)     "meta": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-7)         "description": "Learn essential Python tips...", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-8)         "keywords": "python, programming, tutorial", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-9)         "author": "Jane Developer", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-10)         "viewport": "width=device-width, initial-scale=1", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-12)         # Open Graph tags [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-13)         "og:title": "10 Python Tips for Beginners", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-14)         "og:description": "Essential Python tips for new programmers", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-15)         "og:image": "https://example.com/python-tips.jpg", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-16)         "og:type": "article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-18)         # Twitter Card tags [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-19)         "twitter:card": "summary_large_image", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-20)         "twitter:title": "10 Python Tips", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-22)         # Dublin Core metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-23)         "dc.creator": "Jane Developer", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-24)         "dc.date": "2024-01-15" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-25)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-26)     "link": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-27)         "canonical": [{"href": "https://example.com/blog/python-tips"}], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-28)         "alternate": [{"href": "/feed.xml", "type": "application/rss+xml"}] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-29)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-30)     "jsonld": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-31)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-32)             "@type": "Article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-33)             "headline": "10 Python Tips for Beginners", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-34)             "datePublished": "2024-01-15", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-35)             "author": {"@type": "Person", "name": "Jane Developer"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-36)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-37)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-11-38) }`

This metadata is gold for filtering! You can find exactly what you need without crawling a single page.

### Smart URL-Based Filtering (No Head Extraction)

When `extract_head=False` but you still provide a query, the seeder uses intelligent URL-based scoring:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-1) # Fast filtering based on URL structure alone [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-3)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-4)     extract_head=False,  # Don't fetch page metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-5)     query="python tutorial async", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-6)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-7)     score_threshold=0.3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-9) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-10)     urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-12) # URLs are scored based on: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-13) # 1. Domain parts matching (e.g., 'python' in python.example.com) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-14) # 2. Path segments (e.g., '/tutorials/python-async/') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-15) # 3. Query parameters (e.g., '?topic=python') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-16) # 4. Fuzzy matching using character n-grams [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-18) # Example URL scoring: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-19) # https://example.com/tutorials/python/async-guide.html - High score [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-12-20) # https://example.com/blog/javascript-tips.html - Low score`

This approach is much faster than head extraction while still providing intelligent filtering!

### Understanding Results

Each URL in the results has this structure:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-2)     "url": "https://example.com/blog/python-tips.html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-3)     "status": "valid",        # "valid", "not_valid", or "unknown" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-4)     "head_data": {            # Only if extract_head=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-5)         "title": "Page Title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-6)         "meta": {...}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-7)         "link": {...}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-8)         "jsonld": [...] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-9)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-10)     "relevance_score": 0.85   # Only if using BM25 scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-13-11) }`

Let's see a real example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-1) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-2)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-3)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-4)     live_check=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-6) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-7)     urls = await seeder.urls("blog.example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-9) # Analyze the results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-10) for url in urls[:5]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-11)     print(f"\n{'='*60}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-12)     print(f"URL: {url['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-13)     print(f"Status: {url['status']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-15)     if url['head_data']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-16)         data = url['head_data'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-17)         print(f"Title: {data.get('title', 'No title')}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-19)         # Check content type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-20)         meta = data.get('meta', {}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-21)         content_type = meta.get('og:type', 'unknown') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-22)         print(f"Content Type: {content_type}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-24)         # Publication date [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-25)         pub_date = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-26)         for jsonld in data.get('jsonld', []): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-27)             if isinstance(jsonld, dict): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-28)                 pub_date = jsonld.get('datePublished') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-29)                 if pub_date: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-30)                     break [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-32)         if pub_date: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-33)             print(f"Published: {pub_date}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-35)         # Word count (if available) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-36)         word_count = meta.get('word_count') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-37)         if word_count: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-14-38)             print(f"Word Count: {word_count}")`

Smart Filtering with BM25 Scoring
---------------------------------

Now for the really cool part - intelligent filtering based on relevance!

### Introduction to Relevance Scoring

BM25 is a ranking algorithm that scores how relevant a document is to a search query. With URL seeding, we can score URLs based on their metadata _before_ crawling them.

Think of it like this: - Traditional way: Read every book in the library to find ones about Python - Smart way: Check the titles and descriptions, score them, read only the most relevant

### Query-Based Discovery

Here's how to use BM25 scoring:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-1) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-2)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-3)     extract_head=True,           # Required for scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-4)     query="python async tutorial",  # What we're looking for [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-5)     scoring_method="bm25",       # Use BM25 algorithm [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-6)     score_threshold=0.3          # Minimum relevance score [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-8) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-9)     urls = await seeder.urls("realpython.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-11) # Results are automatically sorted by relevance! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-12) for url in urls[:5]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-13)     print(f"Score: {url['relevance_score']:.2f} - {url['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-15-14)     print(f"  Title: {url['head_data']['title']}")`

### Real Examples

#### Finding Documentation Pages

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-1) # Find API documentation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-3)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-4)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-5)     query="API reference documentation endpoints", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-6)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-7)     score_threshold=0.5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-8)     max_urls=20 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-10) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-11)     urls = await seeder.urls("docs.example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-16-13) # The highest scoring URLs will be API docs!`

#### Discovering Product Pages

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-1) # Find specific products [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-3)     source="sitemap+cc",  # Use both sources [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-4)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-5)     query="wireless headphones noise canceling", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-6)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-7)     score_threshold=0.4, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-8)     pattern="*/product/*"  # Combine with pattern matching [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-10) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-11)     urls = await seeder.urls("shop.example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-13) # Filter further by price (from metadata) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-14) affordable = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-15)     u for u in urls  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-16)     if float(u['head_data'].get('meta', {}).get('product:price', '0')) < 200 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-17-17) ]`

#### Filtering News Articles

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-1) # Find recent news about AI [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-3)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-4)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-5)     query="artificial intelligence machine learning breakthrough", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-6)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-7)     score_threshold=0.35 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-9) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-10)     urls = await seeder.urls("technews.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-12) # Filter by date [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-13) from datetime import datetime, timedelta [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-15) recent = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-16) cutoff = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-18) for url in urls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-19)     # Check JSON-LD for publication date [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-20)     for jsonld in url['head_data'].get('jsonld', []): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-21)         if 'datePublished' in jsonld: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-22)             pub_date = datetime.fromisoformat(jsonld['datePublished'].replace('Z', '+00:00')) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-23)             if pub_date > cutoff: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-24)                 recent.append(url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-18-25)                 break`

#### Complex Query Patterns

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-1) # Multi-concept queries [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-2) queries = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-3)     "python async await concurrency tutorial", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-4)     "data science pandas numpy visualization", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-5)     "web scraping beautifulsoup selenium automation", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-6)     "machine learning tensorflow keras deep learning" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-7) ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-9) all_tutorials = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-11) for query in queries: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-12)     config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-13)         source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-14)         extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-15)         query=query, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-16)         scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-17)         score_threshold=0.4, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-18)         max_urls=10  # Top 10 per topic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-19)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-20)     async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-21)         urls = await seeder.urls("learning-platform.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-22)     all_tutorials.extend(urls) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-24) # Remove duplicates while preserving order [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-25) seen = set() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-26) unique_tutorials = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-27) for url in all_tutorials: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-28)     if url['url'] not in seen: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-29)         seen.add(url['url']) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-30)         unique_tutorials.append(url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-19-32) print(f"Found {len(unique_tutorials)} unique tutorials across all topics")`

Scaling Up: Multiple Domains
----------------------------

When you need to discover URLs across multiple websites, URL seeding really shines.

### The `many_urls` Method

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-1) # Discover URLs from multiple domains in parallel [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-2) domains = ["site1.com", "site2.com", "site3.com"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-4) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-5)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-6)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-7)     query="python tutorial", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-8)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-9)     score_threshold=0.3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-12) # Returns a dictionary: {domain: [urls]} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-13) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-14)     results = await seeder.many_urls(domains, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-16) # Process results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-17) for domain, urls in results.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-18)     print(f"\n{domain}: Found {len(urls)} relevant URLs") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-19)     if urls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-20)         top = urls[0]  # Highest scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-21)         print(f"  Top result: {top['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-20-22)         print(f"  Score: {top['relevance_score']:.2f}")`

### Cross-Domain Examples

#### Competitor Analysis

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-1) # Analyze content strategies across competitors [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-2) competitors = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-3)     "competitor1.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-4)     "competitor2.com",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-5)     "competitor3.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-6) ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-8) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-9)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-10)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-11)     pattern="*/blog/*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-12)     max_urls=100 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-13) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-14) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-15)     results = await seeder.many_urls(competitors, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-17) # Analyze content types [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-18) for domain, urls in results.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-19)     content_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-21)     for url in urls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-22)         # Extract content type from metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-23)         og_type = url['head_data'].get('meta', {}).get('og:type', 'unknown') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-24)         content_types[og_type] = content_types.get(og_type, 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-26)     print(f"\n{domain} content distribution:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-27)     for ctype, count in sorted(content_types.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-21-28)         print(f"  {ctype}: {count}")`

#### Industry Research

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-1) # Research Python tutorials across educational sites [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-2) educational_sites = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-3)     "realpython.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-4)     "pythontutorial.net", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-5)     "learnpython.org", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-6)     "python.org" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-7) ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-9) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-10)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-11)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-12)     query="beginner python tutorial basics", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-13)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-14)     score_threshold=0.3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-15)     max_urls=20  # Per site [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-16) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-17) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-18)     results = await seeder.many_urls(educational_sites, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-20) # Find the best beginner tutorials [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-21) all_tutorials = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-22) for domain, urls in results.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-23)     for url in urls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-24)         url['domain'] = domain  # Add domain info [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-25)         all_tutorials.append(url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-27) # Sort by relevance across all domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-28) all_tutorials.sort(key=lambda x: x['relevance_score'], reverse=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-30) print("Top 10 Python tutorials for beginners across all sites:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-31) for i, tutorial in enumerate(all_tutorials[:10], 1): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-32)     print(f"{i}. [{tutorial['relevance_score']:.2f}] {tutorial['head_data']['title']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-33)     print(f"   {tutorial['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-22-34)     print(f"   From: {tutorial['domain']}")`

#### Multi-Site Monitoring

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-1) # Monitor news about your company across multiple sources [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-2) news_sites = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-3)     "techcrunch.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-4)     "theverge.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-5)     "wired.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-6)     "arstechnica.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-7) ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-9) company_name = "YourCompany" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-11) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-12)     source="cc",  # Common Crawl for recent content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-13)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-14)     query=f"{company_name} announcement news", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-15)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-16)     score_threshold=0.5,  # High threshold for relevance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-17)     max_urls=10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-18) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-19) async with AsyncUrlSeeder() as seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-20)     results = await seeder.many_urls(news_sites, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-22) # Collect all mentions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-23) mentions = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-24) for domain, urls in results.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-25)     mentions.extend(urls) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-27) if mentions: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-28)     print(f"Found {len(mentions)} mentions of {company_name}:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-29)     for mention in mentions: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-30)         print(f"\n- {mention['head_data']['title']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-31)         print(f"  {mention['url']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-32)         print(f"  Score: {mention['relevance_score']:.2f}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-33) else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-23-34)     print(f"No recent mentions of {company_name} found")`

Advanced Integration Patterns
-----------------------------

Let's put everything together in a real-world example.

### Building a Research Assistant

Here's a complete example that discovers, scores, filters, and crawls intelligently:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-2) from datetime import datetime [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncUrlSeeder, AsyncWebCrawler, SeedingConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-5) class ResearchAssistant: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-6)     def __init__(self): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-7)         self.seeder = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-9)     async def __aenter__(self): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-10)         self.seeder = AsyncUrlSeeder() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-11)         await self.seeder.__aenter__() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-12)         return self [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-14)     async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-15)         if self.seeder: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-16)             await self.seeder.__aexit__(exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-18)     async def research_topic(self, topic, domains, max_articles=20): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-19)         """Research a topic across multiple domains.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-21)         print(f"🔬 Researching '{topic}' across {len(domains)} domains...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-23)         # Step 1: Discover relevant URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-24)         config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-25)             source="sitemap+cc",     # Maximum coverage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-26)             extract_head=True,       # Get metadata [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-27)             query=topic,             # Research topic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-28)             scoring_method="bm25",   # Smart scoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-29)             score_threshold=0.4,     # Quality threshold [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-30)             max_urls=10,             # Per domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-31)             concurrency=20,          # Fast discovery [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-32)             verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-33)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-35)         # Discover across all domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-36)         discoveries = await self.seeder.many_urls(domains, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-38)         # Step 2: Collect and rank all articles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-39)         all_articles = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-40)         for domain, urls in discoveries.items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-41)             for url in urls: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-42)                 url['domain'] = domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-43)                 all_articles.append(url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-44) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-45)         # Sort by relevance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-46)         all_articles.sort(key=lambda x: x['relevance_score'], reverse=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-48)         # Take top articles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-49)         top_articles = all_articles[:max_articles] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-50) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-51)         print(f"\n📊 Found {len(all_articles)} relevant articles") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-52)         print(f"📌 Selected top {len(top_articles)} for deep analysis") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-53) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-54)         # Step 3: Show what we're about to crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-55)         print("\n🎯 Articles to analyze:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-56)         for i, article in enumerate(top_articles[:5], 1): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-57)             print(f"\n{i}. {article['head_data']['title']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-58)             print(f"   Score: {article['relevance_score']:.2f}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-59)             print(f"   Source: {article['domain']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-60)             print(f"   URL: {article['url'][:60]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-61) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-62)         # Step 4: Crawl the selected articles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-63)         print(f"\n🚀 Deep crawling {len(top_articles)} articles...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-64) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-65)         async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-66)             config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-67)                 only_text=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-68)                 word_count_threshold=200,  # Substantial content only [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-69)                 stream=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-70)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-71) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-72)             # Extract URLs and crawl all articles [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-73)             article_urls = [article['url'] for article in top_articles] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-74)             results = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-75)             crawl_results = await crawler.arun_many(article_urls, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-76)             async for result in crawl_results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-77)                 if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-78)                     results.append({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-79)                         'url': result.url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-80)                         'title': result.metadata.get('title', 'No title'), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-81)                         'content': result.markdown.raw_markdown, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-82)                         'domain': next(a['domain'] for a in top_articles if a['url'] == result.url), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-83)                         'score': next(a['relevance_score'] for a in top_articles if a['url'] == result.url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-84)                     }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-85)                     print(f"✓ Crawled: {result.url[:60]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-86) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-87)         # Step 5: Analyze and summarize [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-88)         print(f"\n📝 Analysis complete! Crawled {len(results)} articles") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-89) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-90)         return self.create_research_summary(topic, results) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-91) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-92)     def create_research_summary(self, topic, articles): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-93)         """Create a research summary from crawled articles.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-94) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-95)         summary = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-96)             'topic': topic, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-97)             'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-98)             'total_articles': len(articles), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-99)             'sources': {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-100)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-101) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-102)         # Group by domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-103)         for article in articles: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-104)             domain = article['domain'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-105)             if domain not in summary['sources']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-106)                 summary['sources'][domain] = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-107) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-108)             summary['sources'][domain].append({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-109)                 'title': article['title'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-110)                 'url': article['url'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-111)                 'score': article['score'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-112)                 'excerpt': article['content'][:500] + '...' if len(article['content']) > 500 else article['content'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-113)             }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-114) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-115)         return summary [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-116) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-117) # Use the research assistant [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-118) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-119)     async with ResearchAssistant() as assistant: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-120)         #
