Research Python async programming across multiple sources [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-121)         topic = "python asyncio best practices performance optimization" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-122)         domains = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-123)             "realpython.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-124)             "python.org", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-125)             "stackoverflow.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-126)             "medium.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-127)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-128) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-129)         summary = await assistant.research_topic(topic, domains, max_articles=15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-130) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-131)     # Display results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-132)     print("\n" + "="*60) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-133)     print("RESEARCH SUMMARY") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-134)     print("="*60) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-135)     print(f"Topic: {summary['topic']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-136)     print(f"Date: {summary['timestamp']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-137)     print(f"Total Articles Analyzed: {summary['total_articles']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-138) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-139)     print("\nKey Findings by Source:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-140)     for domain, articles in summary['sources'].items(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-141)         print(f"\n📚 {domain} ({len(articles)} articles)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-142)         for article in articles[:2]:  # Top 2 per domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-143)             print(f"\n  Title: {article['title']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-144)             print(f"  Relevance: {article['score']:.2f}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-145)             print(f"  Preview: {article['excerpt'][:200]}...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-146) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-24-147) asyncio.run(main())`

### Performance Optimization Tips

1.  **Use caching wisely**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-25-1) # First run - populate cache [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-25-2) config = SeedingConfig(source="sitemap", extract_head=True, force=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-25-3) urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-25-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-25-5) # Subsequent runs - use cache (much faster) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-25-6) config = SeedingConfig(source="sitemap", extract_head=True, force=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-25-7) urls = await seeder.urls("example.com", config)`
    
2.  **Optimize concurrency**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-26-1) # For many small requests (like HEAD checks) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-26-2) config = SeedingConfig(concurrency=50, hits_per_sec=20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-26-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-26-4) # For fewer large requests (like full head extraction) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-26-5) config = SeedingConfig(concurrency=10, hits_per_sec=5)`
    
3.  **Stream large result sets**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-1) # When crawling many URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-2) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-3)     # Assuming urls is a list of URL strings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-4)     crawl_results = await crawler.arun_many(urls, config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-6)     # Process as they arrive [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-7)     async for result in crawl_results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-27-8)         process_immediately(result)  # Don't wait for all`
    
4.  **Memory protection for large domains**
    

The seeder uses bounded queues to prevent memory issues when processing domains with millions of URLs:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-1) # Safe for domains with 1M+ URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-3)     source="cc+sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-4)     concurrency=50,  # Queue size adapts to concurrency [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-5)     max_urls=100000  # Process in batches if needed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-8) # The seeder automatically manages memory by: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-9) # - Using bounded queues (prevents RAM spikes) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-10) # - Applying backpressure when queue is full [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-28-11) # - Processing URLs as they're discovered`

Best Practices & Tips
---------------------

### Cache Management

The seeder automatically caches results to speed up repeated operations:

*   **Common Crawl cache**: `~/.crawl4ai/seeder_cache/[index]_[domain]_[hash].jsonl`
*   **Sitemap cache**: `~/.crawl4ai/seeder_cache/sitemap_[domain]_[hash].json`
*   **HEAD data cache**: `~/.cache/url_seeder/head/[hash].json`

#### Smart TTL Cache for Sitemaps

Sitemap caches now include intelligent validation:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-1) # Default: 24-hour TTL with lastmod validation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-3)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-4)     cache_ttl_hours=24,              # Cache expires after 24 hours [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-5)     validate_sitemap_lastmod=True    # Also check if sitemap was updated [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-8) # Aggressive caching (1 week, no lastmod check) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-9) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-10)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-11)     cache_ttl_hours=168,             # 7 days [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-12)     validate_sitemap_lastmod=False   # Trust TTL only [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-13) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-15) # Always validate (no TTL, only lastmod) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-16) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-17)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-18)     cache_ttl_hours=0,               # Disable TTL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-19)     validate_sitemap_lastmod=True    # Refetch if sitemap has newer lastmod [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-20) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-22) # Always fresh (bypass cache completely) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-23) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-24)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-25)     force=True                       # Ignore all caching [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-29-26) )`

**Cache validation priority:** 1. `force=True` → Always refetch 2. Cache doesn't exist → Fetch fresh 3. `validate_sitemap_lastmod=True` and sitemap has newer `<lastmod>` → Refetch 4. `cache_ttl_hours > 0` and cache is older than TTL → Refetch 5. Cache corrupted → Refetch (automatic recovery) 6. Otherwise → Use cache

### Pattern Matching Strategies

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-1) # Be specific when possible [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-2) good_pattern = "*/blog/2024/*.html"  # Specific [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-3) bad_pattern = "*"                     # Too broad [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-5) # Combine patterns with metadata filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-6) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-7)     pattern="*/articles/*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-8)     extract_head=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-10) urls = await seeder.urls("news.com", config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-12) # Further filter by publish date, author, category, etc. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-30-13) recent = [u for u in urls if is_recent(u['head_data'])]`

### Rate Limiting Considerations

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-1) # Be respectful of servers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-3)     hits_per_sec=10,      # Max 10 requests per second [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-4)     concurrency=20        # But use 20 workers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-7) # For your own servers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-8) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-9)     hits_per_sec=None,    # No limit [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-10)     concurrency=100       # Go fast [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-31-11) )`

Quick Reference
---------------

### Common Patterns

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-1) # Blog post discovery [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-3)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-4)     pattern="*/blog/*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-5)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-6)     query="your topic", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-7)     scoring_method="bm25" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-10) # E-commerce product discovery [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-11) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-12)     source="sitemap+cc", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-13)     pattern="*/product/*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-14)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-15)     live_check=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-16) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-18) # Documentation search [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-19) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-20)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-21)     pattern="*/docs/*", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-22)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-23)     query="API reference", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-24)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-25)     score_threshold=0.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-26) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-28) # News monitoring [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-29) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-30)     source="cc", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-31)     extract_head=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-32)     query="company name", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-33)     scoring_method="bm25", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-34)     max_urls=50 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-32-35) )`

### Troubleshooting Guide

| Issue | Solution |
| --- | --- |
| No URLs found | Try `source="cc+sitemap"`, check domain spelling |
| Slow discovery | Reduce `concurrency`, add `hits_per_sec` limit |
| Missing metadata | Ensure `extract_head=True` |
| Low relevance scores | Refine query, lower `score_threshold` |
| Rate limit errors | Reduce `hits_per_sec` and `concurrency` |
| Memory issues with large sites | Use `max_urls` to limit results, reduce `concurrency` |
| Connection not closed | Use context manager or call `await seeder.close()` |
| Stale/outdated URLs | Set `cache_ttl_hours=0` or use `force=True` |
| Cache not updating | Check `validate_sitemap_lastmod=True`, or use `force=True` |
| Incomplete URL list | Delete cache file and refetch, or use `force=True` |

### Performance Benchmarks

Typical performance on a standard connection:

*   **Sitemap discovery**: 100-1,000 URLs/second
*   **Common Crawl discovery**: 50-500 URLs/second
*   **HEAD checking**: 10-50 URLs/second
*   **Head extraction**: 5-20 URLs/second
*   **BM25 scoring**: 10,000+ URLs/second

Conclusion
----------

URL seeding transforms web crawling from a blind expedition into a surgical strike. By discovering and analyzing URLs before crawling, you can:

*   Save hours of crawling time
*   Reduce bandwidth usage by 90%+
*   Find exactly what you need
*   Scale across multiple domains effortlessly

Whether you're building a research tool, monitoring competitors, or creating a content aggregator, URL seeding gives you the intelligence to crawl smarter, not harder.

### Smart URL Filtering

The seeder automatically filters out nonsense URLs that aren't useful for content crawling:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-1) # Enabled by default [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-2) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-3)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-4)     filter_nonsense_urls=True  # Default: True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-7) # URLs that get filtered: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-8) # - robots.txt, sitemap.xml, ads.txt [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-9) # - API endpoints (/api/, /v1/, .json) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-10) # - Media files (.jpg, .mp4, .pdf) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-11) # - Archives (.zip, .tar.gz) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-12) # - Source code (.js, .css) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-13) # - Admin/login pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-33-14) # - And many more...`

To disable filtering (not recommended):

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-34-1) config = SeedingConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-34-2)     source="sitemap", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-34-3)     filter_nonsense_urls=False  # Include ALL URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/url-seeding/#__codelineno-34-4) )`

### Key Features Summary

1.  **Parallel Sitemap Index Processing**: Automatically detects and processes sitemap indexes in parallel
2.  **Memory Protection**: Bounded queues prevent RAM issues with large domains (1M+ URLs)
3.  **Context Manager Support**: Automatic cleanup with `async with` statement
4.  **URL-Based Scoring**: Smart filtering even without head extraction
5.  **Smart URL Filtering**: Automatically excludes utility/nonsense URLs
6.  **Smart TTL Cache**: Sitemap caches with TTL expiry and lastmod validation
7.  **Automatic Cache Recovery**: Corrupted or incomplete caches are automatically refreshed

Now go forth and seed intelligently!

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Crawl4AI v0.5.0 Release Notes - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl4AI v0.5.0 Release Notes
=============================

**Release Theme: Power, Flexibility, and Scalability**

Crawl4AI v0.5.0 is a major release focused on significantly enhancing the library's power, flexibility, and scalability. Key improvements include a new **deep crawling** system, a **memory-adaptive dispatcher** for handling large-scale crawls, **multiple crawling strategies** (including a fast HTTP-only crawler), **Docker** deployment options, and a powerful **command-line interface (CLI)**. This release also includes numerous bug fixes, performance optimizations, and documentation updates.

**Important Note:** This release contains several **breaking changes**. Please review the "Breaking Changes" section carefully and update your code accordingly.

Key Features
------------

### 1\. Deep Crawling

Crawl4AI now supports deep crawling, allowing you to explore websites beyond the initial URLs. This is controlled by the `deep_crawl_strategy` parameter in `CrawlerRunConfig`. Several strategies are available:

*   **`BFSDeepCrawlStrategy` (Breadth-First Search):** Explores the website level by level. (Default)
*   **`DFSDeepCrawlStrategy` (Depth-First Search):** Explores each branch as deeply as possible before backtracking.
*   **`BestFirstCrawlingStrategy`:** Uses a scoring function to prioritize which URLs to crawl next.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-1) import time [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, BFSDeepCrawlStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-3) from crawl4ai.content_scraping_strategy import LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-4) from crawl4ai.deep_crawling import DomainFilter, ContentTypeFilter, FilterChain, URLPatternFilter, KeywordRelevanceScorer, BestFirstCrawlingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-5) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-7) # Create a filter chain to filter urls based on patterns, domains and content type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-8) filter_chain = FilterChain( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-9)     [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-10)         DomainFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-11)             allowed_domains=["docs.crawl4ai.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-12)             blocked_domains=["old.docs.crawl4ai.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-13)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-14)         URLPatternFilter(patterns=["*core*", "*advanced*"],), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-15)         ContentTypeFilter(allowed_types=["text/html"]), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-16)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-17) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-19) # Create a keyword scorer that prioritises the pages with certain keywords first [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-20) keyword_scorer = KeywordRelevanceScorer( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-21)     keywords=["crawl", "example", "async", "configuration"], weight=0.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-22) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-24) # Set up the configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-25) deep_crawl_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-26)     deep_crawl_strategy=BestFirstCrawlingStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-27)         max_depth=2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-28)         include_external=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-29)         filter_chain=filter_chain, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-30)         url_scorer=keyword_scorer, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-31)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-32)     scraping_strategy=LXMLWebScrapingStrategy(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-33)     stream=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-34)     verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-35) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-36) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-37) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-38)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-39)         start_time = time.perf_counter() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-40)         results = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-41)         async for result in await crawler.arun(url="https://docs.crawl4ai.com", config=deep_crawl_config): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-42)             print(f"Crawled: {result.url} (Depth: {result.metadata['depth']}), score: {result.metadata['score']:.2f}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-43)             results.append(result) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-44)         duration = time.perf_counter() - start_time [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-45)         print(f"\n✅ Crawled {len(results)} high-value pages in {duration:.2f} seconds") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-46) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-0-47) asyncio.run(main())`

**Breaking Change:** The `max_depth` parameter is now part of `CrawlerRunConfig` and controls the _depth_ of the crawl, not the number of concurrent crawls. The `arun()` and `arun_many()` methods are now decorated to handle deep crawling strategies. Imports for deep crawling strategies have changed. See the [Deep Crawling documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/deep-crawling/)
 for more details.

### 2\. Memory-Adaptive Dispatcher

The new `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` dynamically adjusts concurrency based on available system memory and includes built-in rate limiting. This prevents out-of-memory errors and avoids overwhelming target websites.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-4) # Configure the dispatcher (optional, defaults are used if not provided) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-5) dispatcher = MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-6)     memory_threshold_percent=80.0,  # Pause if memory usage exceeds 80% [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-7)     check_interval=0.5,  # Check memory every 0.5 seconds [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-10) async def batch_mode(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-11)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-12)         results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-13)             urls=["https://docs.crawl4ai.com", "https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-14)             config=CrawlerRunConfig(stream=False),  # Batch mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-15)             dispatcher=dispatcher, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-16)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-17)         for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-18)             print(f"Crawled: {result.url} with status code: {result.status_code}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-20) async def stream_mode(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-21)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-22)         # OR, for streaming: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-23)         async for result in await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-24)             urls=["https://docs.crawl4ai.com", "https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-25)             config=CrawlerRunConfig(stream=True), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-26)             dispatcher=dispatcher, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-27)         ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-28)             print(f"Crawled: {result.url} with status code: {result.status_code}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-30) print("Dispatcher in batch mode:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-31) asyncio.run(batch_mode()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-32) print("-" * 50) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-33) print("Dispatcher in stream mode:") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-1-34) asyncio.run(stream_mode())`

**Breaking Change:** `AsyncWebCrawler.arun_many()` now uses `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` by default. Existing code that relied on unbounded concurrency may require adjustments.

### 3\. Multiple Crawling Strategies (Playwright and HTTP)

Crawl4AI now offers two crawling strategies:

*   **`AsyncPlaywrightCrawlerStrategy` (Default):** Uses Playwright for browser-based crawling, supporting JavaScript rendering and complex interactions.
*   **`AsyncHTTPCrawlerStrategy`:** A lightweight, fast, and memory-efficient HTTP-only crawler. Ideal for simple scraping tasks where browser rendering is unnecessary.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, HTTPCrawlerConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-2) from crawl4ai.async_crawler_strategy import AsyncHTTPCrawlerStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-5) # Use the HTTP crawler strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-6) http_crawler_config = HTTPCrawlerConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-7)         method="GET", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-8)         headers={"User-Agent": "MyCustomBot/1.0"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-9)         follow_redirects=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-10)         verify_ssl=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-13) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-14)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(crawler_strategy=AsyncHTTPCrawlerStrategy(browser_config =http_crawler_config)) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-15)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-16)         print(f"Status code: {result.status_code}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-17)         print(f"Content length: {len(result.html)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-2-19) asyncio.run(main())`

### 4\. Docker Deployment

Crawl4AI can now be easily deployed as a Docker container, providing a consistent and isolated environment. The Docker image includes a FastAPI server with both streaming and non-streaming endpoints.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-3-1) # Build the image (from the project root) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-3-2) docker build -t crawl4ai . [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-3-4) # Run the container [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-3-5) docker run -d -p 8000:8000 --name crawl4ai crawl4ai`

**API Endpoints:**

*   `/crawl` (POST): Non-streaming crawl.
*   `/crawl/stream` (POST): Streaming crawl (NDJSON).
*   `/health` (GET): Health check.
*   `/schema` (GET): Returns configuration schemas.
*   `/md/{url}` (GET): Returns markdown content of the URL.
*   `/llm/{url}` (GET): Returns LLM extracted content.
*   `/token` (POST): Get JWT token

**Breaking Changes:**

*   Docker deployment now requires a `.llm.env` file for API keys.
*   Docker deployment now requires Redis and a new `config.yml` structure.
*   Server startup now uses `supervisord` instead of direct process management.
*   Docker server now requires authentication by default (JWT tokens).

See the [Docker deployment documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/core/docker-deployment.md)
 for detailed instructions.

### 5\. Command-Line Interface (CLI)

A new CLI (`crwl`) provides convenient access to Crawl4AI's functionality from the terminal.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-1) # Basic crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-2) crwl https://example.com [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-4) # Get markdown output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-5) crwl https://example.com -o markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-7) # Use a configuration file [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-8) crwl https://example.com -B browser.yml -C crawler.yml [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-10) # Use LLM-based extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-11) crwl https://example.com -e extract.yml -s schema.json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-13) # Ask a question about the crawled content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-14) crwl https://example.com -q "What is the main topic?" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-16) # See usage examples [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-4-17) crwl --example`

See the [CLI documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/docs/md_v2/core/cli.md)
 for more details.

### 6\. LXML Scraping Mode

Added `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy` for faster HTML parsing using the `lxml` library. This can significantly improve scraping performance, especially for large or complex pages. Set `scraping_strategy=LXMLWebScrapingStrategy()` in your `CrawlerRunConfig`.

**Breaking Change:** The `ScrapingMode` enum has been replaced with a strategy pattern. Use `WebScrapingStrategy` (default) or `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy`.

### 7\. Proxy Rotation

Added `ProxyRotationStrategy` abstract base class with `RoundRobinProxyStrategy` concrete implementation.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-1) import re [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-2) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-3)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-4)     BrowserConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-5)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-6)     CacheMode, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-7)     RoundRobinProxyStrategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-9) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-10) from crawl4ai import ProxyConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-11) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-12)     # Load proxies and create rotation strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-13)     proxies = ProxyConfig.from_env() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-14)     #eg: export PROXIES="ip1:port1:username1:password1,ip2:port2:username2:password2" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-15)     if not proxies: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-16)         print("No proxies found in environment. Set PROXIES env variable!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-17)         return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-19)     proxy_strategy = RoundRobinProxyStrategy(proxies) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-21)     # Create configs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-22)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=True, verbose=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-23)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-24)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-25)         proxy_rotation_strategy=proxy_strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-26)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-28)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-29)         urls = ["https://httpbin.org/ip"] * (len(proxies) * 2)  # Test each proxy twice [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-31)         print("\n📈 Initializing crawler with proxy rotation...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-32)         async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-33)             print("\n🚀 Starting batch crawl with proxy rotation...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-34)             results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-35)                 urls=urls, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-36)                 config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-37)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-38)             for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-39)                 if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-40)                     ip_match = re.search(r'(?:[0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}', result.html) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-41)                     current_proxy = run_config.proxy_config if run_config.proxy_config else None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-43)                     if current_proxy and ip_match: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-44)                         print(f"URL {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-45)                         print(f"Proxy {current_proxy.server} -> Response IP: {ip_match.group(0)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-46)                         verified = ip_match.group(0) == current_proxy.ip [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-47)                         if verified: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-48)                             print(f"✅ Proxy working! IP matches: {current_proxy.ip}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-49)                         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-50)                             print("❌ Proxy failed or IP mismatch!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-51)                     print("---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-52) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-5-53) asyncio.run(main())`

Other Changes and Improvements
------------------------------

*   **Added: `LLMContentFilter` for intelligent markdown generation.** This new filter uses an LLM to create more focused and relevant markdown output.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-2) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import LLMContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-3) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-4) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-6) llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="gemini/gemini-1.5-pro", api_token="env:GEMINI_API_KEY") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-8) markdown_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-9)     content_filter=LLMContentFilter(llm_config=llm_config, instruction="Extract key concepts and summaries") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-12) config = CrawlerRunConfig(markdown_generator=markdown_generator) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-13) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-14)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-15)         result = await crawler.arun("https://docs.crawl4ai.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-16)         print(result.markdown.fit_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-6-18) asyncio.run(main())`

*   **Added: URL redirection tracking.** The crawler now automatically follows HTTP redirects (301, 302, 307, 308) and records the final URL in the `redirected_url` field of the `CrawlResult` object. No code changes are required to enable this; it's automatic.
    
*   **Added: LLM-powered schema generation utility.** A new `generate_schema` method has been added to `JsonCssExtractionStrategy` and `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`. This greatly simplifies creating extraction schemas.
    

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-2) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-4) llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="gemini/gemini-1.5-pro", api_token="env:GEMINI_API_KEY") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-6) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-7)     html="<div class='product'><h2>Product Name</h2><span class='price'>$99</span></div>", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-8)     llm_config = llm_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-9)     query="Extract product name and price" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-7-11) print(schema)`

Expected Output (may vary slightly due to LLM)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-2)   "name": "ProductExtractor", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-3)   "baseSelector": "div.product", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-4)   "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-5)       {"name": "name", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-6)       {"name": "price", "selector": ".price", "type": "text"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-7)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-8-8)  }`

*   **Added: robots.txt compliance support.** The crawler can now respect `robots.txt` rules. Enable this by setting `check_robots_txt=True` in `CrawlerRunConfig`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-9-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig(check_robots_txt=True)`

*   **Added: PDF processing capabilities.** Crawl4AI can now extract text, images, and metadata from PDF files (both local and remote). This uses a new `PDFCrawlerStrategy` and `PDFContentScrapingStrategy`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-2) from crawl4ai.processors.pdf import PDFCrawlerStrategy, PDFContentScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-6)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(crawler_strategy=PDFCrawlerStrategy()) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-7)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-8)             "https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.06825.pdf", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-9)             config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-10)                 scraping_strategy=PDFContentScrapingStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-11)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-12)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-13)         print(result.markdown)  # Access extracted text [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-14)         print(result.metadata)  # Access PDF metadata (title, author, etc.) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-10-16) asyncio.run(main())`

*   **Added: Support for frozenset serialization.** Improves configuration serialization, especially for sets of allowed/blocked domains. No code changes required.
    
*   **Added: New `LLMConfig` parameter.** This new parameter can be passed for extraction, filtering, and schema generation tasks. It simplifies passing provider strings, API tokens, and base URLs across all sections where LLM configuration is necessary. It also enables reuse and allows for quick experimentation between different LLM configurations.
    

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-1) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-2) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-5) # Example of using LLMConfig with LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-6) llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o", api_token="YOUR_API_KEY") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-7) strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy(llm_config=llm_config, schema=...) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-9) # Example usage within a crawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-10) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-11)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-12)         url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-13)         config=CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.5.0/#__codelineno-11-14)     )`

**Breaking Change:** Removed old parameters like `provider`, `api_token`, `base_url`, and `api_base` from `LLMExtractionStrategy` and `LLMContentFilter`. Users should migrate to using the `LLMConfig` object.

*   **Changed: Improved browser context management and added shared data support. (Breaking Change:** `BrowserContext` API updated). Browser contexts are now managed more efficiently, reducing resource usage. A new `shared_data` dictionary is available in the `BrowserContext` to allow passing data between different stages of the crawling process. **Breaking Change:** The `BrowserContext` API has changed, and the old `get_context` method is deprecated.
    
*   **Changed:** Renamed `final_url` to `redirected_url` in `CrawledURL`. This improves consistency and clarity. Update any code referencing the old field name.
    
*   **Changed:** Improved type hints and removed unused files. This is an internal improvement and should not require code changes.
    
*   **Changed:** Reorganized deep crawling functionality into dedicated module. (**Breaking Change:** Import paths for `DeepCrawlStrategy` and related classes have changed). This improves code organization. Update imports to use the new `crawl4ai.deep_crawling` module.
    
*   **Changed:** Improved HTML handling and cleanup codebase. (**Breaking Change:** Removed `ssl_certificate.json` file). This removes an unused file. If you were relying on this file for custom certificate validation, you'll need to implement an alternative approach.
    
*   **Changed:** Enhanced serialization and config handling. (**Breaking Change:** `FastFilterChain` has been replaced with `FilterChain`). This change simplifies config and improves the serialization.
    
*   **Added:** Modified the license to Apache 2.0 _with a required attribution clause_. See the `LICENSE` file for details. All users must now clearly attribute the Crawl4AI project when using, distributing, or creating derivative works.
    
*   **Fixed:** Prevent memory leaks by ensuring proper closure of Playwright pages. No code changes required.
    
*   **Fixed:** Make model fields optional with default values (**Breaking Change:** Code relying on all fields being present may need adjustment). Fields in data models (like `CrawledURL`) are now optional, with default values (usually `None`). Update code to handle potential `None` values.
    
*   **Fixed:** Adjust memory threshold and fix dispatcher initialization. This is an internal bug fix; no code changes are required.
    
*   **Fixed:** Ensure proper exit after running doctor command. No code changes are required.
    
*   **Fixed:** JsonCss selector and crawler improvements.
*   **Fixed:** Not working long page screenshot (#403)
*   **Documentation:** Updated documentation URLs to the new domain.
*   **Documentation:** Added SERP API project example.
*   **Documentation:** Added clarifying comments for CSS selector behavior.
*   **Documentation:** Add Code of Conduct for the project (#410)

Breaking Changes Summary
------------------------

*   **Dispatcher:** The `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` is now the default for `arun_many()`, changing concurrency behavior. The return type of `arun_many` depends on the `stream` parameter.
*   **Deep Crawling:** `max_depth` is now part of `CrawlerRunConfig` and controls crawl depth. Import paths for deep crawling strategies have changed.
*   **Browser Context:** The `BrowserContext` API has been updated.
*   **Models:** Many fields in data models are now optional, with default values.
*   **Scraping Mode:** `ScrapingMode` enum replaced by strategy pattern (`WebScrapingStrategy`, `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy`).
*   **Content Filter:** Removed `content_filter` parameter from `CrawlerRunConfig`. Use extraction strategies or markdown generators with filters instead.
*   **Removed:** Synchronous `WebCrawler`, CLI, and docs management functionality.
*   **Docker:** Significant changes to Docker deployment, including new requirements and configuration.
*   **File Removed**: Removed ssl\_certificate.json file which might affect existing certificate validations
*   **Renamed**: final\_url to redirected\_url for consistency
*   **Config**: FastFilterChain has been replaced with FilterChain
*   **Deep-Crawl**: DeepCrawlStrategy.arun now returns Union\[CrawlResultT, List\[CrawlResultT\], AsyncGenerator\[CrawlResultT, None\]\]
*   **Proxy**: Removed synchronous WebCrawler support and related rate limiting configurations

Migration Guide
---------------

1.  **Update Imports:** Adjust imports for `DeepCrawlStrategy`, `BreadthFirstSearchStrategy`, and related classes due to the new `deep_crawling` module structure.
2.  **`CrawlerRunConfig`:** Move `max_depth` to `CrawlerRunConfig`. If using `content_filter`, migrate to an extraction strategy or a markdown generator with a filter.
3.  **`arun_many()`:** Adapt code to the new `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` behavior and the return type.
4.  **`BrowserContext`:** Update code using the `BrowserContext` API.
5.  **Models:** Handle potential `None` values for optional fields in data models.
6.  **Scraping:** Replace `ScrapingMode` enum with `WebScrapingStrategy` or `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy`.
7.  **Docker:** Review the updated Docker documentation and adjust your deployment accordingly.
8.  **CLI:** Migrate to the new `crwl` command and update any scripts using the old CLI.
9.  **Proxy:**: Removed synchronous WebCrawler support and related rate limiting configurations.
10.  **Config:**: Replace FastFilterChain to FilterChain

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Solving the Virtual Scroll Puzzle: How Crawl4AI Captures What Others Miss - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Solving the Virtual Scroll Puzzle: How Crawl4AI Captures What Others Miss
=========================================================================

_Published on June 29, 2025 • 10 min read_

_By [unclecode](https://x.com/unclecode)
 • Follow me on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/unclecode)
 for more web scraping insights_

* * *

The Invisible Content Crisis
----------------------------

You know that feeling when you're scrolling through Twitter, and suddenly realize you can't scroll back to that brilliant tweet from an hour ago? It's not your browser being quirky—it's virtual scrolling at work. And if this frustrates you as a user, imagine being a web scraper trying to capture all those tweets.

Here's the dirty secret of modern web development: **most of the content you see doesn't actually exist**.

Let me explain. Open Twitter right now and scroll for a bit. Now inspect the DOM. You'll find maybe 20-30 tweet elements, yet you just scrolled past hundreds. Where did they go? They were never really there—just temporary ghosts passing through a revolving door of DOM elements.

This is virtual scrolling, and it's everywhere: Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, Reddit, data tables, analytics dashboards. It's brilliant for performance but catastrophic for traditional web scraping.

The Great DOM Disappearing Act
------------------------------

Let's visualize what's happening:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-1) Traditional Infinite Scroll:         Virtual Scroll: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-2) ┌─────────────┐                     ┌─────────────┐ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-3) │ Item 1      │                     │ Item 11     │  ← Items 1-10? Gone. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-4) │ Item 2      │                     │ Item 12     │  ← Only what's visible [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-5) │ ...         │                     │ Item 13     │    exists in the DOM [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-6) │ Item 10     │                     │ Item 14     │ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-7) │ Item 11 NEW │                     │ Item 15     │ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-8) │ Item 12 NEW │                     └─────────────┘ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-9) └─────────────┘                      [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-0-10) DOM: 12 items & growing             DOM: Always ~5 items`

Traditional scrapers see this and capture... 5 items. Out of thousands. It's like trying to photograph a train by taking a picture of one window.

Why Virtual Scroll Broke Everything
-----------------------------------

When I first encountered this with Crawl4AI, I thought it was a bug. My scraper would perfectly capture the initial tweets, but scrolling did... nothing. The DOM element count stayed constant. The HTML size barely changed. Yet visually, new content kept appearing.

It took me embarrassingly long to realize: **the website was gaslighting my scraper**.

Virtual scroll is deceptively simple: 1. Keep only visible items in DOM (usually 10-30 elements) 2. As user scrolls down, remove top items, add bottom items 3. As user scrolls up, remove bottom items, add top items 4. Maintain the illusion of a continuous list

For users, it's seamless. For scrapers, it's a nightmare. Traditional approaches fail because: - `document.scrollingElement.scrollHeight` lies to you - Waiting for new elements is futile—they replace, not append - Screenshots only capture the current viewport - Even browser automation tools get fooled

The Three-State Solution
------------------------

After much experimentation (and several cups of coffee), I realized we needed to think differently. Instead of fighting virtual scroll, we needed to understand it. This led to identifying three distinct scrolling behaviors:

### State 1: No Change (The Stubborn Page)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-1-1) scroll() → same content → continue trying`

The page doesn't react to scrolling. Either we've hit the end, or it's not a scrollable container.

### State 2: Appending (The Traditional Friend)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-2-1) scroll() → old content + new content → all good!`

Classic infinite scroll. New content appends to existing content. Our traditional tools work fine here.

### State 3: Replacing (The Trickster)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-3-1) scroll() → completely different content → capture everything!`

Virtual scroll detected! Content is being replaced. This is where our new magic happens.

Introducing VirtualScrollConfig
-------------------------------

Here's how Crawl4AI solves this puzzle:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, VirtualScrollConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-3) # Configure virtual scroll handling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-4) virtual_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-5)     container_selector="#timeline",    # What to scroll [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-6)     scroll_count=30,                   # How many times [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-7)     scroll_by="container_height",      # How much each time [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-8)     wait_after_scroll=0.5             # Pause for content to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-11) # Use it in your crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-12) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-13)     virtual_scroll_config=virtual_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-14) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-16) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-17)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-18)         url="https://twitter.com/search?q=AI", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-19)         config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-20)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-4-21)     # result.html now contains ALL tweets, not just visible ones!`

But here's where it gets clever...

The Magic Behind the Scenes
---------------------------

When Crawl4AI encounters a virtual scroll container, it:

1.  **Takes a snapshot** of the initial HTML
2.  **Scrolls** by the configured amount
3.  **Waits** for the DOM to update
4.  **Compares** the new HTML with the previous
5.  **Detects** which of our three states we're in
6.  **For State 3** (virtual scroll), stores the HTML chunk
7.  **Repeats** until done
8.  **Merges** all chunks intelligently

The merging is crucial. We can't just concatenate HTML—we'd get duplicates. Instead, we: - Parse each chunk into elements - Create fingerprints using normalized text - Keep only unique elements - Maintain the original order - Return clean, complete HTML

Real-World Example: Capturing Twitter Threads
---------------------------------------------

Let's see this in action with a real Twitter thread:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-1) async def capture_twitter_thread(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-2)     # Configure for Twitter's specific behavior [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-3)     virtual_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-4)         container_selector="[data-testid='primaryColumn']", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-5)         scroll_count=50,  # Enough for long threads [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-6)         scroll_by="container_height", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-7)         wait_after_scroll=1.0  # Twitter needs time to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-10)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-11)         virtual_scroll_config=virtual_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-12)         # Also extract structured data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-13)         extraction_strategy=LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-14)             provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-15)             schema={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-16)                 "type": "array", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-17)                 "items": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-18)                     "type": "object", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-19)                     "properties": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-20)                         "author": {"type": "string"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-21)                         "content": {"type": "string"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-22)                         "timestamp": {"type": "string"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-23)                         "replies": {"type": "integer"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-24)                         "retweets": {"type": "integer"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-25)                         "likes": {"type": "integer"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-26)                     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-27)                 } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-28)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-29)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-30)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-32)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-33)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-34)             url="https://twitter.com/elonmusk/status/...", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-35)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-36)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-38)         # Parse the extracted tweets [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-39)         import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-40)         tweets = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-42)         print(f"Captured {len(tweets)} tweets from the thread") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-43)         for tweet in tweets[:5]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-5-44)             print(f"@{tweet['author']}: {tweet['content'][:100]}...")`

Performance Insights
--------------------

During testing, we achieved remarkable results:

| Site | Without Virtual Scroll | With Virtual Scroll | Improvement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Twitter Timeline | 10 tweets | 490 tweets | **49x** |
| Instagram Grid | 12 posts | 999 posts | **83x** |
| LinkedIn Feed | 5 posts | 200 posts | **40x** |
| Reddit Comments | 25 comments | 500 comments | **20x** |

The best part? It's automatic. If the page doesn't use virtual scroll, Crawl4AI handles it normally. No configuration changes needed.

When to Use Virtual Scroll
--------------------------

Use `VirtualScrollConfig` when: - ✅ Scrolling seems to "eat" previous content - ✅ DOM element count stays suspiciously constant - ✅ You're scraping Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, Reddit - ✅ Working with modern data tables or dashboards - ✅ Traditional scrolling captures only a fraction of content

Don't use it when: - ❌ Content accumulates normally (use `scan_full_page` instead) - ❌ Page has no scrollable containers - ❌ You only need the initially visible content - ❌ Working with static or traditionally paginated sites

Advanced Techniques
-------------------

### Handling Mixed Content

Some sites mix approaches—featured content stays while regular content virtualizes:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-1) # News site with pinned articles + virtual scroll feed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-2) virtual_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-3)     container_selector=".main-feed",  # Only the feed scrolls virtually [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-4)     scroll_count=30, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-5)     scroll_by="container_height" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-8) # Featured articles remain throughout the crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-6-9) # Regular articles are captured via virtual scroll`

### Optimizing Performance

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-1) # Fast scrolling for simple content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-2) fast_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-3)     container_selector="#feed", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-4)     scroll_count=100, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-5)     scroll_by=500,  # Fixed pixels for speed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-6)     wait_after_scroll=0.1  # Minimal wait [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-9) # Careful scrolling for complex content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-10) careful_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-11)     container_selector=".timeline", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-12)     scroll_count=50, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-13)     scroll_by="container_height", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-14)     wait_after_scroll=1.5  # More time for lazy loading [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-7-15) )`

### Debugging Virtual Scroll

Want to see it in action? Set `headless=False`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-8-1) browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-8-2) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-8-3)     # Watch the magic happen! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-8-4)     result = await crawler.arun(url="...", config=config)`

The Technical Deep Dive
-----------------------

For the curious, here's how our deduplication works:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-1) // Simplified version of our deduplication logic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-2) function createFingerprint(element) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-3)     const text = element.innerText [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-4)         .toLowerCase() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-5)         .replace(/[\s\W]/g, '');  // Remove spaces and symbols [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-6)     return text; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-7) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-9) function mergeChunks(chunks) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-10)     const seen = new Set(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-11)     const unique = []; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-13)     for (const chunk of chunks) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-14)         const elements = parseHTML(chunk); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-15)         for (const element of elements) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-16)             const fingerprint = createFingerprint(element); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-17)             if (!seen.has(fingerprint)) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-18)                 seen.add(fingerprint); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-19)                 unique.push(element); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-20)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-21)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-22)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-24)     return unique; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-9-25) }`

Simple, but effective. We normalize text to catch duplicates even with slight HTML differences.

What This Means for Web Scraping
--------------------------------

Virtual scroll support in Crawl4AI represents a paradigm shift. We're no longer limited to what's immediately visible or what traditional scrolling reveals. We can now capture the full content of virtually any modern website.

This opens new possibilities: - **Complete social media analysis**: Every tweet, every comment, every reaction - **Comprehensive data extraction**: Full tables, complete lists, entire feeds - **Historical research**: Capture entire timelines, not just recent posts - **Competitive intelligence**: See everything your competitors are showing their users

Try It Yourself
---------------

Ready to capture what others miss? Here's a complete example to get you started:
