`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-1) # Save this as virtual_scroll_demo.py [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, VirtualScrollConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-6)     # Configure virtual scroll [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-7)     virtual_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-8)         container_selector="#main-content",  # Adjust for your target [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-9)         scroll_count=20, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-10)         scroll_by="container_height", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-11)         wait_after_scroll=0.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-14)     # Set up the crawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-15)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-16)         virtual_scroll_config=virtual_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-17)         verbose=True  # See what's happening [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-18)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-20)     # Crawl and capture everything [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-21)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-22)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-23)             url="https://example.com/feed",  # Your target URL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-24)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-25)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-27)         print(f"Captured {len(result.html)} characters of content") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-28)         print(f"Found {result.html.count('article')} articles")  # Adjust selector [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-30) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/virtual-scroll-revolution/#__codelineno-10-31)     asyncio.run(main())`

Conclusion: The Future is Already Here
--------------------------------------

Virtual scrolling was supposed to be the end of comprehensive web scraping. Instead, it became the catalyst for smarter, more sophisticated tools. With Crawl4AI's virtual scroll support, we're not just keeping up with modern web development—we're staying ahead of it.

The web is evolving, becoming more dynamic, more efficient, and yes, more challenging to scrape. But with the right tools and understanding, every challenge becomes an opportunity.

Welcome to the future of web scraping. Welcome to a world where virtual scroll is no longer a barrier, but just another feature we handle seamlessly.

* * *

*   📖 [Virtual Scroll Documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/virtual-scroll)
     - Complete API reference and configuration options
*   💻 [Interactive Examples](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/examples/virtual_scroll_example.py)
     - Try it yourself with our test server
*   🚀 [Get Started with Crawl4AI](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/quickstart)
     - Full installation and setup guide
*   🤝 [Join our Community](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai)
     - Share your experiences and get help

!_

# Migration Guide: Table Extraction v0.7.3 - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Migration Guide: Table Extraction v0.7.3
========================================

Overview
--------

Version 0.7.3 introduces the **Table Extraction Strategy Pattern**, providing a more flexible and extensible approach to table extraction while maintaining full backward compatibility.

What's New
----------

### Strategy Pattern Implementation

Table extraction now follows the same strategy pattern used throughout Crawl4AI:

*   **Consistent Architecture**: Aligns with extraction, chunking, and markdown strategies
*   **Extensibility**: Easy to create custom table extraction strategies
*   **Better Separation**: Table logic moved from content scraping to dedicated module
*   **Full Control**: Fine-grained control over table detection and extraction

### New Classes

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-0-1) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-0-2)     TableExtractionStrategy,    # Abstract base class [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-0-3)     DefaultTableExtraction,      # Current implementation (default) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-0-4)     NoTableExtraction           # Explicitly disable extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

Backward Compatibility
----------------------

**✅ All existing code continues to work without changes.**

### No Changes Required

If your code looks like this, it will continue to work:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-1-1) # This still works exactly the same [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-1-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-1-3)     table_score_threshold=7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-1-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-1-5) result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-1-6) tables = result.tables  # Same structure, same data`

### What Happens Behind the Scenes

When you don't specify a `table_extraction` strategy:

1.  `CrawlerRunConfig` automatically creates `DefaultTableExtraction`
2.  It uses your `table_score_threshold` parameter
3.  Tables are extracted exactly as before
4.  Results appear in `result.tables` with the same structure

New Capabilities
----------------

### 1\. Explicit Strategy Configuration

You can now explicitly configure table extraction:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-1) # New: Explicit control [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-2) strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-3)     table_score_threshold=7, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-4)     min_rows=2,              # New: minimum row filter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-5)     min_cols=2,              # New: minimum column filter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-6)     verbose=True             # New: detailed logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-9) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-10)     table_extraction=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-2-11) )`

### 2\. Disable Table Extraction

Improve performance when tables aren't needed:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-3-1) # New: Skip table extraction entirely [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-3-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-3-3)     table_extraction=NoTableExtraction() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-3-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-3-5) # No CPU cycles spent on table detection/extraction`

### 3\. Custom Extraction Strategies

Create specialized extractors:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-1) class MyTableExtractor(TableExtractionStrategy): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-2)     def extract_tables(self, element, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-3)         # Custom extraction logic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-4)         return custom_tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-6) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-7)     table_extraction=MyTableExtractor() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-4-8) )`

Migration Scenarios
-------------------

### Scenario 1: Basic Usage (No Changes Needed)

**Before (v0.7.2):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-5-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-5-2) result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-5-3) for table in result.tables: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-5-4)     print(table['headers'])`

**After (v0.7.3):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-6-1) # Exactly the same - no changes required [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-6-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-6-3) result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-6-4) for table in result.tables: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-6-5)     print(table['headers'])`

### Scenario 2: Custom Threshold (No Changes Needed)

**Before (v0.7.2):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-7-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-7-2)     table_score_threshold=5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-7-3) )`

**After (v0.7.3):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-1) # Still works the same [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-3)     table_score_threshold=5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-6) # Or use new explicit approach for more control [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-7) strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-8)     table_score_threshold=5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-9)     min_rows=2  # Additional filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-11) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-12)     table_extraction=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-8-13) )`

### Scenario 3: Advanced Filtering (New Feature)

**Before (v0.7.2):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-1) # Had to filter after extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-3)     table_score_threshold=5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-5) result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-7) # Manual filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-8) large_tables = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-9)     t for t in result.tables  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-10)     if len(t['rows']) >= 5 and len(t['headers']) >= 3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-9-11) ]`

**After (v0.7.3):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-1) # Filter during extraction (more efficient) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-2) strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-3)     table_score_threshold=5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-4)     min_rows=5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-5)     min_cols=3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-7) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-8)     table_extraction=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-10) result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-10-11) # result.tables already filtered`

Code Organization Changes
-------------------------

### Module Structure

**Before (v0.7.2):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-11-1) crawl4ai/ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-11-2)   content_scraping_strategy.py [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-11-3)     - LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-11-4)       - is_data_table()      # Table detection [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-11-5)       - extract_table_data() # Table extraction`

**After (v0.7.3):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-1) crawl4ai/ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-2)   content_scraping_strategy.py [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-3)     - LXMLWebScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-4)       # Table methods removed, uses strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-6)   table_extraction.py (NEW) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-7)     - TableExtractionStrategy    # Base class [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-8)     - DefaultTableExtraction      # Moved logic here [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-12-9)     - NoTableExtraction          # New option`

### Import Changes

**New imports available (optional):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-13-1) # These are now available but not required for existing code [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-13-2) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-13-3)     TableExtractionStrategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-13-4)     DefaultTableExtraction, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-13-5)     NoTableExtraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-13-6) )`

Performance Implications
------------------------

### No Performance Impact

For existing code, performance remains identical: - Same extraction logic - Same scoring algorithm - Same processing time

### Performance Improvements Available

New options for better performance:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-1) # Skip tables entirely (faster) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-3)     table_extraction=NoTableExtraction() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-6) # Process only specific areas (faster) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-7) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-8)     css_selector="main.content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-9)     table_extraction=DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-10)         min_rows=5,  # Skip small tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-11)         min_cols=3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-14-13) )`

Testing Your Migration
----------------------

### Verification Script

Run this to verify your extraction still works:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-4) async def verify_extraction(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-5)     url = "your_url_here" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-7)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-8)         # Test 1: Old approach [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-9)         config_old = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-10)             table_score_threshold=7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-11)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-12)         result_old = await crawler.arun(url, config_old) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-14)         # Test 2: New explicit approach [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-15)         from crawl4ai import DefaultTableExtraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-16)         config_new = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-17)             table_extraction=DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-18)                 table_score_threshold=7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-19)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-20)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-21)         result_new = await crawler.arun(url, config_new) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-23)         # Compare results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-24)         assert len(result_old.tables) == len(result_new.tables) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-25)         print(f"✓ Both approaches extracted {len(result_old.tables)} tables") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-27)         # Verify structure [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-28)         for old, new in zip(result_old.tables, result_new.tables): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-29)             assert old['headers'] == new['headers'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-30)             assert old['rows'] == new['rows'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-32)         print("✓ Table content identical") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-15-34) asyncio.run(verify_extraction())`

Deprecation Notes
-----------------

### No Deprecations

*   All existing parameters continue to work
*   `table_score_threshold` in `CrawlerRunConfig` is still supported
*   No breaking changes

### Internal Changes (Transparent to Users)

*   `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy.is_data_table()` - Moved to `DefaultTableExtraction`
*   `LXMLWebScrapingStrategy.extract_table_data()` - Moved to `DefaultTableExtraction`

These methods were internal and not part of the public API.

Benefits of Upgrading
---------------------

While not required, using the new pattern provides:

1.  **Better Control**: Filter tables during extraction, not after
2.  **Performance Options**: Skip extraction when not needed
3.  **Extensibility**: Create custom extractors for specific needs
4.  **Consistency**: Same pattern as other Crawl4AI strategies
5.  **Future-Proof**: Ready for upcoming advanced strategies

Troubleshooting
---------------

### Issue: Different Number of Tables

**Cause**: Threshold or filtering differences

**Solution**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-16-1) # Ensure same threshold [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-16-2) strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-16-3)     table_score_threshold=7,  # Match your old setting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-16-4)     min_rows=0,               # No filtering (default) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-16-5)     min_cols=0                # No filtering (default) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-16-6) )`

### Issue: Import Errors

**Cause**: Using new classes without importing

**Solution**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-17-1) # Add imports if using new features [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-17-2) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-17-3)     DefaultTableExtraction, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-17-4)     NoTableExtraction, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-17-5)     TableExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-17-6) )`

### Issue: Custom Strategy Not Working

**Cause**: Incorrect method signature

**Solution**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-18-1) class CustomExtractor(TableExtractionStrategy): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-18-2)     def extract_tables(self, element, **kwargs):  # Correct signature [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-18-3)         # Not: extract_tables(self, html) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-18-4)         # Not: extract(self, element) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/table_extraction_v073/#__codelineno-18-5)         return tables_list`

Getting Help
------------

If you encounter issues:

1.  Check your `table_score_threshold` matches previous settings
2.  Verify imports if using new classes
3.  Enable verbose logging: `DefaultTableExtraction(verbose=True)`
4.  Review the [Table Extraction Documentation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/)
    
5.  Check [examples](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/migration/examples/table_extraction_example.py)
    

Summary
-------

*   ✅ **Full backward compatibility** - No code changes required
*   ✅ **Same results** - Identical extraction behavior by default
*   ✅ **New options** - Additional control when needed
*   ✅ **Better architecture** - Consistent with Crawl4AI patterns
*   ✅ **Ready for future** - Foundation for advanced strategies

The migration to v0.7.3 is seamless with no required changes while providing new capabilities for those who need them.

* * *

\> Feedback

---

# Self-Hosting Guide - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Self-Hosting Crawl4AI 🚀
========================

**Take Control of Your Web Crawling Infrastructure**

Self-hosting Crawl4AI gives you complete control over your web crawling and data extraction pipeline. Unlike cloud-based solutions, you own your data, infrastructure, and destiny.

Why Self-Host?
--------------

*   **🔒 Data Privacy**: Your crawled data never leaves your infrastructure
*   **💰 Cost Control**: No per-request pricing - scale within your own resources
*   **🎯 Customization**: Full control over browser configurations, extraction strategies, and performance tuning
*   **📊 Transparency**: Real-time monitoring dashboard shows exactly what's happening
*   **⚡ Performance**: Direct access without API rate limits or geographic restrictions
*   **🛡️ Security**: Keep sensitive data extraction workflows behind your firewall
*   **🔧 Flexibility**: Customize, extend, and integrate with your existing infrastructure

When you self-host, you can scale from a single container to a full browser infrastructure, all while maintaining complete control and visibility.

Table of Contents
-----------------

*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#prerequisites)
    
*   [Installation](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#installation)
    
*   [Option 1: Using Pre-built Docker Hub Images (Recommended)](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#option-1-using-pre-built-docker-hub-images-recommended)
    
*   [Option 2: Using Docker Compose](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#option-2-using-docker-compose)
    
*   [Option 3: Manual Local Build & Run](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#option-3-manual-local-build--run)
    
*   [MCP (Model Context Protocol) Support](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#mcp-model-context-protocol-support)
    
*   [What is MCP?](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#what-is-mcp)
    
*   [Connecting via MCP](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#connecting-via-mcp)
    
*   [Using with Claude Code](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#using-with-claude-code)
    
*   [Available MCP Tools](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#available-mcp-tools)
    
*   [Testing MCP Connections](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#testing-mcp-connections)
    
*   [MCP Schemas](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#mcp-schemas)
    
*   [Real-time Monitoring & Operations](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#real-time-monitoring--operations)
    
*   [Monitoring Dashboard](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#monitoring-dashboard)
    
*   [Monitor API Endpoints](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#monitor-api-endpoints)
    
*   [WebSocket Streaming](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#websocket-streaming)
    
*   [Control Actions](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#control-actions)
    
*   [Production Integration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#production-integration)
    
*   [Deployment Scenarios](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#deployment-scenarios)
    
*   [Complete Examples](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#complete-examples)
    
*   [Server Configuration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#server-configuration)
    
*   [Understanding config.yml](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#understanding-configyml)
    
*   [JWT Authentication](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#jwt-authentication)
    
*   [Configuration Tips and Best Practices](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#configuration-tips-and-best-practices)
    
*   [Customizing Your Configuration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#customizing-your-configuration)
    
*   [Configuration Recommendations](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#configuration-recommendations)
    
*   [Getting Help](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#getting-help)
    
*   [Summary](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#summary)
    

Prerequisites
-------------

Before we dive in, make sure you have: - Docker installed and running (version 20.10.0 or higher), including `docker compose` (usually bundled with Docker Desktop). - `git` for cloning the repository. - At least 4GB of RAM available for the container (more recommended for heavy use). - Python 3.10+ (if using the Python SDK). - Node.js 16+ (if using the Node.js examples).

> 💡 **Pro tip**: Run `docker info` to check your Docker installation and available resources.

Installation
------------

We offer several ways to get the Crawl4AI server running. The quickest way is to use our pre-built Docker Hub images.

### Option 1: Using Pre-built Docker Hub Images (Recommended)

Pull and run images directly from Docker Hub without building locally.

#### 1\. Pull the Image

Our latest release is `0.8.0`. Images are built with multi-arch manifests, so Docker automatically pulls the correct version for your system.

> 💡 **Note**: The `latest` tag points to the stable `0.8.0` version.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-0-1) # Pull the latest version [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-0-2) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:0.8.0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-0-4) # Or pull using the latest tag [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-0-5) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:latest`

#### 2\. Setup Environment (API Keys)

If you plan to use LLMs, create a `.llm.env` file in your working directory:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-1) # Create a .llm.env file with your API keys [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-2) cat > .llm.env << EOL [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-3) # OpenAI [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-4) OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-your-key [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-6) # Anthropic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-7) ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-anthropic-key [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-9) # Other providers as needed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-10) # DEEPSEEK_API_KEY=your-deepseek-key [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-11) # GROQ_API_KEY=your-groq-key [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-12) # TOGETHER_API_KEY=your-together-key [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-13) # MISTRAL_API_KEY=your-mistral-key [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-14) # GEMINI_API_TOKEN=your-gemini-token [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-16) # Optional: Global LLM settings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-17) # LLM_PROVIDER=openai/gpt-4o-mini [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-18) # LLM_TEMPERATURE=0.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-19) # LLM_BASE_URL=https://api.custom.com/v1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-21) # Optional: Provider-specific overrides [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-22) # OPENAI_TEMPERATURE=0.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-23) # OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://custom-openai.com/v1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-24) # ANTHROPIC_TEMPERATURE=0.3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-1-25) EOL`

> 🔑 **Note**: Keep your API keys secure! Never commit `.llm.env` to version control.

#### 3\. Run the Container

*   **Basic run:**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-2-1) docker run -d \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-2-2)   -p 11235:11235 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-2-3)   --name crawl4ai \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-2-4)   --shm-size=1g \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-2-5)   unclecode/crawl4ai:latest`
    
*   **With LLM support:**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-3-1) # Make sure .llm.env is in the current directory [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-3-2) docker run -d \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-3-3)   -p 11235:11235 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-3-4)   --name crawl4ai \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-3-5)   --env-file .llm.env \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-3-6)   --shm-size=1g \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-3-7)   unclecode/crawl4ai:latest`
    

> The server will be available at `http://localhost:11235`. Visit `/playground` to access the interactive testing interface.

#### 4\. Stopping the Container

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-4-1) docker stop crawl4ai && docker rm crawl4ai`

#### Docker Hub Versioning Explained

*   **Image Name:** `unclecode/crawl4ai`
*   **Tag Format:** `LIBRARY_VERSION[-SUFFIX]` (e.g., `0.8.0`)
    *   `LIBRARY_VERSION`: The semantic version of the core `crawl4ai` Python library
    *   `SUFFIX`: Optional tag for release candidates (`` `) and revisions ( ``r1\`)
*   **`latest` Tag:** Points to the most recent stable version
*   **Multi-Architecture Support:** All images support both `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` architectures through a single tag

### Option 2: Using Docker Compose

Docker Compose simplifies building and running the service, especially for local development and testing.

#### 1\. Clone Repository

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-5-1) git clone https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai.git [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-5-2) cd crawl4ai`

#### 2\. Environment Setup (API Keys)

If you plan to use LLMs, copy the example environment file and add your API keys. This file should be in the **project root directory**.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-6-1) # Make sure you are in the 'crawl4ai' root directory [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-6-2) cp deploy/docker/.llm.env.example .llm.env [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-6-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-6-4) # Now edit .llm.env and add your API keys`

**Flexible LLM Provider Configuration:**

The Docker setup now supports flexible LLM provider configuration through a hierarchical system:

1.  **API Request Parameters** (Highest Priority): Specify per request
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-7-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-7-2)   "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-7-3)   "f": "llm", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-7-4)   "provider": "groq/mixtral-8x7b", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-7-5)   "temperature": 0.7, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-7-6)   "base_url": "https://api.custom.com/v1" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-7-7) }`
    
2.  **Provider-Specific Environment Variables**: Override for specific providers
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-8-1) # In your .llm.env file: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-8-2) OPENAI_TEMPERATURE=0.5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-8-3) OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://custom-openai.com/v1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-8-4) ANTHROPIC_TEMPERATURE=0.3`
    
3.  **Global Environment Variables**: Set defaults for all providers
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-9-1) # In your .llm.env file: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-9-2) LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic/claude-3-opus [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-9-3) LLM_TEMPERATURE=0.7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-9-4) LLM_BASE_URL=https://api.proxy.com/v1`
    
4.  **Config File Default**: Falls back to `config.yml` (default: `openai/gpt-4o-mini`)
    

The system automatically selects the appropriate API key based on the provider. LiteLLM handles finding the correct environment variable for each provider (e.g., OPENAI\_API\_KEY for OpenAI, GEMINI\_API\_TOKEN for Google Gemini, etc.).

**Supported LLM Parameters:** - `provider`: LLM provider and model (e.g., "openai/gpt-4", "anthropic/claude-3-opus") - `temperature`: Controls randomness (0.0-2.0, lower = more focused, higher = more creative) - `base_url`: Custom API endpoint for proxy servers or alternative endpoints

#### 3\. Build and Run with Compose

The `docker-compose.yml` file in the project root provides a simplified approach that automatically handles architecture detection using buildx.

*   **Run Pre-built Image from Docker Hub:**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-10-1) # Pulls and runs the release candidate from Docker Hub [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-10-2) # Automatically selects the correct architecture [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-10-3) IMAGE=unclecode/crawl4ai:latest docker compose up -d`
    
*   **Build and Run Locally:**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-11-1) # Builds the image locally using Dockerfile and runs it [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-11-2) # Automatically uses the correct architecture for your machine [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-11-3) docker compose up --build -d`
    
*   **Customize the Build:**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-12-1) # Build with all features (includes torch and transformers) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-12-2) INSTALL_TYPE=all docker compose up --build -d [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-12-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-12-4) # Build with GPU support (for AMD64 platforms) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-12-5) ENABLE_GPU=true docker compose up --build -d`
    

> The server will be available at `http://localhost:11235`.

#### 4\. Stopping the Service

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-13-1) # Stop the service [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-13-2) docker compose down`

### Option 3: Manual Local Build & Run

If you prefer not to use Docker Compose for direct control over the build and run process.

#### 1\. Clone Repository & Setup Environment

Follow steps 1 and 2 from the Docker Compose section above (clone repo, `cd crawl4ai`, create `.llm.env` in the root).

#### 2\. Build the Image (Multi-Arch)

Use `docker buildx` to build the image. Crawl4AI now uses buildx to handle multi-architecture builds automatically.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-1) # Make sure you are in the 'crawl4ai' root directory [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-2) # Build for the current architecture and load it into Docker [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-3) docker buildx build -t crawl4ai-local:latest --load . [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-5) # Or build for multiple architectures (useful for publishing) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-6) docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -t crawl4ai-local:latest --load . [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-8) # Build with additional options [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-9) docker buildx build \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-10)   --build-arg INSTALL_TYPE=all \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-11)   --build-arg ENABLE_GPU=false \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-14-12)   -t crawl4ai-local:latest --load .`

#### 3\. Run the Container

*   **Basic run (no LLM support):**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-15-1) docker run -d \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-15-2)   -p 11235:11235 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-15-3)   --name crawl4ai-standalone \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-15-4)   --shm-size=1g \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-15-5)   crawl4ai-local:latest`
    
*   **With LLM support:**
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-16-1) # Make sure .llm.env is in the current directory (project root) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-16-2) docker run -d \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-16-3)   -p 11235:11235 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-16-4)   --name crawl4ai-standalone \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-16-5)   --env-file .llm.env \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-16-6)   --shm-size=1g \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-16-7)   crawl4ai-local:latest`
    

> The server will be available at `http://localhost:11235`.

#### 4\. Stopping the Manual Container

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-17-1) docker stop crawl4ai-standalone && docker rm crawl4ai-standalone`

* * *

MCP (Model Context Protocol) Support
------------------------------------

Crawl4AI server includes support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP), allowing you to connect the server's capabilities directly to MCP-compatible clients like Claude Code.

### What is MCP?

MCP is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to LLMs. It allows AI models to access external tools, data sources, and services through a standardized interface.

### Connecting via MCP

The Crawl4AI server exposes two MCP endpoints:

*   **Server-Sent Events (SSE)**: `http://localhost:11235/mcp/sse`
*   **WebSocket**: `ws://localhost:11235/mcp/ws`

### Using with Claude Code

You can add Crawl4AI as an MCP tool provider in Claude Code with a simple command:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-18-1) # Add the Crawl4AI server as an MCP provider [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-18-2) claude mcp add --transport sse c4ai-sse http://localhost:11235/mcp/sse [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-18-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-18-4) # List all MCP providers to verify it was added [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-18-5) claude mcp list`

Once connected, Claude Code can directly use Crawl4AI's capabilities like screenshot capture, PDF generation, and HTML processing without having to make separate API calls.

### Available MCP Tools

When connected via MCP, the following tools are available:

*   `md` - Generate markdown from web content
*   `html` - Extract preprocessed HTML
*   `screenshot` - Capture webpage screenshots
*   `pdf` - Generate PDF documents
*   `execute_js` - Run JavaScript on web pages
*   `crawl` - Perform multi-URL crawling
*   `ask` - Query the Crawl4AI library context

### Testing MCP Connections

You can test the MCP WebSocket connection using the test file included in the repository:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-19-1) # From the repository root [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-19-2) python tests/mcp/test_mcp_socket.py`

### MCP Schemas

Access the MCP tool schemas at `http://localhost:11235/mcp/schema` for detailed information on each tool's parameters and capabilities.

* * *

Additional API Endpoints
------------------------

In addition to the core `/crawl` and `/crawl/stream` endpoints, the server provides several specialized endpoints:

### HTML Extraction Endpoint

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-20-1) POST /html`

Crawls the URL and returns preprocessed HTML optimized for schema extraction.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-21-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-21-2)   "url": "https://example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-21-3) }`

### Screenshot Endpoint

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-22-1) POST /screenshot`

Captures a full-page PNG screenshot of the specified URL.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-23-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-23-2)   "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-23-3)   "screenshot_wait_for": 2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-23-4)   "output_path": "/path/to/save/screenshot.png" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-23-5) }`

*   `screenshot_wait_for`: Optional delay in seconds before capture (default: 2)
*   `output_path`: Optional path to save the screenshot (recommended)

### PDF Export Endpoint

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-24-1) POST /pdf`

Generates a PDF document of the specified URL.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-25-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-25-2)   "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-25-3)   "output_path": "/path/to/save/document.pdf" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-25-4) }`

*   `output_path`: Optional path to save the PDF (recommended)

### JavaScript Execution Endpoint

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-26-1) POST /execute_js`

Executes JavaScript snippets on the specified URL and returns the full crawl result.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-27-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-27-2)   "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-27-3)   "scripts": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-27-4)     "return document.title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-27-5)     "return Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a => a.href)" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-27-6)   ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-27-7) }`

*   `scripts`: List of JavaScript snippets to execute sequentially

* * *

User-Provided Hooks API
-----------------------

The Docker API supports user-provided hook functions, allowing you to customize the crawling behavior by injecting your own Python code at specific points in the crawling pipeline. This powerful feature enables authentication, performance optimization, and custom content extraction without modifying the server code.

> ⚠️ **IMPORTANT SECURITY WARNING**: - **Never use hooks with untrusted code or on untrusted websites** - **Be extremely careful when crawling sites that might be phishing or malicious** - **Hook code has access to page context and can interact with the website** - **Always validate and sanitize any data extracted through hooks** - **Never expose credentials or sensitive data in hook code** - **Consider running the Docker container in an isolated network when testing**

### Hook Information Endpoint

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-28-1) GET /hooks/info`

Returns information about available hook points and their signatures:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-29-1) curl http://localhost:11235/hooks/info`

### Available Hook Points

The API supports 8 hook points that match the local SDK:

| Hook Point | Parameters | Description | Best Use Cases |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `on_browser_created` | `browser` | After browser instance creation | Light setup tasks |
| `on_page_context_created` | `page, context` | After page/context creation | **Authentication, cookies, route blocking** |
| `before_goto` | `page, context, url` | Before navigating to URL | Custom headers, logging |
| `after_goto` | `page, context, url, response` | After navigation completes | Verification, waiting for elements |
| `on_user_agent_updated` | `page, context, user_agent` | When user agent changes | UA-specific logic |
| `on_execution_started` | `page, context` | When JS execution begins | JS-related setup |
| `before_retrieve_html` | `page, context` | Before getting final HTML | **Scrolling, lazy loading** |
| `before_return_html` | `page, context, html` | Before returning HTML | Final modifications, metrics |

### Using Hooks in Requests

Add hooks to any crawl request by including the `hooks` parameter:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-2)   "urls": ["https://httpbin.org/html"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-3)   "hooks": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-4)     "code": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-5)       "hook_point_name": "async def hook(...): ...", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-6)       "another_hook": "async def hook(...): ..." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-7)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-8)     "timeout": 30  // Optional, default 30 seconds (max 120) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-9)   } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-30-10) }`

### Hook Examples with Real URLs

#### 1\. Authentication with Cookies (GitHub)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-1) import requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-3) # Example: Setting GitHub session cookie (use your actual session) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-4) hooks_code = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-5)     "on_page_context_created": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-6) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-7)     # Add authentication cookies for GitHub [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-8)     # WARNING: Never hardcode real credentials! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-9)     await context.add_cookies([ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-10)         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-11)             'name': 'user_session', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-12)             'value': 'your_github_session_token',  # Replace with actual token [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-13)             'domain': '.github.com', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-14)             'path': '/', [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-15)             'httpOnly': True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-16)             'secure': True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-17)             'sameSite': 'Lax' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-18)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-19)     ]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-20)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-21) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-22) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-24) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-25)     "urls": ["https://github.com/settings/profile"],  # Protected page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-26)     "hooks": {"code": hooks_code, "timeout": 30} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-31-27) })`

#### 2\. Basic Authentication (httpbin.org for testing)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-1) # Safe testing with httpbin.org (a service designed for HTTP testing) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-2) hooks_code = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-3)     "before_goto": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-4) async def hook(page, context, url, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-5)     import base64 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-6)     # httpbin.org/basic-auth expects username="user" and password="passwd" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-7)     credentials = base64.b64encode(b"user:passwd").decode('ascii') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-9)     await page.set_extra_http_headers({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-10)         'Authorization': f'Basic {credentials}' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-11)     }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-12)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-13) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-14) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-16) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-17)     "urls": ["https://httpbin.org/basic-auth/user/passwd"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-18)     "hooks": {"code": hooks_code, "timeout": 15} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-32-19) })`

#### 3\. Performance Optimization (News Sites)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-1) # Example: Optimizing crawling of news sites like CNN or BBC [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-2) hooks_code = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-3)     "on_page_context_created": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-4) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-5)     # Block images, fonts, and media to speed up crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-6)     await context.route("**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,webp,svg,ico}", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-7)     await context.route("**/*.{woff,woff2,ttf,otf,eot}", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-8)     await context.route("**/*.{mp4,webm,ogg,mp3,wav,flac}", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-10)     # Block common tracking and ad domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-11)     await context.route("**/googletagmanager.com/*", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-12)     await context.route("**/google-analytics.com/*", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-13)     await context.route("**/doubleclick.net/*", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-14)     await context.route("**/facebook.com/tr/*", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-15)     await context.route("**/amazon-adsystem.com/*", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-17)     # Disable CSS animations for faster rendering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-18)     await page.add_style_tag(content=''' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-19)         *, *::before, *::after { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-20)             animation-duration: 0s !important; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-21)             transition-duration: 0s !important; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-22)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-23)     ''') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-25)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-26) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-27) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-29) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-30)     "urls": ["https://www.bbc.com/news"],  # Heavy news site [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-31)     "hooks": {"code": hooks_code, "timeout": 30} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-33-32) })`

#### 4\. Handling Infinite Scroll (Twitter/X)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-1) # Example: Scrolling on Twitter/X (requires authentication) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-2) hooks_code = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-3)     "before_retrieve_html": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-4) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-5)     # Scroll to load more tweets [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-6)     previous_height = 0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-7)     for i in range(5):  # Limit scrolls to avoid infinite loop [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-8)         current_height = await page.evaluate("document.body.scrollHeight") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-9)         if current_height == previous_height: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-10)             break  # No more content to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-12)         await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-13)         await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)  # Wait for content to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-14)         previous_height = current_height [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-16)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-17) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-18) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-20) # Note: Twitter requires authentication for most content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-21) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-22)     "urls": ["https://twitter.com/nasa"],  # Public profile [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-23)     "hooks": {"code": hooks_code, "timeout": 30} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-34-24) })`

#### 5\. E-commerce Login (Example Pattern)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-1) # SECURITY WARNING: This is a pattern example.  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-2) # Never use real credentials in code! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-3) # Always use environment variables or secure vaults. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-5) hooks_code = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-6)     "on_page_context_created": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-7) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-8)     # Example pattern for e-commerce sites [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-9)     # DO NOT use real credentials here! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-11)     # Navigate to login page first [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-12)     await page.goto("https://example-shop.com/login") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-14)     # Wait for login form to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-15)     await page.wait_for_selector("#email", timeout=5000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-17)     # Fill login form (use environment variables in production!) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-18)     await page.fill("#email", "test@example.com")  # Never use real email [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-19)     await page.fill("#password", "test_password")   # Never use real password [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-21)     # Handle "Remember Me" checkbox if present [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-22)     try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-23)         await page.uncheck("#remember_me")  # Don't remember on shared systems [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-24)     except: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-25)         pass [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-27)     # Submit form [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-28)     await page.click("button[type='submit']") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-30)     # Wait for redirect after login [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-31)     await page.wait_for_url("**/account/**", timeout=10000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-33)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-34) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-35-35) }`

#### 6\. Extracting Structured Data (Wikipedia)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-1) # Safe example using Wikipedia [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-2) hooks_code = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-3)     "after_goto": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-4) async def hook(page, context, url, response, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-5)     # Wait for Wikipedia content to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-6)     await page.wait_for_selector("#content", timeout=5000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-7)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-8) """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-10)     "before_retrieve_html": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-11) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-12)     # Extract structured data from Wikipedia infobox [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-13)     metadata = await page.evaluate('''() => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-14)         const infobox = document.querySelector('.infobox'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-15)         if (!infobox) return null; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-17)         const data = {}; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-18)         const rows = infobox.querySelectorAll('tr'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-20)         rows.forEach(row => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-21)             const header = row.querySelector('th'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-22)             const value = row.querySelector('td'); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-23)             if (header && value) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-24)                 data[header.innerText.trim()] = value.innerText.trim(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-25)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-26)         }); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-28)         return data; [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-29)     }''') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-31)     if metadata: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-32)         print("Extracted metadata:", metadata) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-34)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-35) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-36) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-38) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl", json={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-39)     "urls": ["https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language)"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-40)     "hooks": {"code": hooks_code, "timeout": 20} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-36-41) })`

### Security Best Practices

> 🔒 **Critical Security Guidelines**:

1.  **Never Trust User Input**: If accepting hook code from users, always validate and sandbox it
2.  **Avoid Phishing Sites**: Never use hooks on suspicious or unverified websites
3.  **Protect Credentials**:
4.  Never hardcode passwords, tokens, or API keys in hook code
5.  Use environment variables or secure secret management
6.  Rotate credentials regularly
7.  **Network Isolation**: Run the Docker container in an isolated network when testing
8.  **Audit Hook Code**: Always review hook code before execution
9.  **Limit Permissions**: Use the least privileged access needed
10.  **Monitor Execution**: Check hook execution logs for suspicious behavior
11.  **Timeout Protection**: Always set reasonable timeouts (default 30s)

### Hook Response Information

When hooks are used, the response includes detailed execution information:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-2)   "success": true, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-3)   "results": [...], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-4)   "hooks": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-5)     "status": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-6)       "status": "success",  // or "partial" or "failed" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-7)       "attached_hooks": ["on_page_context_created", "before_retrieve_html"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-8)       "validation_errors": [], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-9)       "successfully_attached": 2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-10)       "failed_validation": 0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-11)     }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-12)     "execution_log": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-13)       { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-14)         "hook_point": "on_page_context_created", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-15)         "status": "success", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-16)         "execution_time": 0.523, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-17)         "timestamp": 1234567890.123 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-18)       } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-19)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-20)     "errors": [],  // Any runtime errors [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-21)     "summary": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-22)       "total_executions": 2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-23)       "successful": 2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-24)       "failed": 0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-25)       "timed_out": 0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-26)       "success_rate": 100.0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-27)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-28)   } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-37-29) }`

### Error Handling

The hooks system is designed to be resilient:

1.  **Validation Errors**: Caught before execution (syntax errors, wrong parameters)
2.  **Runtime Errors**: Handled gracefully - crawl continues with original page object
3.  **Timeout Protection**: Hooks automatically terminated after timeout (configurable 1-120s)

### Complete Example: Safe Multi-Hook Crawling

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-1) import requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-3) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-5) # Safe example using httpbin.org for testing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-6) hooks_code = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-7)     "on_page_context_created": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-8) async def hook(page, context, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-9)     # Set viewport and test cookies [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-10)     await page.set_viewport_size({"width": 1920, "height": 1080}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-11)     await context.add_cookies([ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-12)         {"name": "test_cookie", "value": "test_value", "domain": ".httpbin.org", "path": "/"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-13)     ]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-15)     # Block unnecessary resources for httpbin [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-16)     await context.route("**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg}", lambda route: route.abort()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-17)     return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-18) """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-20)     "before_goto": """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-21) async def hook(page, context, url, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/self-hosting/#__codelineno-38-22)     #
