# C4A-Script - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

C4A-Script: Visual Web Automation Made Simple
=============================================

What is C4A-Script?
-------------------

C4A-Script is a powerful, human-readable domain-specific language (DSL) designed for web automation and interaction. Think of it as a simplified programming language that anyone can read and write, perfect for automating repetitive web tasks, testing user interfaces, or creating interactive demos.

### Why C4A-Script?

**Simple Syntax, Powerful Results**

`` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-0-1) # Navigate and interact in plain English [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-0-2) GO https://example.com [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-0-3) WAIT `#search-box` 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-0-4) TYPE "Hello World" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-0-5) CLICK `button[type="submit"]` ``

**Visual Programming Support** C4A-Script comes with a built-in Blockly visual editor, allowing you to create scripts by dragging and dropping blocks - no coding experience required!

**Perfect for:** - **UI Testing**: Automate user interaction flows - **Demo Creation**: Build interactive product demonstrations  
\- **Data Entry**: Automate form filling and submissions - **Testing Workflows**: Validate complex user journeys - **Training**: Teach web automation without code complexity

Getting Started: Your First Script
----------------------------------

Let's create a simple script that searches for something on a website:

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-1) # My first C4A-Script [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-2) GO https://duckduckgo.com [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-4) # Wait for the search box to appear [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-5) WAIT `input[name="q"]` 10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-7) # Type our search query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-8) TYPE "Crawl4AI" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-10) # Press Enter to search [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-11) PRESS Enter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-13) # Wait for results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-1-14) WAIT `.results` 5``

That's it! In just a few lines, you've automated a complete search workflow.

Interactive Tutorial & Live Demo
--------------------------------

Want to learn by doing? We've got you covered:

**🚀 [Live Demo](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/apps/c4a-script/)
** - Try C4A-Script in your browser right now!

**📁 [Tutorial Examples](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/blob/main/docs/examples/c4a_script/)
** - Complete examples with source code

### Running the Tutorial Locally

The tutorial includes a Flask-based web interface with: - **Live Code Editor** with syntax highlighting - **Visual Blockly Editor** for drag-and-drop programming - **Recording Mode** to capture your actions and generate scripts - **Timeline View** to see and edit your automation steps

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-1) # Clone and navigate to the tutorial [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-2) cd docs/examples/c4a_script/tutorial/ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-4) # Install dependencies [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-5) pip install -r requirements.txt [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-7) # Launch the tutorial server [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-8) python server.py [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-2-10) # Open http://localhost:8000 in your browser`

Core Concepts
-------------

### Commands and Syntax

C4A-Script uses simple, English-like commands. Each command does one specific thing:

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-1) # Comments start with # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-2) COMMAND parameter1 parameter2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-4) # Most commands use CSS selectors in backticks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-5) CLICK `#submit-button` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-7) # Text content goes in quotes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-8) TYPE "Hello, World!" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-10) # Numbers are used directly [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-3-11) WAIT 3``

### Selectors: Finding Elements

C4A-Script uses CSS selectors to identify elements on the page:

`` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-1) # By ID [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-2) CLICK `#login-button` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-4) # By class [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-5) CLICK `.submit-btn` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-7) # By attribute [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-8) CLICK `button[type="submit"]` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-10) # By accessible attributes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-11) CLICK `button[aria-label="Search"][title="Search"]` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-13) # Complex selectors [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-4-14) CLICK `.form-container input[name="email"]` ``

### Variables and Dynamic Content

Store and reuse values with variables:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-1) # Set a variable [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-2) SETVAR username = "john@example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-3) SETVAR password = "secret123" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-5) # Use variables (prefix with $) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-6) TYPE $username [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-7) PRESS Tab [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-5-8) TYPE $password`

Command Categories
------------------

### 🧭 Navigation Commands

Move around the web like a user would:

| Command | Purpose | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GO` | Navigate to URL | `GO https://example.com` |
| `RELOAD` | Refresh current page | `RELOAD` |
| `BACK` | Go back in history | `BACK` |
| `FORWARD` | Go forward in history | `FORWARD` |

### ⏱️ Wait Commands

Ensure elements are ready before interacting:

| Command | Purpose | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `WAIT` | Wait for time/element/text | `WAIT 3` or `WAIT \`#element\` 10\` |

### 🖱️ Mouse Commands

Click, drag, and move like a human:

| Command | Purpose | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `CLICK` | Click element or coordinates | `CLICK \`button\``or`CLICK 100 200\` |
| `DOUBLE_CLICK` | Double-click element | `DOUBLE_CLICK \`.item\`\` |
| `RIGHT_CLICK` | Right-click element | `RIGHT_CLICK \`#menu\`\` |
| `SCROLL` | Scroll in direction | `SCROLL DOWN 500` |
| `DRAG` | Drag from point to point | `DRAG 100 100 500 300` |

### ⌨️ Keyboard Commands

Type text and press keys naturally:

| Command | Purpose | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `TYPE` | Type text or variable | `TYPE "Hello"` or `TYPE $username` |
| `PRESS` | Press special keys | `PRESS Tab` or `PRESS Enter` |
| `CLEAR` | Clear input field | `CLEAR \`#search\`\` |
| `SET` | Set input value directly | `SET \`#email\` "user@example.com"\` |

### 🔀 Control Flow

Add logic and repetition to your scripts:

| Command | Purpose | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `IF` | Conditional execution | `IF (EXISTS \`#popup\`) THEN CLICK \`#close\`\` |
| `REPEAT` | Loop commands | `REPEAT (SCROLL DOWN 300, 5)` |

### 💾 Variables & Advanced

Store data and execute custom code:

| Command | Purpose | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `SETVAR` | Create variable | `SETVAR email = "test@example.com"` |
| `EVAL` | Execute JavaScript | `EVAL \`console.log('Hello')\`\` |

Real-World Examples
-------------------

### Example 1: Login Flow

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-1) # Complete login automation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-2) GO https://myapp.com/login [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-4) # Wait for page to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-5) WAIT `#login-form` 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-7) # Fill credentials [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-8) CLICK `#email` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-9) TYPE "user@example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-10) PRESS Tab [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-11) TYPE "mypassword" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-13) # Submit form [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-14) CLICK `button[type="submit"]` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-16) # Wait for dashboard [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-6-17) WAIT `.dashboard` 10``

### Example 2: E-commerce Shopping

`` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-1) # Shopping automation with variables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-2) SETVAR product = "laptop" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-3) SETVAR budget = "1000" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-5) GO https://shop.example.com [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-6) WAIT `#search-box` 3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-8) # Search for product [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-9) TYPE $product [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-10) PRESS Enter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-11) WAIT `.product-list` 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-13) # Filter by price [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-14) CLICK `.price-filter` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-15) SET `#max-price` $budget [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-16) CLICK `.apply-filters` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-18) # Select first result [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-19) WAIT `.product-item` 3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-7-20) CLICK `.product-item:first-child` ``

### Example 3: Form Automation with Conditions

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-1) # Smart form filling with error handling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-2) GO https://forms.example.com [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-4) # Check if user is already logged in [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-5) IF (EXISTS `.user-menu`) THEN GO https://forms.example.com/new [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-6) IF (NOT EXISTS `.user-menu`) THEN CLICK `#login-link` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-8) # Fill form [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-9) WAIT `#contact-form` 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-10) SET `#name` "John Doe" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-11) SET `#email` "john@example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-12) SET `#message` "Hello from C4A-Script!" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-14) # Handle popup if it appears [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-15) IF (EXISTS `.cookie-banner`) THEN CLICK `.accept-cookies` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-17) # Submit [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-18) CLICK `#submit-button` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-8-19) WAIT `.success-message` 10``

Visual Programming with Blockly
-------------------------------

C4A-Script includes a powerful visual programming interface built on Google Blockly. Perfect for:

*   **Non-programmers** who want to create automation
*   **Rapid prototyping** of automation workflows
*   **Educational environments** for teaching automation concepts
*   **Collaborative development** where visual representation helps communication

### Features:

*   **Drag & Drop Interface**: Build scripts by connecting blocks
*   **Real-time Sync**: Changes in visual mode instantly update the text script
*   **Smart Block Types**: Blocks are categorized by function (Navigation, Actions, etc.)
*   **Error Prevention**: Visual connections prevent syntax errors
*   **Comment Support**: Add visual comment blocks for documentation

Try the visual editor in our [live demo](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/c4a-script/demo)
 or [local tutorial](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/examples/c4a_script/tutorial/)
.

Advanced Features
-----------------

### Recording Mode

The tutorial interface includes a recording feature that watches your browser interactions and automatically generates C4A-Script commands:

1.  Click "Record" in the tutorial interface
2.  Perform actions in the browser preview
3.  Watch as C4A-Script commands are generated in real-time
4.  Edit and refine the generated script

### Error Handling and Debugging

C4A-Script provides clear error messages and debugging information:

`` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-1) # Use comments for debugging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-2) # This will wait up to 10 seconds for the element [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-3) WAIT `#slow-loading-element` 10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-5) # Check if element exists before clicking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-6) IF (EXISTS `#optional-button`) THEN CLICK `#optional-button` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-8) # Use EVAL for custom debugging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-9-9) EVAL `console.log("Current page title:", document.title)` ``

### Integration with Crawl4AI

C4A-Script integrates seamlessly with Crawl4AI's web crawling capabilities:

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-3) # Use C4A-Script for interaction before crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-4) script = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-5) GO https://example.com [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-6) CLICK `#load-more-content` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-7) WAIT `.dynamic-content` 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-8) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-10) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-11)     js_code=script, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-12)     wait_for=".dynamic-content" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-13) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-15) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-16)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-10-17)     print(result.markdown)``

Best Practices
--------------

### 1\. Always Wait for Elements

`` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-11-1) # Bad: Clicking immediately [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-11-2) CLICK `#button` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-11-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-11-4) # Good: Wait for element to appear [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-11-5) WAIT `#button` 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-11-6) CLICK `#button` ``

### 2\. Use Descriptive Comments

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-1) # Login to user account [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-2) GO https://myapp.com/login [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-3) WAIT `#login-form` 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-5) # Enter credentials [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-6) TYPE "user@example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-7) PRESS Tab [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-8) TYPE "password123" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-10) # Submit and wait for redirect [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-11) CLICK `#submit-button` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-12-12) WAIT `.dashboard` 10``

### 3\. Handle Variable Conditions

`` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-13-1) # Handle different page states [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-13-2) IF (EXISTS `.cookie-banner`) THEN CLICK `.accept-cookies` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-13-3) IF (EXISTS `.popup-modal`) THEN CLICK `.close-modal` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-13-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-13-5) # Proceed with main workflow [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-13-6) CLICK `#main-action` ``

### 4\. Use Variables for Reusability

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-14-1) # Define once, use everywhere [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-14-2) SETVAR base_url = "https://myapp.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-14-3) SETVAR test_email = "test@example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-14-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-14-5) GO $base_url/login [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/c4a-script/#__codelineno-14-6) SET `#email` $test_email``

# Crawl4AI v0.7.6 Release Notes - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl4AI v0.7.6 Release Notes
=============================

_Release Date: October 22, 2025_

I'm excited to announce Crawl4AI v0.7.6, featuring a complete webhook infrastructure for the Docker job queue API! This release eliminates polling and brings real-time notifications to both crawling and LLM extraction workflows.

🎯 What's New
-------------

### Webhook Support for Docker Job Queue API

The headline feature of v0.7.6 is comprehensive webhook support for asynchronous job processing. No more constant polling to check if your jobs are done - get instant notifications when they complete!

**Key Capabilities:**

*   ✅ **Universal Webhook Support**: Both `/crawl/job` and `/llm/job` endpoints now support webhooks
*   ✅ **Flexible Delivery Modes**: Choose notification-only or include full data in the webhook payload
*   ✅ **Reliable Delivery**: Exponential backoff retry mechanism (5 attempts: 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 16s)
*   ✅ **Custom Authentication**: Add custom headers for webhook authentication
*   ✅ **Global Configuration**: Set default webhook URL in `config.yml` for all jobs
*   ✅ **Task Type Identification**: Distinguish between `crawl` and `llm_extraction` tasks

### How It Works

Instead of constantly checking job status:

**OLD WAY (Polling):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-1) # Submit job [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-2) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl/job", json=payload) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-3) task_id = response.json()['task_id'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-5) # Poll until complete [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-6) while True: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-7)     status = requests.get(f"http://localhost:11235/crawl/job/{task_id}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-8)     if status.json()['status'] == 'completed': [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-9)         break [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-0-10)     time.sleep(5)  # Wait and try again`

**NEW WAY (Webhooks):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-1) # Submit job with webhook [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-2) payload = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-3)     "urls": ["https://example.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-4)     "webhook_config": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-5)         "webhook_url": "https://myapp.com/webhook", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-6)         "webhook_data_in_payload": True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-7)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-8) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-9) response = requests.post("http://localhost:11235/crawl/job", json=payload) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-11) # Done! Webhook will notify you when complete [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-1-12) # Your webhook handler receives the results automatically`

### Crawl Job Webhooks

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-1) curl -X POST http://localhost:11235/crawl/job \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-2)   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-3)   -d '{ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-4)     "urls": ["https://example.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-5)     "browser_config": {"headless": true}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-6)     "crawler_config": {"cache_mode": "bypass"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-7)     "webhook_config": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-8)       "webhook_url": "https://myapp.com/webhooks/crawl-complete", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-9)       "webhook_data_in_payload": false, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-10)       "webhook_headers": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-11)         "X-Webhook-Secret": "your-secret-token" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-12)       } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-13)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-2-14)   }'`

### LLM Extraction Job Webhooks (NEW!)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-1) curl -X POST http://localhost:11235/llm/job \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-2)   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-3)   -d '{ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-4)     "url": "https://example.com/article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-5)     "q": "Extract the article title, author, and publication date", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-6)     "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"}}}", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-7)     "provider": "openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-8)     "webhook_config": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-9)       "webhook_url": "https://myapp.com/webhooks/llm-complete", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-10)       "webhook_data_in_payload": true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-11)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-3-12)   }'`

### Webhook Payload Structure

**Success (with data):**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-2)   "task_id": "llm_1698765432", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-3)   "task_type": "llm_extraction", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-4)   "status": "completed", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-5)   "timestamp": "2025-10-22T10:30:00.000000+00:00", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-6)   "urls": ["https://example.com/article"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-7)   "data": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-8)     "extracted_content": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-9)       "title": "Understanding Web Scraping", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-10)       "author": "John Doe", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-11)       "date": "2025-10-22" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-12)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-13)   } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-4-14) }`

**Failure:**

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-2)   "task_id": "crawl_abc123", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-3)   "task_type": "crawl", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-4)   "status": "failed", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-5)   "timestamp": "2025-10-22T10:30:00.000000+00:00", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-6)   "urls": ["https://example.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-7)   "error": "Connection timeout after 30s" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-5-8) }`

### Simple Webhook Handler Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-1) from flask import Flask, request, jsonify [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-3) app = Flask(__name__) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-5) @app.route('/webhook', methods=['POST']) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-6) def handle_webhook(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-7)     payload = request.json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-9)     task_id = payload['task_id'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-10)     task_type = payload['task_type'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-11)     status = payload['status'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-13)     if status == 'completed': [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-14)         if 'data' in payload: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-15)             # Process data directly [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-16)             data = payload['data'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-17)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-18)             # Fetch from API [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-19)             endpoint = 'crawl' if task_type == 'crawl' else 'llm' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-20)             response = requests.get(f'http://localhost:11235/{endpoint}/job/{task_id}') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-21)             data = response.json() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-23)         # Your business logic here [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-24)         print(f"Job {task_id} completed!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-26)     elif status == 'failed': [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-27)         error = payload.get('error', 'Unknown error') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-28)         print(f"Job {task_id} failed: {error}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-30)     return jsonify({"status": "received"}), 200 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-6-32) app.run(port=8080)`

📊 Performance Improvements
---------------------------

*   **Reduced Server Load**: Eliminates constant polling requests
*   **Lower Latency**: Instant notification vs. polling interval delay
*   **Better Resource Usage**: Frees up client connections while jobs run in background
*   **Scalable Architecture**: Handles high-volume crawling workflows efficiently

🐛 Bug Fixes
------------

*   Fixed webhook configuration serialization for Pydantic HttpUrl fields
*   Improved error handling in webhook delivery service
*   Enhanced Redis task storage for webhook config persistence

🌍 Expected Real-World Impact
-----------------------------

### For Web Scraping Workflows

*   **Reduced Costs**: Less API calls = lower bandwidth and server costs
*   **Better UX**: Instant notifications improve user experience
*   **Scalability**: Handle 100s of concurrent jobs without polling overhead

### For LLM Extraction Pipelines

*   **Async Processing**: Submit LLM extraction jobs and move on
*   **Batch Processing**: Queue multiple extractions, get notified as they complete
*   **Integration**: Easy integration with workflow automation tools (Zapier, n8n, etc.)

### For Microservices

*   **Event-Driven**: Perfect for event-driven microservice architectures
*   **Decoupling**: Decouple job submission from result processing
*   **Reliability**: Automatic retries ensure webhooks are delivered

🔄 Breaking Changes
-------------------

**None!** This release is fully backward compatible.

*   Webhook configuration is optional
*   Existing code continues to work without modification
*   Polling is still supported for jobs without webhook config

📚 Documentation
----------------

### New Documentation

*   **[WEBHOOK\_EXAMPLES.md](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/deploy/docker/WEBHOOK_EXAMPLES.md)
    ** - Comprehensive webhook usage guide
*   **[docker\_webhook\_example.py](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/docs/examples/docker_webhook_example.py)
    ** - Working code examples

### Updated Documentation

*   **[Docker README](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/deploy/docker/README.md)
    ** - Added webhook sections
*   API documentation with webhook examples

🛠️ Migration Guide
-------------------

No migration needed! Webhooks are opt-in:

1.  **To use webhooks**: Add `webhook_config` to your job payload
2.  **To keep polling**: Continue using your existing code

### Quick Start

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-1) # Just add webhook_config to your existing payload [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-2) payload = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-3)     # Your existing configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-4)     "urls": ["https://example.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-5)     "browser_config": {...}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-6)     "crawler_config": {...}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-8)     # NEW: Add webhook configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-9)     "webhook_config": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-10)         "webhook_url": "https://myapp.com/webhook", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-11)         "webhook_data_in_payload": True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-12)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-7-13) }`

🔧 Configuration
----------------

### Global Webhook Configuration (config.yml)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-1) webhooks: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-2)   enabled: true [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-3)   default_url: "https://myapp.com/webhooks/default"  # Optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-4)   data_in_payload: false [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-5)   retry: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-6)     max_attempts: 5 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-7)     initial_delay_ms: 1000 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-8)     max_delay_ms: 32000 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-9)     timeout_ms: 30000 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-10)   headers: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-8-11)     User-Agent: "Crawl4AI-Webhook/1.0"`

🚀 Upgrade Instructions
-----------------------

### Docker

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-1) # Pull the latest image [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-2) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:0.7.6 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-4) # Or use latest tag [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-5) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:latest [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-7) # Run with webhook support [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-8) docker run -d \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-9)   -p 11235:11235 \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-10)   --env-file .llm.env \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-11)   --name crawl4ai \ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-9-12)   unclecode/crawl4ai:0.7.6`

### Python Package

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-10-1) pip install --upgrade crawl4ai`

💡 Pro Tips
-----------

1.  **Use notification-only mode** for large results - fetch data separately to avoid large webhook payloads
2.  **Set custom headers** for webhook authentication and request tracking
3.  **Configure global default webhook** for consistent handling across all jobs
4.  **Implement idempotent webhook handlers** - same webhook may be delivered multiple times on retry
5.  **Use structured schemas** with LLM extraction for predictable webhook data

🎬 Demo
-------

Try the release demo:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/releases/0.7.6/#__codelineno-11-1) python docs/releases_review/demo_v0.7.6.py`

This comprehensive demo showcases: - Crawl job webhooks (notification-only and with data) - LLM extraction webhooks (with JSON schema support) - Custom headers for authentication - Webhook retry mechanism - Real-time webhook receiver

🙏 Acknowledgments
------------------

Thank you to the community for the feedback that shaped this feature! Special thanks to everyone who requested webhook support for asynchronous job processing.

📞 Support
----------

*   **Documentation**: https://docs.crawl4ai.com
*   **GitHub Issues**: https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai/issues
*   **Discord**: https://discord.gg/crawl4ai

* * *

**Happy crawling with webhooks!** 🕷️🪝
