# Table Extraction Strategies - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Table Extraction Strategies
===========================

Overview
--------

**New in v0.7.3+**: Table extraction now follows the **Strategy Design Pattern**, providing unprecedented flexibility and power for handling different table structures. Don't worry - **your existing code still works!** We maintain full backward compatibility while offering new capabilities.

### What's Changed?

*   **Architecture**: Table extraction now uses pluggable strategies
*   **Backward Compatible**: Your existing code with `table_score_threshold` continues to work
*   **More Power**: Choose from multiple strategies or create your own
*   **Same Default Behavior**: By default, uses `DefaultTableExtraction` (same as before)

### Key Points

✅ **Old code still works** - No breaking changes  
✅ **Same default behavior** - Uses the proven extraction algorithm  
✅ **New capabilities** - Add LLM extraction or custom strategies when needed  
✅ **Strategy pattern** - Clean, extensible architecture

Quick Start
-----------

### The Simplest Way (Works Like Before)

If you're already using Crawl4AI, nothing changes:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-4) async def extract_tables(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-6)         # This works exactly like before - uses DefaultTableExtraction internally [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-7)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/data") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-9)         # Tables are automatically extracted and available in result.tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-10)         for table in result.tables: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-11)             print(f"Table with {len(table['rows'])} rows and {len(table['headers'])} columns") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-12)             print(f"Headers: {table['headers']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-13)             print(f"First row: {table['rows'][0] if table['rows'] else 'No data'}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-0-15) asyncio.run(extract_tables())`

### Using the Old Configuration (Still Supported)

Your existing code with `table_score_threshold` continues to work:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-1-1) # This old approach STILL WORKS - we maintain backward compatibility [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-1-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-1-3)     table_score_threshold=7  # Internally creates DefaultTableExtraction(table_score_threshold=7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-1-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-1-5) result = await crawler.arun(url, config)`

Table Extraction Strategies
---------------------------

### Understanding the Strategy Pattern

The strategy pattern allows you to choose different table extraction algorithms at runtime. Think of it as having different tools in a toolbox - you pick the right one for the job:

*   **No explicit strategy?** → Uses `DefaultTableExtraction` automatically (same as v0.7.2 and earlier)
*   **Need complex table handling?** → Choose `LLMTableExtraction` (costs money, use sparingly)
*   **Want to disable tables?** → Use `NoTableExtraction`
*   **Have special requirements?** → Create a custom strategy

### Available Strategies

| Strategy | Description | Use Case | Cost | When to Use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `DefaultTableExtraction` | **RECOMMENDED**: Same algorithm as before v0.7.3 | General purpose (default) | Free | **Use this first - handles 95% of cases** |
| `LLMTableExtraction` | AI-powered extraction for complex tables | Tables with complex rowspan/colspan | **$$$ Per API call** | Only when DefaultTableExtraction fails |
| `NoTableExtraction` | Disables table extraction | When tables aren't needed | Free | For text-only extraction |
| Custom strategies | User-defined extraction logic | Specialized requirements | Free | Domain-specific needs |

> **⚠️ CRITICAL COST WARNING for LLMTableExtraction**:
> 
> **DO NOT USE `LLMTableExtraction` UNLESS ABSOLUTELY NECESSARY!**
> 
> *   **Always try `DefaultTableExtraction` first** - It's free and handles most tables perfectly
> *   LLM extraction **costs money** with every API call
> *   For large tables (100+ rows), LLM extraction can be **very slow**
> *   **For large tables**: If you must use LLM, choose fast providers:
> *   ✅ **Groq** (fastest inference)
> *   ✅ **Cerebras** (optimized for speed)
> *   ⚠️ Avoid: OpenAI, Anthropic for large tables (slower)
> 
> **🚧 WORK IN PROGRESS**: We are actively developing an **advanced non-LLM algorithm** that will handle complex table structures (rowspan, colspan, nested tables) for **FREE**. This will replace the need for costly LLM extraction in most cases. Coming soon!

### DefaultTableExtraction

The default strategy uses a sophisticated scoring system to identify data tables:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-1) from crawl4ai import DefaultTableExtraction, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-3) # Customize the default extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-4) table_strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-5)     table_score_threshold=7,  # Scoring threshold (default: 7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-6)     min_rows=2,               # Minimum rows required [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-7)     min_cols=2,               # Minimum columns required [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-8)     verbose=True              # Enable detailed logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-11) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-12)     table_extraction=table_strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-2-13) )`

#### Scoring System

The scoring system evaluates multiple factors:

| Factor | Score Impact | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Has `<thead>` | +2  | Semantic table structure |
| Has `<tbody>` | +1  | Organized table body |
| Has `<th>` elements | +2  | Header cells present |
| Headers in correct position | +1  | Proper semantic structure |
| Consistent column count | +2  | Regular data structure |
| Has caption | +2  | Descriptive caption |
| Has summary | +1  | Summary attribute |
| High text density | +2 to +3 | Content-rich cells |
| Data attributes | +0.5 each | Data-\* attributes |
| Nested tables | \-3 | Often indicates layout |
| Role="presentation" | \-3 | Explicitly non-data |
| Too few rows | \-2 | Insufficient data |

### LLMTableExtraction (Use Sparingly!)

**⚠️ WARNING**: Only use this when `DefaultTableExtraction` fails with complex tables!

LLMTableExtraction uses AI to understand complex table structures that traditional parsers struggle with. It automatically handles large tables through intelligent chunking and parallel processing:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-1) from crawl4ai import LLMTableExtraction, LLMConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-3) # Configure LLM (costs money per call!) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-4) llm_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-5)     provider="groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile",  # Fast provider for large tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-6)     api_token="your_api_key", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-7)     temperature=0.1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-10) # Create LLM extraction strategy with smart chunking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-11) table_strategy = LLMTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-12)     llm_config=llm_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-13)     max_tries=3,                      # Retry up to 3 times if extraction fails [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-14)     css_selector="table",             # Optional: focus on specific tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-15)     enable_chunking=True,             # Automatically chunk large tables (default: True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-16)     chunk_token_threshold=3000,       # Split tables larger than this (default: 3000 tokens) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-17)     min_rows_per_chunk=10,            # Minimum rows per chunk (default: 10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-18)     max_parallel_chunks=5,            # Process up to 5 chunks in parallel (default: 5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-19)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-20) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-22) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-23)     table_extraction=table_strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-24) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-3-26) result = await crawler.arun(url, config)`

#### When to Use LLMTableExtraction

✅ **Use ONLY when**: - Tables have complex merged cells (rowspan/colspan) that break DefaultTableExtraction - Nested tables that need semantic understanding - Tables with irregular structures - You've tried DefaultTableExtraction and it failed

❌ **Never use when**: - DefaultTableExtraction works (99% of cases) - Tables are simple or well-structured - You're processing many pages (costs add up!) - Tables have 100+ rows (very slow)

#### How Smart Chunking Works

LLMTableExtraction automatically handles large tables through intelligent chunking:

1.  **Automatic Detection**: Tables exceeding the token threshold are automatically split
2.  **Smart Splitting**: Chunks are created at row boundaries, preserving table structure
3.  **Header Preservation**: Each chunk includes the original headers for context
4.  **Parallel Processing**: Multiple chunks are processed simultaneously for speed
5.  **Intelligent Merging**: Results are merged back into a single, complete table

**Chunking Parameters**: - `enable_chunking` (default: `True`): Automatically handle large tables - `chunk_token_threshold` (default: `3000`): When to split tables - `min_rows_per_chunk` (default: `10`): Ensures meaningful chunk sizes - `max_parallel_chunks` (default: `5`): Concurrent processing for speed

The chunking is completely transparent - you get the same output format whether the table was processed in one piece or multiple chunks.

#### Performance Optimization for LLMTableExtraction

**Provider Recommendations by Table Size**:

| Table Size | Recommended Providers | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Small (<50 rows) | Any provider | Fast enough |
| Medium (50-200 rows) | Groq, Cerebras | Optimized inference |
| Large (200+ rows) | **Groq** (best), Cerebras | Fastest inference + automatic chunking |
| Very Large (500+ rows) | Groq with chunking | Parallel processing keeps it fast |

### NoTableExtraction

Disable table extraction for better performance when tables aren't needed:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-1) from crawl4ai import NoTableExtraction, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-3) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-4)     table_extraction=NoTableExtraction() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-7) # Tables won't be extracted, improving performance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-8) result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-4-9) assert len(result.tables) == 0`

Extracted Table Structure
-------------------------

Each extracted table contains:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-2)     "headers": ["Column 1", "Column 2", ...],  # Column headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-3)     "rows": [                                   # Data rows [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-4)         ["Row 1 Col 1", "Row 1 Col 2", ...], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-5)         ["Row 2 Col 1", "Row 2 Col 2", ...], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-6)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-7)     "caption": "Table Caption",                # If present [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-8)     "summary": "Table Summary",                # If present [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-9)     "metadata": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-10)         "row_count": 10,                       # Number of rows [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-11)         "column_count": 3,                      # Number of columns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-12)         "has_headers": True,                    # Headers detected [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-13)         "has_caption": True,                    # Caption exists [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-14)         "has_summary": False,                   # Summary exists [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-15)         "id": "data-table-1",                   # Table ID if present [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-16)         "class": "financial-data"               # Table class if present [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-17)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-5-18) }`

Configuration Options
---------------------

### Basic Configuration

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-2)     # Table extraction settings [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-3)     table_score_threshold=7,      # Default threshold (backward compatible) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-4)     table_extraction=strategy,     # Optional: custom strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-6)     # Filter what to process [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-7)     css_selector="main",          # Focus on specific area [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-8)     excluded_tags=["nav", "aside"] # Exclude page sections [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-6-9) )`

### Advanced Configuration

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-1) from crawl4ai import DefaultTableExtraction, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-3) # Fine-tuned extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-4) strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-5)     table_score_threshold=5,      # Lower = more permissive [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-6)     min_rows=3,                   # Require at least 3 rows [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-7)     min_cols=2,                   # Require at least 2 columns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-8)     verbose=True                  # Detailed logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-11) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-12)     table_extraction=strategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-13)     css_selector="article.content", # Target specific content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-14)     exclude_domains=["ads.com"],   # Exclude ad domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-15)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS    # Fresh extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-7-16) )`

Working with Extracted Tables
-----------------------------

### Convert to Pandas DataFrame

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-1) import pandas as pd [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-3) async def tables_to_dataframes(url): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-4)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-5)         result = await crawler.arun(url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-7)         dataframes = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-8)         for table_data in result.tables: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-9)             # Create DataFrame [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-10)             if table_data['headers']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-11)                 df = pd.DataFrame( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-12)                     table_data['rows'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-13)                     columns=table_data['headers'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-14)                 ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-15)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-16)                 df = pd.DataFrame(table_data['rows']) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-18)             # Add metadata as DataFrame attributes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-19)             df.attrs['caption'] = table_data.get('caption', '') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-20)             df.attrs['metadata'] = table_data.get('metadata', {}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-22)             dataframes.append(df) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-8-24)         return dataframes`

### Filter Tables by Criteria

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-1) async def extract_large_tables(url): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-2)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-3)         # Configure minimum size requirements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-4)         strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-5)             min_rows=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-6)             min_cols=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-7)             table_score_threshold=6 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-8)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-10)         config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-11)             table_extraction=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-12)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-14)         result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-16)         # Further filter results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-17)         large_tables = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-18)             table for table in result.tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-19)             if table['metadata']['row_count'] > 10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-20)             and table['metadata']['column_count'] > 3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-21)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-9-23)         return large_tables`

### Export Tables to Different Formats

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-2) import csv [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-4) async def export_tables(url): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-6)         result = await crawler.arun(url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-8)         for i, table in enumerate(result.tables): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-9)             # Export as JSON [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-10)             with open(f'table_{i}.json', 'w') as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-11)                 json.dump(table, f, indent=2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-13)             # Export as CSV [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-14)             with open(f'table_{i}.csv', 'w', newline='') as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-15)                 writer = csv.writer(f) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-16)                 if table['headers']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-17)                     writer.writerow(table['headers']) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-18)                 writer.writerows(table['rows']) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-20)             # Export as Markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-21)             with open(f'table_{i}.md', 'w') as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-22)                 # Write headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-23)                 if table['headers']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-24)                     f.write('| ' + ' | '.join(table['headers']) + ' |\n') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-25)                     f.write('|' + '---|' * len(table['headers']) + '\n') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-27)                 # Write rows [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-28)                 for row in table['rows']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-10-29)                     f.write('| ' + ' | '.join(str(cell) for cell in row) + ' |\n')`

Creating Custom Strategies
--------------------------

Extend `TableExtractionStrategy` to create custom extraction logic:

### Example: Financial Table Extractor

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-1) from crawl4ai import TableExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-2) from typing import List, Dict, Any [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-3) import re [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-5) class FinancialTableExtractor(TableExtractionStrategy): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-6)     """Extract tables containing financial data.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-8)     def __init__(self, currency_symbols=None, require_numbers=True, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-9)         super().__init__(**kwargs) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-10)         self.currency_symbols = currency_symbols or ['$', '€', '£', '¥'] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-11)         self.require_numbers = require_numbers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-12)         self.number_pattern = re.compile(r'\d+[,.]?\d*') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-14)     def extract_tables(self, element, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-15)         tables_data = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-17)         for table in element.xpath(".//table"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-18)             # Check if table contains financial indicators [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-19)             table_text = ''.join(table.itertext()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-21)             # Must contain currency symbols [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-22)             has_currency = any(sym in table_text for sym in self.currency_symbols) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-23)             if not has_currency: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-24)                 continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-26)             # Must contain numbers if required [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-27)             if self.require_numbers: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-28)                 numbers = self.number_pattern.findall(table_text) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-29)                 if len(numbers) < 3:  # Arbitrary minimum [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-30)                     continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-32)             # Extract the table data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-33)             table_data = self._extract_financial_data(table) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-34)             if table_data: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-35)                 tables_data.append(table_data) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-36) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-37)         return tables_data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-39)     def _extract_financial_data(self, table): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-40)         """Extract and clean financial data from table.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-41)         headers = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-42)         rows = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-43) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-44)         # Extract headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-45)         for th in table.xpath(".//thead//th | .//tr[1]//th"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-46)             headers.append(th.text_content().strip()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-48)         # Extract and clean rows [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-49)         for tr in table.xpath(".//tbody//tr | .//tr[position()>1]"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-50)             row = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-51)             for td in tr.xpath(".//td"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-52)                 text = td.text_content().strip() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-53)                 # Clean currency formatting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-54)                 text = re.sub(r'[$€£¥,]', '', text) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-55)                 row.append(text) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-56)             if row: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-57)                 rows.append(row) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-58) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-59)         return { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-60)             "headers": headers, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-61)             "rows": rows, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-62)             "caption": self._get_caption(table), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-63)             "summary": table.get("summary", ""), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-64)             "metadata": { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-65)                 "type": "financial", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-66)                 "row_count": len(rows), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-67)                 "column_count": len(headers) or len(rows[0]) if rows else 0 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-68)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-69)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-70) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-71)     def _get_caption(self, table): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-72)         caption = table.xpath(".//caption/text()") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-73)         return caption[0].strip() if caption else "" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-74) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-75) # Usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-76) strategy = FinancialTableExtractor( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-77)     currency_symbols=['$', 'EUR'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-78)     require_numbers=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-79) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-80) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-81) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-82)     table_extraction=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-11-83) )`

### Example: Specific Table Extractor

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-1) class SpecificTableExtractor(TableExtractionStrategy): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-2)     """Extract only tables matching specific criteria.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-4)     def __init__(self,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-5)                  required_headers=None,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-6)                  id_pattern=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-7)                  class_pattern=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-8)                  **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-9)         super().__init__(**kwargs) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-10)         self.required_headers = required_headers or [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-11)         self.id_pattern = id_pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-12)         self.class_pattern = class_pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-14)     def extract_tables(self, element, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-15)         tables_data = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-17)         for table in element.xpath(".//table"): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-18)             # Check ID pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-19)             if self.id_pattern: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-20)                 table_id = table.get('id', '') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-21)                 if not re.match(self.id_pattern, table_id): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-22)                     continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-24)             # Check class pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-25)             if self.class_pattern: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-26)                 table_class = table.get('class', '') [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-27)                 if not re.match(self.class_pattern, table_class): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-28)                     continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-30)             # Extract headers to check requirements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-31)             headers = self._extract_headers(table) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-33)             # Check if required headers are present [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-34)             if self.required_headers: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-35)                 if not all(req in headers for req in self.required_headers): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-36)                     continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-38)             # Extract full table data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-39)             table_data = self._extract_table_data(table, headers) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-40)             tables_data.append(table_data) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-12-42)         return tables_data`

Combining with Other Strategies
-------------------------------

Table extraction works seamlessly with other Crawl4AI strategies:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-1) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-2)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-3)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-4)     DefaultTableExtraction, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-5)     LLMExtractionStrategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-6)     JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-9) async def combined_extraction(url): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-11)         config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-12)             # Table extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-13)             table_extraction=DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-14)                 table_score_threshold=6, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-15)                 min_rows=2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-16)             ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-18)             # CSS-based extraction for specific elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-19)             extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-20)                 "title": "h1", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-21)                 "summary": "p.summary", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-22)                 "date": "time" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-23)             }), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-25)             # Focus on main content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-26)             css_selector="main.content" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-27)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-29)         result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-31)         # Access different extraction results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-32)         tables = result.tables  # Table data [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-33)         structured = json.loads(result.extracted_content)  # CSS extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-35)         return { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-36)             "tables": tables, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-37)             "structured_data": structured, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-38)             "markdown": result.markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-13-39)         }`

Performance Considerations
--------------------------

### Optimization Tips

1.  **Disable when not needed**: Use `NoTableExtraction` if tables aren't required
2.  **Target specific areas**: Use `css_selector` to limit processing scope
3.  **Set minimum thresholds**: Filter out small/irrelevant tables early
4.  **Cache results**: Use appropriate cache modes for repeated extractions

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-1) # Optimized configuration for large pages [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-3)     # Only process main content area [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-4)     css_selector="article.main-content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-6)     # Exclude navigation and sidebars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-7)     excluded_tags=["nav", "aside", "footer"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-9)     # Higher threshold for stricter filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-10)     table_extraction=DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-11)         table_score_threshold=8, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-12)         min_rows=5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-13)         min_cols=3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-14)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-16)     # Enable caching for repeated access [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-17)     cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-14-18) )`

Migration Guide
---------------

### Important: Your Code Still Works!

**No changes required!** The transition to the strategy pattern is **fully backward compatible**.

### How It Works Internally

#### v0.7.2 and Earlier

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-15-1) # Old way - directly passing table_score_threshold [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-15-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-15-3)     table_score_threshold=7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-15-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-15-5) # Internally: No strategy pattern, direct implementation`

#### v0.7.3+ (Current)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-16-1) # Old way STILL WORKS - we handle it internally [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-16-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-16-3)     table_score_threshold=7 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-16-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-16-5) # Internally: Automatically creates DefaultTableExtraction(table_score_threshold=7)`

### Taking Advantage of New Features

While your old code works, you can now use the strategy pattern for more control:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-1) # Option 1: Keep using the old way (perfectly fine!) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-2) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-3)     table_score_threshold=7  # Still supported [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-6) # Option 2: Use the new strategy pattern (more flexibility) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-7) from crawl4ai import DefaultTableExtraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-9) strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-10)     table_score_threshold=7, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-11)     min_rows=2,  # New capability! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-12)     min_cols=2   # New capability! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-13) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-15) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-16)     table_extraction=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-17) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-19) # Option 3: Use advanced strategies when needed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-20) from crawl4ai import LLMTableExtraction, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-22) # Only for complex tables that DefaultTableExtraction can't handle [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-23) # Automatically handles large tables with smart chunking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-24) llm_strategy = LLMTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-25)     llm_config=LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-26)         provider="groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-27)         api_token="your_key" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-28)     ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-29)     max_tries=3, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-30)     enable_chunking=True,  # Automatically chunk large tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-31)     chunk_token_threshold=3000,  # Chunk when exceeding 3000 tokens [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-32)     max_parallel_chunks=5  # Process up to 5 chunks in parallel [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-33) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-35) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-36)     table_extraction=llm_strategy  # Advanced extraction with automatic chunking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-17-37) )`

### Summary

*   ✅ **No breaking changes** - Old code works as-is
*   ✅ **Same defaults** - DefaultTableExtraction is automatically used
*   ✅ **Gradual adoption** - Use new features when you need them
*   ✅ **Full compatibility** - result.tables structure unchanged

Best Practices
--------------

### 1\. Choose the Right Strategy (Cost-Conscious Approach)

**Decision Flow**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-1) 1. Do you need tables?  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-2)    → No: Use NoTableExtraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-3)    → Yes: Continue to #2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-5) 2. Try DefaultTableExtraction first (FREE) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-6)    → Works? Done! ✅ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-7)    → Fails? Continue to #3 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-9) 3. Is the table critical and complex? [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-10)    → No: Accept DefaultTableExtraction results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-11)    → Yes: Continue to #4 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-13) 4. Use LLMTableExtraction (COSTS MONEY) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-14)    → Small table (<50 rows): Any LLM provider [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-15)    → Large table (50+ rows): Use Groq or Cerebras [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-18-16)    → Very large (500+ rows): Reconsider - maybe chunk the page`

**Strategy Selection Guide**: - **DefaultTableExtraction**: Use for 99% of cases - it's free and effective - **LLMTableExtraction**: Only for complex tables with merged cells that break DefaultTableExtraction - **NoTableExtraction**: When you only need text/markdown content - **Custom Strategy**: For specialized requirements (financial, scientific, etc.)

### 2\. Validate Extracted Data

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-1) def validate_table(table): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-2)     """Validate table data quality.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-3)     # Check structure [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-4)     if not table.get('rows'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-5)         return False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-7)     # Check consistency [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-8)     if table.get('headers'): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-9)         expected_cols = len(table['headers']) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-10)         for row in table['rows']: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-11)             if len(row) != expected_cols: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-12)                 return False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-14)     # Check minimum content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-15)     total_cells = sum(len(row) for row in table['rows']) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-16)     non_empty = sum(1 for row in table['rows']  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-17)                     for cell in row if cell.strip()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-19)     if non_empty / total_cells < 0.5:  # Less than 50% non-empty [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-20)         return False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-22)     return True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-24) # Filter valid tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-19-25) valid_tables = [t for t in result.tables if validate_table(t)]`

### 3\. Handle Edge Cases

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-1) async def robust_table_extraction(url): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-2)     """Extract tables with error handling.""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-3)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-4)         try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-5)             config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-6)                 table_extraction=DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-7)                     table_score_threshold=6, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-8)                     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-9)                 ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-10)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-12)             result = await crawler.arun(url, config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-14)             if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-15)                 print(f"Crawl failed: {result.error}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-16)                 return [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-18)             # Process tables safely [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-19)             processed_tables = [] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-20)             for table in result.tables: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-21)                 try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-22)                     # Validate and process [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-23)                     if validate_table(table): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-24)                         processed_tables.append(table) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-25)                 except Exception as e: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-26)                     print(f"Error processing table: {e}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-27)                     continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-29)             return processed_tables [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-31)         except Exception as e: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-32)             print(f"Extraction error: {e}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-20-33)             return []`

Troubleshooting
---------------

### Common Issues and Solutions

| Issue | Cause | Solution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No tables extracted | Score too high | Lower `table_score_threshold` |
| Layout tables included | Score too low | Increase `table_score_threshold` |
| Missing tables | CSS selector too specific | Broaden or remove `css_selector` |
| Incomplete data | Complex table structure | Create custom strategy |
| Performance issues | Processing entire page | Use `css_selector` to limit scope |

### Debug Logging

Enable verbose logging to understand extraction decisions:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-1) import logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-3) # Configure logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-4) logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-6) # Enable verbose mode in strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-7) strategy = DefaultTableExtraction( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-8)     table_score_threshold=7, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-9)     verbose=True  # Detailed extraction logs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-10) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-12) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-13)     table_extraction=strategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-14)     verbose=True  # General crawler logs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/core/table_extraction/#__codelineno-21-15) )`

See Also
--------

     - Using CSS selectors and filters

     - Speed up extraction

     - Complete working examples

# Blog Home - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl4AI Blog
=============

Welcome to the Crawl4AI blog! Here you'll find detailed release notes, technical insights, and updates about the project. Whether you're looking for the latest improvements or want to dive deep into web crawling techniques, this is the place.

Featured Articles
-----------------

### [When to Stop Crawling: The Art of Knowing "Enough"](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/)

_January 29, 2025_

Traditional crawlers are like tourists with unlimited time—they'll visit every street, every alley, every dead end. But what if your crawler could think like a researcher with a deadline? Discover how Adaptive Crawling revolutionizes web scraping by knowing when to stop. Learn about the three-layer intelligence system that evaluates coverage, consistency, and saturation to build focused knowledge bases instead of endless page collections.

[Read the full article →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/adaptive-crawling-revolution/)

### [The LLM Context Protocol: Why Your AI Assistant Needs Memory, Reasoning, and Examples](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/llm-context-revolution/)

_January 24, 2025_

Ever wondered why your AI coding assistant struggles with your library despite comprehensive documentation? This article introduces the three-dimensional context protocol that transforms how AI understands code. Learn why memory, reasoning, and examples together create wisdom—not just information.

[Read the full article →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/llm-context-revolution/)

Latest Release
--------------

### [Crawl4AI v0.8.0 – Crash Recovery & Prefetch Mode](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.8.0.md)

_January 2026_

Crawl4AI v0.8.0 introduces crash recovery for deep crawls, a new prefetch mode for fast URL discovery, and critical security fixes for Docker deployments.

Key highlights: - **🔄 Deep Crawl Crash Recovery**: `on_state_change` callback for real-time state persistence, `resume_state` to continue from checkpoints - **⚡ Prefetch Mode**: `prefetch=True` for 5-10x faster URL discovery, perfect for two-phase crawling patterns - **🔒 Security Fixes**: Hooks disabled by default, `file://` URLs blocked on Docker API, `__import__` removed from sandbox

[Read full release notes →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.8.0.md)

Recent Releases
---------------

### [Crawl4AI v0.7.8 – Stability & Bug Fix Release](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.7.8.md)

_December 2025_

Crawl4AI v0.7.8 is a focused stability release addressing 11 bugs reported by the community. Fixes for Docker deployments, LLM extraction, URL handling, and dependency compatibility.

Key highlights: - **🐳 Docker API Fixes**: ContentRelevanceFilter deserialization, ProxyConfig serialization, cache folder permissions - **🤖 LLM Improvements**: Configurable rate limiter backoff, HTML input format support - **📦 Dependencies**: Replaced deprecated PyPDF2 with pypdf, Pydantic v2 ConfigDict compatibility

[Read full release notes →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.7.8.md)

### [Crawl4AI v0.7.7 – The Self-Hosting & Monitoring Update](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.7.7.md)

_November 14, 2025_

Crawl4AI v0.7.7 transforms Docker into a complete self-hosting platform with enterprise-grade real-time monitoring, comprehensive observability, and full operational control.

Key highlights: - **📊 Real-time Monitoring Dashboard**: Interactive web UI with live system metrics - **🔌 Comprehensive Monitor API**: Complete REST API for programmatic access - **⚡ WebSocket Streaming**: Real-time updates every 2 seconds - **🔥 Smart Browser Pool**: 3-tier architecture with automatic promotion and cleanup

[Read full release notes →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.7.7.md)

### [Crawl4AI v0.7.6 – The Webhook Infrastructure Update](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.7.6.md)

_October 22, 2025_

Crawl4AI v0.7.6 introduces comprehensive webhook support for the Docker job queue API, bringing real-time notifications to both crawling and LLM extraction workflows.

Key highlights: - **🪝 Complete Webhook Support**: Real-time notifications for both `/crawl/job` and `/llm/job` endpoints - **🔄 Reliable Delivery**: Exponential backoff retry mechanism - **🔐 Custom Authentication**: Add custom headers for webhook authentication

[Read full release notes →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.7.6.md)

* * *

Older Releases
--------------

| Version | Date | Highlights |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [v0.7.5](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/release-v0.7.5.md) | September 2025 | Docker Hooks System, enhanced LLM integration, HTTPS preservation |
