# Crawl4AI Complete SDK Documentation - Crawl4AI Documentation (v0.8.x)

Crawl4AI Complete SDK Documentation
===================================

**Generated:** 2025-10-19 12:56 **Format:** Ultra-Dense Reference (Optimized for AI Assistants) **Crawl4AI Version:** 0.7.4

* * *

Navigation
----------

*   [Installation & Setup](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#installation--setup)
    
*   [Quick Start](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#quick-start)
    
*   [Core API](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#core-api)
    
*   [Configuration](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#configuration)
    
*   [Crawling Patterns](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#crawling-patterns)
    
*   [Content Processing](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#content-processing)
    
*   [Extraction Strategies](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#extraction-strategies)
    
*   [Advanced Features](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#advanced-features)
    

* * *

Installation & Setup
====================

Installation & Setup (2023 Edition)
===================================

1\. Basic Installation
----------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-0-1) pip install crawl4ai`

2\. Initial Setup & Diagnostics
-------------------------------

### 2.1 Run the Setup Command

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-1-1) crawl4ai-setup`

\- Performs OS-level checks (e.g., missing libs on Linux) - Confirms your environment is ready to crawl

### 2.2 Diagnostics

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-2-1) crawl4ai-doctor`

\- Check Python version compatibility - Verify Playwright installation - Inspect environment variables or library conflicts If any issues arise, follow its suggestions (e.g., installing additional system packages) and re-run `crawl4ai-setup`.

3\. Verifying Installation: A Simple Crawl (Skip this step if you already run `crawl4ai-doctor`)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below is a minimal Python script demonstrating a **basic** crawl. It uses our new **`BrowserConfig`** and **`CrawlerRunConfig`** for clarity, though no custom settings are passed in this example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-6)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-7)             url="https://www.example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-8)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-9)         print(result.markdown[:300])  # Show the first 300 characters of extracted text [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-11) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-3-12)     asyncio.run(main())`

\- A headless browser session loads `example.com` - Crawl4AI returns ~300 characters of markdown.  
If errors occur, rerun `crawl4ai-doctor` or manually ensure Playwright is installed correctly.

4\. Advanced Installation (Optional)
------------------------------------

### 4.1 Torch, Transformers, or All

*   **Text Clustering (Torch)**  
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-4-1) pip install crawl4ai[torch] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-4-2) crawl4ai-setup`
    
*   **Transformers**  
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-5-1) pip install crawl4ai[transformer] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-5-2) crawl4ai-setup`
    
*   **All Features**  
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-6-1) pip install crawl4ai[all] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-6-2) crawl4ai-setup`
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-7-1) crawl4ai-download-models`
    

5\. Docker (Experimental)
-------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-8-1) docker pull unclecode/crawl4ai:basic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-8-2) docker run -p 11235:11235 unclecode/crawl4ai:basic`

You can then make POST requests to `http://localhost:11235/crawl` to perform crawls. **Production usage** is discouraged until our new Docker approach is ready (planned in Jan or Feb 2025).

6\. Local Server Mode (Legacy)
------------------------------

Summary
-------

1. **Install** with `pip install crawl4ai` and run `crawl4ai-setup`. 2. **Diagnose** with `crawl4ai-doctor` if you see errors. 3. **Verify** by crawling `example.com` with minimal `BrowserConfig` + `CrawlerRunConfig`.

Quick Start
===========

Getting Started with Crawl4AI
=============================

1.  Run your **first crawl** using minimal configuration.
2.  Experiment with a simple **CSS-based extraction** strategy.
3.  Crawl a **dynamic** page that loads content via JavaScript.

1\. Introduction
----------------

*   An asynchronous crawler, **`AsyncWebCrawler`**.
*   Configurable browser and run settings via **`BrowserConfig`** and **`CrawlerRunConfig`**.
*   Automatic HTML-to-Markdown conversion via **`DefaultMarkdownGenerator`** (supports optional filters).
*   Multiple extraction strategies (LLM-based or “traditional” CSS/XPath-based).

2\. Your First Crawl
--------------------

Here’s a minimal Python script that creates an **`AsyncWebCrawler`**, fetches a webpage, and prints the first 300 characters of its Markdown output:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-6)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-7)         print(result.markdown[:300])  # Print first 300 chars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-9) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-9-10)     asyncio.run(main())`

\- **`AsyncWebCrawler`** launches a headless browser (Chromium by default). - It fetches `https://example.com`. - Crawl4AI automatically converts the HTML into Markdown.

3\. Basic Configuration (Light Introduction)
--------------------------------------------

1. **`BrowserConfig`**: Controls browser behavior (headless or full UI, user agent, JavaScript toggles, etc.).  
2. **`CrawlerRunConfig`**: Controls how each crawl runs (caching, extraction, timeouts, hooking, etc.).

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-5)     browser_conf = BrowserConfig(headless=True)  # or False to see the browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-6)     run_conf = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-7)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-10)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_conf) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-11)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-12)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-13)             config=run_conf [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-14)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-15)         print(result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-17) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-10-18)     asyncio.run(main())`

> IMPORTANT: By default cache mode is set to `CacheMode.BYPASS` to have fresh content. Set `CacheMode.ENABLED` to enable caching.

4\. Generating Markdown Output
------------------------------

*   **`result.markdown`**:
*   **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`**:  
    The same content after applying any configured **content filter** (e.g., `PruningContentFilter`).

### Example: Using a Filter with `DefaultMarkdownGenerator`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-2) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-3) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-5) md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-6)     content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.4, threshold_type="fixed") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-9) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-10)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-11)     markdown_generator=md_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-12) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-14) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-15)     result = await crawler.arun("https://news.ycombinator.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-16)     print("Raw Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-11-17)     print("Fit Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.fit_markdown))`

**Note**: If you do **not** specify a content filter or markdown generator, you’ll typically see only the raw Markdown. `PruningContentFilter` may adds around `50ms` in processing time. We’ll dive deeper into these strategies in a dedicated **Markdown Generation** tutorial.

5\. Simple Data Extraction (CSS-based)
--------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-2) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-4) # Generate a schema (one-time cost) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-5) html = "<div class='product'><h2>Gaming Laptop</h2><span class='price'>$999.99</span></div>" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-7) # Using OpenAI (requires API token) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-8) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-9)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-10)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o",api_token="your-openai-token")  # Required for OpenAI [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-13) # Or using Ollama (open source, no token needed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-14) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-15)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-16)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="ollama/llama3.3", api_token=None)  # Not needed for Ollama [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-17) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-19) # Use the schema for fast, repeated extractions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-12-20) strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema)`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-8)         "name": "Example Items", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-9)         "baseSelector": "div.item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-11)             {"name": "title", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-12)             {"name": "link", "selector": "a", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-13)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-14)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-16)     raw_html = "<div class='item'><h2>Item 1</h2><a href='https://example.com/item1'>Link 1</a></div>" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-18)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-19)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-20)             url="raw://" + raw_html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-21)             config=CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-22)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-23)                 extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-24)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-25)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-26)         # The JSON output is stored in 'extracted_content' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-27)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-28)         print(data) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-30) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-13-31)     asyncio.run(main())`

\- Great for repetitive page structures (e.g., item listings, articles). - No AI usage or costs. - The crawler returns a JSON string you can parse or store. - For sites where data is split across sibling elements (e.g. Hacker News), use the `"source"` field key to navigate to a sibling before extracting: `{"name": "score", "selector": "span.score", "type": "text", "source": "+ tr"}`.

> Tips: You can pass raw HTML to the crawler instead of a URL. To do so, prefix the HTML with `raw://`.

6\. Simple Data Extraction (LLM-based)
--------------------------------------

*   **Open-Source Models** (e.g., `ollama/llama3.3`, `no_token`)
*   **OpenAI Models** (e.g., `openai/gpt-4`, requires `api_token`)
*   Or any provider supported by the underlying library
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-1) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-4) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-5) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-6) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-8) class OpenAIModelFee(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-9)     model_name: str = Field(..., description="Name of the OpenAI model.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-10)     input_fee: str = Field(..., description="Fee for input token for the OpenAI model.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-11)     output_fee: str = Field( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-12)         ..., description="Fee for output token for the OpenAI model." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-15) async def extract_structured_data_using_llm( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-16)     provider: str, api_token: str = None, extra_headers: Dict[str, str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-17) ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-18)     print(f"\n--- Extracting Structured Data with {provider} ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-20)     if api_token is None and provider != "ollama": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-21)         print(f"API token is required for {provider}. Skipping this example.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-22)         return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-24)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-26)     extra_args = {"temperature": 0, "top_p": 0.9, "max_tokens": 2000} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-27)     if extra_headers: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-28)         extra_args["extra_headers"] = extra_headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-30)     crawler_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-31)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-32)         word_count_threshold=1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-33)         page_timeout=80000, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-34)         extraction_strategy=LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-35)             llm_config = LLMConfig(provider=provider,api_token=api_token), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-36)             schema=OpenAIModelFee.model_json_schema(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-37)             extraction_type="schema", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-38)             instruction="""From the crawled content, extract all mentioned model names along with their fees for input and output tokens.  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-39)             Do not miss any models in the entire content.""", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-40)             extra_args=extra_args, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-41)         ), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-42)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-43) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-44)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-45)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-46)             url="https://openai.com/api/pricing/", config=crawler_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-47)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-48)         print(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-50) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-51) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-52)     asyncio.run( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-53)         extract_structured_data_using_llm( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-54)             provider="openai/gpt-4o", api_token=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-55)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-14-56)     )`
    
*   We define a Pydantic schema (`PricingInfo`) describing the fields we want.

7\. Adaptive Crawling (New!)
----------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, AdaptiveCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-4) async def adaptive_example(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-5)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-6)         adaptive = AdaptiveCrawler(crawler) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-8)         # Start adaptive crawling [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-9)         result = await adaptive.digest( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-10)             start_url="https://docs.python.org/3/", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-11)             query="async context managers" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-12)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-14)         # View results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-15)         adaptive.print_stats() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-16)         print(f"Crawled {len(result.crawled_urls)} pages") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-17)         print(f"Achieved {adaptive.confidence:.0%} confidence") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-19) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-15-20)     asyncio.run(adaptive_example())`

\- **Automatic stopping**: Stops when sufficient information is gathered - **Intelligent link selection**: Follows only relevant links - **Confidence scoring**: Know how complete your information is

8\. Multi-URL Concurrency (Preview)
-----------------------------------

If you need to crawl multiple URLs in **parallel**, you can use `arun_many()`. By default, Crawl4AI employs a **MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher**, automatically adjusting concurrency based on system resources. Here’s a quick glimpse:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-4) async def quick_parallel_example(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-5)     urls = [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-6)         "https://example.com/page1", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-7)         "https://example.com/page2", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-8)         "https://example.com/page3" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-9)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-11)     run_conf = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-12)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-13)         stream=True  # Enable streaming mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-14)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-16)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-17)         # Stream results as they complete [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-18)         async for result in await crawler.arun_many(urls, config=run_conf): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-19)             if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-20)                 print(f"[OK] {result.url}, length: {len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-21)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-22)                 print(f"[ERROR] {result.url} => {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-24)         # Or get all results at once (default behavior) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-25)         run_conf = run_conf.clone(stream=False) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-26)         results = await crawler.arun_many(urls, config=run_conf) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-27)         for res in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-28)             if res.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-29)                 print(f"[OK] {res.url}, length: {len(res.markdown.raw_markdown)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-30)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-31)                 print(f"[ERROR] {res.url} => {res.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-33) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-16-34)     asyncio.run(quick_parallel_example())`

1\. **Streaming mode** (`stream=True`): Process results as they become available using `async for` 2. **Batch mode** (`stream=False`): Wait for all results to complete

8\. Dynamic Content Example
---------------------------

Some sites require multiple “page clicks” or dynamic JavaScript updates. Below is an example showing how to **click** a “Next Page” button and wait for new commits to load on GitHub, using **`BrowserConfig`** and **`CrawlerRunConfig`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-3) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-5) async def extract_structured_data_using_css_extractor(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-6)     print("\n--- Using JsonCssExtractionStrategy for Fast Structured Output ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-8)         "name": "KidoCode Courses", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-9)         "baseSelector": "section.charge-methodology .w-tab-content > div", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-11)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-12)                 "name": "section_title", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-13)                 "selector": "h3.heading-50", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-14)                 "type": "text", java_script_enabled=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-42)     js_click_tabs = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-43)     (async () => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-44)         const tabs = document.querySelectorAll("section.charge-methodology .tabs-menu-3 > div"); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-45)         for(let tab of tabs) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-46)             tab.scrollIntoView(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-47)             tab.click(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-48)             await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500)); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-49)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-50)     })(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-51)     """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-52) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-53)     crawler_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-54)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-55)         extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-56)         js_code=[js_click_tabs], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-57)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-58) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-59)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-60)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-61)             url="https://www.kidocode.com/degrees/technology", config=crawler_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-62)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-63) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-64)         companies = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-65)         print(f"Successfully extracted {len(companies)} companies") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-66)         print(json.dumps(companies[0], indent=2)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-67) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-68) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-69)     await extract_structured_data_using_css_extractor() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-70) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-71) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-17-72)     asyncio.run(main())`

\- **`BrowserConfig(headless=False)`**: We want to watch it click “Next Page.”  
\- **`CrawlerRunConfig(...)`**: We specify the extraction strategy, pass `session_id` to reuse the same page.  
\- **`js_code`** and **`wait_for`** are used for subsequent pages (`page > 0`) to click the “Next” button and wait for new commits to load.  
\- **`js_only=True`** indicates we’re not re-navigating but continuing the existing session.  
\- Finally, we call `kill_session()` to clean up the page and browser session.

9\. Next Steps
--------------

1.  Performed a basic crawl and printed Markdown.
2.  Used **content filters** with a markdown generator.
3.  Extracted JSON via **CSS** or **LLM** strategies.
4.  Handled **dynamic** pages with JavaScript triggers.

Core API
========

AsyncWebCrawler
===============

The **`AsyncWebCrawler`** is the core class for asynchronous web crawling in Crawl4AI. You typically create it **once**, optionally customize it with a **`BrowserConfig`** (e.g., headless, user agent), then **run** multiple **`arun()`** calls with different **`CrawlerRunConfig`** objects. 1. **Create** a `BrowserConfig` for global browser settings.  2. **Instantiate** `AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config)`.  3. **Use** the crawler in an async context manager (`async with`) or manage start/close manually.  4. **Call** `arun(url, config=crawler_run_config)` for each page you want.

1. Constructor Overview
-----------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-1) class AsyncWebCrawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-2)     def __init__( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-3)         self, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-4)         crawler_strategy: Optional[AsyncCrawlerStrategy] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-5)         config: Optional[BrowserConfig] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-6)         always_bypass_cache: bool = False,           # deprecated [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-7)         always_by_pass_cache: Optional[bool] = None, # also deprecated [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-8)         base_directory: str = ..., [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-9)         thread_safe: bool = False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-10)         **kwargs, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-11)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-12)         """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-13)         Create an AsyncWebCrawler instance. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-15)         Args: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-16)             crawler_strategy:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-17)                 (Advanced) Provide a custom crawler strategy if needed. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-18)             config:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-19)                 A BrowserConfig object specifying how the browser is set up. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-20)             always_bypass_cache:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-21)                 (Deprecated) Use CrawlerRunConfig.cache_mode instead. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-22)             base_directory:      [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-23)                 Folder for storing caches/logs (if relevant). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-24)             thread_safe:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-25)                 If True, attempts some concurrency safeguards. Usually False. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-26)             **kwargs:  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-27)                 Additional legacy or debugging parameters. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-28)         """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-29)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-31) ### Typical Initialization [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-33) ```python [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-34) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-35) browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-36)     browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-37)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-38)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-18-39) crawler = AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg)`

**Notes**:

*   **Legacy** parameters like `always_bypass_cache` remain for backward compatibility, but prefer to set **caching** in `CrawlerRunConfig`.

* * *

2. Lifecycle: Start/Close or Context Manager
--------------------------------------------

### 2.1 Context Manager (Recommended)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-19-1) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-19-2)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-19-3)     # The crawler automatically starts/closes resources`

When the `async with` block ends, the crawler cleans up (closes the browser, etc.).

### 2.2 Manual Start & Close

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-20-1) crawler = AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-20-2) await crawler.start() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-20-3) result1 = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-20-4) result2 = await crawler.arun("https://another.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-20-5) await crawler.close()`

Use this style if you have a **long-running** application or need full control of the crawler’s lifecycle.

* * *

3. Primary Method: `arun()`
---------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-21-1) async def arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-21-2)     url: str, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-21-3)     config: Optional[CrawlerRunConfig] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-21-4)     # Legacy parameters for backward compatibility...`

### 3.1 New Approach

You pass a `CrawlerRunConfig` object that sets up everything about a crawl—content filtering, caching, session reuse, JS code, screenshots, etc.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-2) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-3) run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-4)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-5)     css_selector="main.article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-6)     word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-7)     screenshot=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-8) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-22-9)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/news", config=run_cfg)`

### 3.2 Legacy Parameters Still Accepted

For **backward** compatibility, `arun()` can still accept direct arguments like `css_selector=...`, `word_count_threshold=...`, etc., but we strongly advise migrating them into a **`CrawlerRunConfig`**.

* * *

4. Batch Processing: `arun_many()`
----------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-23-1) async def arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-23-2)     urls: List[str], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-23-3)     config: Optional[CrawlerRunConfig] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-23-4)     # Legacy parameters maintained for backwards compatibility...`

### 4.1 Resource-Aware Crawling

The `arun_many()` method now uses an intelligent dispatcher that:

*   Monitors system memory usage
*   Implements adaptive rate limiting
*   Provides detailed progress monitoring
*   Manages concurrent crawls efficiently

### 4.2 Example Usage

Check page [Multi-url Crawling](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling.md)
 for a detailed example of how to use `arun_many()`.

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-1) ### 4.3 Key Features [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-2) 1. **Rate Limiting** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-3)    - Automatic delay between requests [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-4)    - Exponential backoff on rate limit detection [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-5)    - Domain-specific rate limiting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-6)    - Configurable retry strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-7) 2. **Resource Monitoring** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-8)    - Memory usage tracking [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-9)    - Adaptive concurrency based on system load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-10)    - Automatic pausing when resources are constrained [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-11) 3. **Progress Monitoring** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-12)    - Detailed or aggregated progress display [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-13)    - Real-time status updates [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-14)    - Memory usage statistics [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-15) 4. **Error Handling** [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-16)    - Graceful handling of rate limits [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-17)    - Automatic retries with backoff [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-18)    - Detailed error reporting [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-19) ## 5. `CrawlResult` Output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-20) Each `arun()` returns a **`CrawlResult`** containing: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-21) - `url`: Final URL (if redirected). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-22) - `html`: Original HTML. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-23) - `cleaned_html`: Sanitized HTML. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-24) - `markdown_v2`: Deprecated. Instead just use regular `markdown` [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-25) - `extracted_content`: If an extraction strategy was used (JSON for CSS/LLM strategies). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-26) - `screenshot`, `pdf`: If screenshots/PDF requested. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-27) - `media`, `links`: Information about discovered images/links. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-28) - `success`, `error_message`: Status info. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-29) ## 6. Quick Example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-30) ```python [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-31) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-32) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-33) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-34) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-36) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-37)     # 1. Browser config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-38)     browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-39)         browser_type="firefox", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-40)         headless=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-41)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-42)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-43) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-44)     # 2. Run config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-45)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-46)         "name": "Articles", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-47)         "baseSelector": "article.post", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-48)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-49)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-50)                 "name": "title",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-51)                 "selector": "h2",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-52)                 "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-53)             }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-54)             { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-55)                 "name": "url",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-56)                 "selector": "a",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-57)                 "type": "attribute",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-58)                 "attribute": "href" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-59)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-60)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-61)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-62) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-63)     run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-64)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-65)         extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-66)         word_count_threshold=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-67)         remove_overlay_elements=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-68)         wait_for="css:.post"  # Wait for posts to appear [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-69)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-70) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-71)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-72)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-73)             url="https://example.com/blog", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-74)             config=run_cfg [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-75)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-76) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-77)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-78)             print("Cleaned HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-79)             if result.extracted_content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-80)                 articles = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-81)                 print("Extracted articles:", articles[:2]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-82)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-83)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-84) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-24-85) asyncio.run(main())``

\- We define a **`BrowserConfig`** with Firefox, no headless, and `verbose=True`.  - We define a **`CrawlerRunConfig`** that **bypasses cache**, uses a **CSS** extraction schema, has a `word_count_threshold=15`, etc.  - We pass them to `AsyncWebCrawler(config=...)` and `arun(url=..., config=...)`.

7. Best Practices & Migration Notes
-----------------------------------

1. **Use** `BrowserConfig` for **global** settings about the browser’s environment.  2. **Use** `CrawlerRunConfig` for **per-crawl** logic (caching, content filtering, extraction strategies, wait conditions).  3. **Avoid** legacy parameters like `css_selector` or `word_count_threshold` directly in `arun()`. Instead:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-25-1) run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig(css_selector=".main-content", word_count_threshold=20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-25-2) result = await crawler.arun(url="...", config=run_cfg)`

8. Summary
----------

*   **Constructor** accepts **`BrowserConfig`** (or defaults). 
*   **`arun(url, config=CrawlerRunConfig)`** is the main method for single-page crawls. 
*   **`arun_many(urls, config=CrawlerRunConfig)`** handles concurrency across multiple URLs. 
*   For advanced lifecycle control, use `start()` and `close()` explicitly. 
*   If you used `AsyncWebCrawler(browser_type="chromium", css_selector="...")`, move browser settings to `BrowserConfig(...)` and content/crawl logic to `CrawlerRunConfig(...)`.

`arun()` Parameter Guide (New Approach)
=======================================

In Crawl4AI’s **latest** configuration model, nearly all parameters that once went directly to `arun()` are now part of **`CrawlerRunConfig`**. When calling `arun()`, you provide:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-26-1) await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-26-2)     url="https://example.com",   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-26-3)     config=my_run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-26-4) )`

Below is an organized look at the parameters that can go inside `CrawlerRunConfig`, divided by their functional areas. For **Browser** settings (e.g., `headless`, `browser_type`), see [BrowserConfig](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/parameters.md)
.

1. Core Usage
-------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-3) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-4)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-5)         verbose=True,            # Detailed logging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-6)         cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED,  # Use normal read/write cache [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-7)         check_robots_txt=True,   # Respect robots.txt rules [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-8)         # ... other parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-11)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-12)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-13)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-14)             config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-15)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-17)         # Check if blocked by robots.txt [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-18)         if not result.success and result.status_code == 403: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-27-19)             print(f"Error: {result.error_message}")`

\- `verbose=True` logs each crawl step.  - `cache_mode` decides how to read/write the local crawl cache.

2. Cache Control
----------------

**`cache_mode`** (default: `CacheMode.ENABLED`)  
Use a built-in enum from `CacheMode`: - `ENABLED`: Normal caching—reads if available, writes if missing. - `DISABLED`: No caching—always refetch pages. - `READ_ONLY`: Reads from cache only; no new writes. - `WRITE_ONLY`: Writes to cache but doesn’t read existing data. - `BYPASS`: Skips reading cache for this crawl (though it might still write if set up that way).

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-28-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-28-2)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-28-3) )`

\- `bypass_cache=True` acts like `CacheMode.BYPASS`. - `disable_cache=True` acts like `CacheMode.DISABLED`. - `no_cache_read=True` acts like `CacheMode.WRITE_ONLY`. - `no_cache_write=True` acts like `CacheMode.READ_ONLY`.

3. Content Processing & Selection
---------------------------------

### 3.1 Text Processing

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-29-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-29-2)     word_count_threshold=10,   # Ignore text blocks <10 words [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-29-3)     only_text=False,           # If True, tries to remove non-text elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-29-4)     keep_data_attributes=False # Keep or discard data-* attributes [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-29-5) )`

### 3.2 Content Selection

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-30-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-30-2)     css_selector=".main-content",  # Focus on .main-content region only [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-30-3)     excluded_tags=["form", "nav"], # Remove entire tag blocks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-30-4)     remove_forms=True,             # Specifically strip <form> elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-30-5)     remove_overlay_elements=True,  # Attempt to remove modals/popups [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-30-6) )`

### 3.3 Link Handling

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-31-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-31-2)     exclude_external_links=True,         # Remove external links from final content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-31-3)     exclude_social_media_links=True,     # Remove links to known social sites [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-31-4)     exclude_domains=["ads.example.com"], # Exclude links to these domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-31-5)     exclude_social_media_domains=["facebook.com","twitter.com"], # Extend the default list [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-31-6) )`

### 3.4 Media Filtering

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-32-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-32-2)     exclude_external_images=True  # Strip images from other domains [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-32-3) )`

4. Page Navigation & Timing
---------------------------

### 4.1 Basic Browser Flow

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-33-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-33-2)     wait_for="css:.dynamic-content", # Wait for .dynamic-content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-33-3)     delay_before_return_html=2.0,    # Wait 2s before capturing final HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-33-4)     page_timeout=60000,             # Navigation & script timeout (ms) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-33-5) )`

\- `wait_for`:  
\- `"css:selector"` or  
\- `"js:() => boolean"`  
e.g. `js:() => document.querySelectorAll('.item').length > 10`. - `mean_delay` & `max_range`: define random delays for `arun_many()` calls.  - `semaphore_count`: concurrency limit when crawling multiple URLs.

### 4.2 JavaScript Execution

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-34-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-34-2)     js_code=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-34-3)         "window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-34-4)         "document.querySelector('.load-more')?.click();" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-34-5)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-34-6)     js_only=False [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-34-7) )`

\- `js_code` can be a single string or a list of strings.  - `js_only=True` means “I’m continuing in the same session with new JS steps, no new full navigation.”

### 4.3 Anti-Bot

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-35-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-35-2)     magic=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-35-3)     simulate_user=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-35-4)     override_navigator=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-35-5) )`

\- `magic=True` tries multiple stealth features.  - `simulate_user=True` mimics mouse movements or random delays.  - `override_navigator=True` fakes some navigator properties (like user agent checks).

5. Session Management
---------------------

**`session_id`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-36-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-36-2)     session_id="my_session123" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-36-3) )`

If re-used in subsequent `arun()` calls, the same tab/page context is continued (helpful for multi-step tasks or stateful browsing).

6. Screenshot, PDF & Media Options
----------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-37-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-37-2)     screenshot=True,             # Grab a screenshot as base64 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-37-3)     screenshot_wait_for=1.0,     # Wait 1s before capturing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-37-4)     pdf=True,                    # Also produce a PDF [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-37-5)     image_description_min_word_threshold=5,  # If analyzing alt text [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-37-6)     image_score_threshold=3,                # Filter out low-score images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-37-7) )`

\- `result.screenshot` → Base64 screenshot string. - `result.pdf` → Byte array with PDF data.

7. Extraction Strategy
----------------------

**For advanced data extraction** (CSS/LLM-based), set `extraction_strategy`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-38-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-38-2)     extraction_strategy=my_css_or_llm_strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-38-3) )`

The extracted data will appear in `result.extracted_content`.

8. Comprehensive Example
------------------------

Below is a snippet combining many parameters:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-3) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-6)     # Example schema [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-7)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-8)         "name": "Articles", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-9)         "baseSelector": "article.post", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-10)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-11)             {"name": "title", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-12)             {"name": "link",  "selector": "a",  "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-13)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-14)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-16)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-17)         # Core [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-18)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-19)         cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-20)         check_robots_txt=True,   # Respect robots.txt rules [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-22)         # Content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-23)         word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-24)         css_selector="main.content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-25)         excluded_tags=["nav", "footer"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-26)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-28)         # Page & JS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-29)         js_code="document.querySelector('.show-more')?.click();", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-30)         wait_for="css:.loaded-block", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-31)         page_timeout=30000, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-33)         # Extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-34)         extraction_strategy=JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-36)         # Session [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-37)         session_id="persistent_session", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-39)         # Media [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-40)         screenshot=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-41)         pdf=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-43)         # Anti-bot [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-44)         simulate_user=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-45)         magic=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-46)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-48)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-49)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/posts", config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-50)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-51)             print("HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-52)             print("Extraction JSON:", result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-53)             if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-54)                 print("Screenshot length:", len(result.screenshot)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-55)             if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-56)                 print("PDF bytes length:", len(result.pdf)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-57)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-58)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-59) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-60) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-39-61)     asyncio.run(main())`

1. **Crawling** the main content region, ignoring external links.  2. Running **JavaScript** to click “.show-more”.  3. **Waiting** for “.loaded-block” to appear.  4. Generating a **screenshot** & **PDF** of the final page. 

9. Best Practices
-----------------

1. **Use `BrowserConfig` for global browser** settings (headless, user agent).  2. **Use `CrawlerRunConfig`** to handle the **specific** crawl needs: content filtering, caching, JS, screenshot, extraction, etc.  4. **Limit** large concurrency (`semaphore_count`) if the site or your system can’t handle it.  5. For dynamic pages, set `js_code` or `scan_full_page` so you load all content.

10. Conclusion
--------------

All parameters that used to be direct arguments to `arun()` now belong in **`CrawlerRunConfig`**. This approach: - Makes code **clearer** and **more maintainable**. 

`arun_many(...)` Reference
==========================

> **Note**: This function is very similar to [`arun()`](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/arun.md)
>  but focused on **concurrent** or **batch** crawling. If you’re unfamiliar with `arun()` usage, please read that doc first, then review this for differences.

Function Signature
------------------

``[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-1) async def arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-2)     urls: Union[List[str], List[Any]], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-3)     config: Optional[Union[CrawlerRunConfig, List[CrawlerRunConfig]]] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-4)     dispatcher: Optional[BaseDispatcher] = None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-5)     ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-6) ) -> Union[List[CrawlResult], AsyncGenerator[CrawlResult, None]]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-7)     """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-8)     Crawl multiple URLs concurrently or in batches. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-10)     :param urls: A list of URLs (or tasks) to crawl. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-11)     :param config: (Optional) Either: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-12)         - A single `CrawlerRunConfig` applying to all URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-13)         - A list of `CrawlerRunConfig` objects with url_matcher patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-14)     :param dispatcher: (Optional) A concurrency controller (e.g. MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-15)     ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-16)     :return: Either a list of `CrawlResult` objects, or an async generator if streaming is enabled. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-40-17)     """``

Differences from `arun()`
-------------------------

1. **Multiple URLs**:  
\- Instead of crawling a single URL, you pass a list of them (strings or tasks).  - The function returns either a **list** of `CrawlResult` or an **async generator** if streaming is enabled. 2. **Concurrency & Dispatchers**:  
\- **`dispatcher`** param allows advanced concurrency control.  - If omitted, a default dispatcher (like `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher`) is used internally.  3. **Streaming Support**:  
\- Enable streaming by setting `stream=True` in your `CrawlerRunConfig`. - When streaming, use `async for` to process results as they become available. 4. **Parallel** Execution**:  
\- `arun_many()` can run multiple requests concurrently under the hood.  - Each `CrawlResult` might also include a** `dispatch_result`\*\* with concurrency details (like memory usage, start/end times).

### Basic Example (Batch Mode)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-1) # Minimal usage: The default dispatcher will be used [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-2) results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-3)     urls=["https://site1.com", "https://site2.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-4)     config=CrawlerRunConfig(stream=False)  # Default behavior [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-7) for res in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-8)     if res.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-9)         print(res.url, "crawled OK!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-10)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-41-11)         print("Failed:", res.url, "-", res.error_message)`

### Streaming Example
