`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-2)     stream=True,  # Enable streaming mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-3)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-6) # Process results as they complete [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-7) async for result in await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-8)     urls=["https://site1.com", "https://site2.com", "https://site3.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-9)     config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-10) ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-11)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-12)         print(f"Just completed: {result.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-13)         # Process each result immediately [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-42-14)         process_result(result)`

### With a Custom Dispatcher

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-1) dispatcher = MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-2)     memory_threshold_percent=70.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-3)     max_session_permit=10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-5) results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-6)     urls=["https://site1.com", "https://site2.com", "https://site3.com"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-7)     config=my_run_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-8)     dispatcher=dispatcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-43-9) )`

### URL-Specific Configurations

Instead of using one config for all URLs, provide a list of configs with `url_matcher` patterns:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-1) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig, MatchMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-2) from crawl4ai.processors.pdf import PDFContentScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-3) from crawl4ai.extraction_strategy import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-4) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-5) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-7) # PDF files - specialized extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-8) pdf_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-9)     url_matcher="*.pdf", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-10)     scraping_strategy=PDFContentScrapingStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-13) # Blog/article pages - content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-14) blog_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-15)     url_matcher=["*/blog/*", "*/article/*", "*python.org*"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-16)     markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-17)         content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-18)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-19) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-21) # Dynamic pages - JavaScript execution [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-22) github_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-23)     url_matcher=lambda url: 'github.com' in url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-24)     js_code="window.scrollTo(0, 500);" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-25) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-27) # API endpoints - JSON extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-28) api_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-29)     url_matcher=lambda url: 'api' in url or url.endswith('.json'), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-30)     # Custome settings for JSON extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-31) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-33) # Default fallback config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-34) default_config = CrawlerRunConfig()  # No url_matcher means it never matches except as fallback [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-36) # Pass the list of configs - first match wins! [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-37) results = await crawler.arun_many( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-38)     urls=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-39)         "https://www.w3.org/WAI/ER/tests/xhtml/testfiles/resources/pdf/dummy.pdf",  # → pdf_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-40)         "https://blog.python.org/",  # → blog_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-41)         "https://github.com/microsoft/playwright",  # → github_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-42)         "https://httpbin.org/json",  # → api_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-43)         "https://example.com/"  # → default_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-44)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-45)     config=[pdf_config, blog_config, github_config, api_config, default_config] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-44-46) )`

\- **String patterns**: `"*.pdf"`, `"*/blog/*"`, `"*python.org*"` - **Function matchers**: `lambda url: 'api' in url` - **Mixed patterns**: Combine strings and functions with `MatchMode.OR` or `MatchMode.AND` - **First match wins**: Configs are evaluated in order - `dispatch_result` in each `CrawlResult` (if using concurrency) can hold memory and timing info.  - **Important**: Always include a default config (without `url_matcher`) as the last item if you want to handle all URLs. Otherwise, unmatched URLs will fail.

### Return Value

Either a **list** of [`CrawlResult`](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/crawl-result.md)
 objects, or an **async generator** if streaming is enabled. You can iterate to check `result.success` or read each item’s `extracted_content`, `markdown`, or `dispatch_result`.

Dispatcher Reference
--------------------

*   **`MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher`**: Dynamically manages concurrency based on system memory usage. 
*   **`SemaphoreDispatcher`**: Fixed concurrency limit, simpler but less adaptive. 

Common Pitfalls
---------------

3. **Error Handling**: Each `CrawlResult` might fail for different reasons—always check `result.success` or the `error_message` before proceeding.

Conclusion
----------

Use `arun_many()` when you want to **crawl multiple URLs** simultaneously or in controlled parallel tasks. If you need advanced concurrency features (like memory-based adaptive throttling or complex rate-limiting), provide a **dispatcher**. Each result is a standard `CrawlResult`, possibly augmented with concurrency stats (`dispatch_result`) for deeper inspection. For more details on concurrency logic and dispatchers, see the [Advanced Multi-URL Crawling](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/multi-url-crawling.md)
 docs.

`CrawlResult` Reference
=======================

The **`CrawlResult`** class encapsulates everything returned after a single crawl operation. It provides the **raw or processed content**, details on links and media, plus optional metadata (like screenshots, PDFs, or extracted JSON). **Location**: `crawl4ai/crawler/models.py` (for reference)

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-1) class CrawlResult(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-2)     url: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-3)     html: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-4)     success: bool [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-5)     cleaned_html: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-6)     fit_html: Optional[str] = None  # Preprocessed HTML optimized for extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-7)     media: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-8)     links: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-9)     downloaded_files: Optional[List[str]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-10)     screenshot: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-11)     pdf : Optional[bytes] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-12)     mhtml: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-13)     markdown: Optional[Union[str, MarkdownGenerationResult]] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-14)     extracted_content: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-15)     metadata: Optional[dict] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-16)     error_message: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-17)     session_id: Optional[str] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-18)     response_headers: Optional[dict] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-19)     status_code: Optional[int] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-20)     ssl_certificate: Optional[SSLCertificate] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-21)     dispatch_result: Optional[DispatchResult] = None [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-45-22)     ...`

1\. Basic Crawl Info
--------------------

### 1.1 **`url`** _(str)_

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-46-1) print(result.url)  # e.g., "https://example.com/"`

### 1.2 **`success`** _(bool)_

**What**: `True` if the crawl pipeline ended without major errors; `False` otherwise.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-47-1) if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-47-2)     print(f"Crawl failed: {result.error_message}")`

### 1.3 **`status_code`** _(Optional\[int\])_

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-48-1) if result.status_code == 404: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-48-2)     print("Page not found!")`

### 1.4 **`error_message`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: If `success=False`, a textual description of the failure.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-49-1) if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-49-2)     print("Error:", result.error_message)`

### 1.5 **`session_id`** _(Optional\[str\])_

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-50-1) # If you used session_id="login_session" in CrawlerRunConfig, see it here: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-50-2) print("Session:", result.session_id)`

### 1.6 **`response_headers`** _(Optional\[dict\])_

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-51-1) if result.response_headers: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-51-2)     print("Server:", result.response_headers.get("Server", "Unknown"))`

### 1.7 **`ssl_certificate`** _(Optional\[SSLCertificate\])_

**What**: If `fetch_ssl_certificate=True` in your CrawlerRunConfig, **`result.ssl_certificate`** contains a [**`SSLCertificate`**](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/advanced/ssl-certificate.md)
 object describing the site's certificate. You can export the cert in multiple formats (PEM/DER/JSON) or access its properties like `issuer`, `subject`, `valid_from`, `valid_until`, etc.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-52-1) if result.ssl_certificate: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-52-2)     print("Issuer:", result.ssl_certificate.issuer)`

2\. Raw / Cleaned Content
-------------------------

### 2.1 **`html`** _(str)_

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-53-1) # Possibly large [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-53-2) print(len(result.html))`

### 2.2 **`cleaned_html`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: A sanitized HTML version—scripts, styles, or excluded tags are removed based on your `CrawlerRunConfig`.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-54-1) print(result.cleaned_html[:500])  # Show a snippet`

3\. Markdown Fields
-------------------

### 3.1 The Markdown Generation Approach

*   **Raw** markdown
*   **Links as citations** (with a references section)
*   **Fit** markdown if a **content filter** is used (like Pruning or BM25) **`MarkdownGenerationResult`** includes:
*   **`raw_markdown`** _(str)_: The full HTML→Markdown conversion.
*   **`markdown_with_citations`** _(str)_: Same markdown, but with link references as academic-style citations.
*   **`references_markdown`** _(str)_: The reference list or footnotes at the end.
*   **`fit_markdown`** _(Optional\[str\])_: If content filtering (Pruning/BM25) was applied, the filtered "fit" text.
*   **`fit_html`** _(Optional\[str\])_: The HTML that led to `fit_markdown`.
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-55-1) if result.markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-55-2)     md_res = result.markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-55-3)     print("Raw MD:", md_res.raw_markdown[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-55-4)     print("Citations MD:", md_res.markdown_with_citations[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-55-5)     print("References:", md_res.references_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-55-6)     if md_res.fit_markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-55-7)         print("Pruned text:", md_res.fit_markdown[:300])`
    

### 3.2 **`markdown`** _(Optional\[Union\[str, MarkdownGenerationResult\]\])_

**What**: Holds the `MarkdownGenerationResult`.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-56-1) print(result.markdown.raw_markdown[:200]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-56-2) print(result.markdown.fit_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-56-3) print(result.markdown.fit_html)`

**Important**: "Fit" content (in `fit_markdown`/`fit_html`) exists in result.markdown, only if you used a **filter** (like **PruningContentFilter** or **BM25ContentFilter**) within a `MarkdownGenerationStrategy`.

4\. Media & Links
-----------------

### 4.1 **`media`** _(Dict\[str, List\[Dict\]\])_

**What**: Contains info about discovered images, videos, or audio. Typically keys: `"images"`, `"videos"`, `"audios"`.  
\- `src` _(str)_: Media URL  
\- `alt` or `title` _(str)_: Descriptive text  
\- `score` _(float)_: Relevance score if the crawler's heuristic found it "important"  
\- `desc` or `description` _(Optional\[str\])_: Additional context extracted from surrounding text  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-57-1) images = result.media.get("images", []) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-57-2) for img in images: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-57-3)     if img.get("score", 0) > 5: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-57-4)         print("High-value image:", img["src"])`

### 4.2 **`links`** _(Dict\[str, List\[Dict\]\])_

**What**: Holds internal and external link data. Usually two keys: `"internal"` and `"external"`.  
\- `href` _(str)_: The link target  
\- `text` _(str)_: Link text  
\- `title` _(str)_: Title attribute  
\- `context` _(str)_: Surrounding text snippet  
\- `domain` _(str)_: If external, the domain

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-58-1) for link in result.links["internal"]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-58-2)     print(f"Internal link to {link['href']} with text {link['text']}")`

5\. Additional Fields
---------------------

### 5.1 **`extracted_content`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: If you used **`extraction_strategy`** (CSS, LLM, etc.), the structured output (JSON).  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-59-1) if result.extracted_content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-59-2)     data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-59-3)     print(data)`

### 5.2 **`downloaded_files`** _(Optional\[List\[str\]\])_

**What**: If `accept_downloads=True` in your `BrowserConfig` + `downloads_path`, lists local file paths for downloaded items.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-60-1) if result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-60-2)     for file_path in result.downloaded_files: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-60-3)         print("Downloaded:", file_path)`

### 5.3 **`screenshot`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: Base64-encoded screenshot if `screenshot=True` in `CrawlerRunConfig`.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-61-1) import base64 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-61-2) if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-61-3)     with open("page.png", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-61-4)         f.write(base64.b64decode(result.screenshot))`

### 5.4 **`pdf`** _(Optional\[bytes\])_

**What**: Raw PDF bytes if `pdf=True` in `CrawlerRunConfig`.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-62-1) if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-62-2)     with open("page.pdf", "wb") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-62-3)         f.write(result.pdf)`

### 5.5 **`mhtml`** _(Optional\[str\])_

**What**: MHTML snapshot of the page if `capture_mhtml=True` in `CrawlerRunConfig`. MHTML (MIME HTML) format preserves the entire web page with all its resources (CSS, images, scripts, etc.) in a single file.  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-63-1) if result.mhtml: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-63-2)     with open("page.mhtml", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-63-3)         f.write(result.mhtml)`

### 5.6 **`metadata`** _(Optional\[dict\])_

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-64-1) if result.metadata: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-64-2)     print("Title:", result.metadata.get("title")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-64-3)     print("Author:", result.metadata.get("author"))`

6\. `dispatch_result` (optional)
--------------------------------

A `DispatchResult` object providing additional concurrency and resource usage information when crawling URLs in parallel (e.g., via `arun_many()` with custom dispatchers). It contains: - **`task_id`**: A unique identifier for the parallel task. - **`memory_usage`** (float): The memory (in MB) used at the time of completion. - **`peak_memory`** (float): The peak memory usage (in MB) recorded during the task's execution. - **`start_time`** / **`end_time`** (datetime): Time range for this crawling task. - **`error_message`** (str): Any dispatcher- or concurrency-related error encountered.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-65-1) # Example usage: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-65-2) for result in results: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-65-3)     if result.success and result.dispatch_result: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-65-4)         dr = result.dispatch_result [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-65-5)         print(f"URL: {result.url}, Task ID: {dr.task_id}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-65-6)         print(f"Memory: {dr.memory_usage:.1f} MB (Peak: {dr.peak_memory:.1f} MB)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-65-7)         print(f"Duration: {dr.end_time - dr.start_time}")`

> **Note**: This field is typically populated when using `arun_many(...)` alongside a **dispatcher** (e.g., `MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher` or `SemaphoreDispatcher`). If no concurrency or dispatcher is used, `dispatch_result` may remain `None`.

7\. Network Requests & Console Messages
---------------------------------------

When you enable network and console message capturing in `CrawlerRunConfig` using `capture_network_requests=True` and `capture_console_messages=True`, the `CrawlResult` will include these fields:

### 7.1 **`network_requests`** _(Optional\[List\[Dict\[str, Any\]\]\])_

*   Each item has an `event_type` field that can be `"request"`, `"response"`, or `"request_failed"`.
*   Request events include `url`, `method`, `headers`, `post_data`, `resource_type`, and `is_navigation_request`.
*   Response events include `url`, `status`, `status_text`, `headers`, and `request_timing`.
*   Failed request events include `url`, `method`, `resource_type`, and `failure_text`.
*   All events include a `timestamp` field.
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-1) if result.network_requests: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-2)     # Count different types of events [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-3)     requests = [r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "request"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-4)     responses = [r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "response"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-5)     failures = [r for r in result.network_requests if r.get("event_type") == "request_failed"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-7)     print(f"Captured {len(requests)} requests, {len(responses)} responses, and {len(failures)} failures") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-9)     # Analyze API calls [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-10)     api_calls = [r for r in requests if "api" in r.get("url", "")] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-12)     # Identify failed resources [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-13)     for failure in failures: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-66-14)         print(f"Failed to load: {failure.get('url')} - {failure.get('failure_text')}")`
    

### 7.2 **`console_messages`** _(Optional\[List\[Dict\[str, Any\]\]\])_

*   Each item has a `type` field indicating the message type (e.g., `"log"`, `"error"`, `"warning"`, etc.).
*   The `text` field contains the actual message text.
*   Some messages include `location` information (URL, line, column).
*   All messages include a `timestamp` field.
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-1) if result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-2)     # Count messages by type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-3)     message_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-4)     for msg in result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-5)         msg_type = msg.get("type", "unknown") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-6)         message_types[msg_type] = message_types.get(msg_type, 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-8)     print(f"Message type counts: {message_types}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-10)     # Display errors (which are usually most important) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-11)     for msg in result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-12)         if msg.get("type") == "error": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-67-13)             print(f"Error: {msg.get('text')}")`
    

8\. Example: Accessing Everything
---------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-1) async def handle_result(result: CrawlResult): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-2)     if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-3)         print("Crawl error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-4)         return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-6)     # Basic info [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-7)     print("Crawled URL:", result.url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-8)     print("Status code:", result.status_code) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-10)     # HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-11)     print("Original HTML size:", len(result.html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-12)     print("Cleaned HTML size:", len(result.cleaned_html or "")) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-14)     # Markdown output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-15)     if result.markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-16)         print("Raw Markdown:", result.markdown.raw_markdown[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-17)         print("Citations Markdown:", result.markdown.markdown_with_citations[:300]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-18)         if result.markdown.fit_markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-19)             print("Fit Markdown:", result.markdown.fit_markdown[:200]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-21)     # Media & Links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-22)     if "images" in result.media: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-23)         print("Image count:", len(result.media["images"])) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-24)     if "internal" in result.links: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-25)         print("Internal link count:", len(result.links["internal"])) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-27)     # Extraction strategy result [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-28)     if result.extracted_content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-29)         print("Structured data:", result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-31)     # Screenshot/PDF/MHTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-32)     if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-33)         print("Screenshot length:", len(result.screenshot)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-34)     if result.pdf: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-35)         print("PDF bytes length:", len(result.pdf)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-36)     if result.mhtml: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-37)         print("MHTML length:", len(result.mhtml)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-39)     # Network and console capturing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-40)     if result.network_requests: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-41)         print(f"Network requests captured: {len(result.network_requests)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-42)         # Analyze request types [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-43)         req_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-44)         for req in result.network_requests: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-45)             if "resource_type" in req: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-46)                 req_types[req["resource_type"]] = req_types.get(req["resource_type"], 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-47)         print(f"Resource types: {req_types}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-49)     if result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-50)         print(f"Console messages captured: {len(result.console_messages)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-51)         # Count by message type [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-52)         msg_types = {} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-53)         for msg in result.console_messages: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-54)             msg_types[msg.get("type", "unknown")] = msg_types.get(msg.get("type", "unknown"), 0) + 1 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-68-55)         print(f"Message types: {msg_types}")`

9\. Key Points & Future
-----------------------

1. **Deprecated legacy properties of CrawlResult**  
\- `markdown_v2` - Deprecated in v0.5. Just use `markdown`. It holds the `MarkdownGenerationResult` now! - `fit_markdown` and `fit_html` - Deprecated in v0.5. They can now be accessed via `MarkdownGenerationResult` in `result.markdown`. eg: `result.markdown.fit_markdown` and `result.markdown.fit_html` 2. **Fit Content**  
\- **`fit_markdown`** and **`fit_html`** appear in MarkdownGenerationResult, only if you used a content filter (like **PruningContentFilter** or **BM25ContentFilter**) inside your **MarkdownGenerationStrategy** or set them directly.  
\- If no filter is used, they remain `None`. 3. **References & Citations**  
\- If you enable link citations in your `DefaultMarkdownGenerator` (`options={"citations": True}`), you’ll see `markdown_with_citations` plus a **`references_markdown`** block. This helps large language models or academic-like referencing. 4. **Links & Media**  
\- `links["internal"]` and `links["external"]` group discovered anchors by domain.  
\- `media["images"]` / `["videos"]` / `["audios"]` store extracted media elements with optional scoring or context. 5. **Error Cases**  
\- If `success=False`, check `error_message` (e.g., timeouts, invalid URLs).  
\- `status_code` might be `None` if we failed before an HTTP response. Use **`CrawlResult`** to glean all final outputs and feed them into your data pipelines, AI models, or archives. With the synergy of a properly configured **BrowserConfig** and **CrawlerRunConfig**, the crawler can produce robust, structured results here in **`CrawlResult`**.

Configuration
=============

Browser, Crawler & LLM Configuration (Quick Overview)
=====================================================

Crawl4AI's flexibility stems from two key classes: 1. **`BrowserConfig`** – Dictates **how** the browser is launched and behaves (e.g., headless or visible, proxy, user agent).  
2\. **`CrawlerRunConfig`** – Dictates **how** each **crawl** operates (e.g., caching, extraction, timeouts, JavaScript code to run, etc.).  
3\. **`LLMConfig`** - Dictates **how** LLM providers are configured. (model, api token, base url, temperature etc.) In most examples, you create **one** `BrowserConfig` for the entire crawler session, then pass a **fresh** or re-used `CrawlerRunConfig` whenever you call `arun()`. This tutorial shows the most commonly used parameters. If you need advanced or rarely used fields, see the [Configuration Parameters](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters.md)
.

1\. BrowserConfig Essentials
----------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-1) class BrowserConfig: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-2)     def __init__( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-3)         browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-4)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-5)         proxy_config=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-6)         viewport_width=1080, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-7)         viewport_height=600, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-8)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-9)         use_persistent_context=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-10)         user_data_dir=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-11)         cookies=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-12)         headers=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-13)         user_agent=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-14)         text_mode=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-15)         light_mode=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-16)         extra_args=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-17)         enable_stealth=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-18)         # ... other advanced parameters omitted here [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-19)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-69-20)         ...`

### Key Fields to Note

1.  **`browser_type`**
2.  Options: `"chromium"`, `"firefox"`, or `"webkit"`.
3.  Defaults to `"chromium"`.
4.  If you need a different engine, specify it here.
5.  **`headless`**
6.  `True`: Runs the browser in headless mode (invisible browser).
7.  `False`: Runs the browser in visible mode, which helps with debugging.
8.  **`proxy_config`**
9.  A dictionary with fields like:  
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-70-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-70-2)     "server": "http://proxy.example.com:8080",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-70-3)     "username": "...",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-70-4)     "password": "..." [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-70-5) }`
    
10.  Leave as `None` if a proxy is not required.
11.  **`viewport_width` & `viewport_height`**:
12.  The initial window size.
13.  Some sites behave differently with smaller or bigger viewports.
14.  **`verbose`**:
15.  If `True`, prints extra logs.
16.  Handy for debugging.
17.  **`use_persistent_context`**:
18.  If `True`, uses a **persistent** browser profile, storing cookies/local storage across runs.
19.  Typically also set `user_data_dir` to point to a folder.
20.  **`cookies`** & **`headers`**:
21.  E.g. `cookies=[{"name": "session", "value": "abc123", "domain": "example.com"}]`.
22.  **`user_agent`**:
23.  Custom User-Agent string. If `None`, a default is used.
24.  You can also set `user_agent_mode="random"` for randomization (if you want to fight bot detection).
25.  **`text_mode`** & **`light_mode`**:
26.  `text_mode=True` disables images, possibly speeding up text-only crawls.
27.  `light_mode=True` turns off certain background features for performance.
28.  **`extra_args`**:
    *   Additional flags for the underlying browser.
    *   E.g. `["--disable-extensions"]`.
29.  **`enable_stealth`**:
    *   If `True`, enables stealth mode using playwright-stealth.
    *   Modifies browser fingerprints to avoid basic bot detection.
    *   Default is `False`. Recommended for sites with bot protection.

### Helper Methods

Both configuration classes provide a `clone()` method to create modified copies:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-1) # Create a base browser config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-2) base_browser = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-3)     browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-4)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-5)     text_mode=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-8) # Create a visible browser config for debugging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-9) debug_browser = base_browser.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-10)     headless=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-11)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-71-12) )`

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-3) browser_conf = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-4)     browser_type="firefox", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-5)     headless=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-6)     text_mode=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-9) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_conf) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-10)     result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-72-11)     print(result.markdown[:300])`

2\. CrawlerRunConfig Essentials
-------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-1) class CrawlerRunConfig: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-2)     def __init__( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-3)         word_count_threshold=200, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-4)         extraction_strategy=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-5)         markdown_generator=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-6)         cache_mode=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-7)         js_code=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-8)         wait_for=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-9)         screenshot=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-10)         pdf=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-11)         capture_mhtml=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-12)         # Location and Identity Parameters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-13)         locale=None,            # e.g. "en-US", "fr-FR" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-14)         timezone_id=None,       # e.g. "America/New_York" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-15)         geolocation=None,       # GeolocationConfig object [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-16)         # Resource Management [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-17)         enable_rate_limiting=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-18)         rate_limit_config=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-19)         memory_threshold_percent=70.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-20)         check_interval=1.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-21)         max_session_permit=20, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-22)         display_mode=None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-23)         verbose=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-24)         stream=False,  # Enable streaming for arun_many() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-25)         # ... other advanced parameters omitted [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-26)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-73-27)         ...`

### Key Fields to Note

1.  **`word_count_threshold`**:
2.  The minimum word count before a block is considered.
3.  If your site has lots of short paragraphs or items, you can lower it.
4.  **`extraction_strategy`**:
5.  Where you plug in JSON-based extraction (CSS, LLM, etc.).
6.  If `None`, no structured extraction is done (only raw/cleaned HTML + markdown).
7.  **`markdown_generator`**:
8.  E.g., `DefaultMarkdownGenerator(...)`, controlling how HTML→Markdown conversion is done.
9.  If `None`, a default approach is used.
10.  **`cache_mode`**:
11.  Controls caching behavior (`ENABLED`, `BYPASS`, `DISABLED`, etc.).
12.  If `None`, defaults to some level of caching or you can specify `CacheMode.ENABLED`.
13.  **`js_code`**:
14.  A string or list of JS strings to execute.
15.  Great for "Load More" buttons or user interactions.
16.  **`wait_for`**:
17.  A CSS or JS expression to wait for before extracting content.
18.  Common usage: `wait_for="css:.main-loaded"` or `wait_for="js:() => window.loaded === true"`.
19.  **`screenshot`**, **`pdf`**, & **`capture_mhtml`**:
20.  If `True`, captures a screenshot, PDF, or MHTML snapshot after the page is fully loaded.
21.  The results go to `result.screenshot` (base64), `result.pdf` (bytes), or `result.mhtml` (string).
22.  **Location Parameters**:
23.  **`locale`**: Browser's locale (e.g., `"en-US"`, `"fr-FR"`) for language preferences
24.  **`timezone_id`**: Browser's timezone (e.g., `"America/New_York"`, `"Europe/Paris"`)
25.  **`geolocation`**: GPS coordinates via `GeolocationConfig(latitude=48.8566, longitude=2.3522)`
26.  **`verbose`**:
27.  Logs additional runtime details.
28.  Overlaps with the browser's verbosity if also set to `True` in `BrowserConfig`.
29.  **`enable_rate_limiting`**:
30.  If `True`, enables rate limiting for batch processing.
31.  Requires `rate_limit_config` to be set.
32.  **`memory_threshold_percent`**:
    *   The memory threshold (as a percentage) to monitor.
    *   If exceeded, the crawler will pause or slow down.
33.  **`check_interval`**:
    *   The interval (in seconds) to check system resources.
    *   Affects how often memory and CPU usage are monitored.
34.  **`max_session_permit`**:
    *   The maximum number of concurrent crawl sessions.
    *   Helps prevent overwhelming the system.
35.  **`url_matcher`** & **`match_mode`**:
    *   Enable URL-specific configurations when used with `arun_many()`.
    *   Set `url_matcher` to patterns (glob, function, or list) to match specific URLs.
    *   Use `match_mode` (OR/AND) to control how multiple patterns combine.
36.  **`display_mode`**:
    *   The display mode for progress information (`DETAILED`, `BRIEF`, etc.).
    *   Affects how much information is printed during the crawl.

### Helper Methods

The `clone()` method is particularly useful for creating variations of your crawler configuration:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-1) # Create a base configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-2) base_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-3)     cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-4)     word_count_threshold=200, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-5)     wait_until="networkidle" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-8) # Create variations for different use cases [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-9) stream_config = base_config.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-10)     stream=True,  # Enable streaming mode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-11)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-12) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-14) debug_config = base_config.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-15)     page_timeout=120000,  # Longer timeout for debugging [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-16)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-74-17) )`

The `clone()` method: - Creates a new instance with all the same settings - Updates only the specified parameters - Leaves the original configuration unchanged - Perfect for creating variations without repeating all parameters

3\. LLMConfig Essentials
------------------------

### Key fields to note

1.  **`provider`**:
2.  Which LLM provider to use.
3.  Possible values are `"ollama/llama3","groq/llama3-70b-8192","groq/llama3-8b-8192", "openai/gpt-4o-mini" ,"openai/gpt-4o","openai/o1-mini","openai/o1-preview","openai/o3-mini","openai/o3-mini-high","anthropic/claude-3-haiku-20240307","anthropic/claude-3-opus-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-sonnet-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620","gemini/gemini-pro","gemini/gemini-1.5-pro","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-exp","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-lite-preview-02-05","deepseek/deepseek-chat"`  
    _(default: `"openai/gpt-4o-mini"`)_
4.  **`api_token`**:
    *   Optional. When not provided explicitly, api\_token will be read from environment variables based on provider. For example: If a gemini model is passed as provider then,`"GEMINI_API_KEY"` will be read from environment variables
    *   API token of LLM provider  
        eg: `api_token = "gsk_1ClHGGJ7Lpn4WGybR7vNWGdyb3FY7zXEw3SCiy0BAVM9lL8CQv"`
    *   Environment variable - use with prefix "env:"  
        eg:`api_token = "env: GROQ_API_KEY"`
5.  **`base_url`**:
6.  If your provider has a custom endpoint
    
7.  **Backoff controls** _(optional)_:
    
8.  `backoff_base_delay` _(default `2` seconds)_ – how long to pause before the first retry if the provider rate-limits you.
9.  `backoff_max_attempts` _(default `3`)_ – total tries for the same prompt (initial call + retries).
10.  `backoff_exponential_factor` _(default `2`)_ – how quickly the pause grows between retries. A factor of 2 yields waits like 2s → 4s → 8s.
11.  Because these plug into Crawl4AI’s retry helper, every LLM strategy automatically follows the pacing you define here.
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-75-1) llm_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-75-2)     provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-75-3)     api_token=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-75-4)     backoff_base_delay=1, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-75-5)     backoff_max_attempts=5, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-75-6)     backoff_exponential_factor=3, # optional [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-75-7) )`
    

4\. Putting It All Together
---------------------------

In a typical scenario, you define **one** `BrowserConfig` for your crawler session, then create **one or more** `CrawlerRunConfig` & `LLMConfig` depending on each call's needs:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode, LLMConfig, LLMContentFilter, DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-3) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-6)     # 1) Browser config: headless, bigger viewport, no proxy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-7)     browser_conf = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-8)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-9)         viewport_width=1280, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-10)         viewport_height=720 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-11)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-13)     # 2) Example extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-14)     schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-15)         "name": "Articles", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-16)         "baseSelector": "div.article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-17)         "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-18)             {"name": "title", "selector": "h2", "type": "text"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-19)             {"name": "link", "selector": "a", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-20)         ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-21)     } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-22)     extraction = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-24)     # 3) Example LLM content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-26)     gemini_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-27)         provider="gemini/gemini-1.5-pro",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-28)         api_token = "env:GEMINI_API_TOKEN" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-29)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-31)     # Initialize LLM filter with specific instruction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-32)     filter = LLMContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-33)         llm_config=gemini_config,  # or your preferred provider [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-34)         instruction=""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-35)         Focus on extracting the core educational content. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-36)         Include: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-37)         - Key concepts and explanations [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-38)         - Important code examples [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-39)         - Essential technical details [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-40)         Exclude: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-41)         - Navigation elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-42)         - Sidebars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-43)         - Footer content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-44)         Format the output as clean markdown with proper code blocks and headers. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-45)         """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-46)         chunk_token_threshold=500,  # Adjust based on your needs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-47)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-48)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-50)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-51)         content_filter=filter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-52)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-53)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-54) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-55)     # 4) Crawler run config: skip cache, use extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-56)     run_conf = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-57)         markdown_generator=md_generator, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-58)         extraction_strategy=extraction, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-59)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-60)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-61) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-62)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_conf) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-63)         # 4) Execute the crawl [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-64)         result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com/news", config=run_conf) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-65) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-66)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-67)             print("Extracted content:", result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-68)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-69)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-70) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-71) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-76-72)     asyncio.run(main())`

5\. Next Steps
--------------

*   [BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig & LLMConfig Reference](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/parameters.md)
    
*   **Custom Hooks & Auth** (Inject JavaScript or handle login forms).
*   **Session Management** (Re-use pages, preserve state across multiple calls).
*   **Advanced Caching** (Fine-tune read/write cache modes).

6\. Conclusion
--------------

1. **BrowserConfig** – Controlling the Browser
==============================================

`BrowserConfig` focuses on **how** the browser is launched and behaves. This includes headless mode, proxies, user agents, and other environment tweaks.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-3) browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-4)     browser_type="chromium", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-5)     headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-6)     viewport_width=1280, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-7)     viewport_height=720, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-8)     proxy="http://user:pass@proxy:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-9)     user_agent="Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/116.0.0.0 Safari/537.36", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-77-10) )`

1.1 Parameter Highlights
------------------------

| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **`browser_type`** | `"chromium"`, `"firefox"`, `"webkit"`  <br>_(default: `"chromium"`)_ | Which browser engine to use. `"chromium"` is typical for many sites, `"firefox"` or `"webkit"` for specialized tests. |
| **`headless`** | `bool` (default: `True`) | Headless means no visible UI. `False` is handy for debugging. |
| **`viewport_width`** | `int` (default: `1080`) | Initial page width (in px). Useful for testing responsive layouts. |
| **`viewport_height`** | `int` (default: `600`) | Initial page height (in px). |
| **`proxy`** | `str` (deprecated) | Deprecated. Use `proxy_config` instead. If set, it will be auto-converted internally. |
| **`proxy_config`** | `dict` (default: `None`) | For advanced or multi-proxy needs, specify details like `{"server": "...", "username": "...", ...}`. |
| **`use_persistent_context`** | `bool` (default: `False`) | If `True`, uses a **persistent** browser context (keep cookies, sessions across runs). Also sets `use_managed_browser=True`. |
| **`user_data_dir`** | `str or None` (default: `None`) | Directory to store user data (profiles, cookies). Must be set if you want permanent sessions. |
| **`ignore_https_errors`** | `bool` (default: `True`) | If `True`, continues despite invalid certificates (common in dev/staging). |
| **`java_script_enabled`** | `bool` (default: `True`) | Disable if you want no JS overhead, or if only static content is needed. |
| **`cookies`** | `list` (default: `[]`) | Pre-set cookies, each a dict like `{"name": "session", "value": "...", "url": "..."}`. |
| **`headers`** | `dict` (default: `{}`) | Extra HTTP headers for every request, e.g. `{"Accept-Language": "en-US"}`. |
| **`user_agent`** | `str` (default: Chrome-based UA) | Your custom or random user agent. `user_agent_mode="random"` can shuffle it. |
| **`light_mode`** | `bool` (default: `False`) | Disables some background features for performance gains. |
| **`text_mode`** | `bool` (default: `False`) | If `True`, tries to disable images/other heavy content for speed. |
| **`use_managed_browser`** | `bool` (default: `False`) | For advanced “managed” interactions (debugging, CDP usage). Typically set automatically if persistent context is on. |
| **`extra_args`** | `list` (default: `[]`) | Additional flags for the underlying browser process, e.g. `["--disable-extensions"]`. |
| \- Set `headless=False` to visually **debug** how pages load or how interactions proceed. |     |     |
| \- If you need **authentication** storage or repeated sessions, consider `use_persistent_context=True` and specify `user_data_dir`. |     |     |
| \- For large pages, you might need a bigger `viewport_width` and `viewport_height` to handle dynamic content. |     |     |
| \# 2. **CrawlerRunConfig** – Controlling Each Crawl |     |     |
| While `BrowserConfig` sets up the **environment**, `CrawlerRunConfig` details **how** each **crawl operation** should behave: caching, content filtering, link or domain blocking, timeouts, JavaScript code, etc. |     |     |
| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-3) run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-4)     wait_for="css:.main-content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-5)     word_count_threshold=15, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-6)     excluded_tags=["nav", "footer"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-7)     exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-8)     stream=True,  # Enable streaming for arun_many() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-78-9) )` |     |     |
| \## 2.1 Parameter Highlights |     |     |
| \### A) **Content Processing** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \------------------------------ | \-------------------------------------- | \------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`word_count_threshold`** | `int` (default: ~200) | Skips text blocks below X words. Helps ignore trivial sections. |
| **`extraction_strategy`** | `ExtractionStrategy` (default: None) | If set, extracts structured data (CSS-based, LLM-based, etc.). |
| **`markdown_generator`** | `MarkdownGenerationStrategy` (None) | If you want specialized markdown output (citations, filtering, chunking, etc.). Can be customized with options such as `content_source` parameter to select the HTML input source ('cleaned\_html', 'raw\_html', or 'fit\_html'). |
| **`css_selector`** | `str` (None) | Retains only the part of the page matching this selector. Affects the entire extraction process. |
| **`target_elements`** | `List[str]` (None) | List of CSS selectors for elements to focus on for markdown generation and data extraction, while still processing the entire page for links, media, etc. Provides more flexibility than `css_selector`. |
| **`excluded_tags`** | `list` (None) | Removes entire tags (e.g. `["script", "style"]`). |
| **`excluded_selector`** | `str` (None) | Like `css_selector` but to exclude. E.g. `"#ads, .tracker"`. |
| **`only_text`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, tries to extract text-only content. |
| **`prettiify`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, beautifies final HTML (slower, purely cosmetic). |
| **`keep_data_attributes`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, preserve `data-*` attributes in cleaned HTML. |
| **`remove_forms`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, remove all `<form>` elements. |
| \### B) **Caching & Session** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \------------------------- | \------------------------ | \------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **`cache_mode`** | `CacheMode or None` | Controls how caching is handled (`ENABLED`, `BYPASS`, `DISABLED`, etc.). If `None`, typically defaults to `ENABLED`. |
| **`session_id`** | `str or None` | Assign a unique ID to reuse a single browser session across multiple `arun()` calls. |
| **`bypass_cache`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, acts like `CacheMode.BYPASS`. |
| **`disable_cache`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, acts like `CacheMode.DISABLED`. |
| **`no_cache_read`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, acts like `CacheMode.WRITE_ONLY` (writes cache but never reads). |
| **`no_cache_write`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, acts like `CacheMode.READ_ONLY` (reads cache but never writes). |
| \### C) **Page Navigation & Timing** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \---------------------------- | \------------------------- | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`wait_until`** | `str` (domcontentloaded) | Condition for navigation to “complete”. Often `"networkidle"` or `"domcontentloaded"`. |
| **`page_timeout`** | `int` (60000 ms) | Timeout for page navigation or JS steps. Increase for slow sites. |
| **`wait_for`** | `str or None` | Wait for a CSS (`"css:selector"`) or JS (`"js:() => bool"`) condition before content extraction. |
| **`wait_for_images`** | `bool` (False) | Wait for images to load before finishing. Slows down if you only want text. |
| **`delay_before_return_html`** | `float` (0.1) | Additional pause (seconds) before final HTML is captured. Good for last-second updates. |
| **`check_robots_txt`** | `bool` (False) | Whether to check and respect robots.txt rules before crawling. If True, caches robots.txt for efficiency. |
| **`mean_delay`** and **`max_range`** | `float` (0.1, 0.3) | If you call `arun_many()`, these define random delay intervals between crawls, helping avoid detection or rate limits. |
| **`semaphore_count`** | `int` (5) | Max concurrency for `arun_many()`. Increase if you have resources for parallel crawls. |
| \### D) **Page Interaction** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \---------------------------- | \-------------------------------- | \----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`js_code`** | `str or list[str]` (None) | JavaScript to run **after** `wait_for` and `delay_before_return_html`, on the fully-loaded page. E.g. `"document.querySelector('button')?.click();"`. |
| **`js_code_before_wait`** | `str or list[str]` (None) | JavaScript to run **before** `wait_for`. Use for triggering loading that `wait_for` then checks. |
| **`js_only`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, indicates we're reusing an existing session and only applying JS. No full reload. |
| **`ignore_body_visibility`** | `bool` (True) | Skip checking if `<body>` is visible. Usually best to keep `True`. |
| **`scan_full_page`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, auto-scroll the page to load dynamic content (infinite scroll). |
| **`scroll_delay`** | `float` (0.2) | Delay between scroll steps if `scan_full_page=True`. |
| **`process_iframes`** | `bool` (False) | Inlines iframe content for single-page extraction. |
| **`flatten_shadow_dom`** | `bool` (False) | Flattens Shadow DOM content into the light DOM before HTML capture. Resolves slots, strips shadow-scoped styles, and force-opens closed shadow roots. Essential for sites built with Web Components. |
| **`remove_overlay_elements`** | `bool` (False) | Removes potential modals/popups blocking the main content. |
| **`remove_consent_popups`** | `bool` (False) | Removes GDPR/cookie consent popups from known CMP providers (OneTrust, Cookiebot, TrustArc, Quantcast, Didomi, Sourcepoint, FundingChoices, etc.). Tries clicking "Accept All" first, then falls back to DOM removal. |
| **`simulate_user`** | `bool` (False) | Simulate user interactions (mouse movements) to avoid bot detection. |
| **`override_navigator`** | `bool` (False) | Override `navigator` properties in JS for stealth. |
| **`magic`** | `bool` (False) | Automatic handling of popups/consent banners. Experimental. |
| **`adjust_viewport_to_content`** | `bool` (False) | Resizes viewport to match page content height. |
| If your page is a single-page app with repeated JS updates, set `js_only=True` in subsequent calls, plus a `session_id` for reusing the same tab. |     |     |
| \### E) **Media Handling** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \-------------------------------------------- | \--------------------- | \----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`screenshot`** | `bool` (False) | Capture a screenshot (base64) in `result.screenshot`. |
| **`screenshot_wait_for`** | `float or None` | Extra wait time before the screenshot. |
| **`screenshot_height_threshold`** | `int` (~20000) | If the page is taller than this, alternate screenshot strategies are used. |
| **`pdf`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, returns a PDF in `result.pdf`. |
| **`capture_mhtml`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, captures an MHTML snapshot of the page in `result.mhtml`. MHTML includes all page resources (CSS, images, etc.) in a single file. |
| **`image_description_min_word_threshold`** | `int` (~50) | Minimum words for an image’s alt text or description to be considered valid. |
| **`image_score_threshold`** | `int` (~3) | Filter out low-scoring images. The crawler scores images by relevance (size, context, etc.). |
| **`exclude_external_images`** | `bool` (False) | Exclude images from other domains. |
| \### F) **Link/Domain Handling** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \------------------------------ | \------------------------- | \----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`exclude_social_media_domains`** | `list` (e.g. Facebook/Twitter) | A default list can be extended. Any link to these domains is removed from final output. |
| **`exclude_external_links`** | `bool` (False) | Removes all links pointing outside the current domain. |
| **`exclude_social_media_links`** | `bool` (False) | Strips links specifically to social sites (like Facebook or Twitter). |
| **`exclude_domains`** | `list` (\[\]) | Provide a custom list of domains to exclude (like `["ads.com", "trackers.io"]`). |
| **`preserve_https_for_internal_links`** | `bool` (False) | If `True`, preserves HTTPS scheme for internal links even when the server redirects to HTTP. Useful for security-conscious crawling. |
| \### G) **Debug & Logging** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \---------------- | \-------------------- | \--------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`verbose`** | `bool` (True) | Prints logs detailing each step of crawling, interactions, or errors. |
| **`log_console`** | `bool` (False) | Logs the page’s JavaScript console output if you want deeper JS debugging. |
| \### H) **Virtual Scroll Configuration** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \------------------------------ | \------------------------------ | \------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`virtual_scroll_config`** | `VirtualScrollConfig or dict` (None) | Configuration for handling virtualized scrolling on sites like Twitter/Instagram where content is replaced rather than appended. |
| When sites use virtual scrolling (content replaced as you scroll), use `VirtualScrollConfig`: |     |     |
| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-1) from crawl4ai import VirtualScrollConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-3) virtual_config = VirtualScrollConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-4)     container_selector="#timeline",    # CSS selector for scrollable container [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-5)     scroll_count=30,                   # Number of times to scroll [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-6)     scroll_by="container_height",      # How much to scroll: "container_height", "page_height", or pixels (e.g. 500) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-7)     wait_after_scroll=0.5             # Seconds to wait after each scroll for content to load [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-10) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-11)     virtual_scroll_config=virtual_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-79-12) )` |     |     |
| **VirtualScrollConfig Parameters:** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \------------------------ | \--------------------------- | \------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`container_selector`** | `str` (required) | CSS selector for the scrollable container (e.g., `"#feed"`, `".timeline"`) |
| **`scroll_count`** | `int` (10) | Maximum number of scrolls to perform |
| **`scroll_by`** | `str or int` ("container\_height") | Scroll amount: `"container_height"`, `"page_height"`, or pixels (e.g., `500`) |
| **`wait_after_scroll`** | `float` (0.5) | Time in seconds to wait after each scroll for new content to load |
| \- Use `virtual_scroll_config` when content is **replaced** during scroll (Twitter, Instagram) |     |     |
| \- Use `scan_full_page` when content is **appended** during scroll (traditional infinite scroll) |     |     |
| \### I) **URL Matching Configuration** |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \------------------------ | \------------------------------ | \------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`url_matcher`** | `UrlMatcher` (None) | Pattern(s) to match URLs against. Can be: string (glob), function, or list of mixed types. **None means match ALL URLs** |
| **`match_mode`** | `MatchMode` (MatchMode.OR) | How to combine multiple matchers in a list: `MatchMode.OR` (any match) or `MatchMode.AND` (all must match) |
| The `url_matcher` parameter enables URL-specific configurations when used with `arun_many()`: |     |     |
