| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-1) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig, MatchMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-2) from crawl4ai.processors.pdf import PDFContentScrapingStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-3) from crawl4ai.extraction_strategy import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-5) # Simple string pattern (glob-style) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-6) pdf_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-7)     url_matcher="*.pdf", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-8)     scraping_strategy=PDFContentScrapingStrategy() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-11) # Multiple patterns with OR logic (default) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-12) blog_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-13)     url_matcher=["*/blog/*", "*/article/*", "*/news/*"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-14)     match_mode=MatchMode.OR  # Any pattern matches [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-15) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-17) # Function matcher [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-18) api_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-19)     url_matcher=lambda url: 'api' in url or url.endswith('.json'), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-20)     # Other settings like extraction_strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-21) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-23) # Mixed: String + Function with AND logic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-24) complex_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-25)     url_matcher=[ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-26)         lambda url: url.startswith('https://'),  # Must be HTTPS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-27)         "*.org/*",                               # Must be .org domain [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-28)         lambda url: 'docs' in url                # Must contain 'docs' [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-29)     ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-30)     match_mode=MatchMode.AND  # ALL conditions must match [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-31) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-33) # Combined patterns and functions with AND logic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-34) secure_docs = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-35)     url_matcher=["https://*", lambda url: '.doc' in url], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-36)     match_mode=MatchMode.AND  # Must be HTTPS AND contain .doc [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-37) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-39) # Default config - matches ALL URLs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-80-40) default_config = CrawlerRunConfig()  # No url_matcher = matches everything` |     |     |
| **UrlMatcher Types:** |     |     |
| \- **None (default)**: When `url_matcher` is None or not set, the config matches ALL URLs |     |     |
| \- **String patterns**: Glob-style patterns like `"*.pdf"`, `"*/api/*"`, `"https://*.example.com/*"` |     |     |
| \- **Functions**: `lambda url: bool` - Custom logic for complex matching |     |     |
| \- **Lists**: Mix strings and functions, combined with `MatchMode.OR` or `MatchMode.AND` |     |     |
| **Important Behavior:** |     |     |
| \- When passing a list of configs to `arun_many()`, URLs are matched against each config's `url_matcher` in order. First match wins! |     |     |
| \- If no config matches a URL and there's no default config (one without `url_matcher`), the URL will fail with "No matching configuration found" |     |     |
| Both `BrowserConfig` and `CrawlerRunConfig` provide a `clone()` method to create modified copies: |     |     |
| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-1) # Create a base configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-2) base_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-3)     cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-4)     word_count_threshold=200 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-6) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-7) # Create variations using clone() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-8) stream_config = base_config.clone(stream=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-9) no_cache_config = base_config.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-10)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-11)     stream=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-81-12) )` |     |     |
| The `clone()` method is particularly useful when you need slightly different configurations for different use cases, without modifying the original config. |     |     |
| \## 2.3 Example Usage |     |     |
| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-5)     # Configure the browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-6)     browser_cfg = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-7)         headless=False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-8)         viewport_width=1280, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-9)         viewport_height=720, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-10)         proxy="http://user:pass@myproxy:8080", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-11)         text_mode=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-14)     # Configure the run [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-15)     run_cfg = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-16)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-17)         session_id="my_session", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-18)         css_selector="main.article", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-19)         excluded_tags=["script", "style"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-20)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-21)         wait_for="css:.article-loaded", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-22)         screenshot=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-23)         stream=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-24)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-26)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-27)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-28)             url="https://example.com/news", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-29)             config=run_cfg [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-30)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-31)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-32)             print("Final cleaned_html length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-33)             if result.screenshot: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-34)                 print("Screenshot captured (base64, length):", len(result.screenshot)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-35)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-36)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-38) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-82-39)     asyncio.run(main())` |     |     |
| \## 2.4 Compliance & Ethics |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \----------------------- | \------------------------- | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`check_robots_txt`** | `bool` (False) | When True, checks and respects robots.txt rules before crawling. Uses efficient caching with SQLite backend. |
| **`user_agent`** | `str` (None) | User agent string to identify your crawler. Used for robots.txt checking when enabled. |
| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-83-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-83-2)     check_robots_txt=True,  # Enable robots.txt compliance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-83-3)     user_agent="MyBot/1.0"  # Identify your crawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-83-4) )` |     |     |
| \# 3. **LLMConfig** - Setting up LLM providers |     |     |
| 1\. LLMExtractionStrategy |     |     |
| 2\. LLMContentFilter |     |     |
| 3\. JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate\_schema |     |     |
| 4\. JsonXPathExtractionStrategy.generate\_schema |     |     |
| \## 3.1 Parameters |     |     |
| **Parameter** | **Type / Default** | **What It Does** |
| \----------------------- | \---------------------------------------- | \--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`provider`** | `"ollama/llama3","groq/llama3-70b-8192","groq/llama3-8b-8192", "openai/gpt-4o-mini" ,"openai/gpt-4o","openai/o1-mini","openai/o1-preview","openai/o3-mini","openai/o3-mini-high","anthropic/claude-3-haiku-20240307","anthropic/claude-3-opus-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-sonnet-20240229","anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620","gemini/gemini-pro","gemini/gemini-1.5-pro","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-exp","gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-lite-preview-02-05","deepseek/deepseek-chat"`  <br>_(default: `"openai/gpt-4o-mini"`)_ | Which LLM provider to use. |
| **`api_token`** | 1.Optional. When not provided explicitly, api\_token will be read from environment variables based on provider. For example: If a gemini model is passed as provider then,`"GEMINI_API_KEY"` will be read from environment variables  <br>2\. API token of LLM provider  <br>eg: `api_token = "gsk_1ClHGGJ7Lpn4WGybR7vNWGdyb3FY7zXEw3SCiy0BAVM9lL8CQv"`  <br>3\. Environment variable - use with prefix "env:"  <br>eg:`api_token = "env: GROQ_API_KEY"` | API token to use for the given provider |
| **`base_url`** | Optional. Custom API endpoint | If your provider has a custom endpoint |
| \## 3.2 Example Usage |     |     |
| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-84-1) llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", api_token=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))` |     |     |
| \## 4. Putting It All Together |     |     |
| \- **Use** `BrowserConfig` for **global** browser settings: engine, headless, proxy, user agent. |     |     |
| \- **Use** `CrawlerRunConfig` for each crawl’s **context**: how to filter content, handle caching, wait for dynamic elements, or run JS. |     |     |
| \- **Pass** both configs to `AsyncWebCrawler` (the `BrowserConfig`) and then to `arun()` (the `CrawlerRunConfig`). |     |     |
| \- **Use** `LLMConfig` for LLM provider configurations that can be used across all extraction, filtering, schema generation tasks. Can be used in - `LLMExtractionStrategy`, `LLMContentFilter`, `JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema` & `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy.generate_schema` |     |     |
| `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-85-1) # Create a modified copy with the clone() method [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-85-2) stream_cfg = run_cfg.clone( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-85-3)     stream=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-85-4)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-85-5) )` |     |     |

Crawling Patterns
=================

Simple Crawling
===============

Basic Usage
-----------

Set up a simple crawl using `BrowserConfig` and `CrawlerRunConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-3) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-6)     browser_config = BrowserConfig()  # Default browser configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-7)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig()   # Default crawl run configuration [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-11)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-12)             config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-14)         print(result.markdown)  # Print clean markdown content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-16) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-86-17)     asyncio.run(main())`

Understanding the Response
--------------------------

The `arun()` method returns a `CrawlResult` object with several useful properties. Here's a quick overview (see [CrawlResult](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/api/crawl-result.md)
 for complete details):

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-2)     markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-3)         content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.6), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-4)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-5)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-8) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-9)     url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-10)     config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-13) # Different content formats [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-14) print(result.html)         # Raw HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-15) print(result.cleaned_html) # Cleaned HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-16) print(result.markdown.raw_markdown) # Raw markdown from cleaned html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-17) print(result.markdown.fit_markdown) # Most relevant content in markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-19) # Check success status [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-20) print(result.success)      # True if crawl succeeded [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-21) print(result.status_code)  # HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-23) # Access extracted media and links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-24) print(result.media)        # Dictionary of found media (images, videos, audio) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-87-25) print(result.links)        # Dictionary of internal and external links`

Adding Basic Options
--------------------

Customize your crawl using `CrawlerRunConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-2)     word_count_threshold=10,        # Minimum words per content block [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-3)     exclude_external_links=True,    # Remove external links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-4)     remove_overlay_elements=True,   # Remove popups/modals [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-5)     process_iframes=True           # Process iframe content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-6) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-7) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-8) result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-9)     url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-10)     config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-88-11) )`

Handling Errors
---------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-89-1) run_config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-89-2) result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-89-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-89-4) if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-89-5)     print(f"Crawl failed: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-89-6)     print(f"Status code: {result.status_code}")`

Logging and Debugging
---------------------

Enable verbose logging in `BrowserConfig`:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-90-1) browser_config = BrowserConfig(verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-90-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-90-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-90-4)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-90-5)     result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com", config=run_config)`

Complete Example
----------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-3) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-6)     browser_config = BrowserConfig(verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-7)     run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-8)         # Content filtering [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-9)         word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-10)         excluded_tags=['form', 'header'], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-11)         exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-13)         # Content processing [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-14)         process_iframes=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-15)         remove_overlay_elements=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-17)         # Cache control [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-18)         cache_mode=CacheMode.ENABLED  # Use cache if available [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-19)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-20) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-21)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-22)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-23)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-24)             config=run_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-25)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-27)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-28)             # Print clean content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-29)             print("Content:", result.markdown[:500])  # First 500 chars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-31)             # Process images [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-32)             for image in result.media["images"]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-33)                 print(f"Found image: {image['src']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-35)             # Process links [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-36)             for link in result.links["internal"]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-37)                 print(f"Internal link: {link['href']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-39)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-40)             print(f"Crawl failed: {result.error_message}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-42) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-91-43)     asyncio.run(main())`

Content Processing
==================

Markdown Generation Basics
==========================

1.  How to configure the **Default Markdown Generator**
2.  The difference between raw markdown (`result.markdown`) and filtered markdown (`fit_markdown`)
    
    > *   You know how to configure `CrawlerRunConfig`.
    

1\. Quick Example
-----------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-3) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-5) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-6)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-7)         markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-10)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-11) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-12)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-13)             print("Raw Markdown Output:\n") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-14)             print(result.markdown)  # The unfiltered markdown from the page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-15)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-16)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-18) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-92-19)     asyncio.run(main())`

\- `CrawlerRunConfig( markdown_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator() )` instructs Crawl4AI to convert the final HTML into markdown at the end of each crawl.  
\- The resulting markdown is accessible via `result.markdown`.

2\. How Markdown Generation Works
---------------------------------

### 2.1 HTML-to-Text Conversion (Forked & Modified)

*   Preserves headings, code blocks, bullet points, etc.
*   Removes extraneous tags (scripts, styles) that don’t add meaningful content.
*   Can optionally generate references for links or skip them altogether.

### 2.2 Link Citations & References

By default, the generator can convert `<a href="...">` elements into `[text][1]` citations, then place the actual links at the bottom of the document. This is handy for research workflows that demand references in a structured manner.

### 2.3 Optional Content Filters

3\. Configuring the Default Markdown Generator
----------------------------------------------

You can tweak the output by passing an `options` dict to `DefaultMarkdownGenerator`. For example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-1) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-5)     # Example: ignore all links, don't escape HTML, and wrap text at 80 characters [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-6)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-7)         options={ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-8)             "ignore_links": True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-9)             "escape_html": False, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-10)             "body_width": 80 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-11)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-14)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-15)         markdown_generator=md_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-16)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-18)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-19)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/docs", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-20)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-21)             print("Markdown:\n", result.markdown[:500])  # Just a snippet [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-22)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-23)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-25) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-26)     import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-93-27)     asyncio.run(main())`

Some commonly used `options`: - **`ignore_links`** (bool): Whether to remove all hyperlinks in the final markdown.  
\- **`ignore_images`** (bool): Remove all `![image]()` references.  
\- **`escape_html`** (bool): Turn HTML entities into text (default is often `True`).  
\- **`body_width`** (int): Wrap text at N characters. `0` or `None` means no wrapping.  
\- **`skip_internal_links`** (bool): If `True`, omit `#localAnchors` or internal links referencing the same page.  
\- **`include_sup_sub`** (bool): Attempt to handle `<sup>` / `<sub>` in a more readable way.

4\. Selecting the HTML Source for Markdown Generation
-----------------------------------------------------

The `content_source` parameter allows you to control which HTML content is used as input for markdown generation. This gives you flexibility in how the HTML is processed before conversion to markdown.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-1) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-5)     # Option 1: Use the raw HTML directly from the webpage (before any processing) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-6)     raw_md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-7)         content_source="raw_html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-8)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-11)     # Option 2: Use the cleaned HTML (after scraping strategy processing - default) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-12)     cleaned_md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-13)         content_source="cleaned_html",  # This is the default [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-14)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-17)     # Option 3: Use preprocessed HTML optimized for schema extraction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-18)     fit_md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-19)         content_source="fit_html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-20)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-21)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-23)     # Use one of the generators in your crawler config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-24)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-25)         markdown_generator=raw_md_generator  # Try each of the generators [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-26)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-28)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-29)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-30)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-31)             print("Markdown:\n", result.markdown.raw_markdown[:500]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-32)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-33)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-35) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-36)     import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-94-37)     asyncio.run(main())`

### HTML Source Options

*   **`"cleaned_html"`** (default): Uses the HTML after it has been processed by the scraping strategy. This HTML is typically cleaner and more focused on content, with some boilerplate removed.
*   **`"raw_html"`**: Uses the original HTML directly from the webpage, before any cleaning or processing. This preserves more of the original content, but may include navigation bars, ads, footers, and other elements that might not be relevant to the main content.
*   **`"fit_html"`**: Uses HTML preprocessed for schema extraction. This HTML is optimized for structured data extraction and may have certain elements simplified or removed.

### When to Use Each Option

*   Use **`"cleaned_html"`** (default) for most cases where you want a balance of content preservation and noise removal.
*   Use **`"raw_html"`** when you need to preserve all original content, or when the cleaning process is removing content you actually want to keep.
*   Use **`"fit_html"`** when working with structured data or when you need HTML that's optimized for schema extraction.

5\. Content Filters
-------------------

### 5.1 BM25ContentFilter

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-1) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-2) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import BM25ContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-3) from crawl4ai import CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-5) bm25_filter = BM25ContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-6)     user_query="machine learning", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-7)     bm25_threshold=1.2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-8)     language="english" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-11) md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-12)     content_filter=bm25_filter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-13)     options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-14) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-95-16) config = CrawlerRunConfig(markdown_generator=md_generator)`

\- **`user_query`**: The term you want to focus on. BM25 tries to keep only content blocks relevant to that query.  
\- **`bm25_threshold`**: Raise it to keep fewer blocks; lower it to keep more.  
\- **`use_stemming`** _(default `True`)_: Whether to apply stemming to the query and content. - **`language (str)`**: Language for stemming (default: 'english').

### 5.2 PruningContentFilter

If you **don’t** have a specific query, or if you just want a robust “junk remover,” use `PruningContentFilter`. It analyzes text density, link density, HTML structure, and known patterns (like “nav,” “footer”) to systematically prune extraneous or repetitive sections.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-96-1) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-96-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-96-3) prune_filter = PruningContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-96-4)     threshold=0.5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-96-5)     threshold_type="fixed",  # or "dynamic" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-96-6)     min_word_threshold=50 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-96-7) )`

\- **`threshold`**: Score boundary. Blocks below this score get removed.  
\- **`threshold_type`**:  
\- `"fixed"`: Straight comparison (`score >= threshold` keeps the block).  
\- `"dynamic"`: The filter adjusts threshold in a data-driven manner.  
\- **`min_word_threshold`**: Discard blocks under N words as likely too short or unhelpful. - You want a broad cleanup without a user query.

### 5.3 LLMContentFilter

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, LLMConfig, DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-2) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import LLMContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-5)     # Initialize LLM filter with specific instruction [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-6)     filter = LLMContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-7)         llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o",api_token="your-api-token"), #or use environment variable [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-8)         instruction=""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-9)         Focus on extracting the core educational content. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-10)         Include: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-11)         - Key concepts and explanations [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-12)         - Important code examples [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-13)         - Essential technical details [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-14)         Exclude: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-15)         - Navigation elements [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-16)         - Sidebars [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-17)         - Footer content [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-18)         Format the output as clean markdown with proper code blocks and headers. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-19)         """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-20)         chunk_token_threshold=4096,  # Adjust based on your needs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-21)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-22)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-23)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-24)         content_filter=filter, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-25)         options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-26)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-27)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-28)         markdown_generator=md_generator, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-29)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-31)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-32)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-97-33)         print(result.markdown.fit_markdown)  # Filtered markdown content`

\- **Chunk Processing**: Handles large documents by processing them in chunks (controlled by `chunk_token_threshold`) - **Parallel Processing**: For better performance, use smaller `chunk_token_threshold` (e.g., 2048 or 4096) to enable parallel processing of content chunks 1. **Exact Content Preservation**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-1) filter = LLMContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-2)     instruction=""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-3)     Extract the main educational content while preserving its original wording and substance completely. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-4)     1. Maintain the exact language and terminology [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-5)     2. Keep all technical explanations and examples intact [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-6)     3. Preserve the original flow and structure [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-7)     4. Remove only clearly irrelevant elements like navigation menus and ads [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-8)     """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-9)     chunk_token_threshold=4096 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-98-10) )`

2\. **Focused Content Extraction**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-1) filter = LLMContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-2)     instruction=""" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-3)     Focus on extracting specific types of content: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-4)     - Technical documentation [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-5)     - Code examples [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-6)     - API references [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-7)     Reformat the content into clear, well-structured markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-8)     """, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-9)     chunk_token_threshold=4096 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-99-10) )`

> **Performance Tip**: Set a smaller `chunk_token_threshold` (e.g., 2048 or 4096) to enable parallel processing of content chunks. The default value is infinity, which processes the entire content as a single chunk.

6\. Using Fit Markdown
----------------------

When a content filter is active, the library produces two forms of markdown inside `result.markdown`: 1. **`raw_markdown`**: The full unfiltered markdown.  
2. **`fit_markdown`**: A “fit” version where the filter has removed or trimmed noisy segments.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-3) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-4) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-7)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-8)         markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-9)             content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.6), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-10)             options={"ignore_links": True} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-11)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-13)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-14)         result = await crawler.arun("https://news.example.com/tech", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-15)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-16)             print("Raw markdown:\n", result.markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-18)             # If a filter is used, we also have .fit_markdown: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-19)             md_object = result.markdown  # or your equivalent [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-20)             print("Filtered markdown:\n", md_object.fit_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-21)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-22)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-23) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-24) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-100-25)     asyncio.run(main())`

7\. The `MarkdownGenerationResult` Object
-----------------------------------------

If your library stores detailed markdown output in an object like `MarkdownGenerationResult`, you’ll see fields such as: - **`raw_markdown`**: The direct HTML-to-markdown transformation (no filtering).  
\- **`markdown_with_citations`**: A version that moves links to reference-style footnotes.  
\- **`references_markdown`**: A separate string or section containing the gathered references.  
\- **`fit_markdown`**: The filtered markdown if you used a content filter.  
\- **`fit_html`**: The corresponding HTML snippet used to generate `fit_markdown` (helpful for debugging or advanced usage).

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-101-1) md_obj = result.markdown  # your library’s naming may vary [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-101-2) print("RAW:\n", md_obj.raw_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-101-3) print("CITED:\n", md_obj.markdown_with_citations) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-101-4) print("REFERENCES:\n", md_obj.references_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-101-5) print("FIT:\n", md_obj.fit_markdown)`

\- You can supply `raw_markdown` to an LLM if you want the entire text.  
\- Or feed `fit_markdown` into a vector database to reduce token usage.  
\- `references_markdown` can help you keep track of link provenance.

8\. Combining Filters (BM25 + Pruning) in Two Passes
----------------------------------------------------

You might want to **prune out** noisy boilerplate first (with `PruningContentFilter`), and then **rank what’s left** against a user query (with `BM25ContentFilter`). You don’t have to crawl the page twice. Instead: 1. **First pass**: Apply `PruningContentFilter` directly to the raw HTML from `result.html` (the crawler’s downloaded HTML).  
2. **Second pass**: Take the pruned HTML (or text) from step 1, and feed it into `BM25ContentFilter`, focusing on a user query.

### Two-Pass Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-3) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter, BM25ContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-4) from bs4 import BeautifulSoup [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-7)     # 1. Crawl with minimal or no markdown generator, just get raw HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-8)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-9)         # If you only want raw HTML, you can skip passing a markdown_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-10)         # or provide one but focus on .html in this example [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-11)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-13)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-14)         result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/tech-article", config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-16)         if not result.success or not result.html: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-17)             print("Crawl failed or no HTML content.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-18)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-20)         raw_html = result.html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-22)         # 2. First pass: PruningContentFilter on raw HTML [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-23)         pruning_filter = PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.5, min_word_threshold=50) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-25)         # filter_content returns a list of "text chunks" or cleaned HTML sections [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-26)         pruned_chunks = pruning_filter.filter_content(raw_html) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-27)         # This list is basically pruned content blocks, presumably in HTML or text form [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-29)         # For demonstration, let's combine these chunks back into a single HTML-like string [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-30)         # or you could do further processing. It's up to your pipeline design. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-31)         pruned_html = "\n".join(pruned_chunks) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-33)         # 3. Second pass: BM25ContentFilter with a user query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-34)         bm25_filter = BM25ContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-35)             user_query="machine learning", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-36)             bm25_threshold=1.2, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-37)             language="english" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-38)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-39) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-40)         # returns a list of text chunks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-41)         bm25_chunks = bm25_filter.filter_content(pruned_html)   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-43)         if not bm25_chunks: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-44)             print("Nothing matched the BM25 query after pruning.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-45)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-46) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-47)         # 4. Combine or display final results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-48)         final_text = "\n---\n".join(bm25_chunks) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-50)         print("==== PRUNED OUTPUT (first pass) ====") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-51)         print(pruned_html[:500], "... (truncated)")  # preview [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-52) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-53)         print("\n==== BM25 OUTPUT (second pass) ====") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-54)         print(final_text[:500], "... (truncated)") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-55) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-56) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-102-57)     asyncio.run(main())`

### What’s Happening?

1. **Raw HTML**: We crawl once and store the raw HTML in `result.html`.  
4. **BM25ContentFilter**: We feed the pruned string into `BM25ContentFilter` with a user query. This second pass further narrows the content to chunks relevant to “machine learning.” **No Re-Crawling**: We used `raw_html` from the first pass, so there’s no need to run `arun()` again—**no second network request**.

### Tips & Variations

*   **Plain Text vs. HTML**: If your pruned output is mostly text, BM25 can still handle it; just keep in mind it expects a valid string input. If you supply partial HTML (like `"<p>some text</p>"`), it will parse it as HTML.
*   **Adjust Thresholds**: If you see too much or too little text in step one, tweak `threshold=0.5` or `min_word_threshold=50`. Similarly, `bm25_threshold=1.2` can be raised/lowered for more or fewer chunks in step two.

### One-Pass Combination?

9\. Common Pitfalls & Tips
--------------------------

1. **No Markdown Output?**  
2. **Performance Considerations**  
\- Very large pages with multiple filters can be slower. Consider `cache_mode` to avoid re-downloading.  
3. **Take Advantage of `fit_markdown`**  
4. **Adjusting `html2text` Options**  
\- If you see lots of raw HTML slipping into the text, turn on `escape_html`.  
\- If code blocks look messy, experiment with `mark_code` or `handle_code_in_pre`.

10\. Summary & Next Steps
-------------------------

*   Configure the **DefaultMarkdownGenerator** with HTML-to-text options.
*   Select different HTML sources using the `content_source` parameter.
*   Distinguish between raw and filtered markdown (`fit_markdown`).
*   Leverage the `MarkdownGenerationResult` object to handle different forms of output (citations, references, etc.).

Fit Markdown with Pruning & BM25
================================

1\. How “Fit Markdown” Works
----------------------------

### 1.1 The `content_filter`

In **`CrawlerRunConfig`**, you can specify a **`content_filter`** to shape how content is pruned or ranked before final markdown generation. A filter’s logic is applied **before** or **during** the HTML→Markdown process, producing: - **`result.markdown.raw_markdown`** (unfiltered) - **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`** (filtered or “fit” version) - **`result.markdown.fit_html`** (the corresponding HTML snippet that produced `fit_markdown`)

### 1.2 Common Filters

2\. PruningContentFilter
------------------------

### 2.1 Usage Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-3) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import PruningContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-4) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-7)     # Step 1: Create a pruning filter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-8)     prune_filter = PruningContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-9)         # Lower → more content retained, higher → more content pruned [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-10)         threshold=0.45,            [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-11)         # "fixed" or "dynamic" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-12)         threshold_type="dynamic",   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-13)         # Ignore nodes with <5 words [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-14)         min_word_threshold=5       [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-15)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-17)     # Step 2: Insert it into a Markdown Generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-18)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=prune_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-20)     # Step 3: Pass it to CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-21)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-22)         markdown_generator=md_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-23)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-25)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-26)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-27)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-28)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-29)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-30) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-31)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-32)             # 'fit_markdown' is your pruned content, focusing on "denser" text [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-33)             print("Raw Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.raw_markdown)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-34)             print("Fit Markdown length:", len(result.markdown.fit_markdown)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-35)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-36)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-37) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-38) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-103-39)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 2.2 Key Parameters

*   **`min_word_threshold`** (int): If a block has fewer words than this, it’s pruned.
*   **`threshold_type`** (str):
*   `"fixed"` → each node must exceed `threshold` (0–1).
*   `"dynamic"` → node scoring adjusts according to tag type, text/link density, etc.
*   **`threshold`** (float, default ~0.48): The base or “anchor” cutoff.
*   **Link density** – Penalizes sections that are mostly links.
*   **Tag importance** – e.g., an `<article>` or `<p>` might be more important than a `<div>`.

3\. BM25ContentFilter
---------------------

### 3.1 Usage Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-3) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import BM25ContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-4) from crawl4ai.markdown_generation_strategy import DefaultMarkdownGenerator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-7)     # 1) A BM25 filter with a user query [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-8)     bm25_filter = BM25ContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-9)         user_query="startup fundraising tips", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-10)         # Adjust for stricter or looser results [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-11)         bm25_threshold=1.2   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-14)     # 2) Insert into a Markdown Generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-15)     md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=bm25_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-17)     # 3) Pass to crawler config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-18)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-19)         markdown_generator=md_generator [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-20)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-22)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-23)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-24)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-25)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-26)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-27)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-28)             print("Fit Markdown (BM25 query-based):") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-29)             print(result.markdown.fit_markdown) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-30)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-31)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-33) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-104-34)     asyncio.run(main())`

### 3.2 Parameters

*   **`user_query`** (str, optional): E.g. `"machine learning"`. If blank, the filter tries to glean a query from page metadata.
*   **`bm25_threshold`** (float, default 1.0):
*   Higher → fewer chunks but more relevant.
*   Lower → more inclusive.
    
    > In more advanced scenarios, you might see parameters like `language`, `case_sensitive`, or `priority_tags` to refine how text is tokenized or weighted.
    

4\. Accessing the “Fit” Output
------------------------------

After the crawl, your “fit” content is found in **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`**.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-105-1) fit_md = result.markdown.fit_markdown [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-105-2) fit_html = result.markdown.fit_html`

If the content filter is **BM25**, you might see additional logic or references in `fit_markdown` that highlight relevant segments. If it’s **Pruning**, the text is typically well-cleaned but not necessarily matched to a query.

5\. Code Patterns Recap
-----------------------

### 5.1 Pruning

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-106-1) prune_filter = PruningContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-106-2)     threshold=0.5, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-106-3)     threshold_type="fixed", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-106-4)     min_word_threshold=10 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-106-5) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-106-6) md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=prune_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-106-7) config = CrawlerRunConfig(markdown_generator=md_generator)`

### 5.2 BM25

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-107-1) bm25_filter = BM25ContentFilter( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-107-2)     user_query="health benefits fruit", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-107-3)     bm25_threshold=1.2 [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-107-4) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-107-5) md_generator = DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=bm25_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-107-6) config = CrawlerRunConfig(markdown_generator=md_generator)`

6\. Combining with “word\_count\_threshold” & Exclusions
--------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-1) config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-2)     word_count_threshold=10, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-3)     excluded_tags=["nav", "footer", "header"], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-4)     exclude_external_links=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-5)     markdown_generator=DefaultMarkdownGenerator( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-6)         content_filter=PruningContentFilter(threshold=0.5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-7)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-108-8) )`

1\. The crawler’s `excluded_tags` are removed from the HTML first.  
3\. The final “fit” content is generated in `result.markdown.fit_markdown`.

7\. Custom Filters
------------------

If you need a different approach (like a specialized ML model or site-specific heuristics), you can create a new class inheriting from `RelevantContentFilter` and implement `filter_content(html)`. Then inject it into your **markdown generator**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-109-1) from crawl4ai.content_filter_strategy import RelevantContentFilter [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-109-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-109-3) class MyCustomFilter(RelevantContentFilter): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-109-4)     def filter_content(self, html, min_word_threshold=None): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-109-5)         # parse HTML, implement custom logic [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-109-6)         return [block for block in ... if ... some condition...]`

1\. Subclass `RelevantContentFilter`.  
2\. Implement `filter_content(...)`.  
3\. Use it in your `DefaultMarkdownGenerator(content_filter=MyCustomFilter(...))`.

8\. Final Thoughts
------------------

*   **Summaries**: Quickly get the important text from a cluttered page.
*   **Search**: Combine with **BM25** to produce content relevant to a query.
*   **BM25ContentFilter**: Perfect for query-based extraction or searching.
*   Combine with **`excluded_tags`, `exclude_external_links`, `word_count_threshold`** to refine your final “fit” text.
*   Fit markdown ends up in **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`**; eventually **`result.markdown.fit_markdown`** in future versions.
*   Last Updated: 2025-01-01

Content Selection
=================

Crawl4AI provides multiple ways to **select**, **filter**, and **refine** the content from your crawls. Whether you need to target a specific CSS region, exclude entire tags, filter out external links, or remove certain domains and images, **`CrawlerRunConfig`** offers a wide range of parameters.

1\. CSS-Based Selection
-----------------------

There are two ways to select content from a page: using `css_selector` or the more flexible `target_elements`.

### 1.1 Using `css_selector`

A straightforward way to **limit** your crawl results to a certain region of the page is **`css_selector`** in **`CrawlerRunConfig`**:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-2) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-4) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-5)     config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-6)         # e.g., first 30 items from Hacker News [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-7)         css_selector=".athing:nth-child(-n+30)"   [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-8)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-9)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-10)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-11)             url="https://news.ycombinator.com/newest",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-12)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-13)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-14)         print("Partial HTML length:", len(result.cleaned_html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-16) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-110-17)     asyncio.run(main())`

**Result**: Only elements matching that selector remain in `result.cleaned_html`.

### 1.2 Using `target_elements`

The `target_elements` parameter provides more flexibility by allowing you to target **multiple elements** for content extraction while preserving the entire page context for other features:
