"related_products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-79)                     "selector": "ul.related-products li", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-80)                     "type": "list", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-81)                     "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-82)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-83)                             "name": "name",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-84)                             "selector": "span.related-name",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-85)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-86)                         }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-87)                         { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-88)                             "name": "price",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-89)                             "selector": "span.related-price",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-90)                             "type": "text" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-91)                         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-92)                     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-93)                 } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-94)             ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-95)         } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-96)     ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-161-97) }`

\- **Nested vs. List**:  
\- **`type: "nested"`** means a **single** sub-object (like `details`).  
\- **`type: "list"`** means multiple items that are **simple** dictionaries or single text fields.  
\- **`type: "nested_list"`** means repeated **complex** objects (like `products` or `reviews`). - **Base Fields**: We can extract **attributes** from the container element via `"baseFields"`. For instance, `"data_cat_id"` might be `data-cat-id="elect123"`.  
\- **Transforms**: We can also define a `transform` if we want to lower/upper case, strip whitespace, or even run a custom function.

### Running the Extraction

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-4) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-6) ecommerce_schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-7)     # ... the advanced schema from above ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-8) } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-10) async def extract_ecommerce_data(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-11)     strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(ecommerce_schema, verbose=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-13)     config = CrawlerRunConfig() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-15)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(verbose=True) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-16)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-17)             url="https://gist.githubusercontent.com/githubusercontent/2d7b8ba3cd8ab6cf3c8da771ddb36878/raw/1ae2f90c6861ce7dd84cc50d3df9920dee5e1fd2/sample_ecommerce.html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-18)             extraction_strategy=strategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-19)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-20)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-22)         if not result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-23)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-24)             return [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-25) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-26)         # Parse the JSON output [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-27)         data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-28)         print(json.dumps(data, indent=2) if data else "No data found.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-162-30) asyncio.run(extract_ecommerce_data())`

If all goes well, you get a **structured** JSON array with each "category," containing an array of `products`. Each product includes `details`, `features`, `reviews`, etc. All of that **without** an LLM.

4\. RegexExtractionStrategy - Fast Pattern-Based Extraction
-----------------------------------------------------------

Crawl4AI now offers a powerful new zero-LLM extraction strategy: `RegexExtractionStrategy`. This strategy provides lightning-fast extraction of common data types like emails, phone numbers, URLs, dates, and more using pre-compiled regular expressions.

### Key Features

*   **Zero LLM Dependency**: Extracts data without any AI model calls
*   **Blazing Fast**: Uses pre-compiled regex patterns for maximum performance
*   **Built-in Patterns**: Includes ready-to-use patterns for common data types

### Simple Example: Extracting Common Entities

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-3) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-4)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-5)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-6)     RegexExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-9) async def extract_with_regex(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-10)     # Create a strategy using built-in patterns for URLs and currencies [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-11)     strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-12)         pattern = RegexExtractionStrategy.Url | RegexExtractionStrategy.Currency [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-15)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-18)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-19)             url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-20)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-21)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-23)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-24)             data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-25)             for item in data[:5]:  # Show first 5 matches [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-26)                 print(f"{item['label']}: {item['value']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-27)             print(f"Total matches: {len(data)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-163-29) asyncio.run(extract_with_regex())`

### Available Built-in Patterns

`RegexExtractionStrategy` provides these common patterns as IntFlag attributes for easy combining:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-1) # Use individual patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-2) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(pattern=RegexExtractionStrategy.Email) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-4) # Combine multiple patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-5) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-6)     pattern = ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-7)         RegexExtractionStrategy.Email |  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-8)         RegexExtractionStrategy.PhoneUS |  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-9)         RegexExtractionStrategy.Url [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-10)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-13) # Use all available patterns [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-164-14) strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(pattern=RegexExtractionStrategy.All)`

\- `Email` - Email addresses - `PhoneIntl` - International phone numbers - `PhoneUS` - US-format phone numbers - `Url` - HTTP/HTTPS URLs - `IPv4` - IPv4 addresses - `IPv6` - IPv6 addresses - `Uuid` - UUIDs - `Currency` - Currency values (USD, EUR, etc.) - `Percentage` - Percentage values - `Number` - Numeric values - `DateIso` - ISO format dates - `DateUS` - US format dates - `Time24h` - 24-hour format times - `PostalUS` - US postal codes - `PostalUK` - UK postal codes - `HexColor` - HTML hex color codes - `TwitterHandle` - Twitter handles - `Hashtag` - Hashtags - `MacAddr` - MAC addresses - `Iban` - International bank account numbers - `CreditCard` - Credit card numbers

### Custom Pattern Example

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-3) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-4)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-5)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-6)     RegexExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-9) async def extract_prices(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-10)     # Define a custom pattern for US Dollar prices [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-11)     price_pattern = {"usd_price": r"\$\s?\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*(?:\.\d{2})?"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-13)     # Create strategy with custom pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-14)     strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(custom=price_pattern) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-15)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-17)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-18)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-19)             url="https://www.example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-20)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-21)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-23)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-24)             data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-25)             for item in data: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-26)                 print(f"Found price: {item['value']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-27) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-165-28) asyncio.run(extract_prices())`

### LLM-Assisted Pattern Generation

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-1) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-3) from pathlib import Path [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-4) from crawl4ai import ( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-5)     AsyncWebCrawler, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-6)     CrawlerRunConfig, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-7)     RegexExtractionStrategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-8)     LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-9) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-10) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-11) async def extract_with_generated_pattern(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-12)     cache_dir = Path("./pattern_cache") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-13)     cache_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-14)     pattern_file = cache_dir / "price_pattern.json" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-15) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-16)     # 1. Generate or load pattern [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-17)     if pattern_file.exists(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-18)         pattern = json.load(pattern_file.open()) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-19)         print(f"Using cached pattern: {pattern}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-20)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-21)         print("Generating pattern via LLM...") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-23)         # Configure LLM [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-24)         llm_config = LLMConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-25)             provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-26)             api_token="env:OPENAI_API_KEY", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-27)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-29)         # Get sample HTML for context [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-30)         async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-31)             result = await crawler.arun("https://example.com/products") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-32)             html = result.fit_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-33) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-34)         # Generate pattern (one-time LLM usage) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-35)         pattern = RegexExtractionStrategy.generate_pattern( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-36)             label="price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-37)             html=html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-38)             query="Product prices in USD format", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-39)             llm_config=llm_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-40)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-41) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-42)         # Cache pattern for future use [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-43)         json.dump(pattern, pattern_file.open("w"), indent=2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-44) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-45)     # 2. Use pattern for extraction (no LLM calls) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-46)     strategy = RegexExtractionStrategy(custom=pattern) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-47)     config = CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=strategy) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-49)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-50)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-51)             url="https://example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-52)             config=config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-53)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-54) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-55)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-56)             data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-57)             for item in data[:10]: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-58)                 print(f"Extracted: {item['value']}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-59)             print(f"Total matches: {len(data)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-60) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-166-61) asyncio.run(extract_with_generated_pattern())`

1\. Use an LLM once to generate a highly optimized regex for your specific site 2. Save the pattern to disk for reuse 3. Extract data using only regex (no further LLM calls) in production

### Extraction Results Format

The `RegexExtractionStrategy` returns results in a consistent format:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-1) [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-2)   { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-3)     "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-4)     "label": "email", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-5)     "value": "contact@example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-6)     "span": [145, 163] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-7)   }, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-8)   { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-9)     "url": "https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-10)     "label": "url", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-11)     "value": "https://support.example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-12)     "span": [210, 235] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-13)   } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-167-14) ]`

\- `url`: The source URL - `label`: The pattern name that matched (e.g., "email", "phone\_us") - `value`: The extracted text - `span`: The start and end positions in the source content

5\. Why "No LLM" Is Often Better
--------------------------------

6\. Base Element Attributes & Additional Fields
-----------------------------------------------

It's easy to **extract attributes** (like `href`, `src`, or `data-xxx`) from your base or nested elements using:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-168-1) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-168-2)   "name": "href", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-168-3)   "type": "attribute", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-168-4)   "attribute": "href", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-168-5)   "default": null [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-168-6) }`

You can define them in **`baseFields`** (extracted from the main container element) or in each field's sub-lists. This is especially helpful if you need an item's link or ID stored in the parent `<div>`.

7\. Putting It All Together: Larger Example
-------------------------------------------

Consider a blog site. We have a schema that extracts the **URL** from each post card (via `baseFields` with an `"attribute": "href"`), plus the title, date, summary, and author:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-1) schema = { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-2)   "name": "Blog Posts", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-3)   "baseSelector": "a.blog-post-card", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-4)   "baseFields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-5)     {"name": "post_url", "type": "attribute", "attribute": "href"} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-6)   ], [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-7)   "fields": [ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-8)     {"name": "title", "selector": "h2.post-title", "type": "text", "default": "No Title"}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-9)     {"name": "date", "selector": "time.post-date", "type": "text", "default": ""}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-10)     {"name": "summary", "selector": "p.post-summary", "type": "text", "default": ""}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-11)     {"name": "author", "selector": "span.post-author", "type": "text", "default": ""} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-12)   ] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-169-13) }`

Then run with `JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema)` to get an array of blog post objects, each with `"post_url"`, `"title"`, `"date"`, `"summary"`, `"author"`.

8\. Tips & Best Practices
-------------------------

1.  **Test** your schema on partial HTML or a test page before a big crawl.
2.  **Combine with JS Execution** if the site loads content dynamically. You can pass `js_code` or `wait_for` in `CrawlerRunConfig`.
3.  **Look at Logs** when `verbose=True`: if your selectors are off or your schema is malformed, it'll often show warnings.
4.  **Use baseFields** if you need attributes from the container element (e.g., `href`, `data-id`), especially for the "parent" item.
5.  **Consider Using Regex First**: For simple data types like emails, URLs, and dates, `RegexExtractionStrategy` is often the fastest approach.

9\. Schema Generation Utility
-----------------------------

1.  You're dealing with a new website structure and want a quick starting point
2.  You need to extract complex nested data structures
3.  You want to avoid the learning curve of CSS/XPath selector syntax

### Using the Schema Generator

The schema generator is available as a static method on both `JsonCssExtractionStrategy` and `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`. You can choose between OpenAI's GPT-4 or the open-source Ollama for schema generation:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-1) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy, JsonXPathExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-2) from crawl4ai import LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-3) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-4) # Sample HTML with product information [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-5) html = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-6) <div class="product-card"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-7)     <h2 class="title">Gaming Laptop</h2> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-8)     <div class="price">$999.99</div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-9)     <div class="specs"> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-10)         <ul> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-11)             <li>16GB RAM</li> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-12)             <li>1TB SSD</li> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-13)         </ul> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-14)     </div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-15) </div> [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-16) """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-17) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-18) # Option 1: Using OpenAI (requires API token) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-19) css_schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-20)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-21)     schema_type="css",  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-22)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o",api_token="your-openai-token") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-23) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-25) # Option 2: Using Ollama (open source, no token needed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-26) xpath_schema = JsonXPathExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-27)     html, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-28)     schema_type="xpath", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-29)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="ollama/llama3.3", api_token=None)  # Not needed for Ollama [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-30) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-32) # Use the generated schema for fast, repeated extractions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-170-33) strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(css_schema)`

### Token Usage Tracking

`generate_schema` may make multiple LLM calls internally (field inference, generation, validation retries). Track total token consumption by passing a `TokenUsage` accumulator:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-1) from crawl4ai.models import TokenUsage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-3) usage = TokenUsage() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-4) schema = JsonCssExtractionStrategy.generate_schema( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-5)     url="https://example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-6)     query="extract product name and price", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-7)     usage=usage, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-8) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-171-9) print(f"Total tokens: {usage.total_tokens}")`

The `usage` parameter is optional and fully backward-compatible. Both `generate_schema` (sync) and `agenerate_schema` (async) support it.

### LLM Provider Options

1.  **OpenAI GPT-4 (`openai/gpt4o`)**
2.  Default provider
3.  Requires an API token
4.  Generally provides more accurate schemas
5.  Set via environment variable: `OPENAI_API_KEY`
6.  **Ollama (`ollama/llama3.3`)**
7.  Open source alternative
8.  No API token required
9.  Self-hosted option
10.  Good for development and testing

### Benefits of Schema Generation

### Best Practices

1.  **Choose Provider Wisely**:
2.  Use OpenAI for production-quality schemas
3.  Use Ollama for development, testing, or when you need a self-hosted solution

10\. Conclusion
---------------

With Crawl4AI's LLM-free extraction strategies - `JsonCssExtractionStrategy`, `JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`, and now `RegexExtractionStrategy` - you can build powerful pipelines that: - Scrape any consistent site for structured data.  
\- Support nested objects, repeating lists, or pattern-based extraction.  
\- Scale to thousands of pages quickly and reliably. - Use **`RegexExtractionStrategy`** for fast extraction of common data types like emails, phones, URLs, dates, etc. - Use **`JsonCssExtractionStrategy`** or **`JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`** for structured data with clear HTML patterns

Extracting JSON (LLM)
=====================

**Important**: LLM-based extraction can be slower and costlier than schema-based approaches. If your page data is highly structured, consider using [`JsonCssExtractionStrategy`](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/no-llm-strategies.md)
 or [`JsonXPathExtractionStrategy`](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/no-llm-strategies.md)
 first. But if you need AI to interpret or reorganize content, read on!

1\. Why Use an LLM?
-------------------

2\. Provider-Agnostic via LiteLLM
---------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-172-1) llmConfig = LlmConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", api_token=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))`

Crawl4AI uses a “provider string” (e.g., `"openai/gpt-4o"`, `"ollama/llama2.0"`, `"aws/titan"`) to identify your LLM. **Any** model that LiteLLM supports is fair game. You just provide: - **`provider`**: The `<provider>/<model_name>` identifier (e.g., `"openai/gpt-4"`, `"ollama/llama2"`, `"huggingface/google-flan"`, etc.).  
\- **`api_token`**: If needed (for OpenAI, HuggingFace, etc.); local models or Ollama might not require it.  
\- **`base_url`** (optional): If your provider has a custom endpoint.

3\. How LLM Extraction Works
----------------------------

### 3.1 Flow

1. **Chunking** (optional): The HTML or markdown is split into smaller segments if it’s very long (based on `chunk_token_threshold`, overlap, etc.).  
2. **Prompt Construction**: For each chunk, the library forms a prompt that includes your **`instruction`** (and possibly schema or examples).  
4. **Combining**: The results from each chunk are merged and parsed into JSON.

### 3.2 `extraction_type`

*   **`"schema"`**: The model tries to return JSON conforming to your Pydantic-based schema.
*   **`"block"`**: The model returns freeform text, or smaller JSON structures, which the library collects.  
    For structured data, `"schema"` is recommended. You provide `schema=YourPydanticModel.model_json_schema()`.

4\. Key Parameters
------------------

Below is an overview of important LLM extraction parameters. All are typically set inside `LLMExtractionStrategy(...)`. You then put that strategy in your `CrawlerRunConfig(..., extraction_strategy=...)`. 1. **`llmConfig`** (LlmConfig): e.g., `"openai/gpt-4"`, `"ollama/llama2"`.  
2. **`schema`** (dict): A JSON schema describing the fields you want. Usually generated by `YourModel.model_json_schema()`.  
3. **`extraction_type`** (str): `"schema"` or `"block"`.  
4. **`instruction`** (str): Prompt text telling the LLM what you want extracted. E.g., “Extract these fields as a JSON array.”  
5. **`chunk_token_threshold`** (int): Maximum tokens per chunk. If your content is huge, you can break it up for the LLM.  
6. **`overlap_rate`** (float): Overlap ratio between adjacent chunks. E.g., `0.1` means 10% of each chunk is repeated to preserve context continuity.  
7. **`apply_chunking`** (bool): Set `True` to chunk automatically. If you want a single pass, set `False`.  
8. **`input_format`** (str): Determines **which** crawler result is passed to the LLM. Options include:  
\- `"markdown"`: The raw markdown (default).  
\- `"fit_markdown"`: The filtered “fit” markdown if you used a content filter.  
\- `"html"`: The cleaned or raw HTML.  
9. **`extra_args`** (dict): Additional LLM parameters like `temperature`, `max_tokens`, `top_p`, etc.  
10. **`show_usage()`**: A method you can call to print out usage info (token usage per chunk, total cost if known).  

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-1) extraction_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-2)     llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4", api_token="YOUR_OPENAI_KEY"), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-3)     schema=MyModel.model_json_schema(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-4)     extraction_type="schema", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-5)     instruction="Extract a list of items from the text with 'name' and 'price' fields.", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-6)     chunk_token_threshold=1200, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-7)     overlap_rate=0.1, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-8)     apply_chunking=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-9)     input_format="html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-10)     extra_args={"temperature": 0.1, "max_tokens": 1000}, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-11)     verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-173-12) )`

5\. Putting It in `CrawlerRunConfig`
------------------------------------

**Important**: In Crawl4AI, all strategy definitions should go inside the `CrawlerRunConfig`, not directly as a param in `arun()`. Here’s a full example:

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-1) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-2) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-3) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-4) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-5) from typing import List [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-6) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-7) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-9) class Product(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-10)     name: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-11)     price: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-13) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-14)     # 1. Define the LLM extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-15)     llm_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-16)         llm_config = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4o-mini", api_token=os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY')), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-17)         schema=Product.schema_json(), # Or use model_json_schema() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-18)         extraction_type="schema", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-19)         instruction="Extract all product objects with 'name' and 'price' from the content.", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-20)         chunk_token_threshold=1000, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-21)         overlap_rate=0.0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-22)         apply_chunking=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-23)         input_format="markdown",   # or "html", "fit_markdown" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-24)         extra_args={"temperature": 0.0, "max_tokens": 800} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-25)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-27)     # 2. Build the crawler config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-28)     crawl_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-29)         extraction_strategy=llm_strategy, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-30)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-31)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-32) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-33)     # 3. Create a browser config if needed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-34)     browser_cfg = BrowserConfig(headless=True) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-36)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_cfg) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-37)         # 4. Let's say we want to crawl a single page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-38)         result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-39)             url="https://example.com/products", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-40)             config=crawl_config [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-41)         ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-42) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-43)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-44)             # 5. The extracted content is presumably JSON [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-45)             data = json.loads(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-46)             print("Extracted items:", data) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-47) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-48)             # 6. Show usage stats [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-49)             llm_strategy.show_usage()  # prints token usage [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-50)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-51)             print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-52) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-53) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-174-54)     asyncio.run(main())`

6\. Chunking Details
--------------------

### 6.1 `chunk_token_threshold`

If your page is large, you might exceed your LLM’s context window. **`chunk_token_threshold`** sets the approximate max tokens per chunk. The library calculates word→token ratio using `word_token_rate` (often ~0.75 by default). If chunking is enabled (`apply_chunking=True`), the text is split into segments.

### 6.2 `overlap_rate`

To keep context continuous across chunks, we can overlap them. E.g., `overlap_rate=0.1` means each subsequent chunk includes 10% of the previous chunk’s text. This is helpful if your needed info might straddle chunk boundaries.

### 6.3 Performance & Parallelism

7\. Input Format
----------------

By default, **LLMExtractionStrategy** uses `input_format="markdown"`, meaning the **crawler’s final markdown** is fed to the LLM. You can change to: - **`html`**: The cleaned HTML or raw HTML (depending on your crawler config) goes into the LLM.  
\- **`fit_markdown`**: If you used, for instance, `PruningContentFilter`, the “fit” version of the markdown is used. This can drastically reduce tokens if you trust the filter.  
\- **`markdown`**: Standard markdown output from the crawler’s `markdown_generator`. This setting is crucial: if the LLM instructions rely on HTML tags, pick `"html"`. If you prefer a text-based approach, pick `"markdown"`.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-175-1) LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-175-2)     # ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-175-3)     input_format="html",  # Instead of "markdown" or "fit_markdown" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-175-4) )`

8\. Token Usage & Show Usage
----------------------------

*   **`usages`** (list): token usage per chunk or call.
*   **`total_usage`**: sum of all chunk calls.
*   **`show_usage()`**: prints a usage report (if the provider returns usage data).
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-176-1) llm_strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy(...) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-176-2) # ... [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-176-3) llm_strategy.show_usage() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-176-4) # e.g. “Total usage: 1241 tokens across 2 chunk calls”`
    

9\. Example: Building a Knowledge Graph
---------------------------------------

Below is a snippet combining **`LLMExtractionStrategy`** with a Pydantic schema for a knowledge graph. Notice how we pass an **`instruction`** telling the model what to parse.

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-1) import os [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-3) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-4) from typing import List [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-5) from pydantic import BaseModel, Field [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-6) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode, LLMConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-7) from crawl4ai import LLMExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-9) class Entity(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-10)     name: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-11)     description: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-13) class Relationship(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-14)     entity1: Entity [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-15)     entity2: Entity [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-16)     description: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-17)     relation_type: str [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-18) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-19) class KnowledgeGraph(BaseModel): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-20)     entities: List[Entity] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-21)     relationships: List[Relationship] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-22) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-23) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-24)     # LLM extraction strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-25)     llm_strat = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-26)         llmConfig = LLMConfig(provider="openai/gpt-4", api_token=os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY')), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-27)         schema=KnowledgeGraph.model_json_schema(), [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-28)         extraction_type="schema", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-29)         instruction="Extract entities and relationships from the content. Return valid JSON.", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-30)         chunk_token_threshold=1400, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-31)         apply_chunking=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-32)         input_format="html", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-33)         extra_args={"temperature": 0.1, "max_tokens": 1500} [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-34)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-35) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-36)     crawl_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-37)         extraction_strategy=llm_strat, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-38)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-39)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-40) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-41)     async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=BrowserConfig(headless=True)) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-42)         # Example page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-43)         url = "https://www.nbcnews.com/business" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-44)         result = await crawler.arun(url=url, config=crawl_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-45) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-46)         print("--- LLM RAW RESPONSE ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-47)         print(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-48)         print("--- END LLM RAW RESPONSE ---") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-49) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-50)         if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-51)             with open("kb_result.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-52)                 f.write(result.extracted_content) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-53)             llm_strat.show_usage() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-54)         else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-55)             print("Crawl failed:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-56) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-57) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-177-58)     asyncio.run(main())`

\- **`extraction_type="schema"`** ensures we get JSON fitting our `KnowledgeGraph`.  
\- **`input_format="html"`** means we feed HTML to the model.  
\- **`instruction`** guides the model to output a structured knowledge graph.

10\. Best Practices & Caveats
-----------------------------

4. **Schema Strictness**: `"schema"` extraction tries to parse the model output as JSON. If the model returns invalid JSON, partial extraction might happen, or you might get an error.

11\. Conclusion
---------------

*   Put your LLM strategy **in `CrawlerRunConfig`**.
*   Use **`input_format`** to pick which form (markdown, HTML, fit\_markdown) the LLM sees.
*   Tweak **`chunk_token_threshold`**, **`overlap_rate`**, and **`apply_chunking`** to handle large content efficiently.
*   Monitor token usage with `show_usage()`. If your site’s data is consistent or repetitive, consider [`JsonCssExtractionStrategy`](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/no-llm-strategies.md)
     first for speed and simplicity. But if you need an **AI-driven** approach, `LLMExtractionStrategy` offers a flexible, multi-provider solution for extracting structured JSON from any website. 1. **Experiment with Different Providers**
*   Try switching the `provider` (e.g., `"ollama/llama2"`, `"openai/gpt-4o"`, etc.) to see differences in speed, accuracy, or cost.
*   Pass different `extra_args` like `temperature`, `top_p`, and `max_tokens` to fine-tune your results. 2. **Performance Tuning**
*   If pages are large, tweak `chunk_token_threshold`, `overlap_rate`, or `apply_chunking` to optimize throughput.
*   Check the usage logs with `show_usage()` to keep an eye on token consumption and identify potential bottlenecks. 3. **Validate Outputs**
*   If using `extraction_type="schema"`, parse the LLM’s JSON with a Pydantic model for a final validation step.  
    4. **Explore Hooks & Automation**

Advanced Features
=================

Session Management
==================

*   **Performing JavaScript actions before and after crawling.** Use `BrowserConfig` and `CrawlerRunConfig` to maintain state with a `session_id`:
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-1) from crawl4ai.async_configs import BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-3) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-4)     session_id = "my_session" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-6)     # Define configurations [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-7)     config1 = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-8)         url="https://example.com/page1", session_id=session_id [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-9)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-10)     config2 = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-11)         url="https://example.com/page2", session_id=session_id [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-12)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-13) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-14)     # First request [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-15)     result1 = await crawler.arun(config=config1) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-17)     # Subsequent request using the same session [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-18)     result2 = await crawler.arun(config=config2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-19) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-20)     # Clean up when done [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-178-21)     await crawler.crawler_strategy.kill_session(session_id)`
    
    `[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-179-1) from crawl4ai.async_configs import CrawlerRunConfig [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-179-2) from crawl4ai import JsonCssExtractionStrategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-179-3) from Example 1: Basic Session-Based Crawling
---------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-2) from crawl4ai.async_configs import [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-4) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-5) async def basic_session_crawl(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-9) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-10)         for page in range(3):              js_code="document.querySelector('.load-more-button').click();" if page > 0 else None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-15)                 css_selector=".content-item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-180-16)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS asyncio.run(basic_session_crawl())`

1\. Reusing the same `session_id` across multiple requests. 2. Executing JavaScript to load more content dynamically. 3. Properly closing the session to free resources.

Advanced Technique 1: Custom Execution Hooks
--------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-1) async def advanced_session_crawl_with_hooks(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-2) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-9)                 commit = await page.query_selector("li.commit-item h4") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-11)                 if commit and commit != first_commit: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-12)                     first_commit = commit [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-13)                     break [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-14)                 await asyncio.sleep(0.5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-15)         except Exception as e: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-16)  async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-19)         session_id = "commit_session" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-25)         for page in range(3): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-26)             config =       session_id=session_id, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-181-29)                 js_code=js_next_page if page > 0 else None, Advanced Technique 2: Integrated JavaScript Execution and Waiting
-----------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-1) async def integrated_js_and_wait_crawl(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-2)     async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-3)         session_id = "integrated_session" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-6)         js_next_page_and_wait = """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-7)         (async () => { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-8)             const getCurrentCommit = () => document.querySelector('li.commit-item h4').textContent.trim(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-9)             const initialCommit = getCurrentCommit(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-10)             document.querySelector('a.pagination-next').click(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-11)             while (getCurrentCommit() === initialCommit) { [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-12)                 await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100)); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-13)             } [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-14)         })(); [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-15)         """ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-16) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-17)         for page in range(3): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-18)             config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-19)                 url=url, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-20)                 session_id=session_id, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-21)                 js_code=js_next_page_and_wait if page > 0 else None, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-22)                 css_selector="li.commit-item", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-23)                 js_only=page > 0, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-24)                 cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-25)             ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-26) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-27)             result = await crawler.arun(config=config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-28)             print(f"Page {page + 1}: Found {len(result.extracted_content)} commits") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-29) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-30)         await crawler.crawler_strategy.kill_session(session_id) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-31) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-182-32)
