1. **Authentication Flows**: Login and interact with secured pages. 2. **Pagination Handling**: Navigate through multiple pages. 3. **Form Submissions**: Fill forms, submit, and process results. 4. **Multi-step Processes**: Complete workflows that span multiple actions.

Hooks & Auth in AsyncWebCrawler
===============================

1. **`on_browser_created`** – After browser creation.  
2. **`on_page_context_created`** – After a new context & page are created.  
3. **`before_goto`** – Just before navigating to a page.  
4. **`after_goto`** – Right after navigation completes.  
5. **`on_user_agent_updated`** – Whenever the user agent changes.  
6. **`on_execution_started`** – Once custom JavaScript execution begins.  
7. **`before_retrieve_html`** – Just before the crawler retrieves final HTML.  
8. **`before_return_html`** – Right before returning the HTML content. **Important**: Avoid heavy tasks in `on_browser_created` since you don’t yet have a page context. If you need to _log in_, do so in **`on_page_context_created`**.

> note "Important Hook Usage Warning" **Avoid Misusing Hooks**: Do not manipulate page objects in the wrong hook or at the wrong time, as it can crash the pipeline or produce incorrect results. A common mistake is attempting to handle authentication prematurely—such as creating or closing pages in `on_browser_created`. **Use the Right Hook for Auth**: If you need to log in or set tokens, use `on_page_context_created`. This ensures you have a valid page/context to work with, without disrupting the main crawling flow.

Example: Using Hooks in AsyncWebCrawler
---------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-2) import json [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-3) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-4) from playwright.async_api import Page, BrowserContext [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-5) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-6) async def main(): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-7)     print("🔗 Hooks Example: Demonstrating recommended usage") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-8) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-9)     # 1) Configure the browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-10)     browser_config = BrowserConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-11)         headless=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-12)         verbose=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-13)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-14) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-15)     # 2) Configure the crawler run [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-16)     crawler_run_config = CrawlerRunConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-17)         js_code="window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-18)         wait_for="body", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-19)         cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-20)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-21) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-22)     # 3) Create the crawler instance [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-23)     crawler = AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-24) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-25)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-26)     # Define Hook Functions [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-27)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-28) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-29)     async def on_browser_created(browser, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-30)         # Called once the browser instance is created (but no pages or contexts yet) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-31)         print("[HOOK] on_browser_created - Browser created successfully!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-32)         # Typically, do minimal setup here if needed [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-33)         return browser [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-34) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-35)     async def on_page_context_created(page: Page, context: BrowserContext, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-36)         # Called right after a new page + context are created (ideal for auth or route config). [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-37)         print("[HOOK] on_page_context_created - Setting up page & context.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-38) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-39)         # Example 1: Route filtering (e.g., block images) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-40)         async def route_filter(route): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-41)             if route.request.resource_type == "image": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-42)                 print(f"[HOOK] Blocking image request: {route.request.url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-43)                 await route.abort() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-44)             else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-45)                 await route.continue_() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-46) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-47)         await context.route("**", route_filter) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-48) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-49)         # Example 2: (Optional) Simulate a login scenario [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-50)         # (We do NOT create or close pages here, just do quick steps if needed) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-51)         # e.g., await page.goto("https://example.com/login") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-52)         # e.g., await page.fill("input[name='username']", "testuser") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-53)         # e.g., await page.fill("input[name='password']", "password123") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-54)         # e.g., await page.click("button[type='submit']") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-55)         # e.g., await page.wait_for_selector("#welcome") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-56)         # e.g., await context.add_cookies([...]) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-57)         # Then continue [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-58) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-59)         # Example 3: Adjust the viewport [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-60)         await page.set_viewport_size({"width": 1080, "height": 600}) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-61)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-62) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-63)     async def before_goto( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-64)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext, url: str, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-65)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-66)         # Called before navigating to each URL. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-67)         print(f"[HOOK] before_goto - About to navigate: {url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-68)         # e.g., inject custom headers [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-69)         await page.set_extra_http_headers({ [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-70)             "Custom-Header": "my-value" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-71)         }) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-72)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-73) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-74)     async def after_goto( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-75)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-76)         url: str, response, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-77)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-78)         # Called after navigation completes. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-79)         print(f"[HOOK] after_goto - Successfully loaded: {url}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-80)         # e.g., wait for a certain element if we want to verify [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-81)         try: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-82)             await page.wait_for_selector('.content', timeout=1000) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-83)             print("[HOOK] Found .content element!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-84)         except: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-85)             print("[HOOK] .content not found, continuing anyway.") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-86)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-87) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-88)     async def on_user_agent_updated( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-89)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext,  [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-90)         user_agent: str, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-91)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-92)         # Called whenever the user agent updates. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-93)         print(f"[HOOK] on_user_agent_updated - New user agent: {user_agent}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-94)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-95) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-96)     async def on_execution_started(page: Page, context: BrowserContext, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-97)         # Called after custom JavaScript execution begins. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-98)         print("[HOOK] on_execution_started - JS code is running!") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-99)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-100) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-101)     async def before_retrieve_html(page: Page, context: BrowserContext, **kwargs): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-102)         # Called before final HTML retrieval. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-103)         print("[HOOK] before_retrieve_html - We can do final actions") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-104)         # Example: Scroll again [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-105)         await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-106)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-107) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-108)     async def before_return_html( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-109)         page: Page, context: BrowserContext, html: str, **kwargs [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-110)     ): [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-111)         # Called just before returning the HTML in the result. [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-112)         print(f"[HOOK] before_return_html - HTML length: {len(html)}") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-113)         return page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-114) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-115)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-116)     # Attach Hooks [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-117)     # [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-118) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-119)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook("on_browser_created", on_browser_created) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-120)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-121)         "on_page_context_created", on_page_context_created [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-122)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-123)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook("before_goto", before_goto) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-124)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook("after_goto", after_goto) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-125)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-126)         "on_user_agent_updated", on_user_agent_updated [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-127)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-128)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-129)         "on_execution_started", on_execution_started [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-130)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-131)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-132)         "before_retrieve_html", before_retrieve_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-133)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-134)     crawler.crawler_strategy.set_hook( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-135)         "before_return_html", before_return_html [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-136)     ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-137) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-138)     await crawler.start() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-139) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-140)     # 4) Run the crawler on an example page [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-141)     url = "https://example.com" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-142)     result = await crawler.arun(url, config=crawler_run_config) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-143) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-144)     if result.success: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-145)         print("\nCrawled URL:", result.url) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-146)         print("HTML length:", len(result.html)) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-147)     else: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-148)         print("Error:", result.error_message) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-149) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-150)     await crawler.close() [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-151) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-152) if __name__ == "__main__": [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-183-153)     asyncio.run(main())`

Hook Lifecycle Summary
----------------------

1. **`on_browser_created`**:  
\- Browser is up, but **no** pages or contexts yet.  
\- Light setup only—don’t try to open or close pages here (that belongs in `on_page_context_created`). 2. **`on_page_context_created`**:  
\- Perfect for advanced **auth** or route blocking.  
3. **`before_goto`**:  
4. **`after_goto`**:  
5. **`on_user_agent_updated`**:  
\- Whenever the user agent changes (for stealth or different UA modes). 6. **`on_execution_started`**:  
\- If you set `js_code` or run custom scripts, this runs once your JS is about to start. 7. **`before_retrieve_html`**:  
8. **`before_return_html`**:  
\- The last hook before returning HTML to the `CrawlResult`. Good for logging HTML length or minor modifications.

When to Handle Authentication
-----------------------------

**Recommended**: Use **`on_page_context_created`** if you need to: - Navigate to a login page or fill forms - Set cookies or localStorage tokens - Block resource routes to avoid ads This ensures the newly created context is under your control **before** `arun()` navigates to the main URL.

Additional Considerations
-------------------------

*   **Session Management**: If you want multiple `arun()` calls to reuse a single session, pass `session_id=` in your `CrawlerRunConfig`. Hooks remain the same.
*   **Concurrency**: If you run `arun_many()`, each URL triggers these hooks in parallel. Ensure your hooks are thread/async-safe.

Conclusion
----------

*   **Browser** creation (light tasks only)
*   **Page** and **context** creation (auth, route blocking)
*   **Navigation** phases
*   **Final HTML** retrieval
*   **Login** or advanced tasks in `on_page_context_created`
*   **Custom headers** or logs in `before_goto` / `after_goto`
*   **Scrolling** or final checks in `before_retrieve_html` / `before_return_html`

* * *

Quick Reference
===============

Core Imports
------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-184-1) from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerConfig, CacheMode [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-184-2) from crawl4ai.extraction_strategy import LLMExtractionStrategy, JsonCssExtractionStrategy, CosineStrategy`

Basic Pattern
-------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-185-1) async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-185-2)     result = await crawler.arun(url="https://example.com") [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-185-3)     print(result.markdown)`

Advanced Pattern
----------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-1) browser_config = BrowserConfig(headless=True, viewport_width=1920) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-2) crawler_config = CrawlerConfig( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-3)     cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-4)     wait_for="css:.content", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-5)     screenshot=True, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-6)     pdf=True [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-7) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-8) strategy = LLMExtractionStrategy( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-9)     provider="openai/gpt-4", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-10)     instruction="Extract products with name and price" [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-11) ) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-12) [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-13) async with AsyncWebCrawler(config=browser_config) as crawler: [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-14)     result = await crawler.arun( [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-15)         url="https://example.com", [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-16)         config=crawler_config, [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-17)         extraction_strategy=strategy [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-186-18)     )`

Multi-URL Pattern
-----------------

`[](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-187-1) urls = ["https://example.com/1", "https://example.com/2"] [](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/complete-sdk-reference/#__codelineno-187-2) results = await crawler.arun_many(urls, config=crawler_config)`

* * *

# Crawl4AI LLM Context Builder

🧠 A New Approach to LLM Context
--------------------------------

Traditional `llm.txt` files often fail with complex libraries like Crawl4AI. They dump massive amounts of API documentation, causing **information overload** and **lost focus**. They provide the "what" but miss the crucial "how" and "why" that makes AI assistants truly helpful.

### 💡 The Solution: Multi-Dimensional, Modular Contexts

Inspired by modular libraries like Lodash, I've redesigned how we provide context to AI assistants. Instead of one monolithic file, Crawl4AI's documentation is organized by **components** and **perspectives**.

Memory

#### The "What"

Precise API facts, parameters, signatures, and configuration objects. Your unambiguous reference.

Reasoning

#### The "How" & "Why"

Design principles, best practices, trade-offs, and workflows. Teaches AI to think like an expert.

Examples

#### The "Show Me"

Runnable code snippets demonstrating patterns in action. Pure practical implementation.

**Why this matters:** You can now give your AI assistant exactly what it needs - whether that's quick API lookups, help designing solutions, or seeing practical implementations. No more information overload, just focused, relevant context.

[📖 Read the full story behind this approach →](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/blog/articles/llm-context-revolution)

# Crawl4AI Assistant - Chrome Extension for Visual Web Scraping

 Your browser does not support the video tag.

### You don't need Puppeteer. You need Crawl4AI Cloud.

One API call. JS-rendered. No browser cluster to maintain.

Get API Key →

About Crawl4AI Assistant

Transform any website into structured data with just a few clicks! The Crawl4AI Assistant Chrome Extension provides three powerful tools for web scraping and data extraction.

🎉 NEW: Click2Crawl extracts data INSTANTLY without any LLM! Test your schema and see JSON results immediately in the browser!

🎯

### Click2Crawl

Visual data extraction - click elements to build schemas instantly!

🔴

### Script Builder (Alpha)

Record browser actions to create automation scripts

📝

### Markdown Extraction (New!)

Convert any webpage content to clean markdown with Visual Text Mode

Quick Start
-----------

Installation

1

#### Download the Extension

Get the latest release from GitHub or use the button below

[↓ Download Extension (v1.3.0)](https://docs.crawl4ai.com/apps/crawl4ai-assistant/crawl4ai-assistant-v1.3.0.zip)

2

#### Load in Chrome

Navigate to `chrome://extensions/` and enable Developer Mode

3

#### Load Unpacked

Click "Load unpacked" and select the extracted extension folder

See the Generated Code & Extracted Data
---------------------------------------

🎯 Click2Crawl 🔴 Script Builder 📝 Markdown Extraction

click2crawl\_extraction.py Copy

    #!/usr/bin/env python3
    """
    🎉 NO LLM NEEDED! Direct extraction with CSS selectors
    Generated by Crawl4AI Chrome Extension - Click2Crawl
    """
    
    import asyncio
    import json
    from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, BrowserConfig, CrawlerRunConfig
    from crawl4ai.extraction_strategy import JsonCssExtractionStrategy
    
    # The EXACT schema from Click2Crawl - no guessing!
    EXTRACTION_SCHEMA = {
        "name": "Product Catalog",
        "baseSelector": "div.product-card",  # The container you selected
        "fields": [\
            {\
                "name": "title",\
                "selector": "h3.product-title",\
                "type": "text"\
            },\
            {\
                "name": "price",\
                "selector": "span.price",\
                "type": "text"\
            },\
            {\
                "name": "image",\
                "selector": "img.product-img",\
                "type": "attribute",\
                "attribute": "src"\
            },\
            {\
                "name": "link",\
                "selector": "a.product-link",\
                "type": "attribute",\
                "attribute": "href"\
            }\
        ]
    }
    
    async def extract_data(url: str):
        # Direct extraction - no LLM API calls!
        extraction_strategy = JsonCssExtractionStrategy(schema=EXTRACTION_SCHEMA)
        
        async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler:
            result = await crawler.arun(
                url=url,
                config=CrawlerRunConfig(extraction_strategy=extraction_strategy)
            )
            
            if result.success:
                data = json.loads(result.extracted_content)
                print(f"✅ Extracted {len(data)} items instantly!")
                
                # Save to file
                with open('products.json', 'w') as f:
                    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
                
                return data
    
    # Run extraction on any similar page!
    data = asyncio.run(extract_data("https://example.com/products"))
    
    # 🎯 Result: Clean JSON data, no LLM costs, instant results!

automation\_script.py Copy

    import asyncio
    from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig
    
    # JavaScript generated from your recorded actions
    js_script = """
    // Search for products
    document.querySelector('button.search-toggle').click();
    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500));
    
    // Type search query
    const searchInput = document.querySelector('input#search');
    searchInput.value = 'wireless headphones';
    searchInput.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles: true}));
    
    // Submit search
    searchInput.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown', {
        key: 'Enter', keyCode: 13, bubbles: true
    }));
    
    // Wait for results
    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000));
    
    // Click first product
    document.querySelector('.product-item:first-child').click();
    
    // Wait for product page
    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000));
    
    // Add to cart
    document.querySelector('button.add-to-cart').click();
    """
    
    async def automate_shopping():
        config = CrawlerRunConfig(
            js_code=js_script,
            wait_for="css:.cart-confirmation",
            screenshot=True
        )
        
        async with AsyncWebCrawler() as crawler:
            result = await crawler.arun(
                url="https://shop.example.com",
                config=config
            )
            print(f"✓ Automation complete: {result.url}")
            return result
    
    asyncio.run(automate_shopping())

    # Extracted from Hacker News with Visual Text Mode 👁️
    
    1. **Show HN: I built a tool to find and reach out to YouTubers** (hellosimply.io)
       84 points by erickim 2 hours ago | hide | 31 comments
    
    2. **The 24 Hour Restaurant** (logicmag.io)
       124 points by helsinkiandrew 5 hours ago | hide | 52 comments
    
    3. **Building a Better Bloom Filter in Rust** (carlmastrangelo.com)
       89 points by carlmastrangelo 3 hours ago | hide | 27 comments
    
    ---
    
    ### Article: The 24 Hour Restaurant
    
    In New York City, the 24-hour restaurant is becoming extinct. What we lose when we can no longer eat whenever we want.
    
    When I first moved to New York, I loved that I could get a full meal at 3 AM. Not just pizza or fast food, but a proper sit-down dinner with table service and a menu that ran for pages. The city that never sleeps had restaurants that matched its rhythm.
    
    Today, finding a 24-hour restaurant in Manhattan requires genuine effort. The pandemic accelerated a decline that was already underway, but the roots go deeper: rising rents, changing labor laws, and shifting cultural patterns have all contributed to the death of round-the-clock dining.
    
    ---
    
    ### Product Review: Framework Laptop 16
    
    **Specifications:**
    - Display: 16" 2560×1600 165Hz
    - Processor: AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS
    - Memory: 32GB DDR5-5600
    - Storage: 2TB NVMe Gen4
    - Price: Starting at $1,399
    
    **Pros:**
    - Fully modular and repairable
    - Excellent Linux support
    - Great keyboard and trackpad
    - Expansion card system
    
    **Cons:**
    - Battery life could be better
    - Slightly heavier than competitors
    - Fan noise under load
